title: Quantitative Evaluation Method for Well integrity Risk in Heavy Oil Steam Stimulation Wells
authors: Xueqiang Wang, Haiqing Long, Hongliang Yi, Jian Ding, Jinlong Wang, Chuanlei Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: After multiple high-temperature cyclic steam stimulation (CSS) operations, the barrier performance of heavy oil wells gradually deteriorates, leading to potential risks of high-temperature steam leakage. To address the well integrity risk associated with heavy oil CSS wells, a barrier risk evaluation framework and design criteria were developed based on relevant well integrity standards and the specific characteristics of CSS injection–production processes. The proposed method considers the effects of key operating parameters—including injection temperature, injection pressure, injection rate, and the number of steam stimulation cycles—under high-temperature and multi-cycle working conditions. A barrier damage coefficient was introduced to quantify barrier degradation, while the combined effects of high-temperature steam leakage, thermal diffusion, and overpressure injury were analyzed to evaluate barrier failure risk. On this basis, a quantitative assessment method for well integrity risk in heavy oil CSS wells was established and validated through a field case study. The results show that the proposed model can quantitatively determine the wellbore integrity risk level and identify dominant risk factors. Considering the degradation of barrier performance under high-temperature service conditions, the calculated failure probability increases from 3.523 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−5</sup> to 10.774 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−5</sup>. The introduction of the barrier damage coefficient significantly enhances the objectivity of the evaluation. When the injection temperature rises from 200 °C to 400 °C, the steam leakage influence radius increases from 10.61 m to 11.49 m, indicating that injection temperature is the primary factor influencing wellbore integrity failure risk. Moreover, the proposed method establishes a quantitative and interpretable integrity risk representation, which provides a foundation for foundation-model-driven risk inference, blockchain-based integrity data management, and intelligent monitoring systems for thermal recovery wells.

title: RLMToT: A Thought Tree-Based Model for Sentiment Triplet Extraction from MOOC Reviews
authors: Huaibo Chen, Huibing Zhang, Lei Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Accurately identifying aspect terms and their implicit sentiments in MOOC reviews provides valuable insights for improving course quality. This paper proposes a Thought Tree-based sentiment triplet extraction model for MOOC reviews, aiming to precisely detect aspect terms, opinion expressions, and sentiment polarities. First, a MOOC-specific knowledge base is constructed using retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) techniques based on course metadata and lecture transcripts, enhancing the model’s ability to identify aspect terms. Then, a Tree of Thought (ToT) reasoning mechanism is introduced to guide the large language model (LLM) in step-by-step inference of aspect terms, opinion expressions, and sentiment polarities. At each stage, the model evaluates and refines intermediate outputs to ensure the accuracy and completeness of sentiment triplet extraction. Experimental results on the constructed MOOC dataset show that the proposed model achieves an F1-score of $62.71 \%$. Furthermore, comparative experiments on four benchmark datasets-Lap14, Res14, Res15, and Res16demonstrate that the model outperforms baseline methods, with F1-score improvements of $6.03 \%, 4.84 \%, 9.08 \%$, and $5.69 \%$, respectively.

title: A Deep Feature Extraction Method Based on Variational Inference and Self-Attention Mechanism of Graph Neural Networks
authors: Rui Feng, Qingsen Cai, Aruhan Bao, Hu Li
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Complex hierarchical dependencies and upstream/downstream mappings exist among the items in equipment procurement projects, making anomaly identification difficult to achieve using traditional rule-based or independent sample-based methods. To address this issue, this paper abstracts equipment procurement data into a graph structure and proposes a graph neural network feature extraction method (VGAE-AttnGNN) that integrates variational inference and self-attention mechanisms to model the structural relationships and potential uncertainties among equipment procurement items. Experimental results on a real equipment procurement dataset show that this method achieves $90.6 \%$ accuracy and $84.3 \%$ Macro-F1 scores, respectively, representing a stable improvement over comparative methods.

title: A Multi-Strategy Enhanced Zebra Optimization Algorithm and Its Application
authors: Jiatong Shang, Shengli Xie, Wei Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: Constrained engineering optimization problems are often characterized by high dimensionality, strong nonlinearity, and multiple local optima, posing significant challenges for traditional methods. This paper aims to improve the performance of the zebra optimization algorithm (ZOA) for such complex problems. However, there is an imbalance between exploration and exploitation, a loss of population diversity, and poor local exploitation in the original ZOA. To address these issues, an improved ZOA (IZOA) is proposed by integrating differential evolution (DE), lens imaging opposition-based learning (LIOBL), Lévy flight, and a dynamic scaling factor. These strategies are integrated into many algorithmic phases to create a synergistic framework that improves both local exploitation and global exploration, boosting convergence robustness and accuracy Experimental results on CEC benchmark functions and a compression spring design problem show that IZOA outperforms several state-of-the-art algorithms. In particular, it achieves the best mean objective value of 0.01270956, demonstrating higher accuracy and stability for constrained engineering optimization.

title: Effective Missing Data Imputation Techniques Utilizing Regression Methods
authors: Xiaomei Feng
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of the information age, vast amounts of data are being generated, often leading to the common occurrence of missing values. This study presents a statistical imputation method using regression to effectively fill in these gaps. Two real-world public datasets with $\mathbf{5 0 \%}$ missing values, were analyzed, comparing the performance results to both the original datasets and existing mode imputation methods. The findings indicate that the regression imputation method significantly enhances accuracy, with an improvement of up to 8.95% in prediction accuracy. These results have important implications for data-driven decision-making across various fields.

title: Research on Evaluating the Operational and Maintenance Development Level of Smart Grids under the Generation-Grid-Load-Storage Environment
authors: Guanglin Hu, Chen Yang, Jiao Yan, Yueping Zhong
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: The phenomenon of global warming has drawn significant attention from countries around the world. As the highest proportion of carbon emissions, the power industry’s low-carbon transformation has become the focus of concern. New electricity system, with the integration of generation-grid-load-storage (GGLS), provides an important means for addressing the generation of renewable and clean energy. The quality and safety of power supply are directly related to the effectiveness of intelligent operation and maintenance management of GGLS. Therefore, evaluating its intelligent operation and maintenance management level is of great significance for improving the clean and stable power supply. The paper firstly establishes an indicator system for intelligent operation and maintenance management. Then, based on the indicator system, the fuzzy analytic hierarchy process, capable of scientifically and conveniently handling both qualitative and quantitative data, is selected as the evaluation method. Finally, we conduct empirical analysis in combination with the intelligent operation and maintenance management of project D under GGLS environment. The research results confirm the effectiveness of the indicator system and method proposed, and provide relevant suggestions through examples. They can provide theoretical support and practical guidance for the intelligent operation and maintenance management of GGLS and also offer references and guidance for the intelligent operation and maintenance management of other similar power enterprises.

title: Research on the Design of Information Network Security Defense System Based on GABP Neural Network Algorithm
authors: Qingqing Ren, Kai Yun, Haomiao Tian, Wanpeng Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the rapid development of information network, network security has become a key issue. This paper presents the design and research of information network security defense system based on genetic algorithm back propagation (GABP) neural network algorithm. The system integrates data preprocessing, feature extraction, intrusion detection and defense strategy generation through modular architecture. The two-stage feature extraction method of information gain and PCA is adopted, and the BP neural network is optimized by genetic algorithm to improve the convergence speed and detection performance. The results show that the accuracy rate of GABP neural network is 97.9%, the accuracy rate is 98.1%, the recall rate is 98.2%, and the F1 score is 98.0%, which are better than the traditional model. The system can adaptively generate defense strategies, with a high average interception rate against four types of attacks and a normal traffic passing rate of more than 99.3%. Improve the efficiency and accuracy of network intrusion detection and defense, and provide new methods for information network security defense.

title: Stabilizing Affective Representations with CLAIRE: A Cognitive-Linguistic Architecture for Interpretable Reasoning and Emotion
authors: Vishwa Bhatt, Dylan M. Diaz, Divya Thakar, Mira Kim, Yunfei Hou, Soo Dong Kim, Jennifer Jin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: This study presents CLAIRE (Cognitive-Linguistic Architecture for Interpretable Reasoning and Emotion), a unified framework that integrates cognitively motivated regularization, transformer-based language modeling, and post-hoc neuro-symbolic reasoning to advance affective text classification. CLAIRE incorporates cognitive priors expressed through entropy-based attention regularization to promote stable, psychologically plausible focus patterns during affective representation learning. These priors guide a transformer architecture that combines pretrained encoder fine-tuning with multi-task learning, enabling both coarse-grained and fine-grained emotion recognition under a shared representation. A post-hoc symbolic reasoning layer further provides transparent, rule-based explanations without influencing neural prediction. The framework is evaluated under full-dataset conditions on the GoEmotions and SemEval-2018 Affect in Tweets benchmarks. Experimental results demonstrate that entropy regularization consistently improves classification performance over standard transformer baselines, increasing accuracy from 0.765 to 0.796 on GoEmotions and from 0.703 to 0.728 on SemEval-2018 for coarse-grained emotion classification, with comparable gains observed for fine-grained emotion recognition. These findings indicate that cognitively inspired regularization supports stable performance improvements across multiple affective datasets under in-domain evaluation, while preserving interpretability.

title: Multistage Constant Current Wireless Charging Strategy Based on Multiharmonic Power Transmisson
authors: Wenzhi Zheng, Wei Deng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Due to its safety and convenience, wireless charging technology has been widely adopted in electric vehicle chargers. However, in the typical constant voltage(CV) following constant current(CC) battery charging process, the output current is not adjustable with high flexibility, and the temperature rise is fast, which leads to the reduction of charging speed. In order to solve the above problems, a multistage constant current(MSCC) wireless charging strategy based on multiharmonic power transfer is proposed in this paper for charging lithium batteries in electric vehicles. The charging efficiency is above 92% in the second to sixth stages of the dominant battery charging. In addition, it provides a pre-charging stage for deeply discharged batteries to increase their lifetime.

title: An Algorithm-Centric Computational Framework for Near-Scope 3 Carbon Diagnostics: Fixed-Effects Learning, PSO-NN Ensemble Forecasting, and Uncertainty-Aware Scenario Analytics
authors: Ziyuan Jia, Kaiyuan Bai
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Definition, sample protocol, equations, and robustness design were revised. This paper proposes an algorithm-centric framework for macro near-Scope 3 diagnostics. NearScope 3 is explicitly defined as a macro trade-embedded proxy, $G_{c, t}=E_{c, t}^{{cons }}-E_{c, t}^{{prod }}$, rather than firm-level Scope 3 accounting. We reconcile descriptive, estimation, and forecasting samples, then combine small-sample panel inference (HC3, leave-one-entity-out, year-block bootstrap) with PSO-NN ensemble fore-casting and chronology-consistent validation. Mathematically, the PSO constriction factor, learning-rate schedule, and sigmoid price path are corrected. Uncertainty propagation uses moving-block residual resampling, and scenario analysis separates signed accounting burden from non-negative exposure burden. The revised manuscript is reproducible, computationally explicit, and methodologically defensible for engineering-oriented review.

title: Research on Optimization of Logistics Network Inspection Path Based on Genetic Algorithm
authors: Lixin Xue, Shuang Zhang, Haoyang Liu, Chengyan Liu, Yongyi Sun
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the continuous development of the e-commerce industry, logistics hubs have become deeply intertwined with people’s daily lives. Consumers can purchase goods from thousands of miles away with just a few taps on their smartphones. However, behind this convenience and efficiency, we must also recognize that regulatory oversights at some logistics hubs have been exploited by criminals, enabling the circulation of illicit drugs. Therefore, inspecting logistics hubs is a critical task in public security patrols. To enhance inspection efficiency and overcome the limitations of individual experience in route optimization, this paper introduces genetic algorithms to optimize inspection routes for logistics hubs. Experimental results demonstrate that genetic algorithms effectively address route planning challenges. The optimized routes achieve time savings and shorter distances, highlighting the effectiveness and practicality of genetic algorithms in logistics anti-drug inspection scenarios.

title: Personalized Federated Learning for Sovereign Personal AI Agents: A Review
authors: Adil Akhmetov, Batyr Sharimbayev, Mohammed Alaa Ala'anzy
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: The field of Artificial Intelligence (AI) is at an inflection point, shifting from the “One Model Fits All” philosophy represented by centralized models like ChatGPT to “Hyper-Personalized AI” that honors user data sovereignty while retaining the collaborative learning advantages. This review examines the convergence of Personalized Federated Learning (PFL), Large Language Models (LLMs), and privacy-preserving technologies to create truly sovereign personal AI agents. We review over 39 peer-reviewed articles (2020-2025) on these topics, with particular emphasis on three major areas: architectural innovations in personalization using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) adapters and knowledge graphs, privacy-preserving technologies like Differential Privacy (DP) and secure aggregation, and frameworks that allow users' personal data to be used to train personal agents locally without requiring cloud access. Our review demonstrates that PFL is at the core of creating the “Sovereign Data Ecosystem” where users can retain personal data like their calendar, email, and health records locally while contributing to global model improvements through aggregated gradient sharing. We also outline future research directions towards Federated Agent Swarms and how PFL redefines the relationship between user data ownership, model personalization, and algorithmic transparency.

title: Effect of Rotation on MHD Peristaltic Flow With Heat Transfer in a Tapered Wall
authors: Mohammed Salim Ramadhan, Khalid Khaleefah Jassim
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: This study examines how rotation affects magnetohydrodynamic (MHD) peristaltic movement in a canal with a tapering. The equations that govern the system describing the thermal flow are first formulated. Then, by adopting the lengthy-wavelength and minimal Reynolds number Estimate, the solution is acquired. Expressions the velocity field, temperature distribution, stream function are derived. The results are not left at that. The effects of various physical parameters on the system are examined through numerical methods and graphical representations. The core of this analysis investigates the interplay of rotation, wall tapering, and a transverse magnetic field in determining the peristaltic motion of an electrically conducting Newtonian fluid. Specifically, the model is applied to a two-dimensional asymmetric channel. The momentum and energy equations are constructed while accounting for key effects such as the Lorentz force, the Coriolis force, and viscous dissipation. The problem is simplified and explicit analytical solutions for the temperature and velocity distributions, stream function are obtained by applying the long-wavelength and low Reynolds number computations. The research provides important new information about the flow and heat transmission control mechanisms in these kinds of structures.

title: AIGC Enhanced Anomaly Detection in Industrial Internet of Things
authors: Kamilla Abdullayeva, Diyorbek Gaffarov, Xasan Zikiryoyrv, Jafar Abbas, Wesley O'Quinn
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: With the advent of Industry 4.0, the ubiquity of sensors in large scale industrial equipment increased exponentially. This flood of cheap and reliable sensors allowed monitoring of critical equipment with real-time cloud access to the readings. With most industries squarely in the middle of this paradigm, the ability to utilize this sensor data is at the forefront in many industries. The methodology utilized to accomplish predictions typically revolves around semi-supervised learning, where models are only trained on good data. The reason this methodology is needed is due to the lack of anomalous data to train models on. This is not ideal in some cases, as supervised learning can be a powerful tool, particularly when there is a desire to predict Remaining Useful Life (RUL). Recent advances in generative artificial intelligence–such as diffusion learning–potentially enables the ability to increase the training set in such a way that supervised learning can be used in this anomaly detection framework. This work investigates the use of Artificial Intelligence Generated Content (AIGC) for the purpose of providing augmented training samples. We demonstrate that current methods can generate extremely high quality training samples in this context and improve predictions metrics.

title: PPSplitFed: Privacy-Preserving Splitfed Learning via Functional Encryption
authors: Jing Ma, Xixiang Lyu, Yong Yu, Stephan Sigg
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computing
abstract: Federated learning (FL) and split learning (SL) are two emerging distributed learning mechanisms that enable model training without sharing the local data. The integration of FL and SL, called splitfed learning (SFL), reduces the training burden on the client side and parallelizes the model training to achieve efficient distributed model optimization without sharing the local data and the whole global model. Currently, concerns about the privacy leakage risks of splitfed learning have garnered much attention, but effective solutions have not yet been fully explored. We propose a novel privacy-preserving splitfed learning framework, named PPSplitFed, that guarantees the confidentiality of the intermediate parameters to protect data and model privacy. By designing a secure model training algorithm based on single-input functional encryption and a secure model aggregation algorithm based on multi-input func tional encryption, PPSplitFed achieves secure model optimization. We describe the implementations of PPSplitFed on Multi-layer Perceptron (MLP) and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) models and compare the performance with centralized learning (CL), FL, SL, SFL, and the state-of-the-art privacy-preserving SFL scheme, as well as privacy-preserving model training scheme on multi-sourced datasets. The experimental results show that PPSplitFed is effective and efficient with reduced computation and communication costs while maintaining the model accuracy.

title: DCGFI: Robust and Interpretable Failure Identification Using Dual Causal Graph for Microservices on Multimodal Observability Data
authors: Xiangbo Tian, Shi Ying, Tiangang Li, Ning Li, Chuan Shi, Ding Xiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Accurate failure identification is crucial for ensuring the reliability of microservice systems. However, existing failure identification approaches are usually based on data-driven correlation learning strategies. These approaches focus on statistical correlation in observability data and ignore the causal mechanism in microservice systems, which leads to insufficient robustness and interpretability. In this study, we propose DCGFI, a robust and interpretable failure identification approach based on multimodal observability data, which uses dual causal graphs to integrate causal modeling, data-driven strategy and knowledge driven strategy to achieve effective failure identification for microservice systems. DCGFI first constructs data causal graphs and knowledge causal graphs based on multimodal observability data. Then, DCGFI uses a causal graph convolutional network to learn data causal graphs and update knowledge causal graphs. Finally, DCGFI combines data causal graphs with knowledge causal graphs to jointly optimize the failure identification process. Experimental results on two datasets demonstrate that DCGFI outperforms all baselines on both Macro-F1 and Micro-F1 and exhibits good robustness across different datasets.

title: Health-aware Economic Model Predictive Control of Wave Energy Converters Based on a Reliability Metric
authors: Amin Ziaei, Hafiz Ahsan Said, John V. Ringwood
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Control Systems
abstract: Conventional energy-maximising controllers for wave energy converters (WECs) often exaggerate device motion, which can shorten device lifetime and increase operational costs (OpEx), by reducing maintenance intervals and system reliability. This paper proposes a novel health-aware WEC controller to achieve a suitable trade-off between captured energy and device lifetime, ultimately leading to a lower levelised cost of energy (LCoE). Using a reliability metric, the proposed controller incorporates power take-off degradation into the performance function of an economic model predictive controller (EMPC). The proposed performance function does not require additional (regularisation) terms to convexify the optimisation problem, as is often necessary for typical energy-maximising WEC EMPCs. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed controller in achieving an optimal trade off between captured energy and device longevity. Furthermore, this study examines potential challenges in the implementation of health-aware control for WECs and outlines key directions for future research in the health-aware WEC control domain.

title: Dual Event-Triggered Remote Information-based State Estimation with Measurement Outliers
authors: Tao Cui, Peng Dong, Zhongliang Jing, Kai Shen, Wujun Chen, Yuanbo Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: This paper examines event-triggered remote state estimation in scenarios with measurement outliers. To address the issue of trigger failures caused by outliers, a dual event-triggered criterion is proposed to ensure the transmission of informative and reliable data. Considering that feedback values in closed-loop structures may be corrupted by outliers, the trigger vector is constructed using local sensor information, rather than feedback from the estimator. For state estimation, a minimum-mean-square-error (MMSE) estimation method is first deduced within the information filter framework. To address the exponential growth in posterior Gaussian components induced by the dual trigger mechanism, a mode dependent time-varying transition matrix is introduced, enabling an interacting multiple model (IMM) approximation approach based on conditional transition probabilities. Moreover, the boundedness of error covariance is theoretically established. Simulations validate that the proposed algorithm exhibits strong robustness under measurement outliers and achieves good performance.

title: Unsupervised Domain Adaptation based Cross-Type Deepfake Image Detection
authors: Qin Wang, Xiaofeng Wang, Zinian Liu, Ningning Bai, Minghua Zhao, Shanmin Pang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: In practical application of social media and the Internet, deepfake face images involves a plethora of unlabeled samples. To effectively identify unlabeled deepfake images, the domain adaptation technique has gained significant attention. It applies the knowledge learned from labeled samples (source domain) to unlabeled samples (target domain) in a cross-domain manner. However, the existing domain adaptation-based deepfake detection methods primarily focus on intra-type cross-domain scenarios. In this study, we propose an unsupervised domain adaptation-based deepfake face image detection method for extra-type cross-domain scenarios. The core idea of our approach lies in the development of a domain adaptation model that consists of Domain Tag Adversarial (DTA) and Domain Feature Alignment (DFA) algorithms, called DTA-DFA, which empowers the proposed method with strong cross-domain capability. The DTA is utilized to weaken the specificity within each domain, while DFA aligns the distribution between the source and target domains. Compared with the existing deepfake detection methods, the experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method dramatically enhances the extra-type cross-domain detection performance. Moreover, the DTA-DFA model also exhibits a remarkable ability to perform cross-domain detection from large-shot labeled samples to few-shot labeled samples, further verifying its powerful cross-domain capability. Code is released at https://github.com/QinQin741/DTA-DFA-DA-model.

title: Multi-Priority Reactive Motion Control for Safe and Coordinated Dual-Arm Manipulation in Dynamic Environments_supp1-3686598.mp4
authors: Ze Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: Reactive motion generation for dual-arm robotic systems is challenging due to their high degrees of freedom, nonlinear characteristics as well as the presence of multiple constraints, including kinematic limits, collision avoidance, dual-arm coordination, and other task-specific requirements. These constraints may become incompatible especially in dynamic operational scenarios. This paper presents a multi-priority reactive motion control framework to address the above challenges. First, a novel time-varying control barrier function leveraging multi-body distance blending is proposed to formulate dynamic whole-body collision avoidance constraint. Then, a constraint prioritization mechanism is introduced to incorporate multiple task objectives into a single optimization-based controller, where the constraints are resolved in strict order of priority using hierarchical quadratic programming. The proposed control framework is extensively validated in both simulations and real-world experiments, with results consistently demonstrating its ability to generate both safe and coordinated reactive motions across various dual-arm collaboration tasks.

title: A Multi-View Deep Representation Learning Approach for Drug Discovery and Target Affinity Estimation
authors: Xi Qin, Hongjing Yang, Weijie Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Drug discovery relies on effective representation learning to capture the complex structural and semantic characteristics of molecules and biological targets. However, existing deep learning methods often focus on single-view representations and fail to fully exploit complementary information from heterogeneous molecular descriptors and structural views. In this paper, we propose a multi-view deep representation learning framework for computational drug discovery that integrates multiple molecular and target representations into a unified embedding space. The proposed approach jointly learns view-specific encoders and a shared latent representation through cross-view consistency regularization, enabling the model to capture both local structural patterns and global biochemical semantics. Extensive experiments on benchmark drug discovery datasets demonstrate that the learned representations exhibit strong discriminative capability and generalization performance in downstream screening and similarity retrieval tasks. The proposed framework provides an effective and scalable representation learning paradigm for large-scale drug discovery and molecular analysis.

title: Research on Key Technology of High-quality Scientific and Technological Achievements Screening and Evaluation Based on Deep Learning
authors: Jing Luo
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: This study investigates the challenges in screening and evaluating scientific and technological achievements. With the dramatic surge in research output in recent years, high-quality and innovative achievements now require rigorous multi-stage screening, which currently relies heavily on manual processes. We propose a deep learning-based key technology for high-quality achievement screening and evaluation in academic institutions. The approach first analyzes the computational advantages of deep learning and the challenges in achievement screening, then introduces the DLSAQEM model to streamline the process. Experimental results demonstrate that the DLSAQEM model achieves high screening accuracy, significantly reduces response time, and offers excellent flexibility and practicality.

title: Automatic Generation Technology for Distribution Network Protection Settings Based on Integrated Graph Data Model Approach
authors: Cheng Guo, Qiang Wu, Yi Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Using a method of independent local information calculation, protection setting generation for distribution networks can only be simply configured based on limited conditions, resulting in low accuracy. Therefore, this study investigates an automated generation technique for distribution network protection settings based on integrated Graph Data Model technology. Noise data is processed using filtering algorithms, missing values are filled via linear interpolation, and data is categorized and stored to complete the processing of distribution network operational data. Graphical modeling defines equipment symbol attributes, integrating graphical representations with equipment parameters and operational condition models for validation. This establishes a unified Graph Data Model integrated distribution network model. Key parameters are extracted, protection settings for various protection types are calculated, and validation/optimization is performed to achieve automated generation of distribution network protection settings. Experiments demonstrate that the proposed technology achieves over 90% correct operation rates in most scenarios. The measured sensitivity coefficients for all protection types exhibit minimal deviation from theoretical values. Compared to alternative methods, it exhibits slower growth and lower overall setting errors across varying load levels, thereby enhancing the precision of distribution network protection setting generation.

title: Zero-Shot Autonomous Dialogue Agent for Efficient Electric Power Data Inquiry
authors: Yiliang Wu, Fengchan Guo, Xuliang Luo, Fangfang Zhou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Electric power dialogue systems utilize natural language processing for service interactions and decision support. Traditional methods include pipeline and end-to-end designs, which face challenges such as error accumulation and hallucinations. This paper introduces a novel design that abandons modular frameworks in favor of an instruction following guide for large language models to generate relevant responses. We conduct intent analysis on user requests to autonomously determine actions in each dialogue round, such as retrieving grid information, querying databases, and correcting errors. Additionally, we established a simulation-based evaluation mechanism for dialogue assessment. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach outperforms traditional rule-based methods and pure large language models, with ablation studies showing that intent analysis significantly improves dialogue effectiveness.

title: Ontology Evolution via Renormalization in Latent Semantic Space
authors: C. M. Wyss
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Crucially, data integration involves reconciling schemas by means of mapping ontologies onto each other. Generally, such mappings are labor-intensive to create and require extensive expert intervention. Here, we present a mathematically principled framework for ontology evolution that can assist at each stage of data integration, from evolving a comprehensive centralized ontology to integrating member ontologies. To do this, we view an ontology as a geometric structure within semantic space, then employ standard results from spectral theory and dynamical systems to define the iterative transformation of a seed ontology into a global domain topology. Empirical results on a large-scale mathematics corpus demonstrate that the method reduces a noisy pool of over 130,000 candidate concepts to a high-quality, connected ontology of roughly 2,200 concepts—without manual feature engineering or domain heuristics. This suggests Renormalization-based Semantic Filtering (RSF) is a scalable, theoretically grounded approach for integrating and evolving reliable ontologies from large and heterogeneous corpora.

title: Uncovering Research Trends in Machine Learning Applications for $\text{CO}_{2}$-Equivalent Emissions
authors: Samuel-Soma M. Ajibade, Evans Oteng, Anthonia O. Adediran, Adedotun O. Adetunla, Muhammed Basheer Jasser, Ghassan Saleh ALdharhani
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: This study examines the application of machine learning (ML) to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex>-equivalent emissions (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2} \mathrm{e}$</tex>) through a comprehensive bibliometric analysis, focusing on publication trends, key contributors, and emerging research themes. Bibliographic data were retrieved from the Scopus database covering the period 2010-2024. Bibliometric mapping techniques were employed to analyse publication output, institutional and country contributions, collaboration patterns, and keyword cooccurrence networks. The results reveal a rapid expansion of ML<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2} \mathrm{e}$</tex> research, driven largely by conference papers and highimpact journal publications. China and the United States emerge as leading contributors, supported by major funding bodies such as the U.S. Department of Energy and the Chinese Academy of Sciences. Thematic evolution indicates a shift from general climate-related analyses toward advanced ML-driven applications, including carbon capture optimisation, emissions forecasting, and real-time <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2} \mathrm{e}$</tex> monitoring. While the analysis relies exclusively on Scopusindexed publications potentially omitting studies from other databases, the findings nonetheless provide valuable insights into the intellectual structure and global dynamics of the field. Overall, this study offers one of the earliest structured bibliometric assessments of ML applications in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2} \mathrm{e}$</tex> mitigation, highlighting key research trajectories, influential stakeholders, and opportunities for collaboration to advance data-driven climate action.

title: Generative AI and Ethical Integration in Higher Education: Navigating Innovation and Responsibility
authors: Umar Adam Ibrahim, AbdulAzeez Omeiza Salawu, Zhangl Abilbek, Nurbol Aidarbayev
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: The recent proliferation of generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) technology among institutions within the education sector has opened doors to personalize learning processes beyond imagination while also fueling daunting ethics issues pertaining to academic integrity, algorithm biases, transparency, and equal opportunities. This research project aims to explore the diverse dimensions and challenges arising from the adoption and use of GenAI technology, including the use of ChatGPT, within learning institutions while using a final review of 25 peer-reviewed articles to explore and demonstrate how different learning institutions, frameworks, and mechanisms address these diverse challenges arising from GenAI technology adoptions and use. Findings from the research clearly show that despite 94 % of top institutions within the learning sector having guidelines to use GenAI technology among faculty members, a daunting deficit still exists within various learning sectors pertaining to privacy, biases within GenAI technology, and the digital divide within learning institutions. This research work argues that to effectively integrate GenAI within learning institutions, collective stakeholders must come into play to ensure that GenAI technology does not undermine academic integrity within the learning sector.

title: Learning Adaptive Routing Policies Under Stochastic Service Times
authors: Yelbek Abdumajitov, Oybek Abdukhalimov, Azamkhon Azamov, Shirali Kadyrov
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Field service routing is strongly affected by uncertainty in on-site service durations, where deviations from nominal times propagate through the schedule and induce tardiness. We study the Multi-Vehicle Routing Problem with Stochastic Service Times (MVRP-SST), in which customer service times are revealed only upon arrival and follow customer-specific Gamma distributions. We formulate the problem as a cooperative Dec-POMDP and propose a Multi-Agent Proximal Policy Optimization (MAPPO) approach with centralized training and decentralized execution to learn adaptive routing policies that react to realized service times. The objective minimizes a weighted cost comprising travel distance and tardiness (with an additional penalty for unserved customers). Experiments on synthetic instances with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$K=3$</tex> vehicles and problem sizes <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N \in\{30,50,100\}$</tex> show that MAPPO consistently improves the overall objective compared with classical constructive heuristics and simulated annealing, with particularly strong gains at larger scales. Statistical testing confirms significant differences across methods via the Friedman test, and post-hoc Wilcoxon signedrank tests with Holm correction indicate that MAPPO significantly outperforms all baselines for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N=50$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N=100$</tex>, and all except simulated annealing for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N=30$</tex>.

title: Mechanical Design of Auto Feeder for Poultry and Livestock in Smart Agriculture
authors: Yingke Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the rapid development of smart agriculture, the demand for automated and precise feeding equipment in poultry farming is increasing day by day. In view of feeding requirements of poultry grains, an efficient and precise automatic feeding device has been designed. The device adopts a quantitative screw mechanism, which can achieve accurate quantitative feeding according to the scale the poultry group, ensuring “enough but not excessive” feeding, effectively avoiding feed waste. The feeding system covers core functional modules such as bin storage, conveying transmission, quantitative, and regular feeding. Before and after the improvement: the traditional device feeds about 1 kg each time, which is independent of the number of poultry; the optimized system can feed with a minimum common tolerance of $\mathbf{1 0 ~ g}$. This improvement has significantly reduced the waste of grain feed, with an average of 50 g of feed saved per poultry per, effectively reducing the breeding cost. Through reasonable selection and parameter optimization of each module, the system has achieved stable storage, precise delivery, and uniform regular feeding of grain feed, not improving the automation level and feeding efficiency of poultry farming, but also significantly reducing the input of labor. This provides important equipment support for the deep application of smart agricultural technology in the of poultry farming.

title: Research on modeling and optimization of front-end orchestration tool based on low code engine in power system service convergence
authors: Li Chen, Hao Yang, Qijuan Sun, Juntai Shi, Xiaoxia Kou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the popularization of new energy and the deepening of power market reform, the power system is facing problems such as data islands, difficulties in business process coordination and disconnection between IT and business. In this paper, a modeling and optimization method of front-end scheduling tool based on low code engine is proposed to solve the above challenges and promote the digital transformation of power system. The system architecture design adopts fourlayer decoupling architecture, including business scene layer, visual layout layer, low code engine core layer and power data access layer, which realizes two-way data flow mechanism and dynamic rule injection and improves the flexibility and responsiveness of the system. A power business component model is proposed, which abstracts complex business functions into standardized component meta-models, and integrates rule engines and multi-source data fusion models to realize modular encapsulation and reuse of business functions and efficient integration and processing of data. Strategies such as component loading on demand, rule engine performance optimization and hierarchical data flow update are adopted to improve application performance and resource utilization efficiency and ensure efficient real-time synchronization of key information. In the upgrading project of spot trading platform of a provincial power grid company, the tool scheme effectively improves the development efficiency and flexibility of operation and maintenance, reduces the time-consuming of interface function iteration, rule update response and crossbusiness scenario construction, and significantly improves the real-time data processing performance. The research results show that the low code engine front-end orchestration tool can effectively solve the challenges of power system business integration, and provide theoretical support and practical guidance for digital transformation of power system.

title: Construction of a Retrieval-Enhanced and Dynamically Updated Collocation Knowledge Base System for Second Language Learning
authors: Jiyan Liu, Nan Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: To address the limitations of static collocation knowledge bases in Intelligent Systems for second language learning, an L2 collocation domain Adaptation framework PL2CDAF is proposed. The framework introduces a hierarchical two-stage adaptation process, combining error-aware pretraining and reinforcement to transform a Transformer-based model into a dynamic collocation knowledge base. It integrates a multi-head neural memory network for storing and updating collocation patterns, enabling real-time error detection and contextually appropriate feedback generation. Experimental validation demonstrates its effectiveness in enhancing collocation awareness and corrective feedback, offering a scalable and integrable solution for personalized language learning.

title: The Integration of Big Data Technology in Transformer Condition Monitoring and Detection
authors: Xiaoying Guo, Tian Gao, Dandan Li, Ye Du
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: As power systems become increasingly intelligent and reliable, the continuous monitoring of transformers-critical assets for transmission and distribution-becomes essential for grid security. Conventional monitoring relies on periodic offline tests and manual inspections, yielding low-real-time fidelity, unidimensional data, and belated fault alerts that are incompatible with the fine-grained asset management required by contemporary grids. Big-data analytics-owing to its capacity for high-volume processing, multidimensional analysis, and real-time pattern discovery-offers a transformative approach to transformer monitoring. Drawing on empirical cases, the study underscores the strategic value of transformer monitoring, delineates the advantages of big-data technologies, and examines their deployment in data acquisition, cleansing, and condition assessment. The findings furnish actionable insights for whole-life transformer stewardship and grid resilience.

title: Research on Cooperative Sensing of Multi-Modal Signals and Structural Health Identification Algorithm for Power Transmission and Distribution Equipment Foundation
authors: Xiaoqiong Huang, Huayong Lai, Tao Wang, Xiaoqing Su, Junyu Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Based on the urgent demand of the new power system for the safe operation of power transmission and distribution equipment, this paper puts forward a multi-modal signal collaborative sensing and structural health identification algorithm guided by physical mechanism to solve the problems of poor robustness and poor interpretability of single modal monitoring under complex working conditions. The algorithm constructs a “perception-fusion-prediction” three-layer hybrid framework: firstly, based on the first principles of electro-thermal-mechanical coupling and acoustic-thermal correlation, a cross-modal physical rule base is established to realize feature screening and causal constraint; Secondly, a two-way modal attention mechanism is designed to dynamically allocate the weights of multi-modal features such as temperature, voiceprint and stress, so as to realize information complementarity and adaptive fusion; Finally, a graph convolutional network (GCN) and a long short-term memory network (LSTM) were integrated to capture the spatial dependence and temporal evolution of the device, achieving 72 hour fault probability prediction. The experiment relies on the multimodal data set of 110 kV real transmission scene, and the results show that the accuracy of health state identification of the algorithm is 95.8%, and the F1-Score is 0.955, which is significantly better than the single-mode and conventional fusion methods. The visualization of attention weight verifies the consistency between its decision-making process and physical mechanism, and provides an interpretable and highprecision technical scheme for predictive maintenance of power transmission and distribution equipment.

title: Prediction of Yogurt Quality Attributes from Hyperspectral Images using Deep Learning
authors: Kartha Veerya Reddy Jammuladinne, Ayesha Sarker, Anirudhaa Maiti
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Food quality attributes such as pH, lactic acid concentration, and viscosity are important in evaluating the stability, safety, and textural properties of refrigerated dairy products. This study uses yogurt as a test case for predicting these attributes from hyperspectral images. The proposed method uses raw spectral data collected from yogurt samples made using two different milk sources: cow milk and coconut milk. We developed an automated pipeline for predicting seven food quality attributes of yogurt, including pH, lactic acid concentration, viscosity, and color-based quality values. The pipeline uses a stepwise segmentation method to select the yogurt region, which is then divided into fixed-size spectral patches. Each patch contains pixels from only the yogurt region. We designed a convolutional neural network which is trained using these patches along with temporal information. The network includes a shared encoder and separate prediction heads for the two different milk sources. Our evaluation shows high test-set $R^{2}$ values (above 0.85) for $\mathbf{p H}$ and color attributes ($\mathrm{l}, \mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}$), and moderate values for titratable acidity (0.43) and viscosity (0.68). This result indicates that non-invasive hyperspectral imaging provides reliable prediction for key yogurt quality attributes, especially when combined with temporal information.

title: Application of Machine Vision Technology in Tobacco Quality Inspection System
authors: Wenfeng Guan, Jiamin Lei, Ye Zhang, Wanwen He, Zhenye Chen, Xianyun Zhong, Chunqiu Li, Lulu Feng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: In cigarette manufacturing processes, precision management is critical for quality control. However, traditional manual inspection methods suffer from significant limitations such as low efficiency and high subjectivity, failing to meet the modern tobacco industry’s stringent demands for parameter accuracy and real-time performance. To overcome this bottleneck, this study focuses on machine vision technology and meticulously designs an online detection system for tobacco quality parameters. Leveraging core technologies in machine vision—including high-precision imaging, multispectral analysis, and deep learning algorithms—the system achieves automated and accurate detection of key indicators, such as the color, extension degree, and blending uniformity of leaf and tobacco shreds. System validation results demonstrate that machine vision technology achieves a 96.6% accuracy rate in insect spot detection, with temperature measurement errors controlled within $2^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, and blending uniformity analysis errors of less than 20%. This provides robust data support for optimizing cigarette production processes.

title: Exploring the Effect of Virtual Reality (VR) Simulation to Disaster Preparedness of Children
authors: Jomar V. Sayaman, Francis F. Balahadia
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Disasters pose serious risks to communities, particularly to children, who are among the most vulnerable populations during emergency situations. Developing effective strategies to enhance children's disaster preparedness is therefore critical, and emerging technologies such as virtual reality (VR) offer promising educational possibilities. This study investigated the effectiveness of VR simulations in improving children's disaster preparedness in terms of knowledge, skills, and abilities. A within-subject experimental design was employed involving twenty child participants who were identified as having low levels of disaster preparedness based on a self-report scale. The participants underwent three VR simulation sessions designed to educate and prepare them for potential disaster scenarios. Disaster preparedness levels were measured using pretest and posttest assessments. The results indicated no statistically significant difference between pretest and posttest scores (p<0.05), suggesting that the VR simulation, as implemented, did not significantly enhance disaster preparedness among the participants. These findings indicate that while VR holds potential as an educational tool, its effectiveness may depend heavily on age-appropriate design considerations. The study underscores the need for further research focused on optimizing VR content and interaction models to align with children's cognitive and emotional characteristics, as well as exploring alternative instructional strategies to improve learning outcomes in disaster preparedness education.

title: Early Prediction of Student Academic Status Using SMOTE-Driven Multiclass Classification
authors: Samuel-Soma M. Ajibade, Ivy Batican, Lislee Valle, Edralin General, Joshlen Lim, Sarah Jane Colina, Severina P. Velos, Johnry P. Dayupay, Mbiatke Anthony Bassey
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Early identification of students at academic risk is essential for timely intervention and improved educational outcomes. However, predictive modelling in this domain is challenged by class imbalance and the need for interpretable models that support institutional decision-making. This study proposes a SMOTE-driven multiclass machine learning framework for the early prediction of student academic status. Using institutional student records, relevant academic, demographic, and engagement features were preprocessed through appropriate encoding and normalization techniques. Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) was applied to address class imbalance across multiple outcome categories. Several machine learning models, including Logistic Regression and tree-based classifiers, were trained and evaluated using stratified k -fold cross-validation to ensure robustness and reproducibility. Model performance was assessed using accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and multiclass confusion matrices. The results demonstrate that class balancing significantly improves predictive performance, particularly for minority academic outcome classes. Feature interpretation revealed that prior academic performance, assessment engagement, and attendancerelated variables were the most influential predictors across models. The findings confirm the effectiveness of combining data balancing with interpretable machine learning for early academic risk detection. This work contributes a practical and explainable framework that can support data-driven academic monitoring and targeted student support strategies in higher education institutions.

title: Performance Implications of Node Graph Complexity in Real-Time Shader Systems
authors: Aidiye Aidarbekov, Dias Rustembek, Gulmira Shakhmetova
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Procedural shading provides flexibility, scalability, and parametric control that are difficult to achieve with manual texturing. Procedural techniques are incorporated into numerous 3D packages and graphics applications. In these systems, materials are constructed by controlling directed acyclic graphs of shader operations. Despite the popularity of node-based materials, the relationship between structural complexity and rendering performance remains poorly understood. This paradigm improves accessibility and artistic productivity but can obscure the computational costs associated with increasing the complexity of the shader graph. This study examines the relationship between node graph structural complexity and rendering speed in the Eevee real-time engine of Blender. Six versions (V1-V6) of node graphs were created while preserving the visual result. Complexity was varied by increasing the number of nodes (from 11 to 352) and the depth of the graph (from 4 to 9). Each configuration was tested 25 times under controlled conditions to separate the steady-state rendering time from the initial overhead. Rendering time increases nonlinearly, with a sharp jump between graphs of medium and high complexity. Shader compilation metrics, on the other hand, showed non-monotonic behavior, which can be explained by the influence of internal optimization processes and engine caching mechanisms. These findings indicate that the structural features of graphs significantly affect performance and must be taken into account when designing materials for real-time applications.

title: Deepandable AI: A Generative Defense Against Voice-Based Intrusion for Voice-Controlled Services
authors: Tasnia Ashrafi Heya, Yuvaraj Singh Naval, Abdul Serwadda
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Voice-controlled services like Siri, Alexa, etc. offer convenience in our day-to-day life, but also introduce serious privacy risks due to their open voice channels. With the rapid emergence of deepfake technology and its potential for misuse, the threat of voice-based user profiling has become increasingly critical. In this paper, we propose Deepandable AI, a novel mitigation technique that leverages generative AI to transform user voice commands into randomly synthesized voices. This transformation masks the speaker’s identity and prevents voice fingerprinting of original users, thereby hindering profiling attacks. We evaluated the effectiveness of our defense against three common profiling attacks: speaker recognition, gender prediction, and estimation of the number of unique speakers in a household. Implementing Deepandable AI on a custom-built voice assistant (VA) device, we observed significant reductions in profiling accuracy: speaker recognition dropped to $11.6 \%$, gender prediction to $57.69 \%$, and the error rate in speaker count estimation increased from $4.5 \%$ to $39.3 \%$. Additionally, we evaluated our mitigation system’s usability by measuring response latency across different text-to-speech (TTS) models and found an average delay of $\mathbf{2} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{3}$ seconds. Next, we conducted a user study $(n=75)$ in which most participants were willing to accept this delay in exchange for enhanced privacy while using voiceinteractive services. These results suggest that generative AI and deepfake technologies, often associated with malicious use, can be repurposed as powerful tools for privacy preservation in voiceinteractive systems.
