title: Real-Time Voice Liveliness Detection for Securing Voice Assistants from Deepfake Audio Attacks
authors: Tasnia Ashrafi Heya, Sai Woon Tip
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Voice assistants (VAs) such as Amazon Alexa, Google Assistant, and Apple Siri have become integral to daily life, managing tasks from home automation to financial transactions. However, their exponential growth has made them increasingly vulnerable to different attacks like deepfake audio attacks, where synthetically generated voices are used to impersonate legitimate users for verification or to issue malicious commands-such as unlocking smart locks, disabling alarms, transferring funds, or accessing personal data. The rapid democratization of generative AI and advanced Text-to-Speech (TTS) models has amplified this threat, indicating the urgent necessity for effective voice liveliness detection mechanisms. This research performs a comparative analysis of machine learning techniques for voice liveliness detection against deepfake voice verification or issuing voice commands in VA systems. In this study, we constructed a dataset of 192 audio sam-ples-including real human speech and synthetic voices produced by four state-of-the-art TTS engines which was then, used to evaluate two methodological paradigms: (1) classical machine learning models (e.g., Random Forest, SVM) trained on engineered acoustic features, and (2) deep learning models (e.g., AlexNet, ResNet) trained on mel-spectrogram representations. Results show that while both achieve high accuracy, featurebased classical models performed best, reaching 98% accuracy in detecting deepfake voice commands. The work establishes a baseline framework and empirical benchmark for strengthening the resilience of voice-based interfaces against evolving AI-driven audio spoofing attacks.

title: LLM-Enhanced Reinforcement Learning for Time Series Anomaly Detection
authors: Bahareh Golchin, Banafsheh Rekabdar, Danielle Justo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Detecting anomalies in time series data is crucial for finance, healthcare, sensor networks, and industrial monitoring applications. However, time series anomaly detection often suffers from sparse labels, complex temporal patterns, and costly expert annotation. We propose a unified framework that integrates Large Language Model (LLM)-based potential functions for reward shaping with reinforcement learning (RL), Variational Autoencoder (VAE)-enhanced dynamic reward scaling, and active learning with label propagation. An LSTM-based RL agent leverages LLM-derived semantic rewards to guide exploration, while VAE reconstruction errors add unsupervised anomaly signals. Active learning selects the most uncertain samples, and label propagation expands labeled data efficiently. Evaluations on Yahoo-A1 and SMD benchmarks demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art detection accuracy under limited labeling budgets and operates effectively in data-constrained settings. This study highlights the promise of combining LLMs with RL and advanced unsupervised techniques for robust, scalable anomaly detection in real-world applications.<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>

title: WiseOWL: A Methodology for Evaluating Ontological Descriptiveness and Semantic Correctness for Ontology Reuse and Ontology Recommendations
authors: Aryan Singh Dalal, Maria Baloch, Asiyah Yu Lin, Anna Maria Masci, Kathleen M. Jagodnik, Hande Küçük McGinty
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: The Semantic Web standardizes concept meaning for humans and machines, enabling machine-operable content and consistent interpretation that improves advanced analytics. Reusing ontologies speeds development and enforces consistency, yet selecting the optimal choice is challenging because authors lack systematic selection criteria and often rely on intuition that is difficult to justify, limiting reuse. To solve this, WiseOWL is proposed, a methodology with scoring and guidance to select ontologies for reuse. It scores four metrics: (i) Well-Described, measuring documentation coverage; (ii) Well-Defined, using state-of-the-art embeddings to assess label-definition alignment; (iii) Connection, capturing structural interconnectedness; and (iv) Hierarchical Breadth, reflecting hierarchical balance. WiseOWL outputs normalized $\mathbf{0 - 1 0}$ scores with actionable feedback. Implemented as a Streamlit app, it ingests OWL format, converts to RDF Turtle, and provides interactive visualizations. Evaluation across six ontologies, including the Plant Ontology (PO), Gene Ontology (GO), Semanticscience Integrated Ontology (SIO), Food Ontology (FoodON), Dublin Core (DC), and GoodRelations, demonstrates promising effectiveness.

title: Statistical Characteristics and Forecasting of Electricity Market Trading Price Volatility Based on ARIMA-GARCH and Hybrid Deep Learning Models-A Case Study of the Liaoning Electricity Market
authors: Pengpeng Liu, Wei Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Electricity market reform has introduced extreme uncertainty into trading prices. This poses a severe challenge to risk prevention and control for market participants. This study focuses on the Liaoning electricity market from March to August 2025. Its objective is to develop a forecasting framework capable of accurately capturing the characteristics of extreme price fluctuations. Statistical tests were first conducted. The results confirm that the electricity return series does not follow a normal distribution. Instead, it exhibits significant “leptokurtosis and heavy tails,” volatility clustering, and long memory properties. Building on this finding, we propose a hybrid model that integrates econometrics and deep learning. Specifically, the ARIMA-GARCH model captures the linear trends and conditional heteroskedasticity of prices. Then, an LSTM neural network is introduced to refine the fitting of nonlinear residuals. Empirical results demonstrate the superior performance of our hybrid model. It significantly outperforms individual models in predicting price “spikes,” such as those during summer evening peak hours. The Mean Absolute Error (MAE) is reduced to 32.7 yuan/MWh. This research provides a quantitative basis for power generation companies to formulate bidding strategies. It also offers valuable decision-making references for regulatory authorities to enhance market risk early-warning mechanisms.

title: Edge-aware Efficient Diffusion Model for High-Fidelity Sketch-to-Product Generation
authors: Jinrun Sun, Jianshu Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Translating concept sketches into realistic renderings in industrial design requires strict adherence to geometric constraints, a challenge for existing diffusion models prone to detail hallucination. To address this, we propose an EdgeAware Efficient Diffusion Model. Architecturally, we employ a Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) strategy-freezing the SD UNet, VAE, and CLIP encoders and training only a lightweight ControlNet branch—to reduce memory consumption and improve efficiency. Our core innovation, the Edge-Aware Module, analytically estimates clean pixel-level images directly from noisy latents during training. By utilizing a Sobel operator to compute an Edge Consistency Loss, this mechanism explicitly enforces the designer’s structural intent. Experimental results demonstrate that our method improves the baseline ControlNet in structural fidelity without sacrificing photorealism, offering a highly reliable AI-assisted solution for rapid concept validation in industrial design.

title: PC-RLNet: A High-Precision PM2.5 Prediction Framework via Residual Fusion of Pretrained LSTM and Lightweight Transformer
authors: Xinbao Liu, Jianing Liu, Xinlin Zhao, Ying Ren, Weilong Li, Yan Liang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Accurate PM2.5 forecasting is critical for public health protection, yet remains challenging due to the high volatility and nonlinearity of atmospheric pollutant concentrations [2]. While traditional Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks demonstrate deployment efficiency, they struggle to capture global temporal dependencies in time series. Conversely, state-of-the-art Transformer models, despite their superior performance, impose prohibitive computational costs that preclude deployment on resource-constrained edge Internet of Things (IoT) devices. To address this fundamental trade-off, we propose PC-RLNet, a novel lightweight hybrid PM2.5 prediction framework employing a two-stage “freeze-and-enhance” training strategy. Specifically, a frozen pretrained LSTM extracts stable low-frequency PM2.5 concentration trends, while a co-trained ultra-lightweight Transformer models high-frequency residual errors between LSTM predictions and ground truth values. This decoupled architectural design effectively eliminates optimization interference between modules and minimizes parameter overhead. Extensive experiments on the Beijing Dongcheng District dataset demonstrate that PC-RLNet achieves a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of $6.91 \mu \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{m}^{3}$ and a coefficient of determination ($R^{2}$) of 0.9720, outperforming all compared lightweight baselines in capturing abrupt PM2.5 pollution episodes. With merely 5,922 trainable parameters (approximately 23.7 KB in storage), PC-RLNet provides a robust ultra-lightweight solution suitable for resourceconstrained IoT air quality monitoring systems deployed at the network edge, such as distributed monitoring nodes in urban streets and communities.

title: Research on Intelligent Modeling and Algorithm Inference Method for Multi source Data of Food and Drug Environmental Intellectual Property
authors: Qianqian Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: The fields of food and drug, ecological environment and intellectual property supervision have long faced the problem of data fusion. It is difficult to effectively integrate multi-source heterogeneous data, semantic expression is fragmented, and the interpretability of reasoning process is also insufficient. This article constructs a joint modeling framework that integrates ontology-driven semantic alignment, heterogeneous graph attention network and counterfactual causal inference. By establishing cross-domain ontology system and context-aware entity link mechanism, this article realizes the unified representation of unstructured text and structured records. On this basis, MH-HGAT (Multi-head Heterogeneous Graph Attention Network) is designed to dynamically aggregate multitype relationships, and the conditional generation confrontation module is embedded to support intervention simulation and causal effect estimation. The experiment is based on real samples collected between 2018 and 2025. Experiments show that the proposed model achieves 0.932 on AUC-ROC, the macro average of F1 is 0.897, and CEEB (Causal Effect Estimation Bias) is significantly lower than the baseline method, and effective signals are issued 14 days in advance in the early warning of lowfrequency high-risk events. This method not only improves the accuracy of cross-domain risk identification, but also provides an interpretable technical path for quantitative assessment of policy intervention effect.

title: Gene-Disease Association Extraction Based on PiSSA Fine-tuning of Qwen3-8B
authors: Jiaxu Dao, Zhuoying Li, Huahong Zhang, Gen Long, Xiaoli Lan, Youbang Su, Qingsong Zhou, Xiuzhong Tang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Mining Gene-Disease Associations (GDA) from biomedical literature is pivotal for deciphering disease pathogenesis and advancing precision medicine. While Large Language Models (lLMs) have demonstrated exceptional semantic understanding, adapting these general-purpose models to specific vertical domains like biomedicine remains a significant challenge due to substantial computational requirements. This paper proposes an efficient fine-tuning strategy for the Qwen3-8B model using PiSSA (Principal Singular Values and Singular Vectors Adaptation) within the LLaMA-Factory framework. Unlike standard low-rank adaptation, PiSSA initializes adapters by performing Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) on pretrained weight matrices, enabling a “warm start” that prioritizes the most significant features. Comprehensive experiments conducted on the GAD dataset demonstrate that PiSSA achieves superior performance across key evaluation metrics under the optimal rank configuration, significantly outperforming traditional LoRA and RSLoRA methods. Our findings suggest that spectrally-informed initialization effectively preserves pretrained capabilities while optimizing domain-specific adaptation. This work provides a high-performance, resource-efficient methodology for relation extraction tasks in the biomedical domain.

title: Multipath Collective Communication Beyond Scale-up Networks in GPU Clouds
authors: Yuchen Xu, Jianglong Nie, Baojia Li, Mingzhuo Chen, Hao Lu, Guanyu Qu, Zhenchuan Liu, Shuangshuang Yin, Xiaojie Huang, Chunzhi He, Yinben Xia, Quan Wen, Xiang Li, Zekun He, Yachen Wang, Xianneng Zou, Congcong Miao, Wenfei Wu
publish: Proceedings of the 21st European Conference on Computer Systems

title: Adversarial Optimization of the $\delta$-TriBound Objective for Robust Community Detection Attacks Across Multiple Graphs
authors: Bay-Yuan Hsu, Kuo-Chieh Sun, Ching Chi Yeh
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Adversarial attacks on community detection (CD) have attracted increasing attention due to CD’s importance in numerous applications and its privacy implications. While existing studies have demonstrated the effectiveness of adversarial perturbations in degrading CD performance, most prior attacks are restricted to single-graph settings. This limitation contrasts with the growing adoption of multigraph CD frameworks, where community structures are inferred from multiple heterogeneous or cross-platform graphs. Moreover, existing attack methods suffer from two fundamental weaknesses: 1) proxy metrics neglect community structures; and 2) lacking budget allocation ability and the capability to handle multiple graphs simultaneously. To address these challenges, we propose a new research problem, multigraph perturbation for community detection via <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\delta$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-TriBound minimization (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\delta$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-MPCD). At the core of our formulation is <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\delta$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-TriBound, a proxy metric designed to capture higher-order triangle patterns anchoring community cohesion and to enable effective adversarial perturbations across multiple graphs. We theoretically justify the utility of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\delta$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-TriBound, prove that <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\delta$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-MPCD is NP-hard, and design a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$({1}/{24})$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-approximation algorithm, multiple graph <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\delta$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-TriBounds minimization with edge addition (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\delta$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-MGMA). Extensive experiments on real-world datasets demonstrate that our method consistently degrades community detection performance across diverse CD algorithms in multigraph settings, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art baselines.

title: Forgettable Federated Linear Learning With Certified Data Unlearning_supp1-3683398.pdf
authors: Qiong Zhang
publish: N/A
abstract: The advent of federated learning (FL) has revolutionized the way distributed systems handle collaborative model training while preserving user privacy. Recently, federated unlearning (FU) has emerged to address demands for the “right to be forgotten” and unlearning of the impact of poisoned clients without requiring retraining in FL. Most FU algorithms require the cooperation of retained or target clients (clients to be unlearned), introducing additional communication overhead and potential security risks. In addition, some FU methods need to store historical models to execute the unlearning process. These challenges hinder the efficiency and memory constraints of the current FU methods. Moreover, due to the complexity of nonlinear models and their training strategies, most existing FU methods for deep neural networks (DNNs) lack theoretical certification. In this work, we introduce a novel FL training and unlearning strategy in DNN, termed forgettable federated linear learning (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathtt {F^{2}L^{2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>). <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathtt {F^{2}L^{2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> considers a common practice of using pretrained models to approximate DNN linearly, allowing them to achieve similar performance as the original networks via federated linear training (<monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">FLT</monospace>). We then present <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">FedRemoval</monospace>, a certified, efficient, and secure unlearning strategy that enables the server to unlearn a target client without requiring client communication or adding additional storage. We have conducted extensive empirical validation on small- to large-scale datasets, using both convolutional neural networks and modern foundation models (FMs). These experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathtt {F^{2}L^{2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> in balancing model accuracy with the successful unlearning of target clients. <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathtt {F^{2}L^{2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> represents a promising pipeline for efficient and trustworthy FU. The code is available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://anonymous.4open.science/r/2F2L-Federated-Unlearning-D57D/README.md</uri>

title: PiLLM: Resource-Efficient LLM Inference Using Workload Prediction
authors: Yunqian Fan, Shihao Bai, Ruihao Gong, Zaijun Wang, Rui Fan

title: Enhanced Collision Avoidance in Quadrotor Surrounding Control via Adaptive APF and Event-Triggered MPC
authors: Peng Li, Yuanqing Xia, Hongjiu Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: This paper presents a collision avoidance strategy for quadrotors to perform surrounding control of an autonomous vehicle in the presence of tall roadside obstacles. An adaptive artificial potential field (APF) is employed to dynamically adjust the surrounding radius of the quadrotor according to the distance between the vehicle and the nearest tall obstacle. An adaptive event-triggered model predictive control (MPC) scheme is introduced to improve tracking accuracy and reduce computational burden when solving the optimization problem. Furthermore, recursive feasibility of the optimization problem and stability of the resulting closed-loop quadrotor system are rigorously analyzed. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed collision avoidance strategy.

title: Event-Triggered Adaptive Dynamic Programming for Path Tracking Control of Autonomous Ground Vehicles via Generalized Fuzzy Hyperbolic Models
authors: Bing Hu, Zhenyang Xue, Jian Liu, Zhixin Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: In this paper, a novel optimal control scheme for path tracking of autonomous ground vehicles is proposed based on the adaptive dynamic programming algorithm. Unlike existing modeling methods, this paper uses generalized fuzzy hyperbolic models (GFHM) to approximate the model uncertainties induced by the actual environmental disturbances. The optimal tracking control problem is formulated as solving the Hamilton-Jacobi-Isaacs (HJI) equation, which involves the nonlinearity of the model. Meanwhile, a dynamic memory event-triggered mechanism (DMETM) is introduced, whose triggering threshold is capable of dynamic adjustment according to the historical system states. The uniform ultimate boundedness (UUB) stability of the designed control system is rigorously proved. Furthermore, a reinforcement learning algorithm is used to solve the constructed HJI equation and address the path tracking control problem. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm is verified through theoretical analysis and numerical simulations.

title: Tendon-Driven Robotic Shoulder Joint With Passive Stabilization for Large Workspace
authors: Sudong Lee, Kieran Gilday, Josie Hughes
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: The shoulder is important for humanoid robots since it has a significant impact on the arm’s range of motion and its versatility. Therefore, research on the shoulder joint, which is known as the spherical joint or ball–socket joint, is ongoing to mimic its wide workspace and high control accuracy. In this article, we propose a shoulder joint with a unique tendon-routing method. This method combines straight actuation tendons, passive helical tendons, and springs, preventing joint dislocation even in extreme conditions and ensuring controllability in all positions. We can also adjust dislocation characteristics by changing the number of tendons and tendon-routing method. The passive spring connections impose inherent compliance and improve stability in harsh situations. The tendons imitate human ligament and muscle in order to achieve structural and functional similarity to a human shoulder. The proposed joint has a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula> 99.87<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{\circ }$</tex-math></inline-formula> workspace with respect to roll and pitch rotation and withstands high impact with compliance. It has key advantages including the wide range of motion, diverse dislocation map, lightweight, and similarities with human shoulder motion and structure.

title: Dynamic Search Depth Allocation for Patent Prior Art Search: An Empirical Study of Retrieval Limitations in 64-Dimensional Embeddings
authors: Soichi Onozuka, Takaaki Ohnishi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: The rapid growth of global patent filings demands efficient prior art search systems capable of identifying documents that challenge the novelty and inventive step of new applications. The Google Patents Public Datasets enable scalable semantic retrieval using precomputed 64-dimensional dense vector embeddings; however, our analysis reveals a fundamental limitation, which we term the dimensional compression bottleneck. This bottleneck obscures technical distinctions critical for patentability assessment, causing highly relevant documents to be ranked unexpectedly low despite high similarity scores. We evaluate retrieval performance using approximately 40,000 ground-truth prior art pairs from Japanese patent examination records and analyze ranking distributions within the top 300 results, a depth sufficient for recall-oriented search. The results show a Pearson correlation coefficient of -0.47 between cosine similarity and document rank, with many relevant documents failing to appear within this range. To address this issue, we propose an adaptive retrieval framework that dynamically adjusts search depth according to query characteristics. Threshold analysis identifies a similarity score of 0.88, above which $85 \%$ of relevant documents are concentrated within the top 30 candidates, defining an effective retrieval range for high-confidence queries. For queries with similarity scores between 0.80 and 0.88, sparse autoencoders are used to reconstruct discriminative features for reranking. The reranked candidates are then examined through a multi-stage evaluation process in which role-based large language model agents engage in adversarial debate on novelty and inventive step. By allocating computational resources based on predicted query difficulty, the proposed method overcomes the recall-efficiency tradeoff inherent in fixed-depth retrieval systems.

title: TAAformer: Transposed Angular Attention for Channel Estimation With Fluid Antennas
authors: Weibiao Zhao, Yongyi Miao, Mengya Liu, Xin Liang, Die Hu, Yu Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Fluid antenna systems (FASs) can enhance communication system performance by flexibly switching antenna positions to ports with favorable channel conditions. To fully realize this benefit, acquiring accurate channel state information is essential. Existing Transformer-based FAS channel estimation methods mainly rely on conventional self-attention (SA), whose quadratic complexity poses challenges to network efficiency, especially when the number of switchable ports is large. To address this issue, we propose a novel transposed angular attention (TAA) that reduces complexity to linear levels while retaining global modeling capabilities. Unlike conventional SA directly modeling relationships between ports, TAA first disentangles the combined contributions of multi-path components (MPCs) at each port through an angular-domain transformation, and then, via a transposed operation, shifts the attention focus to model the relationships between angular features underlying all disentangled MPCs. We further extend TAA into transposed angular self- and cross-attention mechanisms. Building on these, we develop an efficient TAAformer network for FAS channel estimation, a UNet-like encoder-decoder architecture with multi-scale feature capabilities. Simulation results show that the proposed TAAformer can achieve higher channel estimation accuracy compared to existing methods.

title: FAST-RTFM: Fast RGB–Thermal Fusion for Point Cloud Mapping in Low-Light Environments
authors: Jiaming Xu, Tianyong Ye, Shikuan Shi, Man He, Chunran Zheng, Tingwen Huang, Yukang Cui
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Thermal cameras, capable of operating independently of ambient light while acquiring extra-spectral radiation data, present distinct advantages over conventional RGB cameras that require adequate illumination in low-light environments. However, traditional visual simultaneous localization and mapping methods struggle with thermal imagery due to limitations, such as lower image resolution, constrained dynamic range, and imaging interruptions during nonuniformity correction. Although existing deep learning-based approaches have partially improved the performance of thermal imaging in localization and mapping tasks, their generalization capability across diverse environments remains weaker than traditional geometric methods. Integrating thermal information as complementary data with other sensors, such as LiDAR and RGB cameras, has proven to be an effective approach to address this issue. This article introduces a pointwise RGB–thermal pixel fusion strategy for efficient real-time point cloud coloring based on LiDAR–inertial–visual odometry frameworks. By effectively combining the complementary strengths of thermal cameras (illumination robustness), RGB sensors (textural details), and LiDAR's high-precision geometric structures, we successfully implement an efficient mapping system for target detection and nighttime reconnaissance. The system achieves both high real-time performance and accuracy, demonstrating broad application potential in security operations and emergency response scenarios.

title: Medical Image Volumetric Arbitrary Scale Super-Resolution Via Learnable Adaptive Upsampling and Weight Dynamic Balancing
authors: Shulin Li, Jiapeng Qi, Chaolu Feng, Yang Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering
abstract: Volumetric Computed Tomography (CT and Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) are crucial in clinical diagnosis. However, practical limitations often result in anisotropic volumes with high in-plane but low through plane resolution. Volumetric SR therefore focuses on recovering missing through-plane details. Stronger anatomical variation along the through-plane makes direct adaptation of 2D restoration components prone to inefficient context aggregation and limited robustness in volumetric settings. In addition, although recent arbitrary-scale SR methods enable flexible scaling, they often face a trade-off between scale flexibility and parameter efficiency. To overcome these challenges, we propose a Learnable Adaptive Upsampling-based Volumetric SR network (LAUVSR). The first key innovation is a volumetric-anisotropy-driven upsampling core, which integrates a learnable weight generation module employing in-plane weight sharing to capture fine details and through-plane independent generation to model anatomical variations, augmented by a volume specific criss-cross attention mechanism through mapping attention to in-plane and through-plane interactions, ensuring both high-fidelity and parameter-efficient SR at arbitrary integer and decimal query scales. Furthermore, we in corporate a reinforcement-learning-guided weight dynamic balancing strategy to improve training robustness across datasets, modalities, and target scales. Extensive image quality assessments were conducted on four datasets across two modalities (CT and MRI) using synthetically downsampled LR–HR pairs. These assessments demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed method in achieving high-quality SR results across a dense range of integer and decimal upsampling scales.Additionally, evaluations at unseen upsampling scales and downstream task analyses further support its robustness and practical potential.

title: Efficient Attribute-Based Searchable Encryption with Dual Privacy Protection_supp1-3686439.pdf
authors: Meiyan Xiao
publish: N/A
abstract: With the rapid evolution of Internet of Things (IoT) devices, privacy-sensitive data is exploding, creating significant obstacles in the sharing and retrieval of information. To enable high-precision data retrieval and fine-grained sharing, only the integration of Attribute-Based Searchable Encryption (ABSE) with cloud storage can fulfill these requirements while ensuring security. However, existing ABSE schemes face major limitations in IoT scenarios. Publicly accessible access policies and unprotected private key attribute sets pose risks of information leakage. In addition, the limited expressiveness of access policies and high computational overhead hinder their practical deployment in IoT environments. In this paper, we propose an Efficient Attribute-Based Searchable Encryption with Dual Privacy Protection (EASDP). By fully hiding access policies and attributes, our scheme ensures strong privacy against cloud inference attacks.We enhance the expressiveness of access control through sub-policy division and improve efficiency for resource-constrained IoT devices using techniques such as offline encryption, outsourced decryption, and keyword–attribute matching. The scheme is formally proven secure. The experimental results demonstrate its high performance in encryption and decryption at the user side and its suitability for various IoT devices, confirming its effectiveness in secure and flexible data sharing across IoT healthcare applications.

title: AI-Based Detection of Evasive Malware Using Machine Learning and Deep Learning Techniques
authors: Mohammad Imtiyaz Gulbarga, Ruslan Isaev, Ruslan Amanov
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Modern malware increasingly employs sophisticated evasion techniques, including obfuscation, polymorphism, and anti-analysis mechanisms, to bypass traditional detection systems. Memory analysis captures runtime behavioral characteristics that persist regardless of code-level alterations, limiting practical deployment. To address this crucial gap, we propose a framework that combines memory forensics with ensemble machine learning. We extended the Advanced-VolMemLyzer tool from 75 to over 250 behavioral features, targeting evasive malware indicators. A balanced dataset of 58,596 memory samples covering three malware families Ransomware, Spyware, and Trojan Horse was assembled from real-world specimens. The two-layer stacked ensemble reached 99% detection accuracy at 0.23 seconds per sample, outperforming traditional signature-based approaches (78.4%) and remaining feasible for real-time deployment.

title: Optimizing Memory Usage in Streaming Pipelines Through Information Lifecycle Management (ILM)
authors: Alua Onayeva
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Streaming systems often struggle with memory overload when large amounts of data are processed in real time. This paper presents a lightweight Information Lifecycle Management (ILM) strategy designed to control hot-tier memory usage in Redis by dynamically archiving stale records to cold storage. The proposed method combines time-based expiration with an asynchronous sweeper thread that migrates inactive keys after a configurable threshold. The approach was implemented and tested within a Kafka-Spark-Redis pipeline over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0}$</tex>-minute workloads. The results show that Redis memory usage was reduced by 83 %, falling from 8.1 GB to just 1.4 GB, with only a 1 % decrease in Kafka throughput and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 \%}$</tex> increase in Spark latency. These findings demonstrate that effective memory control can be achieved without compromising real-time performance. The architecture remains fully containerized and uses only native TTL and file operations, making it easily deployable in resourceconstrained environments.

title: Enterprise Financial Data Analysis and Decision Support System Based on Algorithm
authors: Jianlin Li, Wanli Liu, Cong Meng, Xingguang Guo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Enterprise financial management faces many challenges in the era of big data. This article aims to build an algorithm-based enterprise financial data analysis and decision support system to improve the level of enterprise financial management. Based on the in-depth analysis of the relevant theories and current situation of financial data in enterprises, this article designs the system architecture, functional modules and data processing flow by using regression algorithm and clustering algorithm. In an application case of a large-scale enterprise, the average error rate of budget forecast is reduced to 4%, the accuracy of financial risk assessment is 92%, the data processing time is greatly shortened, and the single complex analysis is reduced from 120 minutes to 25 minutes. The performance of the system is significantly improved, and it meets the financial management needs of enterprises in terms of accuracy, efficiency and stability. This provides strong support for enterprises to rationally plan funds, prevent financial risks and optimize resource allocation, and promotes the modernization and transformation of financial management in enterprises.

title: Software-Hardware Co-Design of a Distributed Energy Access System Based on a Unified Sensing-Computing-Communication Chip
authors: Xiaolong Lyu, Qin Ma, Jie Du
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Confronting the challenges of poor compatibility, complex communication protocols, and insufficient operational reliability in existing distributed energy resource (DER) integration systems, this paper proposes a hardware-software codesign method based on an integrated sensing-computingcommunication chip. Centered around this chip, the method establishes a modular hardware platform for multi-source energy integration. At the software level, a lightweight embedded operating system is designed, which, alongside unified driver interfaces and a multi-core task scheduling strategy, enables synergistic hardware-software optimization. Furthermore, this paper introduces a protocol adaptation mechanism and a firmware self-recovery mechanism to address communication protocol switching and system fault restoration in complex environments. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system achieves improvements in energy efficiency utilization, task response time, and operational stability compared to conventional solutions.

title: Feature Extraction and Abnormal Pattern Recognition Technology for Fault Diagnosis of Electrical Automation Systems
authors: Wenhao Xu, Yaoyao Luo, Ziheng Xiao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the widespread application of electrical automation systems in industrial production, the reliability and fault diagnosis capabilities of the system have become a focus of attention. To improve the precision of fault diagnosis in electrical automation systems, this paper designs a feature extraction and anomaly pattern recognition model that combines deep learning (DL) and Bayesian neural networks (BNN). Firstly, the relevant variables are evaluated by distance correlation coefficient, and the modeling variables are screened using minimum redundancy maximum correlation (mRMR) algorithm, followed by feature extraction using convolutional neural network (CNN). Secondly, this article uses Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) networks to explore the correlation characteristics of multiple parameters and the temporal characteristics of historical data, and establishes a system operation state prediction model. In response to the diversity of abnormal patterns, this paper combines LSTM and BNN for recognition, and uses LSTM to achieve efficient classification of known fault categories; Introducing BNN to quantify uncertainty in model predictions, effectively distinguishing between known faults and unknown anomalies. The analysis of experimental results shows that the model proposed in this paper can improve the precision of fault diagnosis, effectively support intelligent operation and maintenance in industrial production, and enhance the efficiency of fault handling.

title: Bridging Classic and Modern AI for Analyzing Disagreement Between Lexical and Neural Code Retrieval
authors: Vishwa Bhatt, Jerry Cervantes-Fernandez, Zhixin Yu, Bryan Vera, Yunfei Hou, Jennifer Kim Jin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Aligning natural-language documentation with source code remains a core challenge in semantic code search, particularly when documentation varies in abstraction and lexical grounding. This work evaluates lexical, neural, and hybrid retrieval approaches under a global code search setting using the Java subset of the CodeSearchNet benchmark. A symbolic BM25 baseline is compared with a neural semantic encoder and two integration strategies: hybrid reranking and disagreement-aware routing. Neural retrieval consistently outperforms lexical retrieval, improving mean reciprocal rank from 0.271 to 0.318, while hybrid reranking further increases performance $(M R R=0.341)$. A disagreement-aware routing strategy achieves the best results (MRR $=0.345$), demonstrating that principled integration of symbolic and neural representations improves robustness. Analysis further shows that lexical–semantic rank disagreement systematically correlates with semantic abstraction, indicating that such disagreement reflects meaningful alignment differences rather than retrieval noise.

title: Multi-Strategy Improved Crested Porcupine Optimizer and Its Application
authors: Songye Yang, Shengli Xie, Wei Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: This study develops a multi-strategy improved Crested Porcupine Optimizer (MCPO), to improve the search behavior of the original Crested Porcupine Optimizer (CPO). The proposed algorithm introduces three modifications. First, Sobol Sequence combined with opposition-based learning is adopted to construct a more representative initial population. Second, a nonlinear inertia-weight mechanism is embedded into the fourth defense stage so that the search step can change adaptively during evolution. Third, a t-distribution mutation operator is applied to selected individuals to reduce stagnation and strengthen the ability to escape poor local regions. To examine its engineering applicability, MCPO is used to solve the speed reducer design problem. Over 30 independent runs, MCPO obtains Best = 2994.473, Mean = 2994.474, and Std = 1.60×10−5, outperforming the original CPO and four related improved CPO variants. These results indicate that MCPO provides a stable and accurate optimizer for constrained mechanical design and offers a practical reference for related engineering optimization problems within the conference scope.

title: A Dual-Branch Feature Enhancement Approach for Unsupervised Person Re-identification
authors: Guanghui Deng, Xiaoyong Song, Xuehong Sun, Liping Liu, Yingdong Li, Xiaolong Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: In addressing the challenges of insufficient globallocal joint modeling and limited discriminative feature representation in most existing unsupervised person reidentification methods, this paper proposes a pure Transformerbased dual-branch feature enhancement approach for unsupervised person re-identification. A deep-shallow dualbranch network architecture is designed to improve the discriminability of feature representations, while average fusion is employed to enhance feature diversity. Additionally, a dual attention mechanism is introduced, consisting of two stages: feature aggregation and feature redistribution. This mechanism dynamically enhances informative features and suppresses noise, thereby improving both the recognition accuracy and robustness of the model. The effectiveness of the proposed method is validated on two benchmark datasets: Market-1501 and DukeMTMC-reID.

title: An Adaptive Gmapping Algorithm Integrating IMU Observations and Lightweight Loopback
authors: Xinyu Ye, Zhenyu Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: To address odometer drift, particle degeneracy, and cumulative error during long-term operation of the traditional GMapping algorithm, this paper proposes a triple-improved simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM) method. First, an Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) is introduced to fuse inertial measurement unit (IMU) data with odometry data, providing a more robust motion prior for particle propagation. Second, an adaptive particle filter based on the Kullback–Leibler divergence (KLD) criterion is designed to dynamically adjust the particle number according to environmental complexity, thereby balancing estimation accuracy and computational cost. Third, a lightweight loop closure detection strategy based on Scan Context is employed to suppress long-term accumulated drift. Simulation experiments conducted on the Robot Operating System (ROS) platform show that the proposed method increases the average effective particle ratio from 0.53 to 0.72 and the minimum effective particle ratio from 0.24 to 0.38, while improving map IoU from 80.2% to 87.6%. In the ablation study, the complete model achieves the highest map IoU of 88.7% with an average Neff/M of 0.71, demonstrating the collaborative effectiveness of the three modules. These results indicate that the proposed method provides a lightweight yet effective enhancement for filter-based 2D LiDAR SLAM, and it is of practical significance for mobile robot mapping tasks requiring robustness, efficiency, and long-term consistency.

title: Improved White Shark Optimizer and Its Application in Path Planning
authors: Haolong Wu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: White Shark Optimizer (WSO) is an efficient metaheuristic algorithm, yet it suffers from issues like slow convergence, unbalanced exploration and exploitation, insufficient local search capability, and parameter sensitivity, often resulting in premature convergence. To overcome these drawbacks, this paper proposes an Improved White Shark Optimizer (IWSO). It adopts uniform initialization to enhance population diversity, introduces an adaptive step size to balance global and local search, and employs perturbation with a greedy selection mechanism. Validated on the path planning task, experimental results demonstrate that IWSO outperforms several state-of-the-art algorithms.

title: Intelligent Parameter Optimization for Degradable Cassava-Chitosan Liquid Mulch Film Driven by a Genetic Algorithm
authors: Zhi Li, Huixin Yang, Shangyun Yang, Xun Lan, Tiancheng Gao, Sitong Meng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Conventional preparation processes for degradable cassava-chitosan liquid mulch film rely on single-factor gradient experimental schemes, a method plagued by low parameter exploration efficiency, difficulties in balancing multiple performance metrics, and inadequate control precision-all of which fails to meet the requirements of large-scale intelligent production. As a pivotal technology in computerized intelligent optimization, genetic algorithms can tackle these bottlenecks by virtue of their global optimization capability and iterative evolutionary properties. Based on experimental data from cassava-chitosan liquid mulch film fabrication, this study establishes a full-process genetic algorithm optimization system. Covering experimental data preprocessing for constructing the algorithm input dataset, designing core algorithm modules (including decision variable quantification, fitness function modeling, and constraint condition formulation), and conducting full-linkage iterative optimization and result validation, the system ultimately replaces the traditional empirical control mode. The results demonstrate that after 200 generations of iteration, the genetic algorithm accurately outputs the optimal preparation parameter combination. This research confirms that genetic algorithms are the core driver for realizing the “data-algorithm dual-driven” mode of mulch film preparation, and the constructed optimization framework can provide a technical paradigm for the intelligent fabrication of analogous agricultural materials.

title: Research on 3D Reconstruction of Pick-and-Hang Robot Workspace Based on Binocular Vision and IMU Fusion
authors: Yuqiang He, Shiqiang Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Monocular vision 3D reconstruction mainly relies on the image information acquired by a single camera to infer the 3D structure of the scene, and the acquired image is one-sided, which leads to the low quality of 3D reconstruction, in order to study the 3D reconstruction method of pick-and-hang robot workspace based on binocular vision and IMU fusion. The left and right eye images are synchronously acquired through the checkerboard calibration board, and the precise calibration of the binocular system is completed to obtain the camera parameters. The IMU data are combined in image processing to compensate the position error and correct the boundary of the reachable space. Based on the calibration parameters and fusion data, a dense 3D model of the workspace is constructed to provide a decision basis for robot grasping and obstacle avoidance. The experimental results show that the errors of the method in the $X, Y$, and Z directions are $-0.15 \sim 0.22 \mathrm{~mm}$, $-0.02 \sim 0.06 \mathrm{~mm}$, and $0.95 \sim 1.42 \mathrm{~mm}$, respectively, and the completeness reaches $0.91 \sim 0.95$, and the success rate of collision detection is stabilized at $96 \% \sim 99 \%$, and all the indexes are superior to those of the comparison method, which verifies that the method is of high precision and practicability, and it can effectively support the pick-and-hang robot’s practical operational needs.

title: Cross-Dataset Speech Emotion Recognition Based on Resampling Normalization and Attentional LSTM Networks
authors: Bo Zhu, Lu Cao, Zhanbo Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: This study proposes a cross-dataset speech emotion recognition framework using resampling normalization and attentional LSTM to address domain variability. The method standardizes utterance length and applies spectral normalization, then uses a bidirectional LSTM with selfattention to process 1582-dimensional acoustic features. Experiments on CREMA-D and IEMOCAP for four emotions achieve 66.5% average Unweighted Accuracy across Leave-One-Dataset-Out protocol, outperforming the best baseline BiLSTM by 3.1 percentage points. Ablation shows resampling and spectral normalization provide 7.4% and 6.1% gains respectively. Limitations include reliance on acted datasets and computational overhead. Future work will evaluate on spontaneous speech and explore efficient attention mechanisms.

title: Research on High-Precision INS Gravity Information Measurement and Compensation Technology
authors: Wenxia Ma, Xin Zhao, Yuanming Wang, Wangyang Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: This paper focuses on the research of gravity information measurement and compensation technology in high-precision inertial navigation systems (INS). The correlation between the gravity field and the geomagnetic field is analyzed, providing a theoretical basis for underwater integrated navigation. The characteristics, error sources, and real-time compensation methods of underwater gravity measurement are discussed, with emphasis on key technologies for motion acceleration compensation, Eötvös correction, horizontal acceleration correction, depth correction, and zero drift correction in GNSS-denied environments, based on INS, Doppler Velocity Log (DVL), and depth sensors. An overall design scheme for an underwater gravity measurement system combining azimuth strapdown resolution and dual-axis stabilized platform control is proposed, aiming to improve the real-time performance and accuracy of gravity anomaly measurement, assist inertial navigation, and enhance comprehensive underwater navigation capabilities.

title: Faithful or Plausible? A Counterfactual Analysis of LLM-Generated Explanations for Machine Learning Models
authors: Shaun Colegado, Jennifer Jin, Yunfei Hou
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to generate natural language explanations of predictive machine learning models, yet their outputs often emphasize plausibility over faithfulness to the underlying model logic. This study applies counterfactual testing to evaluate the fidelity of LLM explanations in a structured setting: decision tree models predicting customer churn (i.e., when customers stop using a company’s product or service-a classic benchmark problem in predictive modeling research). We design two counterfactual tests across four prompting strategies that vary in whether dataset and/or model information are provided. Results from 1,200 generated explanations show that supplying model context substantially improves faithfulness whereas dataset context provides little benefit, and LLMs often broaden explanations beyond the model’s true decision path. Notably, even when given full model logic, LLMs remain predisposed toward generating plausible rather than strictly faithful explanations. These findings highlight both the promise and the limits of LLMs as interpreters of predictive models and introduce counterfactual testing as a potential framework for evaluating explanation faithfulness in data-heavy contexts.

title: Temporal Evolution Prediction of TCM Syndromes in Anorexic Children Based on LSTM-CNN Hybrid Network
authors: Shuya Tang, Kun Yang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: To address the difficulty in predicting the temporal evolution of traditional Chinese medicine (TCM) syndromes in anorexic children, a hybrid network combining Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is proposed. This study simplifies the background and related work, and focuses on the detailed design of the prediction model and experimental verification. First, the clinical data of anorexic children are preprocessed to construct a temporal sequence dataset of TCM syndromes. Then, the CNN layer is used to extract local correlation features of syndromic symptoms, and the LSTM layer is employed to capture the long-term temporal dependence of syndrome evolution. Finally, the effectiveness of the model is verified on the self-built dataset. Experimental results show that the proposed LSTM-CNN hybrid model outperforms single LSTM, CNN and other baseline models in terms of prediction accuracy, precision, recall and F 1 -score. It can accurately predict the temporal evolution trend of TCM syndromes in anorexic children, providing a reliable decision-making basis for clinical TCM diagnosis and treatment. The proposed method enriches the technical means of TCM syndrome prediction and has important clinical application value.

title: Day-Ahead PV Power Profile Forecasting Using Wavelet-BiLSTM Driven by Numerical Weather Prediction
authors: Yanguo Huang, Weifeng Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Accurate day-ahead photovoltaic (PV) power profile forecasting is crucial for grid scheduling but remains challenging due to nonstationary weather and seasonal shifts. We propose a wavelet-enhanced bidirectional LSTM (WT-BiLSTM) that augments NWP inputs with multi-scale wavelet components to forecast a 96-point PV power curve. Experimental results under a rolling-origin evaluation framework demonstrate that the proposed model consistently outperforms the BiLSTM baseline across four seasons. Moreover, the proposed approach demonstrates improved robustness to seasonal distribution shifts, supporting its effectiveness for day-ahead PV power profile forecasting.

title: Spatiotemporal Deformation Prediction in Permafrost Regions Based on CNN-LSTM Multi-Factor Fusion and SBAS-InSAR
authors: Yongwei Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: To address the low accuracy of traditional methods in predicting spatiotemporal deformation of permafrost regions affected by multiple factors, this paper proposes a prediction method combining SBAS-InSAR technology, CNN-LSTM network and multi-factor fusion. First, SBAS-InSAR technology is used to process Sentinel-1 SAR images to obtain the historical spatiotemporal deformation sequence of the study area. Then, environmental and anthropogenic factors affecting permafrost deformation are selected to construct a multi-factor dataset, which is fused with InSAR deformation features through a feature normalization and weighting strategy. The CNN-LSTM hybrid model is designed to extract spatial features of deformation data via CNN and capture long-term temporal dependencies via LSTM, realizing end-to-end spatiotemporal deformation prediction. Taking the Wudaoliang permafrost region on the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau as the study area, comparative experiments are carried out with single CNN, LSTM, SVR and MLP models. The results show that the proposed method achieves the optimal performance in evaluation indicators such as MAE ($\mathbf{2. 1 3 ~ m m}$), RMSE ($\mathbf{2. 8 7 ~ m m}$) and SSIM (0.942), which is significantly superior to traditional models and single deep learning models. It can accurately capture the nonlinear spatiotemporal deformation law of permafrost regions and provide technical support for geological disaster early warning and permafrost stability evaluation.

title: Design and Application of a Railway Infrastructure Fault Early Warning System Based on Deep Learning Object Detection Technology
authors: Yi Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: To improve the accuracy of railway infrastructure fault warnings, this study investigates the design and application of a railway infrastructure fault warning system based on deep learning object detection technology. The system hardware design adheres to modular principles, encompassing seven functional subsystems, including a core processing unit and power management. Low-power chips and protective circuits are selected to ensure stable operation in complex environments. Multi-parameter monitoring is performed using track inspection instruments, strain sensors, high-definition cameras, and other equipment, tailored to the specific characteristics of railway infrastructure. Targeted deployment is planned based on scientific formulas, resulting in a monitoring network covering tracks, bridges, tunnels, and overhead lines. An improved FLA-GSFPN deep learning model is constructed. The FLA attention module filters multi-scale features, and the anchor frame selection attention guidance module optimizes detection. The loss function is adjusted to improve railway infrastructure detection accuracy. Based on the fault probability output by the detection model, a status value is calculated. Dynamic warning thresholds are set, and health values are categorized into four warning levels. Corresponding operation and maintenance strategies are developed to achieve real-time, graded warnings for railway infrastructure faults. System testing results demonstrate that the designed system accurately captures infrastructure status changes at different locations along the railway line and issues timely warnings. Across 11 data sets, the average MAE (Massive Error Efficiency) was 0.6284, the average MSE (Meanwhile Average Sequential Error) was 0.2501, and the average RMSE (Residual Value Error) was 0.5001. The model prediction residuals were mostly within the range of [-2.0, 3.5], with very few outliers. The test results demonstrate that the designed system improves the accuracy of railway infrastructure fault warning by leveraging deep learning object detection technology.

title: Real-Time Path Planning for Multiple Autonomous Underwater Vehicles in Dense Obstacle Fields: The DA-IFDS Approach
authors: Xizhe Chen, Shihong Yin, Yujing Li, Zhengrong Xiang
publish: IEEE Journal of Oceanic Engineering
abstract: Multiple autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) face dense static and dynamic obstacle threats during path planning in complex and dynamic subsea environments. Therefore, each AUV must achieve safe, reliable, and real-time path planning under limited local observations. This article proposes a 3-D path planning method for multiple AUVs, named the dynamically adaptive-interfered fluid dynamical system (DA-IFDS) algorithm. It employs a two-layer real-time decision-making framework: the high-level decision-making module outputs step-by-step parameters based on proximal policy optimization (PPO), while the low-level module determines the velocity direction of each step according to these parameters. Such a hierarchical design improves the learning efficiency and adaptability of multiple AUVs. This algorithm models obstacles as disturbances in the flow field and incorporates these parameters into the action space. It employs bidirectional gated recurrent units to process variable-length observation sequences, effectively addressing the observation inconsistency faced by traditional methods in dynamic obstacle scenarios. In addition, it designs a diversified reward function. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed DA-IFDS algorithm outperforms soft actor-critic-IFDS, deep deterministic policy gradient-IFDS, and conventional PPO algorithms in terms of success rate, obstacle-avoidance performance, and planning efficiency, validating its effectiveness and superiority in real-time path planning for multiple AUVs in dense static and dynamic obstacle scenarios.

title: AV-Unified: A Unified Framework for Audio-Visual Scene Understanding
authors: Guangyao Li, Xin Wang, Wenwu Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: When humans perceive the world, they naturally integrate multiple audio-visual tasks within dynamic, real-world scenes. However, current works such as event localization, parsing, segmentation and question answering are mostly explored individually, making it challenging to comprehensively understand complex audio-visual scenes and explore inter-task relationships. Hence, we propose AV-Unified, a unified framework that enables joint learning across a wide range of audio-visual scene understanding tasks. AV-Unified standardizes the diverse input-output formats of each task and incorporates a multi-scale spatiotemporal perception network to effectively capture audio-visual associations. Specifically, we unify the inputs and outputs of all supported tasks by converting them into sequences of discrete tokens, establishing a shared representation that allows a single architecture to be trained jointly across heterogeneous varied datasets. Considering the varying temporal granularity of audio-visual events, a multi-scale temporal perception module is designed to capture key cues. Meanwhile, to overcome the lack of auditory supervision in the visual domain, we design a cross-modal guidance-based spatial perception module that models spatial audio-visual associations. Furthermore, task-specific text prompts are employed to enhance the model's adaptability and task-awareness. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets (e.g., AVE, LLP, MUSIC-AVQA, VGG-SS and AVS) demonstrate the effectiveness of AV-Unified across temporal, spatial, and spatiotemporal tasks.

title: Bilevel Optimized Implicit Neural Representation for Scan-Specific Accelerated MRI Reconstruction_supp1-3686724.mp4
authors: Yun Jiang
publish: N/A
abstract: Deep learning (DL) methods can reconstruct highly accelerated magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scans, but they rely on application-specific large training datasets and often generalize poorly to out-of-distribution data. Self-supervised deep learning algorithms perform scan-specific reconstructions, but still require complicated hyperparameter tuning based on the acquisition and often offer limited acceleration. This work develops a bilevel-optimized implicit neural representation (INR) approach for scan-specific MRI reconstruction. The method explicitly formulates the undersampled MRI reconstruction problem as a bilevel optimization problem and automatically optimizes the multidimensional hyperparameters of the reconstruction method for a given acquisition protocol, enabling a tailored reconstruction without training data. The proposed algorithm uses Gaussian process regression to optimize INR hyperparameters, accommodating various acquisitions. The INR includes a trainable positional encoder for high-dimensional feature embedding and a small multilayer perceptron for decoding. The bilevel optimization is computationally efficient, requiring only a few minutes per typical 2D Cartesian scan. On the scanner hardware, the subsequent scan-specific reconstruction—using offline-optimized hyperparameters—is completed within seconds, while achieving comparable or improved image quality compared to previous model-based and self-supervised learning methods.

title: A CVaR-Constrained Safe Reinforcement Learning Framework with Action Repair for Practical Portfolio Optimization
authors: Himanshu Choudhary, Arishi Orra, Manoj Thakur, Xiao-Zhi Gao, Prabhat Kumar Sahu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence
abstract: Portfolio optimization refers to allocating capital across multiple assets to attain an optimal balance between potential returns and associated risks under dynamic market conditions. While Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) has shown significant promise in learning adaptive allocation strategies through continuous interaction with market, most existing approaches struggle to incorporate practical investment constraints and robust risk measures. To address these limitations, this paper introduces a constraint-aware and risk-sensitive DRL framework for portfolio optimization that operates within a Constrained Markov Decision Process (CMDP) settings. The proposed model embeds Conditional Value-at-Risk (CVaR) as a hard safety constraint using an Augmented Lagrangian approach on the agent to prevent high-loss outcomes. Additionally, a repair mechanism is introduced to adjust the agent’s actions which ensures the resulting portfolio weights remain feasible and compliant with real-world investment constraints. Our approach is evaluated using real-world stock data from multiple global indices and consistently outperforms existing DRL-based and traditional portfolio optimization strategies, achieving superior returns while strictly adhering to defined risk constraints.

title: TFS-Net: Towards Reliable and Interpretable Lower-Limb sEMG Recognition Under Non-Ideal Signal Conditions
authors: Qianchen Liu, Liuyi Ling, Weiyan Lu, Liyu Wei, Yiming Liu, Jixiang Jia
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: In practical applications, lower-limb surface electromyography (sEMG) signals are susceptible to disturbances such as electrode displacement and transient noise, leading to unstable recognition outcomes. To address this, this study develops a novel, lightweight, and robust Time-Frequency masking and multi-Scale convolutional Network (TFS-Net) for lower-limb gait recognition. First, multichannel sEMG signals are transformed into time-frequency images to capture dynamic features via a multi-scale CNN module. During training, a Fixed-Time Masking Aligned (FTMA) strategy is explicitly employed to simulate real-world disturbances, thereby enhancing the model’s noise robustness. Furthermore, interpretability is validated both visually via Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) and quantitatively using a novel Biomechanical Alignment Score (BAS), ensuring the model’s decision logic aligns with neuromuscular mechanisms.Experiments on the ENABL3S dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a maximum intra-subject accuracy of 95.34% on ideal data and exhibits outstanding stability, retaining over 85% accuracy even under simulated random distortion where half of the channels are distorted. Moreover, the quantitative analysis confirms valid feature learning, with the BAS improving by up to 154.6% over random guessing. In summary, the proposed model effectively balances accuracy, stability, and interpretability, exhibiting significant potential for rehabilitation assistance and human-machine interaction.
