title: Integrated Energy-Efficient Scheduling of Distributed Flow Shop and Distribution Using a Q-Learning-Based Multi-Objective Cooperation Evolutionary Algorithm
authors: Zhengpei Zhang, Yaoguang Hu, Lixiang Zhang, Yanzi Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Production and distribution are indispensable in a supply chain. Practically, finding an overall optimization of the two processes has become an urgent problem. This article addresses an integrated energy-efficient scheduling problem of distributed flow shop and distribution in the context of economic globalization and environment sustainability. First, a mixed integer programing model is formulized to achieve minimal maximum completion time, minimal total energy consumption and minimal maximum workload of factories. Second, a Q-learning-based multi-objective cooperation evolutionary algorithm with problem-specific knowledge is specially developed. A cooperation strategy is devised to search a solution space using three populations. Three heuristic rules are proposed to initialize the populations. A knowledge-based local search is devised to refine better individuals. A Q-learning-based approach is designed to adaptively choose premium search strategies at each iteration. Finally, the designed method is compared with three meta-heuristics in prior research and a mathematical programming solver CPLEX. The comparison experiments are conducted by using a group of benchmark instances and a real-world case. The outcomes confirm outstanding capacities of the developed model and algorithm for dealing with the considered problems.

title: ASIC-based Compression Accelerators for Storage Systems: Design, Placement, and Profiling Insights
authors: Tao Lu, Jiapin Wang, Yelin Shan, Xiangping Zhang, Xiang Chen
publish: Proceedings of the 21st European Conference on Computer Systems

title: An Improved Hybrid Evolutionary Algorithm with Sine-Based Parameter Adaptation
authors: Ya’nan Sun, Yang Cao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: Linear Population Size Reduction Success-History based Adaptive Differential Evolution with Semi-Parameter Adaptation based Covariance Matrix Adaptation (LSHADE-SPACMA) is an evolutionary algorithm proposed in 2017 that combines Linear Population Size Reduction SHADE (LSHADE) and Covariance Matrix Adaptation Evolution Strategy (CMA-ES), showing strong performance when tackling high-dimensional complex optimization problems. However, since it employs CMA-ES during computation, some issues arise, such as insufficient local exploitation capability and relatively high computational complexity. To address these problems, this paper proposes an improved algorithm called LSHADE-XSCMA (LSHADE with eXponential Simplified CMA-ES). In the proposed method, a CMA variant, Exponential Simplified Covariance Matrix Adaptation Evolution Strategy (xSCMA-ES), is used to replace CMA-ES. Moreover, a sine-perturbation-based parameter self-adaptation mechanism is introduced to enhance the balance between exploration and exploitation. Finally, a new mutation strategy is designed to make full use of the elite individuals generated by xSCMA-ES, effectively improving interactions among components within the hybrid framework. The proposed algorithm is validated on the CEC2017 benchmark suite, and compared with several state-of-the-art algorithms. Experimental results on the CEC2017 benchmark suite show that LSHADE-XSCMA outperforms several state-of-the-art algorithms on most benchmark functions, achieving competitive Friedman rankings and improved accuracy and robustness. These results indicate that the proposed method provides an efficient and effective framework for solving high-dimensional complex optimization problems.

title: Research on the Algorithm Model of the Periodic Detection and Slip Early Warning System of the Belt Outlet Based on Visual Recognition
authors: Jiamin Lei, Yunting Liang, Ziyang Zeng, Huanchao Liu, Wenyu Liao, Long Zhao, Turui Ning, Yingxiang Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: This study presents a vision-based conveyor belt slot cycle detection and slip warning system designed to address production stability issues caused by belt slippage in tobacco production. The system relies on visual inspection devices at both the feeding and discharging ends of the conveyor belt, utilizing an optimized BKD-YOLOv8 algorithm to monitor slot positions in real-time. Through time-series analysis, it calculates slot movement velocity and cycle patterns, comparing them against preset parameters to achieve intelligent monitoring of conveyor belt operation. The system's core innovation lies in its dual verification mechanism: real-time displacement changes at the slot position detect velocity anomalies, while coordinated detection points validate cycle deviations. This significantly enhances detection accuracy. Rigorously designed warning triggers prevent false alarms, and the system maintains comprehensive operational data records for anomaly tracing and analysis, providing reliable maintenance documentation ${ }^{[1]}$. This technology effectively prevents production accidents caused by belt slippage, ensuring continuous and stable tobacco production with notable practical value. Its modular design enables adaptable implementation across conveyor belt systems of varying specifications ${ }^{[2]}$.

title: Smart Deep Learning for Managing Renewable-Powered Microgrids: A Digital Twin Implementation
authors: Qinghui Li, Xingjun Wang, Ruixiang Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: This study introduces an innovative demand response program for microgrids dominated by tidal and solar energy. A multi-objective optimization model is established to concurrently minimize total operating costs and mitigate grid failure risks associated with component reliability. To efficiently solve this complex, nonlinear problem, an enhanced Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO) is proposed. The algorithm incorporates a novel Dynamic Three-Phase Correction (DPC) mechanism, which enhances population diversity and global search ability, leading to faster convergence and a reduced likelihood of local optima entrapment. Furthermore, to address the inherent uncertainty of renewable generation, a forecasting model based on Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) is developed to provide accurate hourly predictions for both tidal and solar power output. The adversarial training process enables the model to better capture the complex patterns of real generation data. The entire integrated framework is validated through comprehensive simulations on a modified IEEE 33-bus test system configured as an islanded microgrid. Results demonstrate that the proposed methodology significantly outperforms conventional benchmark algorithms, achieving superior economic efficiency and enhanced operational reliability for the scheduling of smart microgrids with high renewable penetration.

title: Research on Particle Statistical Analysis of High-Voltage Switchgear
authors: MingMing Yao, XiangKe Wang, Yi Yang, LiuCheng Hao, ZhiJun Wang, Hao Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the rapid development of China’s power grid, the scale of the grid is increasing day by day. Gas-insulated switchgear (GIS) is an important substation equipment and is widely used in the power system.Based on the operating experience over the past 40 years, insulation failures have always been one of the key factors affecting the reliability of GIS, and conductive particle contamination is the most significant cause of insulation failures. The particles generated under different voltage levels and different numbers of mechanical disconnection operations were statistically analyzed and studied.By using descriptive statistical analysis methods, different devices, shapes, sizes, materials and distribution locations were classified. Scanning electron microscopy was employed to collect and analyze the shapes of the particles.By defining the shape of the particles, the main particles generated under different voltage levels and their distribution locations can be obtained. This further provides practical statistical data support for the insulation reliability of high-voltage switchgear.

title: Bridging Lexical and Contextual Semantics for Adolescents’ Health Sentiment Analysis: A Cross-Platform Study Using Large Language Models
authors: Amima Shifa, G. G. Md. Nawaz Ali, Dylan Henricks
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: To understand, how adolescents emotionally engage with online health-related content, we require both contextual reasoning and semantic interpretation. This study presents a hybrid semantic framework that bridges classic and modern AI techniques for sentiment analysis of obesity-related discourse on social media platforms like TikTok and Instagram. The approach integrates lexicon-based polarity estimation with large language model’s (e.g., GPT-4) contextual refinement to enhance the derivation and integration of affective semantics from user-generated text. The dataset comprises of approximately 21,000 public comments related to adolescence obesity, healthy diet etc. categorized across five different content source types, including, healthcare professionals, fitness coaches, influencers, food and beverage creators, and others. Comparative results show that Instagram content exhibits cohesive, positive sentiment, while TikTok demonstrates greater emotional polarization and variability. By combining symbolic sentiment modeling with generative AI reasoning, this work contributes to semantic computing for health communication and highlights pathways for bridging lexical semantics and contextual intelligence in real-world social media analysis. The results also underscore the value of integrating large language models in health sentiment research and highlight the need for platform-sensitive, credibility-aware communication strategies when addressing adolescent audiences online.

title: LLM-based Zero-shot Triple Extraction for Automated Ontology Generation from Software Engineering Standards
authors: Songhui Yue
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Ontologies have supported knowledge representation and white-box reasoning for decades; thus, the automated ontology generation (AOG) plays a crucial role in scaling their use. Software engineering standards (SES) consist of long, unstructured text (with high noise) and paragraphs with domain-specific terms. In this setting, relation triple extraction (RTE), together with term extraction, constitutes the first stage toward AOG. This work proposes an open-source large language model (LLM)-assisted approach to RTE for SES. Instead of solely relying on prompt-engineering-based methods, this study promotes the use of LLMs as an aid in constructing ontologies and explores an effective AOG workflow that includes document segmentation, candidate term mining, LLM-based relation inference, term normalization, and cross-section alignment. Expert-annotated reference sets at three granularities are constructed and used to evaluate the ontology generated from the study. The results show that it is comparable and potentially superior to the OpenIE method of triple extraction.

title: Machine Learning-Based Barrier Gate System for Security and Monitoring
authors: Jerome Carl R. Dagle, James Randall B. Gira, Renyl Gione C. Medina, Rafael Caesar M. Sebastian, Juliet O. Niega, Adrian M. Manalo
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: This study presents a machine learning-based automated barrier gate system for vehicle access control and security monitoring using real-time License Plate Recognition (LPR) integrated with Internet of Things (IoT) technologies. The system employs an IP camera to capture live vehicle images, where YOLOv8 performs real-time license plate localization and Optical Character Recognition (OCR) extracts alphanumeric characters from diverse Philippine license plate formats, including modern plates and older designs featuring the Rizal Monument. Through IoTenabled communication, recognition results are transmitted instantly to a centralized processing unit and cloud database, allowing immediate verification and automated barrier gate actuation without manual intervention]. Recognized license plate data are validated against a Firebase/Firestore database, while unregistered vehicles trigger system alerts that prompt security personnel to record driver information such as name, destination, and contact number. Vehicle entries are logged automatically, achieving 100% successful data synchronization across all test sessions, and corresponding video recordings are stored for each granted access to support real-time monitoring, auditing, and postincident review through a centralized dashboard. The system was trained using Philippine license plate datasets and evaluated under varying environmental conditions, including clean, dirty, and low-light plates, following ISO/IEC 25010 quality standards. Experimental results show a license plate detection accuracy of 96.67% across 180 test samples, reliable OCR performance, 100% access verification accuracy, fast system response suitable for realtime operation, and 100 % logging reliability. These results demonstrate that the proposed IoT-enabled system provides a practical, cost-effective, and scalable solution for modern vehicle access control applications.

title: Autonomous Mobile Robot Navigation in Multi-Room Environments Using Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3)
authors: Ayaulym Parmash, Ingkar Baidaly, Yernar Akhmetbek
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Autonomous navigation in complex indoor environments remains a significant challenge due to the sparsity of rewards and the high dimensionality of sensor data. In order to enable a Pioneer 3-DX mobile robot to navigate a six-room environment, this work provides a Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) technique using the Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) algorithm. The robot learns to accomplish randomly generated goals while avoiding obstacles like tables and chairs by combining visual input via a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) with scalar state information via a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP). Our findings show how well the suggested reward function and architecture work to achieve reliable navigation in multi-room arrangements, as measured by Success weighted by Path Length (SPL) and Success Rate.

title: Anthropomorphized Interfaces and User Perception in Financial AI Assistants
authors: Moldir Akhmetova, Alina Gutoreva
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Financial decision-making is increasingly supported by AI-based assistants, yet user trust remains a critical challenge in high-stakes such as finance. Anthropomorphization, defined as the inclusion of human-like characteristics in artificial intelligence, has been proposed as a means of fostering trust in AI-human interaction; however, its effects in financial decisionmaking contexts remain insufficiently understood. This study investigates how varying levels of anthropomorphism in AI financial advisors influence user trust and intention to use, with a focus on the perceptual mechanisms underlying these effects. An experiment was conducted in which participants interacted with one of four AI interface conditions representing increasing degrees of anthropomorphism. The results indicate that anthropomorphic design does not directly increase trust, but instead exerts an indirect effect through user perceptions, particularly perceived empathy and interaction quality. Trust was found to strongly predict intention to use AI-based financial advice. These findings suggest that effective AI financial advisor design should prioritize meaningful, empathetic interaction over surface-level humanization to support appropriate and responsible trust.

title: AI-Powered Medical Chatbot with Neural Network Symptom Analysis and Automated Appointment Scheduling
authors: Abdullayev Meirkhan, Praveen Kumar
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: The proposed research work describes an AIassisted medical chatbot system incorporating a topic-related large language model, convolutional neural networks to analyze medical images, and an automatic appointment system. The proposed system utilizes the Meditron medical language model to obtain patient symptoms and determine the level of urgency through intuitive chat interactions. A convolutional neural network, trained with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5, 0 0 0}$</tex> marked medical images of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2}$</tex> typical symptoms, enables visual symptoms to be determined with an average accuracy of 94.3 % on the hold-out set. The appointment system incorporates a rule-based priority algorithm, which takes into account symptoms, specialties, and availability of consulting physicians. The pilot test conducted with 250 users confirmed that average waiting time in consultations was reduced by 47 %, with response times of less than 5 seconds even in cases of simultaneous interaction of numerous users. The study confirms the effectiveness of combining topic-related language models and deep learning algorithms in implementing preliminary patient screening in telemedicine systems.

title: Establishing a Reliable FBCSP Baseline for Robust BCI Systems
authors: Aigerim Aitim, Zhuldyz Bagybek
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Filter Bank Common Spatial Patterns (FBCSP) is widely used as a classical baseline in motor imagery BCI research. Despite its popularity, many published implementations suffer from methodological shortcomings that limit both reproducibility and practical relevance. Typical issues include information leakage caused by feature selection on the full dataset, superficial use of ICA without consistent artifact removal, and evaluation protocols that ignore class imbalance or subject variability. In this work, we present a fully reproducible FBCSP pipeline designed to address these limitations. The approach employs nested stratified cross-validation to ensure unbiased performance estimation, integrates ICA with automatic component rejection to improve signal quality, and reports results using chance-corrected metrics such as Cohen's Kappa. All processing steps are explicitly defined and implemented using standard, open-source tools. Evaluation on the BCI Competition IV-2a dataset demonstrates a mean classification accuracy of 81.8 percent, corresponding to a Cohen's Kappa of 0.757. This represents an improvement of 7.5 percentage points over the original FBCSP baseline. The proposed pipeline provides a robust and transparent reference point for future studies, particularly in neurorehabilitationoriented BCI system development, and all code is released publicly to support independent verification and reuse.

title: Dynamic Load Balancing Algorithms in Cloud Computing: A Review
authors: Raiymbek Zhanuzak
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Load balancing is a critical component of cloud computing that ensures efficient distribution of workloads across available resources. Dynamic load balancing algorithms adapt to real-time changes in workload and resource availability, making them more effective than static approaches in cloud environments. This review presents a systematic analysis of dynamic load balancing algorithms with a focus on key performance metrics such as makespan, response time, throughput, scalability, and fault tolerance. The algorithms are classified and compared based on their scheduling strategies and optimisation objectives, highlighting their impact on makespan reduction and system scalability. The paper also identifies existing challenges and research gaps, providing insights for the development of more efficient and scalable load balancing solutions in cloud computing.

title: An Enhanced Artificial Bee Colony with Dual Search Strategies and Local Search
authors: Yang Cao, Yunpeng Jia
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: To solve continuous optimization problems, this paper proposes a Dual-search based Localized Improvement Enhanced Artificial Bee Colony Algorithm (EDABC). This algorithm balances global search and local development using two complementary search strategies. A local search is performed following the scout bee phase to improve convergence. In addition, we implement a self-adaptation rule which changes the probability of choosing each strategy according to their current fitness during evolution. The proposed algorithm is evaluated on 22 benchmark functions and compared with several ABC and DE variants. Experimental results show that EDABC achieves the highest optimization accuracy on most test functions, obtains the best Friedman rank among all compared algorithms, and demonstrates improvements in convergence speed and solution quality. These results confirm its effectiveness for complex engineering optimization problems.

title: Projection Neural Network for Solving Interval Quadratic Programming Problems
authors: Feng Zhou, Shenglan Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: This paper presents a second-order projection neural network to solve interval quadratic programming problems. The existence and uniqueness of the solution trajectories of the neural network are analyzed. Relying on Lyapunov analysis, the generated trajectories converge asymptotically to the optimal solution of the quadratic programming problem. The exponential convergence of the trajectories is also guaranteed under strong convexity. Numerical experiment is conducted to demonstrate the effectiveness and applicability of the proposed neural network approach.

title: Early Warning Signals Appear Long Before Dropping Out: An Idiographic Approach Grounded in Complex Dynamic Systems Theory

publish: Proceedings of the 13th ACM IKDD International Conference on Data Science

title: PGCSPose: Physics-Constrained Generation and Causal Semantic Fusion for Robust In-Hand Pose Estimation
authors: Peiliang Wu, Yao Li, Mingyue Niu, Wenbai Chen, Guowei Gao
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Accurate in-hand pose estimation is essential for dexterous robotic manipulation but remains fragile under severe visual occlusion ($>$50%) and intermittent tactile contact. Existing visuo-tactile fusion methods treat vision and touch symmetrically, overlooking the asymmetric dependency: visual observations (object geometry and grasp) influence tactile feedback through contact mechanics. We present PGCSPose, a framework that leverages this vision$\rightarrow$tactile dependency via two components: TacDiffusion, a physics-constrained diffusion model that synthesizes plausible tactile signals under contact loss, and CauCon, a reasoning module inspired by causal principles that integrates semantic priors from language models for robust inference. Extensive evaluation on ObjectInHand and VinT-6D demonstrates that PGCSPose reduces pose error by $>$50% under 70% occlusion and 60% tactile dropout, achieving position errors of 0.44–0.47 cm and angular errors of 0.075–0.081 rad across benchmarks, while maintaining real-time performance (16.1 FPS). These results demonstrate that physics-constrained generation and causally-inspired semantic reasoning enable reliable manipulation under degraded sensing conditions.

title: Evaluating Explainable AI Implementation and User Agency Across Major Social Media Platforms
authors: Shafiq Alam, Aditya Pawade, Muhammad Sohaib Ayub, Saeed Ur Rehman, Asma Ayub
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Big Data (BigData)
abstract: AI-driven recommendation algorithms increasingly shape user experience on social media, raising concerns about transparency, accountability, and user agency. This paper presents a comparative analysis of Explainable AI (XAI) implementations on Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, and Twitter/X. Using a structured framework, we assess two key dimensions: Explanation Adequacy, defined by clarity, specificity, relevance, and verifiability, and Explanation Actionability, defined by proximity, granularity, and reversibility of controls. Our evaluation combines feature audits, cross-platform comparisons, and rubric-based scoring. Results show Facebook provides the strongest balance of adequacy (4/5) and actionability (4/5), Twitter/X offers limited adequacy (2/5) but moderate actionability (3/5), TikTok demonstrates strong actionability (4/5) but generic explanations, and Instagram performs moderately (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 / 5$</tex> on both dimensions). We further extend the analysis by linking adequacy and actionability to perceived usefulness, trust, satisfaction, and algorithmic scepticism. Findings highlight tensions between algorithmic sophistication, user comprehension, and engagement optimization, while also revealing regulatory implications under GDPR and the DSA. This work contributes a standardized evaluation framework for XAI in social computing, empirical evidence of platform disparities, and practical design guidelines for enhancing transparency and user empowerment in recommender systems.

title: Mathematical Models in Disease Transmission: A Case Study of Measles with an Optimized SIRS Model
authors: Danyu Yuan
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Mathematical modeling constitutes a fundamental tool for understanding infectious disease transmission dynamics and guiding evidence-based public health policy decisions. This paper advances the application of compartmental models to measles transmission by developing an enhanced Susceptible-Infectious-Recovered-Susceptible (SIRS) framework that incorporates vital dynamics (birth and death rates) and waning immunity. The research employs a rigorous data-driven approach, utilizing nearly five decades of annual measles surveillance data from the United States (1974–2021) sourced from the World Health Organization. Through rigorous data preprocessing, encompassing systematic cleaning, validation, and quality assessment procedures, this study establishes a robust empirical foundation for model development. The optimized SIRS model demonstrates superior capability in capturing the endemic persistence of measles in highly vaccinated populations compared to traditional SIR models. The analysis reveals that vaccination programs have successfully reduced the effective reproduction number from approximately 15 in the pre-vaccine era to 1.2 in the current two-dose vaccination period, representing a 92% reduction in the intrinsic transmission potential of the virus. This research contributes to the field by providing a more realistic mathematical framework for long-term disease dynamics and demonstrates the critical importance of maintaining high vaccination coverage to prevent measles resurgence.

title: A Memory-Efficient Grey Wolf Optimization Algorithm and Its Performance Evaluation in High-Dimensional Problems
authors: Weixi Wu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: The Gray Wolf Optimization Algorithm is widely applied to continuous optimization problems due to its simple structure and strong global search capability. However, in high-dimensional scenarios, it often faces issues of high memory consumption and reduced convergence efficiency. This paper proposes a memory-efficient Gray Wolf Optimization algorithm that reduces the algorithm’s space complexity by minimizing intermediate state storage during individual updates and simplifying the leader individual update strategy. To validate its effectiveness, comprehensive experiments were conducted on over 20 classic continuous benchmark functions across 30 to 100 dimensions. Experimental results demonstrate that the improved algorithm achieves $20 \%-35 \%$ faster convergence while reducing peak memory consumption by approximately $30 \%$. Stability assessments reveal a consistent reduction in solution variance under high-dimensional complex landscapes. Furthermore, parameter sensitivity analysis confirms the algorithm’s robustness to changes in contraction and weighting factors. A real-world antenna array synthesis task further validates its practical performance, achieving notable improvements in pattern stability and memory efficiency. These findings indicate that the proposed method not only enhances search effectiveness but also provides substantial memory benefits, making it highly suitable for resource-constrained or large-scale optimization tasks in real engineering contexts.

title: Spatio-Temporal Delay Modeling and Dynamic Parameter Optimization for Traffic Flow Prediction
authors: Weibin Deng, Sen Liu, Wei Wang, Jin Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Spatio-temporal traffic forecasting is challenged by time-varying, non-instantaneous spatial propagation, and strong node-specific and time-specific heterogeneity. Most existing methods either capture temporal patterns without explicitly quantifying inter-node propagation delays, or lack mechanisms to adapt model parameters to evolving heterogeneous dynamics. We propose Spatio-Temporal Delay Capture and Parameter Optimization (STDCPO), a framework that (1) explicitly estimates propagation delays using bandwidth-constrained DTW with similarity-threshold filtering and (2) introduces a Dynamic Parameter Optimization Module (DPOM) to generate node- and time-specific parameters for heterogeneity-aware fusion within a Transformer architecture. Experiments on four PeMS datasets demonstrate that STDCPO consistently outperforms strong baselines across standard forecasting metrics.

title: A Supervised Learning-Based Early Warning Model for Learning Risk in the Teaching Process
authors: Liheng Zhao, Lisong Ma, Xinyi Wang, Jinyi Wang, Bohong Ye, Chenxi Xiong
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: The increasing integration of artificial intelligence into education has made analyzing teaching effectiveness a key focus for improving instructional quality. Accurately identifying learning risks among diverse students prior to final assessments to enable personalized learning support has become an essential strategy for enhancing teaching effectiveness. To address this practical need, a supervised learning-based early warning model for identifying learning risks in student coursework is proposed. The model identifies course-related learning risks by analyzing student performance in regular assignments and laboratory sessions. Using academic data from the Data Structures course at Chengdu Jincheng College, regular performance scores were first normalized to eliminate dimensional inconsistencies, followed by weighting to amplify the influence of key assessments. To mitigate the long-tail distribution issue caused by a low proportion of positive samples (failing students), the “decoupling representation & classifier” method was adopted. The model was then trained and validated. Experimental results demonstrate high predictive accuracy and strong robustness, providing accurate and objective decision support for identifying students at learning risk.

title: Teacher Emotion Perception and Adaptive Regulation Based on ARTEMIS Artwork Annotations
authors: Wu Wang, Qiang Wan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: This study proposes a media-based interactive emotion perception and adaptive regulation framework for teachers based on emotion annotations from the ARTEMIS dataset. Discrete emotion categories are mapped into a continuous valence-arousal (V-A) space following the Russell circumplex model. A dynamic target emotion is defined as a moderately positive and low-arousal state (v=0.5, a=0.4). Teachers’ workday emotion dynamics (08:00–16:00) are modeled using a time-series approach, where morning elevation and sin(π(t−8)/4) and −sin(π(t−12)/4), respectively. The Euclidean distance between the current emotional state and the target is used to quantify regulation demand, and an adaptive strategy is generated via a sliding window algorithm (window size = 5). Emotion density analysis and stability metrics are further employed to identify optimal and critical intervention moments, based on which personalized artwork recommendation sequences are produced to support indirect emotion regulation for teachers.

title: Research on Digital Twin-Based 3D Character Modeling and Real-time Interaction Technology
authors: Kun Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: The convergence of digital twin technology with 3D character modeling has opened new frontiers in creating interactive virtual humans capable of real-time synchronization with their physical counterparts. This paper presents a comprehensive framework for digital twin-based 3D character modeling and real-time interaction, addressing the critical challenges of realistic avatar generation, dynamic data synchronization, and natural user interaction. Our methodology integrates multi-source data acquisition, hybrid 3D reconstruction techniques, and a bidirectional communication architecture that enables seamless cyberphysical interaction. Through systematic experiments, we validate our approach using multiple datasets and performance metrics, demonstrating significant improvements in modeling accuracy (with geometric errors reduced by 23.7% compared to baseline methods) and real-time responsiveness (achieving synchronization latencies under $\mathbf{5 0 m s}$). The research contributes to advancing virtual human technology with applications spanning immersive entertainment, industrial simulation, and telepresence systems, while identifying critical challenges in uncanny valley mitigation, computational optimization, and ethical considerations for future development. The proposed framework represents a significant step toward creating photorealistic, interactive digital twins that bridge the physical and virtual realms.

title: Efficient Uncertainty Propagation in Continuous Bayesian Networks Using GUM and Monte Carlo Methods
authors: Luisa Hoffmann, Michael Heizmann, Ali Darijani, Jürgen Beyerer
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: In large and complex systems such as the Circular Factory, vast amounts of heterogeneous and uncertain data are generated. Classical Bayesian Networks (BNs) provide a principled framework for reasoning under uncertainty, but their reliance on discrete Conditional Probability Tables (CPTs) limits their applicability in continuous domains. In the context of semantic computing, where heterogeneous sensor data and knowledge graph information must be integrated, this limitation prevents probabilistic reasoning from being consistently applied to continuous quantities and measurement-based information. This paper introduces a framework for continuous Bayesian Networks that replaces discrete CPTs with functional dependencies and integrates the Guide to the Expression of Uncertainty in Measurement (GUM) as a standardized approach for analytical uncertainty propagation. The proposed method enables probabilistic reasoning over continuous variables while explicitly accounting for measurement and parameter uncertainty in a mathematically transparent way. To complement the analytical formulation, Monte Carlo (MC) simulations with Gaussian Mixture Models (GMMs) are employed to handle non-linear and non-Gaussian cases, allowing for numerical validation of the GUM approximation. We present illustrative examples comparing both approaches and discuss their agreement and limitations. The results demonstrate that GUM-based inference provides accurate and computationally efficient uncertainty estimates in locally linear regions, while Monte Carlo methods serve as a reliable benchmark for more complex, non-linear dependencies. This framework thus lays the foundation for robust and interpretable uncertainty reasoning in environments such as the Circular Factory. Furthermore, it provides a bridge between probabilistic reasoning and semantic representations by enabling continuous uncertainty information to be embedded within knowledge-based systems, thus advancing uncertainty-aware semantic computing.

title: Hyper-Geri: Multimodal Hypergraph Learning Fusing Vitals and EHR for Geriatric Healthcare
authors: Dongying Li, Shiyang Lian
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: The management of multimorbidity and polypharmacy in older adults offers critical insights into patient safety and healthcare quality. However, geriatric clinical data is particularly complex due to drug-drug interactions and fragile health states, impeding reliable prediction of unplanned readmission. Conventional approaches that compartmentalize modalities are ineffective, as they fail to simultaneously capture structural disease-drug associations and dynamic vital sign changes, often missing acute deterioration risks and leading to suboptimal medication safety and frequent readmissions. This paper proposes a novel multimodal hypergraph learning method, HyperGeri, to address these issues. Specifically, a Vital Encoder first extracts high-frequency temporal representations, which serve as physiological priors in a Knowledge-Aware Hypergraph, enabling comprehensive semantic linking of patient state with disease and therapy. Concurrent multi-task optimization jointly tunes readmission prediction and medication recommendation. Experiments on the geriatric-specific MIMIC-4 dataset demonstrate favorable outcomes, with consistent improvements in AUC and F1 scores and effective generalization. The results further show that our approach surpasses current state-of-the-art methods in predictive accuracy and medication safety.

title: Research on Short-Term Estimation of Power Carbon Intensity (IECEF) based on Deep Learning
authors: Kangzhe Yi, Qiaofeng Wu, Jiaxi Chen, Li Pan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: There are significant differences in electricity carbon intensity (IECEF) between countries and years, and its change is affected by many factors such as power generation structure, fuel price, demand side characteristics and policies. Based on the existing panel data flow, this paper constructs a short-term estimation study of electric power carbon intensity (IECEF) based on deep learning, and carries out short-term estimation of IECEF. The method consists of three deep learning modules. Firstly, a multi-layer perceptron (MLP) is used for static regression, which is partially dependent on the curve (PDP) and permutation importance to improve interpretability. Secondly, based on the TARNet structure of representation learning, the high proportion of renewable energy is used as the treatment, and the low proportion is used as the control to learn the conditional average treatment effect (CATE) to reveal the heterogeneous effects under different combinations of price and renewable proportion. Finally, the national sequence LSTM is constructed, and the countries are sorted by year to form a sequence, so as to capture time continuity. In terms of data, when the panel file is not provided locally, the power carbon intensity and renewable proportion data of OWID are automatically downloaded and docked to construct the minimum panel. The experiment was divided into training and verification according to national stratification to reduce the optimistic bias caused by national repetition. The representative operation results show that MLP achieves about 0.011 RMSE (unit $\mathbf{t C O}_{\mathbf{2}} / \mathbf{M W} \cdot \mathbf{h}$) on the validation set, TARNet ‘s CATE heat map shows that there is a more significant marginal emission reduction effect in the middle and high renewable proportion and median price area, and LSTM shows a closer fitting than static regression when the sequence length and the number of countries are sufficient. This paper combines predictive performance, interpretability and heterogeneous effect analysis to provide a reusable technical path for energy system analysis.

title: Differential Privacy-Preserving Personalized Federated Learning via MetaLearning
authors: Guixin Wang, Yukun Zheng, Zhiming Cai
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: To effectively protect user data privacy and realize distributed personalized training in edge computing scenarios, a personalized federated learning method based on differential privacy is proposed. A personalized federated learning framework was built that allowed participating clients to train models using local data and apply differential privacy techniques, introducing Gaussian noise that met local differential privacy requirements to protect data privacy. The client uploads the model parameters with differential privacy protection to the server, which aggregates and updates the global model before distributing it to the client for the next round of updates. This process is repeated until the model meets the differential privacy requirements. By integrating differential privacy mechanisms with personalized federated learning, all clients can jointly train a global meta model and adaptively adjust it based on their respective privacy datasets to generate personalized models that conform to local data characteristics, ensuring the utility and communication efficiency of personalized models while ensuring privacy and security of the data. The experimental results show that the method can achieve a balance between data privacy and model performance for different privacy budgets. At the same time, compared with other methods, this method shows significant advantages in model convergence accuracy, training time, etc., and can maintain a stable classification accuracy of over 90% for clothing images while ensuring data privacy and security, significantly improving the efficiency and model performance of distributed personalized training.

title: Testing Bias Tolerance of Large Language Models with Mirrored Paradoxical Ambiguity Probing
authors: Md Nur Amin, Phil Nemeth, Alexander Jesser
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Unmasking bias in language models requires more than superficial tests. This paper dives deep with fresh and precise probing methods; Incoherent Sentence Blending, Nonlinear Contextual Jumps, and Stereotype Saturation, designed to push language models beyond their comfort zones and expose underlying biases. Each probe method targets a distinct facet of model behavior: Incoherent Sentence Blending tests the model’s reaction to nonsensical inputs, Nonlinear Contextual Jumps challenge the model’s adaptability to sudden topic shifts, and Stereotype Saturation evaluates repeated exposure to biased contexts. We measure how consistently these models stick to stereotypes, respond to absurd inputs, or manage sharp topic shifts using the metrics of Consistency, Contradiction, and Ambiguity. The findings are eye-opening: despite advances, models still show signs of bias lurking beneath diverse outputs, revealing their tendency to rely on memorized, stereotyped patterns.

title: AgroExpert KG: A Hybrid Machine Learning and Rule-Based Decision Support System for Crop Yield Prediction in Kyrgyzstan
authors: Akylai Topoeva, Tattybubu Arap Kyzy, Timur Turatali, Ruslan Isaev, Emilbek Zholdoshbekov
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: This paper presents the AgroExpert KG decision support system, developed to address the important problem of data scarcity in precision agriculture for small farmers in Kyrgyzstan. The system combines the Random Forest regressor, trained on synthetic data, with transparent, rule-based agronomic recommendations. A new method for creating synthetic data is presented, which transforms limited expert standards into 5,000 training samples by including penalty functions that take into account nutrient deficiencies, pH imbalances, and insufficient precipitation. The model achieves a MAE of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.51 \mathrm{t} / \text{ha}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> of 0.993 on synthetic test data, demonstrating successful approximation of the expert-defined agronomic response functions. Robustness evaluations confirm stability: noise <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{~} \mathbf{1 0 - 2 0 \%})$</tex> increases MAE by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{i} 10 \%$</tex>, out-of-range testing (extreme rainfall) shows graceful degradation, variance across seeds is low (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{s t d}=\mathbf{0. 0 5} \mathbf{~ t} / \mathbf{h a}$</tex>), and sensitivity to penalty coefficients (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha_{i} \pm \mathbf{2 0 \%}$</tex>) affects predictions by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{;} \mathbf{1 0 \%}$</tex>. Uncertainty intervals via <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R F}$</tex> variance average <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 3}$</tex> t/ha. The integrated Decision Support System (DSS) provides operational alerts for nutrient management, while SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) ensures interpretability of the model. The Streamlit web application provides farmers with personalized yield forecasts and recommendations with a user-friendly interface. The analysis of the importance of traits determines the type of crop and phosphorus level as the most influential predictors. The proposed approach represents a practical solution for improving the efficiency of fertilizer use and crop selection decisions in seven regions of Kyrgyzstan, creating the basis for sustainable agricultural intensification in regions with limited access to data. Note: Results are based on synthetic data; future validation on real fields is planned.

title: Ensemble Learning Techniques to Predict Student Success Based on Online Engagement Patterns in a Virtual Learning Environment
authors: Samuel-Soma M. Ajibade, Mary Angelie P. Velos, Leomar A. Ypanto, Rosie Fe B. Legaspino, Jiña Marie R. Mabanto, Mersin C. Villagonzalo, Feliciana G. Cababat, Johnry P. Dayupay
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Virtual Learning Environments (VLEs) have changed higher education by giving students easy, flexible, and digital access to all of their learning materials. By looking at how engaged students are on these platforms, we can better understand how well they are doing in school and help students who are at danger. Even though there is a lot of VLE data available, many schools have trouble using it to make accurate early predictions about how well kids will do, which makes it harder to help students who are at danger of failing. This project seeks to forecast academic performance by analyzing online interaction patterns from the Open University Learning Analytics Dataset (OULAD), utilizing machine learning to pinpoint significant factors and facilitate early interventions. The OULAD dataset, which comes from the UCI Machine Learning Repository, has anonymous student engagement measures like how often they log in, how much time they spend on VLE, how often they post in forums, and how many assignments they turn in. Python libraries like Pandas, Scikit-learn, and Seaborn have been used to create machine learning models including logistic regression, decision trees, and random forests for data preprocessing, modeling, and visualization. Initial findings indicate that persistent early-term engagement, especially frequent logins and assignment submissions, is a substantial predictor of superior academic performance, whereas irregular engagement is associated with diminished grades. These findings empower instructors to execute tailored interventions, enhancing retention and individualizing the learning experience. Subsequent study may amalgamate multimodal data to fortify model resilience across various educational settings.

title: Governing Algorithmic Collusive Pricing Strategies in Repeated Bertrand Games: A Comparative Analysis of Q-Learning, Sarsa, and Deep Q-Network Agents
authors: Ekanaka Hasudungan Pratama, Evaristus Didik Madyatmadja, Hendry Hartono
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Pricing algorithms are the dominant form in the digital economy today. This raises grave antitrust concerns regarding autonomous collusion. Although economists have proven that simple table-based agents are able to collude through the application of economic theory, the same cannot be said for the more advanced Deep Learning agents. This study attempts to bridge the knowledge gap. This study employs a simulation to replicate the repeated Bertrand Duopoly competition between the three models: Q-Learning, Sarsa, and Deep Q-Networks (DQN). To ensure the study is methodologically sound, the results were validated through multiple independent statistical tests. The results reveal that the Tabular agents quietly converge to the supra-competitive price equilibrium. The DQN agents are highly volatile. Instead of converging to the supra-competitive price equilibrium, they initiate “Edgeworth Cycles” characterized by price volatilities that are ever-increasing. Through an ablation study on the experience replay buffer of the agents, this study confirms that the high volatilities are inherent in the Deep Learning model. To ensure stability in the economy, this study employs the “Hard Constraint” governance approach. By means of a sensitivity analysis, this study shows that soft ethical constraints are not sufficient to curb the formation of algorithmic cartels. However, the application of the disgorgement penalty successfully breaks the collusive loops. This specific constraint forces even the most erratic DQN agents back to the competitive Nash Equilibrium, ultimately safeguarding consumer welfare.

authors: Kouyakou-Abalo Simsoba, Ngesa Oscar, Thomas Mageto

title: Research on Cross Mechanisms and Optimization Methods Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning and Multi-Task Learning
authors: Wenjia Jiang, Zhuang Bai, Xinsheng Shi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the rapid development of artificial intelligence, the integration of deep reinforcement learning (DRL) and multi-task learning (MTL) has emerged as a key research paradigm for enhancing agents’ generalization ability and learning efficiency. This paper systematically reviews recent advancements in this field, focusing on core methodologies and representative applications. We discuss how MTL frameworks address fundamental challenges in DRL, such as low sample efficiency and limited generalization, by enabling effective knowledge sharing across multiple tasks. Key techniques, including shared representations, policy distillation, and metalearning, are analyzed together with their formal mathematical formulations. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of these approaches in improving agent performance. Finally, current limitations and promising future research directions are highlighted, including more efficient knowledge transfer, dynamic task structuring, and secure MTL.

title: Algorithm Design and Application in Enterprise Supply Chain Optimization
authors: Ting Yu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: This article focuses on the optimization of enterprise supply chain, aiming at solving many problems faced by enterprises in supply chain management and improving the overall efficiency of supply chain through scientific algorithm design and application. Taking the manufacturing enterprise as an example, this article uses dynamic programming algorithm, improved GA (Genetic algorithm) and the combination of analytic hierarchy process and fuzzy comprehensive evaluation method to construct the algorithm models of inventory management, transportation scheduling and supplier selection respectively. The experimental verification and practical case analysis show that the inventory management model based on dynamic programming algorithm narrows the fluctuation range of inventory cost from 300,000 390,000 yuan in the traditional strategy to $230,000-280,000$ yuan. The improved GA transportation scheduling model can reduce the average transportation cost from 5282.5 yuan to 4125 yuan, and shorten the delivery time from 5.35 days to 4.1 days. Based on the combination of AHP and fuzzy comprehensive evaluation model, the comprehensive index of cooperation performance remains at 85-91, which is much higher than 70-78 for empirical suppliers. It can be seen that the algorithm model has achieved remarkable results in improving the efficiency of enterprise supply chain management and reducing costs, and provided reliable decision support for the actual operation of enterprises.

title: An Explainable GNN-Based Framework for Short-Term Bus Passenger Flow Forecasting
authors: A. R. Sana Ullah, Asiye Baghbani, Nizar Bouguila, Zachary Patterson
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Accurate short-term passenger flow forecasting in bus networks is vital for optimizing transit operations and improving service reliability. However, due to complex spatial-temporal dependencies and dynamic demand patterns, this task remains challenging. While several deep learning models have been applied to passenger flow prediction, the increasing availability of real-time data has led to the growing use of graph neural networks (GNNs) combined with temporal models that effectively capture spatial and temporal dependencies by modeling transit networks as graphs of nodes and links. Yet, like many deep learning models, GNN-based approaches suffer from limited explainability, restricting their practical use. To address this challenge, we propose BNConv-LSTM—an explainable spatiotemporal graph-based framework for forecasting short-term bus passenger flow. Our proposed model captures spatial and temporal dependencies on the bus network by integrating a graph convolutional LSTM architecture with learnable spatial and temporal soft masks. These masks help improve the interpretability of the model’s predictions by highlighting the most influential bus stops and time steps contributing to each forecast. Evaluated on real-world data from the Ames Bus Network in Iowa, USA, BNConv-LSTM consistently demonstrates reliable performance in both prediction and explainability. The model’s learnable spatial and temporal soft masks offer interpretable insights into the spatial and temporal patterns influencing passenger demand, making it a powerful tool for both effective forecasting and well-informed transit planning.

title: User Identity-Based IPv6 Address Policy Allocation Model in a Software-Defined Network Architecture
authors: Xuanzhong Wang, Zhutian Li, Jigao Song
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: IPv6 address policy allocation randomly selects addresses from a pre-defined IPv6 address range and assigns them to access devices, ignoring the actual network status and user needs. This leads to a mismatch between address allocation and actual needs, impacting allocation efficiency. Therefore, a user identity-based IPv6 address policy allocation model for a software-defined network architecture is studied. A user identity integration and authentication system is constructed, and an authentication module is designed to clarify user priority. The authenticated user identity is integrated into the IPv6 address structure, and the IPv6 address format is embedded in the identity binding information. Dynamic IPv6 address allocation policy decisions are made within the software-defined network architecture, transforming the address allocation problem into a mathematical problem. After solving the problem, nodes determine the IPv6 address length, and the SDN controller allocates the address. Experimental results show that the model performs well across multiple terminals and operating systems in multi-terminal compatibility testing. In policy delivery time overhead testing, this method reduces time overhead by 30 to 103 milliseconds compared to other methods in scenarios with 10 to 100 users, demonstrating significant efficiency. In address conflict rate testing, the address conflict rate is 0.5% to 1.5% for 200 to 1000 terminals. This model can provide a reliable address allocation solution for large-scale terminal access networks and has high practical value.

title: SIAgent: Spatial Interaction Agent Via LLM-Powered Eye-Hand Motion Intent Understanding in VR_supp1-3686395.mp4
authors: Feng Lu
publish: N/A
abstract: Eye-hand coordinated interaction is becoming a mainstream interaction modality in Virtual Reality (VR) user interfaces. Current paradigms for this multimodal interaction require users to learn predefined gestures and memorize multiple gesture-task associations, which can be summarized as an “Operation-to-Intent” paradigm. This paradigm increases users' learning costs and has low interaction error tolerance. In this paper, we propose SIAgent, a novel “Intent-to-Operation” framework allowing users to express interaction intents through natural eye-hand motions based on common sense and habits. Our system features two main components: (1) intent recognition that translates spatial interaction data into natural language and infers user intent, and (2) agent-based execution that generates an agent to execute corresponding tasks. This eliminates the need for gesture memorization and accommodates individual motion preferences with high error tolerance. We conduct two user studies across over 60 interaction tasks, comparing our method with two “Operation-to-Intent” techniques. Results show our method achieves higher intent recognition accuracy than gaze + pinch interaction (97.2% vs 93.1%) while reducing arm fatigue and improving usability, and user preference. Another study verifies the function of eye gaze and hand motion channels in intent recognition. Our work offers valuable insights into enhancing VR interaction intelligence through intent-driven design. Our source code and LLM prompts will be made available upon publication. Our project page is at https://zhimin-wang.github.io/SIAgent.html

title: Deep-Learning enabled inverse design of Single-Layer Holographic Metasurface Antenna with Aperture Efficiency Enhancement
authors: Jie Zhang, Ping Wang, Yicong Qian, Lei Zhang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: The paper proposes an inverse design method for the holographic metasurface antennas (HMSAs) to enhance the aperture efficiency (AE) based on deep learning. To overcome the limitations of scalar impedance modulation, a novel anisotropic tensor unit is introduced to achieve flexible wavefront control, but this inevitably brings high-dimensional optimization challenges. To address this, a differentiable cascaded surrogate model comprising a convolutional autoencoder and an electromagnetic response predictor is constructed. This framework enables the efficient optimization of tensor parameters within a compressed latent space via gradient descent to maximize the AE. Validated by a 12-GHz prototype, the measured results agree well with simulations, and an AE of 44.16% and peak gain of 18.36 dBi are presented. The proposed approach offers an effective solution for the AE enhancement of the HMSA.

title: Application of Computer Vision for Livestock Farming: A Bibliometric Analysis
authors: Claudia Rachel Wijaya, Andrea Stevens Karnyoto, Bens Pardamean
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Computer vision is widely used for livestock farming for automated behaviour monitoring, biometric measurement, and real-time tracking. The amount of research on this topic is growing, making it important to understand the field's structure and direction through bibliometric analysis. In response, this study examines Scopus-indexed publications from 2020 to 2025 on computer vision applications for livestock farming. The dataset contained 429 documents, and using VOSviewer to analyze keyword co-occurrences, citations, and authorship to map the field's structure. Power BI was used to visualize citation distribution, publication distributions, and the most active sources. The findings show a steady rise in research output, peaking in 2024 with 110 publications, with China emerging as both the most cited country and most productive. The most active publishing source is Computers and Electronics in Agriculture. Keyword mapping showed 5 thematic clusters, emphasizing deep learning methods, real-time monitoring, animal welfare, and farm automation. The analysis also showed research gaps, including limited species diversity, dominance of CNNs and YOLO methods, underrepresentation of health monitoring applications, and a lack of standardized datasets. Overall, the study provides a thorough view of the research landscape and suggests future studies to diversify methodologies, expand species coverage, and improve dataset availability.

title: Transmission Line Effect of Dynamic Capacitive Power Transfer System and Its Suppression Method Based on Switchable Terminal Reactance
authors: Wei Zhou, Qiang Zhang, Yifei Zhang, Yiwei Sun, Weiling Wan, Jing Ai, Ruikun Mai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: In dynamic wireless power transfer systems, long segmented transmitter couplers facilitate simplified control and mitigate voltage fluctuations. However, dynamic capacitive power transfer (CPT) systems typically operate in the MHz range, where the high-frequency excitation wavelength is comparable to the dimensions of long segmented transmitter plates. This induces significant transmission line effects (TLE) in the system couplers, resulting severe electric-field inhomogeneity and even near-zero-field regions. These issues severely constrain the system’s power transfer capacity and stability. This article develops a mathematical model for dynamic CPT systems accounting for the TLE in long segmented transmitter plates, and analyzes the electric-field distribution around transmitter plates as well as the receiver output characteristics. It also proposes an open-loop output voltage fluctuation suppression method based on switchable terminal reactance, along with a corresponding terminal reactance parameters optimization method and switching strategy. Integrated with the transmitter’s DC–DC converter, it enables closed-loop control of output voltage. An experimental prototype was built with an operating frequency of 6.78 MHz, 10 m-long transmitter plates, and a system power of 30 W. Experimental results demonstrate distinct near-zero-power operation intervals in the output voltage without terminal reactance, with the voltage fluctuation rate approaching nearly 100%. Adopting the proposed method reduces the open-loop output voltage fluctuation rate to 30.1%, and implementing closed-loop control further decreases it to within 5%. The fluctuation trends and amplitudes of the system output voltage before and after using the proposed method are consistent with theoretical values, verifying that the proposed method realizes stable constant-voltage output across all segments of dynamic CPT systems.

title: A Problem-Centric Ontology-Driven Framework for Grounding Large Language Models in Enterprise Financial Knowledge
authors: Sergiy Lunyakin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Recent advances in modern artificial intelligence have demonstrated strong capabilities in natural language understanding and generation; however, their enterprise adoption remains constrained by the lack of semantic grounding, explainability, and trust. This poster presents a systematic and repeatable framework for constructing ontology-driven knowledge systems that ground Large Language Models in verified, context-rich financial knowledge. The framework addresses the cold start problem of building meaningful knowledge structures from unstructured enterprise data by introducing a problem-centric methodology aligned with real business questions in Financial Planning and Analysis. It consists of six iterative stages: (1) problem scoping through competency questions, (2) ontology bootstrapping via a minimum viable graph, (3) schema-guided knowledge extraction from financial reports, data warehouses or data lakes, and documents, (4) contextual knowledge representation with relevance and provenance annotations, (5) hybrid automated and human validation, and (6) context aware relevance propagation for retrieval-augmented generation. Unlike purely vector-based approaches, the proposed method combines symbolic reasoning and statistical learning to retrieve semantically coherent and auditable subgraphs, improving answer accuracy and interpretability. The framework also integrates financial data warehouse structures with business semantics, enabling traceable and auditable responses grounded in authoritative data sources. This work demonstrates how semantic computing can effectively bridge modern and classic artificial intelligence to support explainable, reliable, and decision-ready AI analytics in enterprise financial domains.

title: LIBPipe: Efficient Load Imbalance Pipeline Model Parallelism for Large Models Training_supp1-3686206.pdf
authors: Zeyu JI
publish: N/A
abstract: With the increasing size of datasets and the expansion of Deep Neural Networks (DNNs), the training process has become exceedingly time-consuming. Distributed training, specifically the Pipeline Model Parallelism (PMP) method, commonly mitigates this problem but suffers from bubble time delays. This paper proposes LIBPipe, a pipeline training framework that explicitly incorporates a load-imbalance method to reduce bubble time in PMP. Within LIBPipe, a model-unequal-partitioning method is designed from the perspective of load imbalance to reshape the pipeline execution pattern and significantly shorten idle periods during training. On top of this method, a performance-guided unequal-partitioning search algorithm is developed to efficiently identify near-optimal partitioning strategies under memory constraints. The paper theoretically proves the time efficiency of adopting load imbalance in pipeline models. Comprehensive experiments are conducted on an 8-GPU server to evaluate the efficiency of LIBPipe, using the IMDB and mini-ImageNet datasets as well as well-known models such as BERT and ResNet. The BERT-series models achieve a maximum throughput improvement of 60.3%, while the ResNet-series models achieve a maximum improvement of 74.1%.

title: Distributed Data-Driven Evolutionary Optimization With Surrogate Ensemble Strategies_supp1-3683108.pdf
authors: Wei-Neng Chen
publish: N/A
abstract: Surrogate-assisted evolutionary algorithms (SAEAs) have emerged as effective solutions for addressing data-driven optimization problems (DOPs). However, the advent of distributed DOPs, attributed to the widespread existence of distributed data scenarios, has promoted the development of distributed SAEAs. Distributed SAEAs encounter the challenge of disparate nodes independently constructing local models based on their observed data, complicating evolutionary coordination. To address this challenge, we propose a novel surrogate model ensemble framework, which aims to effectively guide the evolution across distributed nodes and enhance the performance of distributed SAEAs. The framework consists of two main components, an Adaptive boosting Surrogate training strategy (AdaSurrogate, AS) and an Adaptive model Aggregation strategy (AdaAggregation, AA). AS dynamically adjusts the sample weights of surrogate models on edge clients, aiming to enhance the accuracy of local surrogate models. AA aggregates different client models across rounds, aiming to improve the accuracy of the global surrogate model and its adaptability to distributed data heterogeneity. AS and AA are applied to improve the Edge-Cloud Co-Evolutionary Algorithm (ECCoEA), an existing advanced distributed SAEA, resulting in ECCoEA-ASAA. Experiments show that ECCoEA-ASAA not only exhibits a huge improvement over the original ECCoEA in both independent and identically distributed (i.i.d.) and non-i.i.d. scenarios, but also outperforms some state-of-the-art SAEAs in non-i.i.d. scenarios. Additionally, an ablation study is also conducted to demonstrate and analyze the effectiveness of AS and AA in enhancing the performance of distributed SAEAs.

title: Electric Train Energy Model Considering SOGI for Low-Frequency Oscillation Analysis in Railway Train Network System
authors: Yawen Li, Haitao Hu, Haidong Tao, Haijun Liu, Xugang Guo, Songhai Fan, Xiaopeng Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: Frequent low-frequency oscillations (LFO) in the railway train network system (RTNS) pose a serious threat to railway operational safety. In view of this problem, this paper proposes a stability analysis method that combines mechanistic interpretability with online oscillation tracing. First, an energy-based model for the electric train incorporating the second-order generalized integrator (SOGI) is established. Then, an interaction energy criterion is proposed to link control loop interactions in electric trains to damping contributions, enabling the identification of key control loops. Parameter sensitivity analysis is conducted to evaluate the impact of key parameters. This evaluation provides a solid foundation for LFO suppression via the control parameters of the electric train. Furthermore, an oscillation source traceability method based on stored energy is proposed. A centralized-distributed architecture is also introduced for oscillation energy calculation to realize online traceability. Moreover, a method based on the energy gap to calculate the critical virtual impedance value is proposed to suppress the LFO. Finally, hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) tests validate the effectiveness of the proposed method for the LFO analysis of the RTNS.
