title: Multi-Logical Extended Functional Dependencies for Flight Data Completeness Repair
authors: Simin Yan, Chuang Gu, Meng Yue
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: With the rapid growth in global flights, flight data quality has gained significant attention. Data completeness is a key indicator, yet existing integrity constraints fail to capture the complex semantics of flight data, limiting repair effectiveness. This paper proposes Multi-Logical Extended Functional Dependencies (MLE-FDs), which capture both functional dependencies and numerical logical dependencies (e.g., comparison and arithmetic operations) in flight data. We design the MLE-TANE mining algorithm, which employs breadth-first search with pruning strategies to discover MLE-FDs in near-linear time. Furthermore, we propose an MLE-FD-based completeness repair method that handles missing values in categorical and continuous attributes using functional and numerical dependencies respectively, and introduces multi-source data fusion through entity recognition to enhance accuracy. Experiments on real-world and simulated flight datasets demonstrate that the proposed method achieves an average precision of 99.07% and recall of 92.42% in completeness repair, significantly outperforming traditional approaches.

title: Research on the Mechanism of Data Duration Effects in LSTM-Based Proxy Electricity Price Prediction
authors: Zhe Zhang, Xuejie Lin, Yinan Zhang, Xueyu Zhang, Dan Qi, Kun Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: The impact of training data duration on LSTMbased electricity price forecasting for proxy purchases in China’s developing spot markets is investigated in this study. Using Henan market data from January 2025, we compare 24-day and 31-day training sets and find that extending data length by 7 days reduces predictive accuracy: $R^{2}$ drops from 0.3982 to -0.4041, while MAPE rises from $11.95 \%$ to $22.10 \%$. These results challenge the “more data is better” assumption, underscoring that data quality and market-pattern alignment outweigh volume in volatile electricity markets. Findings offer critical guidance for data strategy and model design in proxy purchasing decision-support systems.

title: Improved YOLOv11 Algorithm Based on Feature Pyramid and Attention Mechanism and Its Application on VisDrone Dataset
authors: Yongchao Li
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) target detection faces challenges such as small object sizes, dense distributions, complex backgrounds, and scale variations in datasets like VisDrone, limiting the accuracy and robustness of the original YOLOv11 algorithm. To address these, this paper presents an improved YOLOv11 approach that integrates an Enhanced Feature Pyramid Network (EFPN) and a Contract-and-Broadcast Self-Attention (CBSA) mechanism. The EFPN enhances multiscale feature fusion through cross-scale connections and feature enhancement modules, improving detection of small- and mediumsized targets. The CBSA mechanism adaptively focuses on key regions, suppressing background noise and improving precision in complex scenes. Additionally, Dynamic Focal Loss (DFL) dynamically adjusts loss weights based on sample difficulty, mitigating the class imbalance issue. Extensive experiments on the VisDrone2019 dataset show that the proposed algorithm achieves a $6.3 \%$ improvement in mean Average Precision (mAP@0.5), reaching $\mathbf{5 8. 7 \%}$, with real-time performance at $\mathbf{5 2 ~ F P S}$. Compared to YOLOv8, YOLOv10, and MASF-YOLO, the proposed method demonstrates superior accuracy and efficiency, highlighting its effectiveness for UAV target detection.

title: Construction and Application of Customer Emotional Feature Extraction Model on the Agent Side for Power Customer Service Scenarios
authors: Qing Zhu, Shuo Zhang, Jiaqi Shi, Wei Yu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Relying solely on single text semantic analysis can only interpret the surface meaning of customers’ sentences in isolation, ignoring multi-dimensional information such as intonation and communication rhythm, leading to low accuracy of feature extraction. To solve the above problem, this study focuses on the construction and application of a customer emotional feature extraction model on the agent side for power customer service scenarios. Voice and text data are processed separately: voice is collected, recognized, converted into text, and memory sample mode is constructed; text is processed by a word segmentation tool and converted into word vectors. Then, each data source is normalized and weighted averaged to achieve multi-modal data fusion of customer emotions on the agent side. The BERT model is used to extract text modal features, and the initial vector extracted by the pre-training toolkit is processed by a single-layer bidirectional LSTM and a linear projection layer for voice modal features. The fused features and cross-modal interaction features are concatenated and input into the fully connected layer network to output emotional scores. During model training, the RMSProp optimization algorithm is adopted to prevent overfitting, the binary cross-entropy loss function is selected, and the expected cross-entropy method is used to select and compress features to mine representative emotional features, thereby constructing the customer emotional feature extraction model on the agent side. Experimental results show that the proposed method can effectively extract customer emotional features on the agent side. In terms of accuracy indicators: for positive emotions in rounds 21-30, the precision rate reaches 0.98 and the F1-score is 0.95; for neutral emotions in rounds 21-30, the recall rate is 0.98 and the F1-score is 0.99; for negative emotions, the F1-score exceeds 0.94 in most rounds. In the confusion matrix comparison: 183 samples of positive emotions are truly positive and correctly predicted, far exceeding the comparison methods; 192 samples of neutral emotions are truly neutral and correctly predicted, showing an outstanding performance; $\mathbf{1 8 1}$ samples of negative emotions are truly negative and correctly predicted with few misjudgments, which improves the accuracy of emotional feature extraction.

title: Constraint-Consistent Skill Composition for Reliable Zero-Shot Task Generalization in LLM Agents
authors: Ru Meng, Shaoyu Huang, Xinyue Zhang, Yourong Yang, Kewei Zeng, Zhibin Huang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Addressing the frequent emergence of new tasks and long-tail demands in open environments for large language model agents, this paper proposes a task transfer and zero-shot generalization method for composable skill libraries. This transforms the agent’s capability organization from an end-to-end strategy into a problem of structured construction and combinatorial scheduling of reusable skill units. First, skills are uniformly encapsulated in a structured manner, explicitly describing interface parameter patterns, preconditions, posteffects, and semantic representations, enabling skills to possess searchable and verifiable invocation semantics across different tasks and execution contexts. Second, a shared semantic space for tasks and skills is constructed. Candidate skill retrieval and selection are achieved through joint scoring of semantic relevance and constraint consistency, reducing misselection and combinatorial instability caused by relying solely on text similarity. Third, task solving is modeled as a joint optimization process of skill sequence planning and state evolution. Precondition satisfiability constraints and state transition consistency checks are introduced during the planning process to improve the executability and controllability of the generated sequences. Finally, cross-task shared priors are introduced to constrain policy distribution deviations, enhancing stable transfer capabilities under changes in task descriptions and environmental differences. Comparative experiments based on public datasets verify the comprehensive advantages of the proposed method in terms of task success rate, tool invocation accuracy, constraint satisfaction, and average steps, demonstrating that the structured skill library and constraint consistency mechanism can effectively support zero-shot task adaptation and reliable execution of agents.

title: Performance Evaluation of Function-Controlled Variable Step-Size LMS in Delayed-Reference Adaptive Noise Cancellation
authors: Cemil Turan, Rashid Baimukashev
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: This paper presents a systematic performance evaluation of three adaptive noise-cancellation algorithmsLeast Mean Squares (LMS), Normalized LMS (NLMS), and Function-Controlled Variable Step-Size LMS (FCLMS)within a delayed-reference adaptive filtering framework. While LMS-type algorithms are widely used, their behavior under delayed-reference conditions without an external noise measurement remains insufficiently explored. From a signalprocessing perspective, the delayed-reference configuration introduces temporal dependencies that influence convergence dynamics and steady-state performance. All algorithms are implemented in MATLAB and evaluated using a sinusoidal test signal contaminated by colored AR(1) noise. A delayed version of the noisy observation is employed as a partially decorrelated reference input, and performance is assessed using signal-tonoise ratio (SNR) and minimum mean-square error (MMSE). To improve reproducibility and statistical reliability, simulations are conducted within a controlled framework with repeated trials and clearly defined parameters. The results indicate that the function-controlled step-size strategy provides improved convergence behavior and reduced steady-state error compared with conventional LMS and NLMS under identical delayed-reference conditions. Rather than introducing a new adaptive algorithm, this study offers a practical signalprocessing analysis of variable step-size adaptation in scenarios where classical reference-based assumptions are relaxed.

title: Generating Conversational Recommendation Datasets from Real User Interactions
authors: Vinaik Chhetri, Yousaf Raza, Moghis Fereidouni, Srijata Maji, Umar Farooq, A.B. Siddique
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Modern recommendation systems typically follow two complementary paradigms: collaborative filtering, which models long-term user preferences from historical interactions, and conversational recommendation systems (CRS), which interact with users in natural language to uncover immediate needs. While CRS models often do not have access to collaborative signals, resulting in generic suggestions, traditional recommenders lack mechanisms to elicit users’ immediate needs interactively. Unifying these paradigms promises richer personalization but remains challenging due to the lack of conversational datasets grounded in real user behavior. We present ConvRecStudio, a novel framework that leverages large language models (LLMs) to automatically simulate realistic, multi-turn dialogs grounded in real timestamped user-item interactions and user reviews. ConvRecStudio follows a three-stage pipeline: (1) Temporal Profiling, which constructs user profiles and community-level item sentiment trajectories across fine-grained aspects; (2) Semantic Dialog Planning, which generates a structured plan over dialog acts; and (3) Multi-Turn Simulation, which instantiates the plan using paired LLM agents for the user and system, constrained by executional and behavioral fidelity checks. We apply ConvRecStudio to three domains, MobileRec, Yelp, and Amazon Electronics, producing over 12k multi-turn dialogs for each dataset. Human and automatic evaluations confirm the naturalness, coherence, and behavioral grounding of the generated conversations. To demonstrate utility, we build a cross-attention-based transformer model that jointly encodes user history and dialog context, achieving significant gains in Hit@K and NDCG@K metrics over baselines relying on either signal alone or naive fusion. These results establish ConvRecStudio as a scalable and effective foundation for unified conversational recommendations.

title: Bridging Human Perception and Vision-Language Models: A Perspective on Explainable Aesthetic Evaluation
authors: Supatta Viriyavisuthisakul, Toshihiko Yamasaki
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Aesthetic evaluation of images is subjective, culturally dependent, and shaped by complex perceptual factors. Several studies aim to automate this assessment using deep neural networks, but significant challenges remain, such as the lack of objective ground truth and the difficulty of providing explanations for aesthetics grounded in human perception. Recently, vision-language models (VLMs) and multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have been explored to offer new opportunities for both aesthetic prediction and natural language reasoning; however, their explanations often diverge from human perception, revealing persistent gaps in interpretability and alignment. This perspective synthesizes recent approaches in explainable image aesthetics, including prompt-based CLIP evaluation, SHAP-based factor attribution, multimodal reasoning, and explanation-guided image retouching, and integrates insights from these methods. We highlight limitations related to prompt sensitivity, cultural bias, and inconsistencies among explanation modalities. We propose a unified framework that bridges quantitative attribute analysis with semantic reasoning. By grounding aesthetic evaluation in human-centered perception and aligning VLM explanations with interpretable visual evidence, this work aims to provide a roadmap for future research on transparent and user-centric aesthetic assessment systems.

title: Timely Trajectory Reconstruction in Finite Buffer Remote Tracking Systems_supp1-3686356.pdf
authors: Sunjung Kang
publish: N/A
abstract: Remote tracking systems play a critical role in applications such as IoT, monitoring, surveillance and healthcare. In such systems, maintaining both real-time state awareness (for online decision making) and accurate reconstruction of historical trajectories (for offline post-processing) are essential. While the Age of Information (AoI) metric has been extensively studied as a measure of freshness, it does not capture the accuracy with which past trajectories can be reconstructed. In this work, we investigate reconstruction error as a complementary metric to AoI, addressing the trade-off between timely updates and historical accuracy. Specifically, we we consider a remote tracking system where the source evolves as a Wiener process and examine three policies, each prioritizing different aspects of information management: Keep-Old, Keep-Fresh, and our proposed Inter-arrival-Aware dropping policy. We compare these policies in terms of impact on both AoI and reconstruction error in a remote tracking system with a finite buffer. Through theoretical analysis and numerical simulations of queueing behavior, we demonstrate that while the Keep-Fresh policy minimizes AoI, it does not necessarily minimize reconstruction error. In contrast, our proposed Inter-arrival-Aware dropping policy dynamically adjusts packet retention decisions based on generation times, achieving a balance between AoI and reconstruction error. Our results provide key insights into the design of efficient buffer management policies for resource-constrained IoT networks.

title: Internal Temperature Estimation of Pouch-Type Lithium-Ion Battery by a 2-D Semilinear PDE Model With Space-Dependent Diffusivity
authors: Fudong Ge, YangQuan Chen, Zhiqiang Zuo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cybernetics
abstract: This article aims to propose an internal temperature estimation strategy for pouch-type lithium-ion battery using a 2-D semilinear partial differential equation (PDE) model with space-dependent diffusivity and in-domain uncertainty. The distributed linear measurements are considered, where only limited linear information is measured. Initially, we divide the interested domain into several subdomains based on the number of sensors. A Luenberger-type PDE observer is then designed for battery internal temperature estimation by the uncertainty-free plant model, and the exponential stability of corresponding estimation error system is established via the Lyapunov functional method and linear matrix inequalities (LMIs). Moreover, when in-domain uncertainty is presented, a robust internal temperature estimation strategy is developed to ensure that the corresponding uncertain observer error systems are input-to-state stable (ISS). Simulation results are finally provided to verify the effectiveness of the proposed methods.

title: An Intelligent Tactile Sensor Based on Triboelectric Nanogenerator and Support Vector Machine: A New Approach for Material and Fingerprint Recognition
authors: Chengpeng Ge, Lidong He, Yutong Ming, Guocheng Shen, Yaling Weng, Tao Wang, Ling Yang, Jijie Ma, Yingting Wang, Zhonghua Zhang, Jianming Wen
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Tactile sensing plays an important role in human-machine interaction, providing sensing capabilities for intelligent devices. This article proposes an intelligent tactile sensor based on a triboelectric nanogenerator and support vector machine (TS-TENG). It comprises two single-electrode units, which can be used both in front and in reverse. A thermoplastic polyurethane film is placed in the middle of the tactile sensor for isolation. The experiments indicate that the peak power density of the TS-TENG can reach 42 mW/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> across an external resistance of 30 MΩ. Furthermore, the proposed TS-TENG can be used for material and fingerprint recognition by the two single-electrode units. The upper single-electrode unit contacts materials and generates different voltage signals due to the various electronegativities. Due to the generation of distinctive voltage signals under varying pressure levels, the lower single-electrode unit can be used to identify fingerprints. Finally, combined with the support vector machine algorithm, it shows high material and fingerprint identification accuracy. It can be seen that the proposed tactile sensor has good prospects in smart plants and smart security.

title: The data-driven framework for real-time operation of the power system and the visualization of internal operation risks, monitoring and early warning
authors: Minghui Yan, Ziyu Chen, Chaoyi Peng, Yanjie Liang, Zekun Zhuang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: This paper proposes a data visualization monitoring and early warning framework for solving the problems of data islands, channel delay warning, and poor risk monitoring effect of operation data for internal operational risks in the operation of real-time power system servers. The framework consists of “data layer, feature layer, visualization layer and server warning layer”, which integrates multiple sources of data in the server, including dispatch signal data cleaning, SCADASCADA measurement, and dispatch signal signal anomaly identification. After multi-dimensional fusion processing, the risk characteristics of signal transmission amplitude, cross-regional information communication and visual encrypted transmission in the server are extracted, and a highly interactive monitoring and early warning framework is established. At the same time, two-way data-driven points are set up to complete the two-way engine early warning of macro overview, meso-correlation, and micro-entity intuitive presentation. Experimental verification shows that the data-driven framework can effectively handle the data delay of multi-point servers in the power system, accurately identify abnormal data characteristics, and control the recognition time at 3.2 ${\sim} \mathbf{4. 5 s}$. The data warning recognition rate of single-point and multi-point data exceeded 89%, which was significantly better than that of the non-data-driven mode. Therefore, the data-driven framework can provide effective support for operational risk identification, effective data transmission, and multi-server point interaction in power system servers.

title: A System Development Code Auto-Completion Method Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Ping Zhou, Jianfei Xiao, Xiaolong Zhang, Haowen Tian, Junlong Kang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: In the field of software development, only simple encrypted system development code data can be used, resulting in low accuracy of completed code. To address this issue, a system development code auto completion method based on deep reinforcement learning is proposed. Firstly, in terms of developing code data for encryption systems, design a dynamic key negotiation scheme based on elliptic curve cryptography and a hierarchical cache scheduling mechanism, using a dual authentication mechanism to ensure data security. Then, a completion model is constructed, using a dual channel feature encoder to extract encrypted code features. The dynamic context encoding layer models historical dependencies, and the reinforcement learning decision layer optimizes the completion strategy using the PPO algorithm and designs a reward function. Finally, a completion framework based on triplet contrastive learning and multi granularity feature fusion is proposed, which constructs triplet data with functional modules as anchor points, calculates matching probabilities and dynamic weight vectors, and integrates multi-dimensional information to achieve automatic completion. The experimental results show that in terms of data processing, the design method automatically generates data with a highly consistent trend with the actual code written after 30 seconds. From the perspective of accuracy and time consumption, the design method takes 200 ms to achieve 90% accuracy, and only 100 ms to 150 ms to increase from 70% to 85% accuracy. Compared with literature, the design method has flexible scalability, platform independence, and fully automated integration. The code generation efficiency reaches $\mathbf{2 5}$ lines per second, far exceeding other literature methods, and can accurately and efficiently complete the system development code.

title: Research on Transformer-based Large Language Model Generation Technology
authors: Zhuang Bai, Wenjia Jiang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: This paper focuses on the generation technology of large language models (LLMs) based on the Transformer architecture. First, it systematically elaborates on the core principles of the Transformer, including the self-attention mechanism and the encoder-decoder structure, and provides the core formulas. Then, it analyzes the technical evolution of the generative pre-training (GPT) model based on the decoderonly architecture. This paper uses the speech generation model SpeechGEN as a frontier case study to analyze how it successfully extends the generation and context-learning capabilities of LLMs to continuous speech signals through a unified speech tokenizer and prompt mechanism. Finally, the paper concludes with a summary and outlook on the future development trends of LLM generation technology.

title: Explainable Artificial Intelligence for Early Cardiovascular Disease Diagnosis: a Global Systematic Review With Central Asian Gap Analysis
authors: Khaled Mohamad, Baimakhan Nur-Akhmet
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases (CVDs) are the leading cause of mortality globally, with a disproportionate impact on low- and middle-income countries, including Kazakhstan and Central Asia [1], [2], [3]. Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) has emerged as a promising approach to facilitate early CVD diagnosis by increasing model transparency, supporting clinical confidence, and enhancing decision support in preventive care settings [4], [5]. This systematic review evaluates 33 studies published between 2020 and 2025 that employed XAI approaches for early CVD diagnosis, following PRISMA 2020 guidelines. The studies were analyzed for dataset characteristics, modeling techniques, explainability methods, validation strategies, and clinical interpretability. Ensemblebased machine learning models, particularly Random Forest (36%), Decision Tree (30%), and XGBoost (27%), were most frequently utilized, achieving a median area under the curve (AUC) of 0.89 (range: 0.74-0.97). Deep learning methods, mainly Convolutional Neural Networks (21%) and Long ShortTerm Memory networks (12%), were primarily applied to ECG and imaging data. SHAP (54.5%) and LIME (27.2%) were the predominant explainability techniques, yet only three studies (9%) reported clinician-based validation of interpretability results. Despite methodological advances, 91% of the included studies relied on non-Central Asian datasets, highlighting a significant regional evidence gap. Limited access to local data, insufficient external validation, and minimal clinician involvement restrict the applicability of current XAI solutions to Kazakhstan and similar low-resource health systems. These findings underscore the need for standardized explanation evaluation frameworks and clinician-in-the-loop validation to support trustworthy and context-sensitive XAI deployment for early CVD diagnosis in Central Asia.

title: Multi-Task Learning with CORAL-Based Feature Alignment for Cross-Domain Plant Disease Detection
authors: Mohina Rustamova, Shirali Kadyrov, Rashid Baimukashev
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Deep learning models for plant disease detection achieve near-perfect accuracy on controlled datasets such as PlantVillage, yet their performance degrades substantially when deployed on real-world field images due to domain shift. This gap between laboratory and field conditions limits practical applicability in agricultural settings. In this paper, we investigate whether multi-task learning (MTL), combined with CORALbased domain alignment, can improve cross-domain generalization for plant disease recognition. Our framework jointly predicts plant species and disease class, encouraging the learning of structured and domain-invariant representations. Domain shift is explicitly addressed through covariance alignment between source and target feature distributions using unlabeled target-domain data. All models are trained on PlantVillage and evaluated on PlantDoc in a strict cross-domain setting. Experimental results show that MTL consistently outperforms single-task baselines, while CORAL-based alignment further improves target-domain accuracy and macro-F1 score, with the strongest gains achieved by a self-adaptive multi-scale MTL variant.

title: Leveraging Language Models for Automated Requirements to Design Transformation in MDA
authors: Abdykhalyk Arystan
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Model-Driven Architecture (MDA) promises traceable development but still depends on manual translation of natural-language requirements into UML. The research describes an easy-to-use method which converts requirements into UML diagrams for the small multilingual Almaty pilot project. The system employs three main components which include a restricted intermediate JSON schema generated by a big language model (LLM) and PlantUML conversion through deterministic methods and XMI export for tool verification and basic rule-based comparison. The system evaluates structural extraction quality for vital UML elements by using precision/recall/F1 metrics which measure against a reference standard and assesses engineering value through XMI import success rates and human postediting time requirements. The following terms appear in this document: Model-Driven Architecture (MDA) and Requirements Engineering and Large Language Models (LLMs) and UML class diagrams and Model transformation and Accessibility.

title: AdaTrack: Dynamic Feature Fusion for Occlusion-Robust Multi-Object Tracking
authors: Yiyao Zheng, Zainura Idrus, Yiming Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: In complex scenes, target dense occlusion, appearance degradation, and short-term disappearance have long been challenges in multi-object tracking (MOT). This paper proposes a dual-module enhanced tracking framework. By combining an adaptive feature fusion module with a short-term trajectory recovery module, it significantly improves tracking robustness and identity preservation in scenarios with severe occlusion. The adaptive feature fusion module employs a dynamic weighting mechanism to automatically adjust the fusion ratio between motion features and appearance features based on scene complexity, enhancing motion consistency when occlusion is severe and reinforcing visual matching when appearance is reliable. The short-term trajectory recovery module effectively addresses identity recovery after complete occlusion by analyzing motion patterns and spatio-temporal consistency. To demonstrate the generality of our method, we conduct validation on challenging benchmark datasets from real-world scenarios. The results indicate that our method possesses strong generalization capabilities. This work provides a new perspective for the future development of MOT.

title: DTE-RAG: A Dual-Tree Structure Contextual Enhanced Knowledge Retrieval Modeling Both Similarity and Relatedness in Multi-Hop Reasoning
authors: Yao Fan, Shuming Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) is often hindered by semantic fragmentation from naïve chunking and limited coverage of single retrieval methods. We propose DTERAG, a context-enhanced framework featuring Dual-Tree indexing and Dual-Channel retrieval. To ensure semantic coherence, we introduce context-enhanced chunking that preserves inter-chunk relations. The core innovation, Dual-Tree indexing, constructs parallel semantic-similarity and lexicalrelevance trees to capture complementary document associations. Dual-Channel retrieval integrates information via Maximal Marginal Relevance (MMR) re-ranking to optimize both precision and diversity. Benchmarked on datasets, DTE-RAG achieves $54.10 \%$ EM and $63.90 \%$ F1, outperforming vanilla RAG by $67.5 \%$ and $82.1 \%$ respectively. These results validate DTE-RAG as a paradigm for complex multi-hop reasoning and cross-document retrieval.

title: Development of a GPT-3 Based Model for Estimating Temperature Enhancements in Mainland China Using Random Forests
authors: Jiongtao Fang, Junyu Li, Chen Liu, Tang Ning, Li Long Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: An accurate atmospheric temperature field is a critical prior for tropospheric delay correction. However, the GPT3 temperature model exhibits pronounced height-dependent, correlated errors over the complex terrain of mainland China, largely due to its underlying assumption of vertical linearity. To address this limitation, we develop a Random Forest-based GPT3 temperature enhancement model (EGPT3_T). The model is trained using radiosonde (RS) observations from 80 stations across mainland China during 2016-2019, and it performs regionalized bias correction by learning the nonlinear relationships between GPT3-RS temperature residuals and spatiotemporal predictors. Independent validation using observations from the same 80 radiosonde stations in 2020 demonstrates that EGPT3_T achieves an MAE of 3.16 K, an STD of 3.97 K, and an RMSE of 4.15 K. In terms of accuracy, EGPT3_T outperforms GPT3 and CPT_T by $\mathbf{4 1. 0 4 \%} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{3 0. 5 5 \%} \boldsymbol{(}$ MAE), $\mathbf{3 7. 4 8 \%} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{2 2. 3 1 \%} \boldsymbol{(}$ STD), and $\mathbf{4 1. 5 5 \%} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{2 6. 9 4 \%}$ (RMSE), respectively. Moreover, EGPT3_T maintains more consistent performance and reduced dispersion across elevation layers, indicating improved spatial adaptability and temporal stability. These results suggest that EGPT3_T can provide more reliable temperature priors for tropospheric correction and related applications in regions with complex topography.

title: HammerBlade in Silicon: A 12-nm 2048-Core RISC-V Manycore SoC With Ruche Networks
authors: Paul Gao, Dai Cheol Jung, Scott Davidson, Daniel Ruelas-Petrisko, Yuan-Mao Chueh, Max Ruttenberg, Kangli Li, Farzam Gilani, Dustin Richmond, Mark Oskin, Michael Bedford Taylor
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: This brief presents the 99 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, 12-nm FinFET, 2048-core HammerBlade (HB) RISC-V manycore system-on-chip (SoC) which includes the world’s first implementation of Ruche Networks, a wire-maximal network-on-chip (NoC) topology. Prior architecture research argues for HB’s physical and logical scalability, programmability, and high density; this brief shows a concrete realization in silicon. This brief demonstrates strong results running in silicon on 2048 cores over a diverse set of representative parallelized applications, and also a world record on CoreMark (CM) score. This brief further describes in detail the silicon implementation of Ruche Networks. Operating at 1.49 GHz at 0.8 V, the NoC delivers a peak aggregate bandwidth of 2572.6 Tb/s and a bisection bandwidth of 53.2 Tb/s. The design achieves an exceptionally high routing density of 4289 bit/mm. Finally, this brief demonstrates how a small team of Ph.D. students overcame large-chip backend computer-aided design (CAD) challenges using on-chip source-synchronous interconnect (OCSSI), a globally asynchronous locally synchronous (GALS) style top-level integration methodology, combined with a turnaround-time optimized hierarchical design flow.

title: Direct Adaptive Control of Photovoltaic Boost Converters With Ultra-Low Input Capacitance
authors: Andrei C. Lima, Charles Schardong, Lucas V. Bellinaso, Rodrigo V. Tambara, Leandro Michels
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Industry Applications
abstract: Boost converters are widely used in the voltage step-up stage of photovoltaic (PV) inverters due to their simple construction. These converters must operate in both Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) and Limited Power Tracking (LPT) modes, which requires effective regulation of both PV voltage and inductor current using a dual-loop control structure. Reducing the input capacitance to eliminate electrolytic capacitors and increase lifetime, however, introduces significant parametric variations, making the control design challenging and limiting the dynamic performance of the voltage controller. This work proposes a dual-loop adaptive control strategy, comprising an outer voltage loop with Model Reference Adaptive Control (MRAC) and an inner current loop with a fixed-gain PI controller. The proposed method resolves issues observed in previous works in both steady-state and transient operation, enabling fast PV voltage tracking with an ultra-low 2 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathrm{\mu }$</tex-math></inline-formula>F input capacitor. Experimental results demonstrate stable operation in both CCM and DCM modes, with a settling time of 7 ms at a switching frequency of 40 kHz, improved dynamic response, and applicability in PV systems under rapidly changing irradiance conditions.

title: A Survey on Neuromorphic Navigation: Implementation Resources, Challenges and Perspectives
authors: Youdong Zhang, Xu He, Xiaolin Meng, Xiangdong An, Lingfei Mo, Wenxuan Yin, Fangwen Yu
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Although traditional navigation technologies have seen continued progress, they remain constrained by persistent issues such as high power consumption, significant latency, and limited dynamic adaptability, especially in challenging environments like urban canyons and underground spaces. Neuromorphic technologies, leveraging event-driven sensing, spike-based computing, and brain-inspired compute-in-memory, offer a promising new avenue to overcome these limitations. However, as an emerging field, neuromorphic navigation currently lacks dedicated survey efforts to systematically map the ecosystem and resources that support its development. Motivated by this, this survey provides a comprehensive overview of implementation resources across three key dimensions: data, hardware, and software. Building on this foundation, we identify main challenges, including the absence of evaluation benchmarks, fragmented ecosystem resources, the lack of a full-stack theoretical framework, and emerging concerns regarding security, trust, and data privacy. We also propose perspectives aimed at supporting community-driven growth and ecosystem maturation. A curated list of resources is available at: https://github.com/BINUCOE/Awesome-Neuromorphic-Navigation-Resources.

title: SAFA: Sequential Recommendation with Adaptive Sparse Attention and Frequency-Aware Encoding_supp1-3686613.pdf
authors: Wenming CAO
publish: N/A
abstract: Recommendation systems alleviate the issue of information overload via modeling user preferences from interaction sequences. Although self-attention based sequential models effectively capture long-range dependencies, they are susceptible to noise amplification in sparse sequences and over-smoothing of item representations, which obscures true user intent and reduces sensitivity to fine-grained behavioral changes. To overcome these challenges, we propose SAFA, a sparse sequential recommendation framework comprising: (1) an adaptive sparse attention mechanism that suppresses noisy interactions while preserving embedding diversity; (2) a frequency-aware encoder that decomposes interaction sequences into low-frequency components for long-term preference modeling and high-frequency components for short-term intent dynamics; and (3) a simplified focal loss that removes the class-balancing term while preserving the focusing factor, emphasizing hard-to-predict samples rather than class priors. Experiments on seven benchmark datasets demonstrate that SAFA consistently achieve state-of-the-art performance with average improvements of up to 3.77%, 4.10% and 4.25% in terms of HR@5, HR@10 and HR@20, respectively, and 4.30%, 4.78% and 4.58% in terms of NDCG@5, NDCG@10 and NDCG@20, respectively, over the best competing model. Ablation studies verify the importance of each component, with notable performance degradation upon removing the sparse attention or frequency-aware encoder. Overall, SAFA enhances sequential recommendation by improving robustness and discriminative learning under noisy and sparse conditions.

title: Interference-Aware Peak Localization in OTFS-Based Radar Sensing
authors: Malek Ali, Roman Marsalek, Mohammed Alaa Ala'anzy
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Orthogonal Time Frequency Space radar sensing provides a compact delay-Doppler representation for joint range and velocity estimation, but practical indoor measurements are often corrupted by fractional delay-Doppler misalignment, static clutter, and multipath interference. These effects distort the DD response and make classical peak detection unreliable, particularly when target energy leaks across neighboring bins and overlaps with structured interference. This paper formulates DD peak localization as a supervised peak prediction problem and proposes a physics-guided deep learning framework that maps corrupted DD frames to the underlying target peak coordinates. The approach is trained using a ray-tracing-based OTFS simulation pipeline that generates diverse indoor trajectories with realistic multipath and clutter, where fractional interference emerges naturally from continuous motion and finite DD resolution. Experiments demonstrate robust peak localization and strong generalization to unseen motion conditions, and additional validation on real radar measurements confirms the practical effectiveness of the proposed method for interferenceaware OTFS sensing.

title: Design and Implementation of a Motor Imagery-Based Brain-Computer Interface System for Real-Time Control
authors: Liting Li, Anning Song, Cheng Ma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) technology faces critical bottlenecks of low electroencephalogram (EEG) signal acquisition accuracy and high command transmission latency when applied to automated control scenarios. To address these challenges, this study designs and implements a hierarchical motor imagery-based BCI system for real-time robotic control. Experimental validation involving 10 subjects demonstrates that subjects exhibiting significant Event-Related De-synchronization/Event-Related Synchronization (ERD/ERS) phenomena achieve a real-time classification accuracy of 92.3% ±2.1% for two types of motor imagery signals after 5 days of training. The system exhibits excellent stability and practicality in robot automated control scenarios, thereby establishing a novel technical framework for human-machine collaborative automation.

title: The Development of Information Retrieval Model for Hadith Data from Knowledge Graph
authors: Nurul Najwa Norhisham, Noryanti Muhammad, Mohd Izhar Firdaus Ismail, Aleta Caras Fabregas
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Retrieving and understanding Hadith information is challenging due to its complex structure, such as complicated narrator chains (isnad) and various in linguistic styles. The powerful Large Language Models (LLMs), often struggle with processing domain-specific content and tend to generate hallucinated or inaccurate responses when there is lacking structured context. This study proposes a RetrievalAugmented Generation (RAG) approach integrated with a Hadith knowledge graph to address these limitations. The goal is to develop an efficient information retrieval model customized for Hadith data by using the advantages of both structured semantic representation and language generation. The methodology includes a comprehensive data preparation pipeline, that involves Extract-Transform-Load (ETL) procedures, Natural Language Processing (NLP), Named Entity Recognition (NER), and fuzzy matching algorithms to build a solid Hadith knowledge graph. The knowledge graph acts as the foundation for RAG-based retrieval system that improves responses accuracy by providing factual grounding for LLM outputs. The Entity-Aware RAG model is evaluated using retrieval relevance metrics (Precision@5, MRR@5, and Relevance Score@5) and coverage, which measure the system's ability to retrieve appropriate Hadith evidence and generate grounded responses. This study shows how knowledge graphs and RAG can be combined to overcome the limitation of LLM in domain-specific situations, leading to more precise, comprehensible, and easily accessible Hadith information retrieval for analytical applications. hg

title: An Uncertainty-Aware Deformable Collaborative Network Approach for Face Super-Resolution
authors: Yi Sun, Yanlong Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: Face super-resolution (FSR) aims to recover high-fidelity facial structures and fine-grained textures from low-resolution inputs, which is of practical importance for face recognition, intelligent surveillance, and visual enhancement systems. To address the problems of spatial misalignment and unreliable geometric priors in existing prior-guided FSR methods under complex poses, occlusions, and extremely low-resolution conditions, this paper proposes an Uncertainty-Aware Deformable Collaborative Network (UA-DCNNet). Specifically, a deformable prior alignment module is introduced to achieve adaptive spatial correction between geometric priors and image features. An uncertainty-aware estimation branch is further designed to model the confidence of prior information and suppress the interference caused by inaccurate priors. Meanwhile, a multi-scale spatial feature transformation module progressively injects reliable structural cues into texture reconstruction, enabling geometry-texture collaborative restoration. Experimental results on the CelebA and Helen datasets show that UA-DCNNet achieves 32.55 dB/0.9248, 28.04 dB/0.8215, and 24.21 dB/0.6974 in Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) and Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM) at ×4, ×8, and ×16 scales on CelebA, respectively, outperforming representative Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) Transformer-, and diffusion-based methods. In addition, the proposed model obtains a Learned Perceptual Image Patch Similarity (LPIPS) of 0.142 and an ID Similarity of 0.754 in the ×8 setting, indicating advantages in perceptual fidelity and identity preservation. These results demonstrate that the proposed method provides an effective technical solution for robust low-quality face restoration and is meaningful for conference-related research fields such as image restoration, intelligent visual perception, and face analysis.

title: CRACK-YOLO: Road Damage Detection Method for Urban-Rural Fringe Roads
authors: Dawei Liu, Shuai Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: To solve the suboptimal detection accuracy of road damage detection models on urban-rural fringe roads with complex backgrounds, this paper proposes CRACK-YOLO for road damage detection in such areas. We introduce a Recursive Multi-layer Feature Pyramid Network (RM-FPN) and a Multi-Distance IoU Loss (MDIoU-Loss) to enhance localization precision for road damage. Experimental results show that on the GRDDC2020 dataset, the model achieves an F1-Score of 69.84%, Precision of 67.34% and Recall of 72.54%. Comparisons with mainstream methods like YOLOv10x prove the effectiveness of CRACK-YOLO.

title: Inception-ResNet-Crop-Based Deep Learning for Multi-Cell Intelligent Beamforming Optimization
authors: Wei Guo, Kai Liang, Haiyan Tu, Xiaoli Chu, Gan Zheng, Kai-Kit Wong, Chan-Byoung Chae
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Coordinated beamforming across multiple base stations (BSs) is a crucial technology for enhancing the performance of mobile networks. This paper investigates the design of multi-cell coordinated beamforming in multi-user multiple-input single-output (MU-MISO) downlink transmission. Specifically, we formulate a sum rate maximization problem across all users while ensuring that the transmit power constraints of each BS are satisfied. Traditionally, this problem is solved with iterative optimization algorithms, which however suffer from high computational complexity and long convergence time. To overcome these challenges, we first derive the optimal structure of multi-cell coordinated beamforming, transforming the complicated beamforming design into three types of simplified learnable parameters. Building upon this, we propose a deep learning (DL)-based framework. To enhance multi-scale feature extraction and improve both training and inference efficiency, we propose an Inception-ResNet-Crop (IRC)-based DL model and apply it to solve the sum rate maximization problem. The proposed DL model is evaluated under scenarios with or without perfect channel state information at the BSs. Numerical results demonstrate that our proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art deep unrolling methods in terms of both the sum rate performance and execution speed.

title: Design and Field Application of an Intelligent Error-Proofing Device for Cigarette Carton Rejection based on Visual Code Reading
authors: Zhongsheng Wei, Haiquan Luo, Feifei Li, Shihua Tang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: In order to improve the reliability and intelligence of the cigarette box rejection system of the sealing machine for defective cigarette boxes, an intelligent error-proofing device was designed based on visual code reading technology [1]. Based on the original rejection system, the device integrates a high-precision visual code reader as a verification device, combines a photoelectric sensor for detecting cigarette box arrival, and reconstructs the PLC control logic to realize visual recognition and intelligent verification of defective cigarette boxes. Unlike traditional cigarette rejection systems that lack a verification workflow for detection and rejection, this system incorporates visual code reading verification and on-time logic checks before rejection, effectively identifying and issuing warnings for missed and incorrect rejections. Field applications demonstrate that this system significantly improves the accuracy and reliability of rejecting defective cigarette boxes, reducing quality risks.

title: The Uneven Fight: Demographic Gaps in Breast Cancer Mortality
authors: Hannah-Grace Enriquez, Cheng Qian, Aijuan Dong
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Breast cancer remains one of the most common cancers in women, with survival outcomes strongly influenced by demographic and socioeconomic factors. This study utilizes the SEER database ($N=1,025,586$) to evaluate how demographic, tumor, and treatment variables predict mortality using a multimodal approach combining machine learning, explainable AI, and time-to-event survival analysis. Models were validated using stratified 5-fold cross-validation and temporal split (diagnoses $\leq 2014$ vs. $\geq 2015$), achieving a temporal ROCAUC of 0.8366. Race, income, tumor size, and age emerged as independent predictors of mortality risk. Notably, non-Hispanic Black, elderly, and low-income patients experienced significantly worse outcomes, even after accounting for clinical characteristics. Kaplan-Meier survival curves further demonstrated widening disparities in long-term survival over a $\mathbf{2 7 5}$-month period. These findings reveal persistent inequities in breast cancer outcomes, and highlight the need for targeted interventions to improve equity in cancer care.

title: Smart Kirana Onboarding: A Vision-Based Framework for Kirana Inclusion in ONDC

title: Systematic Abductive Reasoning via Diverse Relation Representations in Vector-Symbolic Architecture_supp1-3684958.pdf
authors: Hongzhi You
publish: N/A
abstract: In abstract visual reasoning, monolithic deep learning models suffer from limited interpretability and generalization, while existing neuro-symbolic approaches fall short in capturing the diversity and systematicity of attribute and relation representations. To address these challenges, we propose a systematic abductive reasoning model with diverse relation representations (Rel-SAR) in vector-symbolic architecture (VSA) to solve Raven’s progressive matrices (RPM). To derive attribute representations with symbolic reasoning potential, we introduce not only various types of atomic high-dimensional (HD) encodings that capture numeric, periodic, and logical semantics, but also the structured HD representation (SHDR) for the overall grid component. For systematic reasoning, we further propose novel numerical and logical relation functions and perform rule abduction and execution in a unified framework built upon these relation representations. Experimental results demonstrate that Rel-SAR achieves significant performance improvements on RPM tasks. By synergistically combining HD attribute representations with symbolic reasoning, Rel-SAR enables systematic abductive reasoning with both interpretable and computable semantics.

title: Detecting Malicious Concepts without Image Generation in AI-Generated Content (AIGC)
authors: Kun Xu, Wenying Wen, Shuren Qi, Tao Wang, Yushu Zhang, Yuming Fang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: The task of text-to-image generation has achieved tremendous success in practice, with emerging concept generation models capable of producing highly personalized and customized content. Fervor for concept generation is increasing rapidly among users, and platforms for concept sharing have sprung up. The concept owners may upload malicious concepts and disguise them with non-malicious text descriptions and example images to deceive users into downloading and generating malicious content. The platform needs a quick method to determine whether a concept is malicious to prevent the spread of malicious concepts. However, simply relying on concept image generation to judge whether a concept is malicious requires time and computational resources. Especially, as the number of concepts uploaded and downloaded on the platform continues to increase, this approach becomes impractical and poses a risk of generating malicious content. In this paper, we propose Concept QuickLook, the first systematic work to incorporate malicious concept detection into research, which performs detection based solely on concept files without generating any images. We define malicious concepts and design two operational modes for detection: concept matching and fuzzy detection. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed Concept QuickLook can detect malicious concepts and demonstrate practicality in concept sharing platforms. We also design robustness experiments to further validate the effectiveness of the solution. We hope this work can initiate malicious concept detection tasks and provide some inspiration.

title: Construction of Generalized Force-Deformation Theoretical Model: Towards Efficient Systematic Optimization of Fin-Ray Effect Grippers_supp1-3686201.mp4
authors: Huixu Dong
publish: N/A
abstract: Soft grippers leveraging bioinspired fin-ray effect (FRE) have garnered significant attention for handling geometrically complex objects across diverse scenarios. Despite their benefits, the fundamental mechanics governing morphological transformation under external loading remain insufficiently understood, hindering practical grasping performance. To address this gap, this article derives, presents, and validates a generalized theoretical model for FRE employing co-rotation concept, providing comprehensive insights for design optimization. First, the co-rotation modeling and force-displacement relationship are derived, eliminating the small deformation assumption while providing high-fidelity predictions with enhanced computational efficiency. Second, we present comprehensive insights into FRE gripper design, encompassing univariate ablation studies of four key design parameters and Bayesian-Optimization-integrated structure optimization. Third, the approach is validated via comprehensive simulations and physical experiments. Simulation results demonstrate excellent agreements with finite element analysis, with an average error of 6% and greatly enhanced efficiency, as fast as 0.035 seconds. Physical experiments under point, multi-point, and distributed contact validate the model's generalizability and robustness, with relative deviations within approximately 2-6% under different loading and contact conditions. Finally, grasping evaluation with diverse daily objects demonstrates the framework's capacity to advance compliant robotic systems through synergistic mechanics modeling and data-driven optimization.

title: IoST-Enabled Segmentation of Obscenity for Media Security Using Multifilter Federated W-Net
authors: Sonali Samal, Thippa Reddy Gadekallu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: The emergence of social media in the COVID-19 has contributed to the proliferation of explicit materials, which are frequently used by cybercriminals. To overcome this, the federated fusion W-Net (Gabor + PL) model, an intelligence of social things (IoST) enabled cooperative learning model is proposed that can be used to solve the problem of segmentation and moderation of obscene images. It is a hybrid of Gabor filtering to extract texture and power-law transformation to enhance contrast that will extract both a textural and intensity-based feature. Data augmentation methods such as region-of-interest filtering and unsharp masking enhance the clarity of edges and segmentation accuracy. We maximize the image quality using sigman (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\bm{\sigma} = \mathbf{5,10,15}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>), and mask weights(k = 1, 4.5). The model exhibits a high level of performance, with a precision, recall, and IoU of 98.85% precision, 97.73% recall, 98.85% IoU, and a Dice Score of 97.70%. It identifies obscene areas with minimal instances of false-positive and false-negative. The reliability of the result is attested by a large Matthews correlation coefficient and the 0.038 s processing time makes it usable in real time. These findings highlight its ability to do low-latency privacy-preserving obscene content detection in socially connected IoT settings. This method exemplifies the strength of IoST-enabled federated intelligence, as it allows cooperation among social devices without centralizing user data, thereby maintaining user privacy while enabling behavioral and cultural analysis.

title: Time and frequency response of a microdisk laser under optical pulse excitation
authors: Alexey Nadtochiy, Konstantin Ivanov, Ivan Makhov, Zhongqing Jia, Ruizhan Zhai, Lili Han, Zhaowei Wang, Natalia Kryzhanovskaya, Alexey Zhukov
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: By up-conversion technique, the time response was measured for a room temperature operating 12 μm microdisk laser with InAs/InGaAs quantum dots. Mixed optical excitation by sub-ps pulsed and continuous-wave lasers was used. Data processing based on the Fourier transform allowed us to obtain internal (i.e. not affected by capacitance) frequency response functions under various operating conditions. Maximum -3 dB modulation bandwidth of 4.15 GHz was estimated. The presented approach to data processing allows one to compare the results obtained using small-signal modulation or impulse response.

title: Direct Fabrication and Hybrid Physical-Data Modeling Strategy of Electrothermal Liquid Crystal Elastomer Actuators With Surface Microstructuring
authors: Zixiang Zhou, Yuyan Zhang, Haiying Yao, Yue Di, Zhixin Ren, Yintang Wen, Xiaoyuan Luo
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Liquid crystal elastomers (LCE)-based soft electrothermal actuators have attracted much attention due to their ability to generate large stress and reversible deformation. However, integrated electrothermal materials with LCE often suffer delamination or deformation failure during shrinking due to the differences in thermal expansion coefficients and weak adhesion. In this article, an LCE-based electrothermal actuator is proposed, in which micron-scale rough conductive microstructures are directly fabricated on the LCE surface via a friction process, effectively avoiding interfacial delamination issues. Typical actuation yielded 70% strain and 0.43-MPa actuating stress at 6.16-W average power. Resistance changes only 2.21% after 1000 peeling cycles, confirming high durability. To quantitatively describe the deformation characteristics of the actuator, a hybrid physical-data modeling strategy is proposed. This strategy establishes the relationship between deformation and temperature through thermodynamic analysis, with unknown parameters identified using nonlinear least squares based on experimental data. The proposed model achieves <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$R^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> values of 98.34% and 99.78% for fitting actuator behavior under unloaded conditions, demonstrating high accuracy. Furthermore, a programmable photonic crystal color tuner based on the proposed actuator is developed, exhibiting chameleon-like color-shifting. The model maintains high fitting accuracy under dynamic loading, with <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$R^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> values of 99.42% and 99.64%.

title: A Time-Optimal Motion Planning Algorithm for Multiple Aerial Transportation Systems Based on Compact Capsule Volume
authors: Yongtao Zhou, Yiming Wu, Jiuxiang Dong
publish: IEEE Systems Journal
abstract: This work develops a motion planning algorithm for multiple aerial transportation systems that generates time-optimal and collision-aware trajectories, addressing strong dynamic coupling, complex collision conditions, and the conservatism of traditional bounding volumes. First, the complicated nonlinear and underactuated characteristics of the multiple aerial transportation systems are managed through model transforming without any linearization, thereby retaining all dynamic model information. Second, a novel capsule bounding volume is developed that explicitly incorporates the payload state into the geometric encapsulation of each aerial transportation system, which reflects the specific payload state in anti-collision. Compared to existing methods, the proposed bounding volume method fully considers the system dynamics, which brings greater safety to real-world experiments. Furthermore, the proposed volume is more compact, thereby resulting in a larger solution space during motion planning. Then, an idea of scale is presented, which assists in the detection of collisions. Finally, the dynamics and all the constrains are equivalently transformed to formulate the to-be-solved optimal problem, which is finally solved by Gauss pseudospectral method-based algorithms. Comparative experiments verify that the proposed method yields safer and more aggressive trajectories than existing approaches, ensuring strict compliance with swing angle constraints even during time-optimal flight.

title: A Novel Opportunistic Symbiotic Backscatter-NOMA System
authors: Hancheng Yang, Haiyang Ding, Maged Elkashlan, Xiaoyi Huang, Zhen Wen, Qiyuan Wang, Bin Tang, Zhe Luo, Yuchen Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: This paper proposes an opportunistic symbiotic backscatter-NOMA (non-orthogonal multiple access) system, where far and near users engage in reciprocal backscattering after completing the NOMA decoding. Specifically, after decoding the NOMA signals, Users 1 and 2, each equipped with a backscatter device (BD), are opportunistically scheduled as a backscatter transmitting node or a receiving node based on the instantaneous channel fading conditions, enabling them to engage in a backscatter communication. To begin with, we derive the analytical expression of the coexistence outage probability. Next, we present the asymptotic analysis at high signal-to-noise ratio, which shows that this opportunistic backscatter transmission improves the outage scaling law from <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\frac{\ln (P_{s})}{P_{s}}$</tex-math></inline-formula> to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$P_{s}^{-1}$</tex-math></inline-formula> (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$P_{s}$</tex-math></inline-formula> denotes the transmit power) at high SNR levels, approaching toward the outage behavior of a classical NOMA system. In addition, it is analytically shown that the diversity-multiplexing tradeoff (DMT) is enhanced considerably with the incorporation of opportunistic backscattering.

title: Joint Covert Communications and Physical Layer Security for STAR-RIS Assisted NOMA Systems With Friendly Jamming
authors: Yuan Ren, Chuang Wang, Xuewei Zhang, Fan Jiang, Jing Jiang, Tiejun Lv
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: In this paper, we investigate joint covert communications and physical layer security (PLS) in non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) networks based on simultaneous transmitting and reflecting reconfigurable intelligent surface (STAR-RIS) and friendly jamming. In the scenario with obstacles between the transmitter and users, a novel covert and secure transmission scheme, utilizing the reflection and transmission functions of STAR-RIS to serve the covert user (CU) and secrecy user (SU), respectively, is proposed. The average detection error probability (AMDEP) and detection threshold of Willie, are theoretically derived to characterize the overall performance of covert communications. Owing to the proposed active jamming mechanism, the detection accuracy of Willie is further reduced, and the system covertness is significantly improved. In addition, the transmission reliability of the system is evaluated by deriving the outage probabilities of the CU and SU. Aiming to characterize the secrecy performance, a closed-form expression for the secrecy outage probability (SOP) of the SU is derived, and the impact of different parameters on the secrecy performance is analyzed. Moreover, a power allocation strategy is designed to maximize the covert rate, while satisfying the covertness constraint of the CU, the secrecy outage constraint of the SU, and the quality of service (QoS) constraints of both the CU and SU. We solve this optimization by first narrowing the feasible range of the power allocation factor, then using bisection search to find the optimal value. Simulation results validate our analysis, and confirm that the proposed scheme outperforms existing schemes in terms of improving the covertness, secrecy and reliability of the system.

title: Event-Triggered Dynamic Gains for Practical Prescribed-Time Tracking in Euler-Lagrange Systems
authors: Gewei Zuo, Xuecong Huang, Lijun Zhu, Han Ding
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: In this article, we aim to achieve practical prescribed-time tracking control for Euler-Lagrange systems (ELSs) with event-triggered dynamic gains. We integrate event-triggered mechanisms into the configuration of time-varying gains, enabling these gains to continue increasing beyond the prescribed timeframe when specific triggering conditions are satisfied. As a result, tracking errors converge into a prescribed neighborhood of zero within a finite time, rather than a broader neighborhood influenced by external disturbance amplitudes. Furthermore, when ELS encounter unexpected external disturbances, the event-triggered dynamic gains can swiftly minimize tracking errors back to the prescribed neighborhood of zero. It is rigorously demonstrated that these dynamic gains and all internal signals within the closed-loop ELS are bounded over the global time interval. Numerical simulations and real-robot experiments illustrate the effectiveness and innovation of the proposed algorithm.

title: Artificial-Intelligence-Based Constant Frequency ZVS Modulation Scheme for Four-Switch Buck–Boost Converter With Optimal Efficiency
authors: Zhifu Wang, Jianping Xu, Zhengge Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Pseudocontinuous conduction mode (PCCM) zero-voltage switching (ZVS) modulation scheme is widely used in four-switch buck–boost (FSBB) converter to achieve high efficiency and voltage step-up/step-down. However, the numerous combinations of PCCM modulation variables make the optimization and efficiency improvement of the FSBB converter challenging. To reduce computational burden, three-dimensional (3-D) or two-dimensional (2-D) look-up table (LUT), variable switching frequency, or mode transition is typically required. In this article, an artificial-intelligence (AI)-based constant frequency ZVS modulation scheme is proposed, which employs particle swarm optimization (PSO) for loss minimization and adaptive-network-based fuzzy inference system (ANFIS) for accurate modulation. The proposed AI-based modulation scheme enables highly automated optimization of PCCM modulation variables for optimal efficiency and eliminates the need for LUTs or mode transition over the full input/output voltage range and load power range. A 300 W FSBB prototype is built, and the experimental results verify the effectiveness of the proposed AI-based modulation scheme.

title: Secure Optical Network Transmission of 16QAM Signals Enabled by Phase Coding and Dispersive Stretching
authors: Rui Li, Xiaoxue Gong, Wenshuai Qin, Qihan Zhang, Weigang Hou, Yuxin Xu, Lei Guo
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: High-order modulation format optical signals are widely deployed in modern optical networks but remain insufficiently protected at the physical layer, leading to significant security vulnerabilities. This work proposes an all-optical secure transmission scheme for 16QAM signals based on phase coding and dispersive stretching. The scheme employs symmetric cascaded in-phase and quadrature Mach–Zehnder modulators (IQMZMs) to realize phase encoding and decoding, providing a simple structure with good suitability for photonic integration. The universality of constant-envelope key signals within the proposed architecture is investigated, providing references for key-format and device selection in practical applications and important guidance for future research on all-optical digital encryption. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first demonstration of all-optical digital encryption and decryption of 16QAM signals using high-order modulated key signals. Compared with existing schemes of the same category, the key space increases by a factor of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2^{n}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, where <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$n$</tex-math></inline-formula> denotes the number of symbols. Furthermore, the key signal undergoes dynamic temporal broadening through dispersive processing, enabling steganographic transmission. Simulation results at a bit rate of 100 Gb/s demonstrate significant security enhancement with high stability and reliability. Overall, the proposed scheme provides an effective approach to enhancing physical-layer security in high-order modulation optical transmission systems.

title: A Cross-Modal Semantic Change Detection Framework for Coastal Wetland Monitoring Using Optical and SAR Remote Sensing Imagery
authors: Haoyong Qi, Shenfu Zhang, Jinfang Shu, Liang Chen, Gang Zheng, Guhong Zhang, Hongwei Li, Xiaorun Li, Weiwei Sun, Xiangchao Meng
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Coastal wetlands, as highly dynamic and sensitive ecosystems, require precise identification of spatial change locations and semantic transition pathways for effective environmental monitoring and management. However, semantic change modeling across heterogeneous optical and SAR modalities remains underexplored and is largely hindered by severe modality discrepancy, feature misalignment, and semantic ambiguity caused by their fundamentally different imaging mechanisms. To address these challenges, we propose a Cross-Modality Adversarial Collaborative Semantic Change Detection Network (CMAC-SCDNet). Specifically, the proposed framework reduces cross-modal discrepancy and promotes semantic consistency through change-prior-guided adversarial collaborative learning, while avoiding uniform feature alignment and preserving modality-specific discriminative details. To improve robustness in highly heterogeneous coastal wetlands, we further design an Attention-guided Differential Feature Extraction Module to enhance change sensitivity, together with a Pyramid Temporal Correlation module and a Semantic Feature Refinement Module to improve temporal-semantic consistency. In addition, a dynamic multi-task weighting strategy is introduced to jointly optimize change detection and semantic segmentation. Experiments on two representative coastal wetland datasets, Yancheng and Hangzhou Bay, show that CMAC-SCDNet consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving improvements of 7.64% and 7.49% in the comprehensive Score metric, respectively. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of CMAC-SCDNet for optical–SAR semantic change detection and highlight its potential for robust monitoring in complex coastal wetland environments.

title: INNFusion: A Diffusion-based Blind Image Super Resolution Scheme using Reversible Degradation Process with Invertible Neural Networks
authors: Morteza Poudineh, Alireza Esmaeilzehi, M. Omair Ahmad
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Deep neural networks using generative diffusion prior have provided the state-of-the-art performances for the task of blind image super resolution. Thanks to their powerful image generation capability, these deep networks are able to produce high-quality visual signals with realistic textures and structures. However, since these schemes employ a very large number of parameters, their training process is often difficult, and therefore, their performances can be limited. In order to address this, in this paper, we propose a diffusion-based blind image super resolution scheme, which by using a novel learning algorithm with invertible neural networks, is able to provide superior results. Specifically, we argue that because of the reversibility property of invertible neural networks, they are able to generate degraded low-quality images, whose super resolved versions are the upper bound of the image super resolution function space. The inclusion of such visual signals in the training process of our blind image super resolution network leads to facilitating the learning paradigm and achieving higher performances. We show that our proposed blind image super resolution scheme is able to outperform the state-of-the-art methods.

title: A Multi-Beam Synthesis Method for Uniform Sparse Arrays Based on Visible Region Analysis
authors: Songsong Qian, Zhoujiao Tan, Xiaohan Zhou, Wei Kang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This paper proposes a multi-beam synthesis method for uniform sparse arrays based on visible region analysis. By analyzing the distribution characteristics of the array pattern within the visible region, the number and arrangement of the sparse array elements are determined. The element excitations are then calculated using either the pseudo-inverse method or convex optimization, and mutual coupling effects can be further incorporated by introducing the active element patterns. The designed array features a symmetrical structure and maximized uniformity, which effectively reduces system complexity and implementation cost. Validation through several one- and two-dimensional multi-beam synthesis examples demonstrates that this method achieves high sparsity, a compact aperture, and a large minimum element spacing while preserving shaped beam performance. Meanwhile, its synthesis efficiency is significantly superior to fully numerical optimization methods, indicating strong potential for engineering applications.

title: Toward Battery-Free Airflow Monitoring: Groove Enhanced Piezoelectric Wind Energy Harvester for Underground Mines_supp1-3686330.pdf
authors: Binghao Li
publish: N/A
abstract: Underground mines require continuous airflow monitoring, yet cabling or frequent battery replacement for distributed sensors is costly and hazardous. In this study, we present a piezoelectric wind energy harvester (PWEH) that also functions as a self-powered sensor for monitoring ventilation airflow conditions in underground mines. A shallow groove machined into the windward face of a D‑shaped bluff body boosts harvested power by 29.3% at a wind speed of 4.5 m/s compared with an ungrooved benchmark. The resulting voltage waveform supports two embedded sensing functions without external power: (i) ventilation fault diagnosis based on a stacking ensemble that integrates one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1D CNN), random forest (RF) and transfer-learned AlexNet classifier, enhanced by manifold‑based data augmentation, to identify 16 fault scenarios in a laboratory mine ventilation layout with up to 97.4% accuracy; and (ii) wind speed estimation using Random Forest that delivers a median mean absolute error below 0.12 m/s and worst‑case error under 0.5 m/s across 1.5–5.5 m/s airflow speeds, matching the performance of commercial anemometers. Laboratory trials in a scaled mine ventilation layout validate these capabilities across 16 duct networks with fan speeds ranging from 200–700 rpm, while energy measurements show the device produces a root mean square power output of 5.2 μW at a 4.5 m/s wind speed, which is sufficient to run a Bluetooth Low Energy-based SensorTag for fog data transmission every 50 seconds. By combining wind energy harvesting and airflow sensing in a single device, the proposed PWEH offers a battery-free and low-maintenance path to safer and smarter Mine Internet of Things deployments.
