title: From CAD Data to Semantic Graphs: Integration and Derivation of Domain-Specific Knowledge within Classified Graphs
authors: Robin Taba, Joshua Falkenhain, Dr. Frank Köster
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid framework that integrates machine learning and ontological reasoning in graph-based representations of CAD assemblies to enable functional analysis. Building on the Core Product Model (CPM), we define multiple levels of information richness within our hybrid-data CAD assembly graphs, which fuse geometric and parametric data from STEP and CATIA sources into a unified semantic representation. By embedding CAD domain expertise into ontological rule sets and coupling them with machine learning models refined through ontology-guided calibration, our method elevates low-information assembly graphs to higher levels within a defined CPM hierarchy of semantic significance. The enriched graphs support the inference of implicit degrees of freedom and the automated propagation of motion by operationalizing ontological rules. Demonstrated on gearbox assemblies, the approach reconstructs complete kinematic chains from input to output. By uniting rule-based reasoning with machine-learned prediction, this work establishes a foundation for semantic, interpretable, and knowledge-driven CAD graph analysis.

title: Multi-Task Learning for Uzbek News Text Classification
authors: Bahor Eshmirzayeva, Shirali Kadyrov
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: We explore multi-task learning for Uzbek news text classification in a low-resource setting. We introduce a new dataset of more than sixteen thousand Uzbek news articles collected from Kun.uz, annotated for three supervised tasks: topic classification, author gender identification, and publication year prediction. Using a shared-encoder, multi-head architecture based on a pretrained Uzbek transformer model, we compare singletask baselines with several multi-task learning variants, including layer-wise aggregation and cross-task attention. Experimental results show that transformer-based models outperform classical baselines across all tasks. Multi-task learning produces generally stable performance, with modest gains for topic classification and consistent results for gender and temporal prediction. Our analysis using confusion matrices and shared-representation visualizations shows that, in Uzbek news classification under lowresource conditions, task difficulty is mainly determined by how well the labels align with the underlying semantic representations, rather than by architectural complexity. Our study provides the first systematic evaluation of multi-task learning for Uzbek news classification and contributes an annotated dataset and empirical insights for future low-resource natural language processing research.

title: Explainable AI for Meniscus Segmentation on MRI
authors: Akbota Kumarkanova, Aizhan Tlebaldinova, Saule Kumargazhanova, Markhaba Karmenova, Zbigniew Omiotek
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Meniscus tears are among the most common injuries of the knee joint and require accurate and timely diagnosis. Deep learning–based segmentation models are widely used to automate the analysis of medical images; however, the limited interpretability of their predictions necessitates the application of explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) methods. In this study, the Grad-CAM and RISE methods are compared for the explainable interpretation of meniscus tear segmentation in magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) using the YOLOv8m-seg model. The study was conducted on a clinical dataset comprising 4000 2D MRI slices (normal/tear) with manually annotated segmentation masks. The evaluation of the methods is based on the Insertion and Deletion metrics, as well as on visual analysis of the explanation maps on the test set. The results demonstrate the superiority of the RISE method in generating more localized explanations supported by quantitative evaluation, thereby confirming the potential of model-agnostic XAI approaches for medical image analysis.

title: Review of Explainable Artificial Intelligence Methods for Alzheimer's Disease
authors: Nuray Nurbolat, Khaled Mohamad
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Neural networks demonstrate exceptional efficiency in processing and analyzing big data. In medicine, these advantages open up great opportunities for early diagnosis. However, their implementation is limited by the “black box” problem: doctors cannot trust a diagnosis without understanding the logic of decision-making. To eliminate this barrier, Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) is used. These methods not only indicate the signs on the basis of which the forecast was made, but can also help identify previously unknown correlations. We systematically screen and then critically analyze recent studies XAI methods applied to Alzheimer's disease screening and diagnosis that integrate machine learning and deep learning models with post-hoc and embedded explainability techniques across multiple data modalities. The review highlights how XAI methods contribute to biomarker identification, model transparency, and clinical trust, while also exposing persistent challenges related to data bias, computational complexity, and limited clinical validation.

title: Bi-level Optimization Scheduling of Microgrids Based on Flexible Demand Response
authors: Furong Tu, Maochun Wei, Sumei Zheng, Meina Xie, Ying Zhao, Hongchao Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: To enhance the efficient mobilization of flexible resources on the user side within microgrids, an optimization scheduling model has been developed that integrates economic compensation and carbon emission cost-sharing mechanisms. Initially, based on the multi-energy response characteristics of users, flexible loads are categorized into three distinct classes for scheduling purposes. A joint incentive strategy incorporating economic compensation and carbon emission cost-sharing is introduced. Subsequently, a bi-level scheduling model is established, aiming to maximize the profits of microgrid operator (MGO) while optimizing the utility of load aggregator (LA). The model employs the Grey Wolf Optimization algorithm (GWO) in conjunction with Gurobi solver for solution derivation. Finally, simulations conducted under various scenarios demonstrate that the proposed model significantly enhances the benefits for both MGO and LA while effectively reducing carbon emission costs.

title: Design and Implementation of an Intelligent Substation Test Analysis and Judgment Database System Based on the BS Architecture
authors: Hao Hong, Xinlan Yan, Yanping Zhu, Yihua Xie, Wei Zhou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: To improve the reliability of substation test analysis and judgment, this study investigates the design and implementation of an intelligent substation test analysis and judgment database system based on the BS architecture. The system hardware is designed based on the BS architecture, clarifying the detailed parameters and selection of servers, network equipment, and data acquisition devices to provide a stable operating environment. In the software design, key substation test entities are extracted, entity relationships are designed, a substation test data model is constructed, and the database table structure and fields are planned. Substation test data is processed, date and time formats are standardized, a hybrid sampling strategy is employed to balance data, an improved Z-score is used to identify outliers, and global and local cleaning is performed to ensure data quality. Improved wavelet packet decomposition technology is used to extract data features, and an intelligent analysis and judgment model is constructed and optimized to achieve accurate analysis and judgment. Finally, the data model, processed data, and analysis and judgment model are stored in the database, completing the design of an intelligent substation test analysis and judgment database system based on the BS architecture. Test results show that when the information missing rate is only 10%, the designed system keeps the ROC curve close to the upper left corner, demonstrating excellent performance with a high true positive rate and a low false positive rate. When the data noise ratio reaches 50%, the system accuracy remains at 92.1%. As the data volume increases, the system throughput exhibits a stable and reasonable change trend. These characteristics collectively enhance the system’s analysis and judgment reliability.

title: Constrained inverse inference and counterfactual auditing for dual-signal elimination systems
authors: Juxian Chen, Yiping Yin
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Many selection systems fuse an auditable expert signal with an unobserved public-preference signal. When only outcomes and the fusion rule are visible, assessing rule effects becomes a constrained inverse problem. We propose an interpretable closed-loop framework that rewrites eliminations as rule-consistency inequalities on the latent public share over the simplex, selects a representative solution by maximizing decisionboundary margin with weak regularization, and reports marginand uncertainty-based diagnostics for flip risk and intrinsic ambiguity. Using round-wise counterfactual audits that fix inputs and swap percent-based versus rank-based fusion, we find disagreements concentrate in low-margin rounds: percent preserves magnitude while rank compresses magnitude and amplifies rank swaps. The framework yields deployable inference outputs and risk-stratified evidence for auditing dual-signal decision rules.

title: Practice of Optimization Scheme for Improving Address Matching Accuracy of Intelligent Customer Service
authors: Wei Wang, E Lili, Yan Zong, Jinxuan Gao, Chengliang Feng
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: In the intelligent transformation of the power industry, intelligent customer service address matching faces challenges such as insufficient accuracy and weak generalization ability caused by colloquial and regional expressions of addresses. To address this, this paper proposes and practices a systematic optimization scheme of “problem attribution, phased optimization, and closed-loop verification”. By combining rule engineering, knowledge graph fusion, and confidence evaluation, the scheme sequentially achieves three-stage capability optimization: “noise cleaning, semantic alignment, and robust matching”. This study realizes a significant improvement in address matching accuracy from $\mathbf{5 1 \%}$ to $\mathbf{9 1 \%}$, verifies the effectiveness of the scheme in real business scenarios, and provides a referable systematic optimization path for fields such as power customer service.

title: Lithium battery life prediction based on artificial intelligence
authors: Yuxuan Liu, Zhixin Zhai, Yu Niu, Congzhuang Yu, Yingying Xiong
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: To address the challenges of nonlinear degradation, inter-battery variability, and insufficient uncertainty assessment in lithium-ion battery life prediction, we established a “whole-battery reserve + time-rolling validation” evaluation system using NASA public data. Data preprocessing and labeling were performed according to unified standards. For online computable observation channels, health indicators derived from cycles, intervals/curves, and time series were extracted. These indicators were then filtered using variance, correlation, and stability to form a transferable feature set. We compared lightweight baseline models, including linear, ridge, and elastic net, with deep time series models such as LSTM, CNN/TCN, and Transformer/Seq2Seq. Uncertainty and calibration were then assessed using metrics such as quantile regression, reliability curves, and ECE. Experimental results show that on an external test set, the Transformer/Seq2Seq achieves a $\mathbf{2 0 \%}$ - $\mathbf{2 5 \%}$ reduction in MAE compared to the best traditional baseline. Even in the initial 20 to 60 iterations, this model maintains a significant advantage. Experimental ablation results show that curve-level and timing-derived factors are extremely important in the early stages, uncertainty modeling significantly improves interval coverage, and engineering evaluation further sets upper bounds for volume, latency, and online feature ratio. This indicates that the conditions for BMS/edge platform deployment are in place. This paper introduces a reproducible evaluation framework, a parseable health indicator system, and a feasible model combination for battery life prediction.

title: Knowledge Graph Interoperability and Reasoning: A Brief Survey of Alignment Techniques and Retrieval-Augmented Generation Approaches
authors: Adishesh Gonibeed Ravishankar, Srividya Bansal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: This survey investigates the technical mechanisms for achieving interoperability between heterogeneous Knowledge Graphs (KG) and their application in grounding Large Language Models (LLMs). It details the spectrum of alignment methodologies, moving from fundamental terminological and structural measures such as string edit distances and graph homomorphism to advanced representation learning techniques like translational models and Graph Convolutional Networks (GCN). Furthermore, the document analyzes the convergence of structured data with generative AI, specifically examining how frameworks like Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) and neuro-symbolic alignment utilize KGs to enforce factual consistency and mitigate hallucinations in Transformer-based architectures.

title: A Lightweight Device Management Platform Integrating Multi-source Data Modeling and Intelligent Scheduling
authors: Wenyu Liao, Shibo Ouyang, Xin Yao, Xin Li, Ke Xu, Xiaohui Jiang, Yang Zhou, Jiamin Lei
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: In storage and transportation systems, electrical and mechanical equipment such as conveyors, pumps, valves, and loading arms operate under prolonged high-load, corrosive, and frequent start-stop conditions. Traditional Time-Based Maintenance (TBM) and empirical risk assessment methods struggle to fully utilize historical failures and multi-source data, making it challenging to support department-level precision maintenance and workforce allocation. This paper addresses scenarios in storage and transportation departments by constructing a multi-source data model centered on a five-level BOM/EBS structure. The model integrates a six-dimensional degradation risk matrix, historical failure records, and maintenance plans, employing gradient boosting trees to predict future equipment failure probabilities. These predictions are combined with expert ratings to generate comprehensive risk levels. Building on this foundation, the maintenance cycle and position workload optimization are modeled as a resource-constrained 0-1 integer programming problem. By balancing risk exposure, downtime duration, and labor costs as multi-objective criteria, the system generates equipment-level maintenance cycle and position workload allocation plans. The platform adopts a front-end and backend separated Web architecture to visually display multisource data, risk predictions, and optimization results. Realworld data from a storage and transportation department demonstrates that compared to fixed-cycle TBM schemes, the proposed AI-driven strategy significantly improves high-risk equipment identification rates, focuses maintenance efforts on critical equipment, reduces failure rates and downtime duration, and provides quantitative evidence for workforce allocation. learning; integer programming; equipment management platform; storage and transportation system

title: Research on Intelligent Sensing and Monitoring of Railway Facilities and Equipment Based on Digital Twins
authors: Hanyu Feng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Railway facilities and equipment are exposed to complex and ever-changing natural and operational environments for extended periods. They are subjected to the coupling effects of multiple physical fields such as extreme temperature variations, high-frequency dynamic loads, and electromagnetic interference. Traditional monitoring methods, limited by single-parameter threshold criteria, struggle to capture the potential correlations and degradation trends of equipment status, resulting in limited diagnostic accuracy. Therefore, this paper proposes an intelligent sensing and monitoring method for railway facilities and equipment based on digital twins. By deploying a multi-source heterogeneous sensor network, real-time equipment operating parameters are collected; a high-fidelity digital twin model is constructed to achieve dynamic bidirectional mapping between physical entities and virtual spaces; based on this, a historical fault database and a deep convolutional neural network are integrated to extract key features from complex multidimensional data, enabling multidimensional and accurate diagnosis of equipment status and health level determination. Experimental results show that the proposed method can accurately capture equipment temperature fluctuations $\left(\pm 5^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\right)$ without triggering the warning threshold $\left(65^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\right)$, highly consistent with bridge maintenance records; the confusion matrix accuracy reaches 1, and the AUC value is 0.99, significantly outperforming existing methods, demonstrating excellent status recognition capabilities and engineering applicability.

title: Transitioning from Classical to Hybrid Search: A Metric-Driven and Explainable Approach
authors: Lennart Kapanke, Philipp Stephan, Robert Mertens
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Lexical, index-based search engines such as OpenSearch, which retrieve documents through lexical similarity, have been widely adopted for keyword-based retrieval. However, the increasing importance of semantic similarity has motivated the adoption of vector-based retrieval methods. Many organizations face the challenge of transitioning from an established classical search system to a hybrid approach that combines lexical and vector search. This paper presents a transition tool that supports this process in two ways: (a) by providing a novel metric that evaluates retrieval quality without relying on ground truth, and (b) by enabling explainability of vector search results, helping practitioners understand why specific documents are retrieved. Our approach allows search practitioners to ensure that hybrid search maintains the strengths of lexical retrieval while unlocking the benefits of vector-based methods.

title: Intelligent Anomaly Detection for Hydraulic System in Deep-Sea Submersible
authors: Xing Fang, Xin Tan, Chengxi Zhang, Xiang Gao, Choon Ki Ahn
publish: IEEE Journal of Oceanic Engineering
abstract: The hydraulic system used in the deep-sea submersible of China works in the extreme conditions of the deep-sea environment, where the pressure is high, the temperature is low, and the landscape is complicated, which contributes to the risks of critical failures. Anomaly detection is essential to the safety of crews and mission success. However, traditional techniques have two problems: small samples of faults and severe class imbalance (with few faults hidden in the vast sea of normal activity). To cope with these issues, we suggest the Aux-BiGAN, an unsupervised anomaly detector that is specific to the hydraulic system. The approach enhances learning normal data distributions and increases the sensitivity of anomaly detection but traines on normal data only by auxiliary losses. It is tested on actual hydraulic system defects and artificial data of hard failures and incipient soft anomalies. In real-world conditions, it is able to identify severe failures, including hydraulic oil leakage, electronic control unit burnout, and sticking solenoid valves, and its results are supported by postdive maintenance reports by pilots. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method, which achieves an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.9104 on hard anomalies. It outperforms the baseline BiGAN across all metrics. It surpasses state-of-the-art methods in terms of accuracy, F1-score, and false alarm rate across both failure types, validating its enhanced reliability for deep-sea missions.

title: Smooth Variable Structure Filters with Application to Battery State Estimation: A Review
authors: Farzaneh Ebrahimi, Peyman Setoodeh, Ryan Ahmed, Saeid Habibi
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: The Smooth Variable-Structure Filter (SVSF) provides a complementary approach to Bayesian filters, exemplified by the Kalman Filter and its variants. The SVSF is based on the sliding mode concept to achieve comparatively better robustness against modeling uncertainties and environmental disturbances. Bayesian filters are derived to provide approximations for the optimal filtering solution. From an algorithmic point of view, both families of filters can be formulated in a predictor-corrector form, but they present different trade-offs between robustness and optimality in estimation. This paper presents a comprehensive review of the state-of-the-art SVSF variants as well as the methods for combining SVSF and Bayesian filters. A key contribution of this work is the systematic comparison of 15 SVSF variants with several nonlinear Kalman Filters (EKF, UKF, CDKF) in the context of battery State-of-Charge (SOC) estimation. Simulation results using real-world battery data demonstrate that several SVSF variants offer superior estimation accuracy and robustness compared to traditional filters. This work also highlights performance under various initial conditions and evaluates the impact of the accuracy of SOC estimation on the estimation of terminal voltage. Since the governing dynamics of lithium-ion batteries change with aging and varying environmental conditions, the robustness of SVSF-based algorithms makes them promising candidates for reliable SOC estimation in Battery Management Systems (BMS).

title: Bio-Inspired Flower Robot for Emotional Human-Robot Interaction_supp1-3686643.mp4
authors: Hongqiang WANG
publish: N/A
abstract: Mental health problems are common worldwide, but the available support systems and healthcare services are insufficient and inadequate. Social robots offer a promising solution to achieve social interaction and emotional connection. Nature-inspired architectural robotics can seamlessly blend into human daily life by reshaping future living environments. In this work, inspired by natural flowers, we designed a flower robot with a realistic morphology that simulates the opening-closing, rhythmic movements, and stem-bending movement. Building on the morphological design, we integrated a flex sensor into the petals to enable touch-induced tactile feedback, mimicking the touch-induced plant-like seismonastic movement. Furthermore, we incorporated voice interaction powered by a local large language model (LLM) to achieve adaptive intent understanding and personalized semantic engagement, thereby enhancing interaction flexibility compared to the repetitive tasks and rigid protocols of traditional social robots. Finally, the human-robot interaction (HRI) experiment demonstrated that the flower robot significantly reduced participants' negative emotions and provided positive social engagement. This work developed a bio-inspired flower robot, offering a promising pathway to social interaction and soothing presence.

title: Incremental Feedback-Enabled MIMO-OTFS Communication Systems
authors: Naresh Kolla, Tanveer Sharma, Kumar Appaiah, Ribhu Chopra
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: High-mobility wireless communication systems are significantly affected by Doppler spread and multipath delay. Orthogonal Time Frequency Space (OTFS) modulation is a robust solution to handle such impairments by mapping information symbols to the delay-Doppler (DD) domain, where the channel exhibits inherent sparsity. In this context, accurate channel state information at the transmitter (CSIT) is essential for fully exploiting the potential of multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems. In this work, we propose a novel, low-overhead channel state information (CSI) feedback mechanism tailored to MIMO-OTFS systems that leverages the sparsity of the DD channel representation. The proposed method performs path-wise quantization of dominant channel coefficients and incremental refinement of CSI using binary-structured feedback, enabling adaptive precision and progressive updates. Unlike conventional one-shot scalar quantization or codebook-based approaches, our method reduces feedback load while maintaining fidelity even under high-mobility scenarios. We derive tractable closed-form lower bound expressions for ergodic and outage achievable rates under inaccurate CSI, explicitly accounting for channel estimation and quantization errors. Rate-distortion bounds are used to quantify the trade-off between the number of feedback bits and achievable rate. Simulation results confirm the utility of our approach in terms of spectral efficiency, robustness to feedback imperfections, and scalability to large MIMO settings.

title: A Fusion Design Paradigm of Traditional Semantic Analysis and Large Language Model Agent for Question Assessment in Complex Scenarios
authors: Xinbao Liu, Ying Ren, Xinlin Zhao, Jianing Liu, Weilong Li, Yan Liang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Automated assessment of subjective questions in complex educational contexts requires balancing accuracy, robustness, and interpretability. This paper proposes a designcentric fusion paradigm that integrates traditional natural language analysis (vectorization, semantic similarity, keyword coverage) with large language model (LLM) agent-based structured scoring via composable pipelines. We present design principles, methodologies, and implementation pathways, and summarize challenges and solutions to provide a reference for educational subjective question assessment.

title: CODA-MVSC: Multi-View Subspace Clustering via Consensus-Overlap Decoupling
authors: Miaomiao Zong, Wenke Zang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Multi-view subspace clustering needs to exploit both the structure shared across views and the complementary information specific to each view, yet coordinating these two aspects is still difficult. To this end, we develop CODA-MVSC, a clustering model based on consensus-overlap decoupling. The proposed model uses a shared coefficient matrix to characterize common structure and introduces view-specific deviation matrices to capture complementary variations. A structured sparsity constraint is imposed on the consensus matrix to encourage a clustering-friendly representation, while a Hadamard-productbased overlap penalty is used to reduce redundant deviation patterns across views. The resulting objective is optimized by an ADMM-based alternating scheme with Sylvester-type updates and singular-value shrinkage. Experiments on benchmark multiview datasets show that CODA-MVSC delivers competitive and stable clustering performance.

title: Full-Range Head Pose Estimation Algorithm Based on an Improved 6D RepNet
authors: Wenbo Hou
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: To address the challenges of Head Pose Estimation (HPE) under complex backgrounds, including background noise interference, insufficient multi-scale feature utilization, and the trade-off between model stability and prediction accuracy, this paper proposes an improved 6DRepNet-based framework for full-range head pose estimation. Different from the original 6DRepNet, which mainly relies on a RepVGG backbone and direct 6D rotation regression, the proposed method introduces two targeted improvements in network architecture and loss design. In the feature extraction stage, a Kernel Space Modulation (KSM) module is embedded into Stage 3 to Stage 5 of the backbone to achieve pose-aware dynamic reweighting of convolution kernels, while a Stage-Adaptive Squeeze-and-Excitation (SA-SE) module is introduced to preserve shallow geometric details and strengthen deep semantic representation. In the optimization stage, a hybrid loss integrating geodesic loss, Euler angle regression loss, and pose manifold consistency loss is constructed to improve geometric consistency, convergence stability, and local continuity in pose space. Experimental results show that, compared with the original 6DRepNet, the proposed method improves overall accuracy by 6.36% on AFLW2000 and 14.17% on BIWI, reducing the overall Mean Absolute Error (MAE) from 3.93° to 3.68° and from 3.74° to 3.21°, respectively. These results indicate that the proposed framework provides an effective solution for robust head pose estimation in computer vision applications such as intelligent classroom behavior analysis and complex-scene visual perception.

title: Research on the Application of Multimodal Perception and Fusion Interaction Technology in Smart Parks
authors: Junhao Zeng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: To tackle the core challenges in the intelligent management of people, vehicles, facilities, objects, and operations within full-scenario smart parks, this paper develops a multimodal perception system integrating visual, acoustic, biometric, and environmental sensing technologies, drawing on multiple practical park projects. Leveraging the Lingyun Middle Platform of Flying Connected, this system enables unified access to various terminal devices, and accomplishes intelligent management of personnel, vehicles, environments, and emergency responses through three core processes: data denoising, cross-modal feature alignment, and fusion decision-making. Moreover, by virtue of the large-model adaptation capability of Lingyun Agent Studio, a full-link intelligent architecture is constructed. This research proposes a multi-modal fusion framework tailored to smart park scenarios and establishes a lightweight edge-cloud collaborative deployment solution. The industrial value of the proposed technology is verified through practical applications, which provides a replicable technical paradigm for the vertical application of multimodal perception and fusion interaction technologies as well as the digital transformation of smart parks.

title: Detection of Tiny Objects on 16×16 Pixel Images
authors: Duleep Rathgamage Don, Ramaza Aygun, Mahmut Karakaya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Object detection on ultra-low-resolution imagery remains virtually unattainable within existing deep learning frameworks. To address this end, we introduce the Reduced Fully Connected Detection Network (RFCDet), a lightweight architecture capable of processing $16 \times 16$-pixel inputs while employing a substantially reduced number of parameters compared to all known object detection models. Remarkably, RFCDet achieves reliable detection of even smaller objects within these extremely constrained image scales. Our experiments conducted on a curated patch dataset of AI-TOD v2 show promising results.

title: Research on Work Order-Driven O&M Training System for Distribution Network
authors: Yanjie Zhang, Lin Niu, Wei Liu, Chenyu Yan, Xiaoxiao Zhao, Lei Wang, Zhenhai Zhang, Hongbo Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: To address issues in the training of distribution network O&M (Operation and Maintenance) personnel-such as the lack of real-scenario support, insufficient practical operation, and incomplete evaluation function, a work orderdriven active operation and maintenance training system is designed for intelligent distribution network operation. Firstly, the development background and research status of work order-driven systems is investigated in distribution networks. Secondly, the composition structure of the practical training system, including the Web main station terminal, mobile practical training APP terminal, and multi-system interaction interfaces is proposed, while extracting the core structural elements of typical work orders. Then, it constructs a multi-dimensional work order execution evaluation model covering 10 key indicators (e.g, information processing, fault diagnosis, and process standardization), then proposed the WS-TA and ML-RFR models to optimize the indicator weights and scoring rules. Finally, through illustrative experiments, it statistically compares the scores of O&M personnel’ s various capabilities before and after the system application, verifying the system’s effectiveness. The proposed system can provide technical support for the efficient training of distribution network O&M personnel.

title: Cooperative Protection Decision-Making for Multi-Unmanned Systems in Dynamic Fuzzy Perceptual Environments
authors: Yiheng Yang, Meili Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: In large-scale infrastructure protection, autonomous patrol, and emergency response tasks, multiple unmanned agents are often required to cooperatively operate in environments characterized by incomplete perception, dynamic target behaviors, and strong decision-environment coupling. These factors introduce significant uncertainty and nonstationarity, which limit the effectiveness of conventional rulebased and static optimization approaches. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a hierarchical cooperative protection decision-making method for multi-unmanned systems operating in dynamic fuzzy perceptual environments (DFPEs). From an engineering-oriented systems perspective, the cooperative protection process is formulated as an extended Dec-POMDP with partial observability, dynamic uncertainty, and a discrete intention variable. Potential target behaviors are abstracted into a finite intention set and inferred online using historical observations. The inferred intention belief provides adaptive guidance to a low-level cooperative policy learned under centralized training and distributed execution. Specifically, the low-level module integrates a Shapley-valueconstrained graph attention multi-agent reinforcement learning method to improve coordination efficiency and interpretable credit assignment. Simulation results under varying levels of perceptual fuzziness demonstrate improved system security maintenance performance and enhanced robustness compared with representative baselines, indicating the effectiveness and engineering applicability of the proposed framework.

title: Research on Tennis Pose-based Action Recognition and Evaluation Based on Skeletal Key Points
authors: Hengyi Zhang, Jianrui Fu, Juan Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of insufficient professional teachers and backward teaching methods in tennis teaching in colleges and universities, this paper proposed a tennis action recognition and evaluation method based on skeleton key points. The YOLOv3 target detector with ResNet - 50 pose estimation model to extract human body skeleton key points, and coordinate system reconstruction method is proposed, the origin of coordinates transform for the halfway point of the left shoulder and right shoulder, effectively solve the image problem of the relative position of the human body. The tennis action da-ta set is fine-grained processed by Angle calculation, and the start and end points of the action are divided, so as to eliminate redundant and invalid frames in the data set. Then, using ST - GCN, AGCN, PoseC3D three kinds of gesture recognition model based on skeleton key training, the experimental results show that using fine-grained data sets, PoseC3D model accuracy increased from 69.17% to 88.33%. Finally, a multi-dimensional dynamic time warping evaluation algorithm based on the important key points and center of gravity of the human body was proposed, and the independent sample t-test verified that there was no significant difference between the score of the algorithm and the score of the coach (p=0.619>0.05). This study provides theoretical basis and technical support for the development of intelligent tennis teaching system.

title: A Performance and Scalability Evaluation of Monolithic (Django) vs. Microservice (FastAPI) Architectures for Asynchronous API Workloads in Python
authors: Mohammed Alaa Ala'anzy, Zhandos Yeshpatov
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: As few empirical performance comparisons of modern Python web frameworks exist, it is hard to choose an appropriate architecture under real I/O-bound workloads. This study compares a monolithic back-end implemented with Django, a popular framework implementing the model-view-template software design pattern, with a microservice back-end built using FastAPI. Running load tests using Apache JMeter inside a Docker container indicated a several-fold improvement in performance with the microservice architecture. FastAPI, in a microservice architecture, yielded more than 6 times the throughput of the monolithic Django architecture: 67.8 req/sec versus 10.1 req/sec. Additionally, FastAPI had a significantly lower average latency with the same 0.00% error rate. The empirical results of this work show that for I/O-bound applications, a modern asynchronous microservice architecture can provide much better performance and scalability than a customary monolithic architecture.

title: Design and Evaluation of a Google Earth Engine-Based Training Framework for Water Resource Analysis in Educational Settings
authors: Orazbek Zhanatayev, Shakhnazar-Sultan Manbay, Baglan Tolebay, Azamat Serek
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: This study will outline the establishment and evaluation of a training module based on Google Earth Engine (GEE) as a simple learning data analytics tool. The suggested framework will be based on a three-part pedagogical model that involves (i) conceptual backgrounds of remote sensing and hydrological processes, (ii) analytical experiences through Google Earth Engine capabilities of image collections, reducers, time-series analysis, spectral indices (NDWI, MNDWI), and (iii) application-based projects where the learners will be expected to study realworld water systems independently. A pilot group comprising 22 geography, computer science and environmental science students were provided with a threesession program, which included the basics of remote sensing, GEE based water resource analysis and the utility of the Normalized Difference Water Index (NDWI) to detect surface water. All the course participants completed the course and carried out their own research projects with openaccess datasets in GEE. This indicated the ease and flexibility of the framework. The test result increased significantly, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$58.6 \%(S D=12.4)$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$86.3 \%(S D=8.9)(p<0.01)$</tex>. The accuracy of water masks constructed by participants, when compared to the reference JRC Global Surface Water dataset, was 91.7 %, with a Kappa coefficient of 0.88. The time to complete a task reduced by 27 percent and the usefulness, clarity, and overall experience average ratings stood at 4.7, 4.6 and 4.8 respectively. While most participants improved their coding and data processing skills, 14 % initially encountered difficulties with JavaScript syntax. This highlights the need for introductory programming support in future iterations of the framework. The framework is effective in enhancing the analytical skills and understanding hydrological systems. It provides a resource-saving model of teaching environmental data that is easy to expand especially in schools which do not possess many computers. Programming assistance should be provided to beginners that is specific in future versions.

title: Encrypted Control for Timed Discrete Event Systems using Homomorphic Encryption
authors: Raphael Fritz, Nico Krebs, Ping Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: The rapid increase of networked cyberphysical system (CPS), especially in industrial environments, necessitates safe and secure communication. One possibility to increase the security of CPS is encryption. In this paper, a controller encryption scheme for discrete event systems (DES) is proposed that allows not only the encryption of the communication, but also the controller itself. The considered controller is modeled by a type of DES, namely signal interpreted Petri nets (SIPN). A new representation of SIPN controllers is presented which allows the use of homomorphic encryption schemes to encrypt the controller parameters, the states and the input and output information. To increase the range of applications to more types of DES such as timed SIPN, it is shown how encrypted counters and encrypted timers can be realized. The encrypted controller scheme only requires encryption and decryption on the plant side. Thus the data transmitted between controller and plant and the information inside the controller are more secure against eavesdropping attacks.

title: High-Probability Convergence Theory for Distributed Composite Optimization with Sub-Weibull Noises
authors: Zhan Yu, Zhongjie Shi, Deming Yuan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: With the rapid development of distributed optimization (DO) theory, the distributed stochastic gradient methods (DSGMs) occupy an important position. Although the theory of different DSGMs has been widely established, the main-stream results of existing work are still derived under the condition of light-tailed stochastic gradient noises. Increasing examples from various fields, indicate that, the light-tailed noise model is overly idealized in many practical instances, failing to capture the complexity and variability of noises in real-world scenarios, such as the presence of outliers or extreme values from data science and statistical learning. To address this issue, we propose a new DO framework that incorporates stochastic gradients under sub-Weibull randomness. We study a distributed composite stochastic mirror descent scheme with sub-Weibull gradient noise (DCSMD-SW) for solving a convex distributed composite optimization (DCO) problem over the time-varying multi-agent network. By investigating sub-Weibull randomness in DCSMD for the first time, we show that the algorithm is applicable in some common heavier-tailed noise environments while also guaranteeing good convergence properties. We comprehensively study the convergence performance of DCSMD-SW. Satisfactory high-probability convergence rates are derived for DCSMD-SW without any smoothness requirement. The work also offers a unified analytical framework for several critical cases of both algorithms and noise environments.

title: Efficient Multimodal Serving via Module Multiplexing
authors: Zicong Hong, Yuyan Chen, Haoyue Zhang, Peng Li, Wuhui Chen, Song Guo, Xiaowei Shen
publish: Proceedings of the 21st European Conference on Computer Systems

title: Boundary-Aware Attention with Dual-Stream Frequency Fusion for Medical Image Segmentation
authors: Mingjun Geng, Runmei Hu, Xiangpeng Bi, Zhiqiang Wei, Pin Sun, Shugang Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Medical image segmentation remains challenging due to inherent characteristics such as low contrast, blurred boundaries, and complex anatomical structures. While state space models, particularly Mamba, have demonstrated remarkable capabilities in capturing long-range dependencies with linear computational complexity, existing Mamba-based approaches for medical segmentation still struggle with precise boundary delineation and effective local-global feature fusion. To address these limitations, we propose BDFNet, a unified architecture that integrates Boundary-Aware Attention (BAA) and Dual-Stream Frequency Attention Module (DFAM) within the Mamba framework. The BAA module explicitly enhances boundary information through multi-branch attention mechanisms and provides auxiliary boundary supervision signals. The DFAM module employs frequency domain analysis to effectively fuse local boundary features with global semantic representations through com plementary attention weighting. Comprehensive experiments on ACDC cardiac segmentation, Synapse multi organ segmentation, and ISIC skin lesion datasets validate the effectiveness of our approach, with notable improvements in both segmentation accuracy and boundary precision. Experimental results show that our method achieves superior performance compared to existing approaches across cardiac, abdominal organ, and skin lesion segmentation benchmarks. Ourcodeispublicly available at https://github.com/apellidole/Mamba-BDF

title: Wide Axial Ratio Scanning Circularly Polarized Arrays Using Spiral Sequential Rotation Technique
authors: Zhanyu Kang, Lianxing He, Chengxin Liu, Guang Liang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This paper introduces a wide 3-dB axial ratio (AR) scanning circularly polarized (CP) antenna array using spiral sequential rotation (SSR) technique. Firstly, a single-layer center fed microstrip antenna with wide AR beamwidth (ARBW) and symmetrical radiation patterns is designed as the radiating element. The radiation element is composed of a circular patch with a O-slot and multiple sectors, which can significantly improve the symmetry of the radiation patterns. And four parasitic units, consists of a L-shaped parasitic branch and a vertical metal via, are added to broaden the 3-dB ARBW. The measured results show that the element has an extremely low profile of 0.04λ and wide 3 dB AR beamwidth of more than 195°. Additionally, the maximum gain difference between E- and H-planes within the elevation region of θ = ±60° is below 0.2 dB. Secondly, an array based on SSR subarrays with subarray-level sequential rotation (ASSR-S) using the proposed elements is presented, which can effectively overcome the problem of high XPL and result in significant improvement of AR beam-scanning. One prototype of an 8×8 ASSR-S has been fabricated and the measured results demonstrate that the proposed ASSR-S achieves a wide 3-dB AR scanning angle of ±55° with low cross-polarization level, particularly in the D-plane. Moreover, owing to the symmetrical radiation patterns of the proposed element, the proposed ASSR-S maintains a minimal boresight gain variation within 0.8 dB among E-, D- and H-planes at a beam steering angle of 55°. All the merits of wide 3-dB AR scanning angle, low XPL, ultra-low profile, uniform radiation patterns and the regular subarray arrangement with center-fed technique make our proposed ASSR-S be highly attractive candidate for satellite applications.

title: Toward Automated Circuit Schematic Design for Power Electronics: A Hierarchical Agent for Component Prediction
authors: Bowen Su, Huan Chen, Jiahua Ying, Ran Ou, Zengyang Liu, Kai Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Power electronic equipment is a cornerstone of renewable energy generation systems. However, the current design process of power electronics remains highly dependent on engineers’ expertise, making it labor-intensive and unable to meet the rapidly growing application demands. This article aims to automate one of the most time-consuming stages in power electronics design—the circuit schematic design process. By representing schematic components as nodes, the circuit design task is reformulated as a graph node prediction problem. To address the challenges of over-smoothing caused by the uneven distribution of circuit components and the inability of large language models (LLMs) to directly process graph-structured schematic data, we propose a hierarchical agent for power electronic schematic design. This agent integrates the graph processing capabilities of graph learning models with the powerful inference abilities of LLMs. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is validated through a buck converter case study and extensive comparisons between the trained graph learning module and baseline models, including comprehensive ablation studies. Experimental results demonstrate an improvement of up to 28.5% in TOP-5 accuracy over the baseline. This work represents a preliminary step toward fully automated power electronics design and lays the foundation for future research and development in this field.

title: Decarbonizing Manufacturing through Automation: The Role of Industrial Robots in Emission Reduction Strategies
authors: Ye Pan, Shenggang Ren, Shuya Rao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management
abstract: The rapid proliferation of industrial robots is profoundly reshaping production processes and engineering management models in the manufacturing sector. However, existing literature provides limited insight into how the use of industrial robots affects carbon emissions in manufacturing firms. This study leverages a unique firm-level dataset containing granular energy consumption and emissions information to assess the impact of industrial robot adoption on carbon emissions in the manufacturing firms. Our empirical results indicate that a 1% increase in industrial robot exposure is associated with a 0.258% reduction in firm-level carbon emissions, which corresponds to a reduction of approximately 506 tons of carbon emissions per manufacturing firm per year. Calculated at the latest EU carbon price, this reduction yields roughly 48,000 USD in annual carbon cost savings for per firm. This result is robust to a range of checks, including instrumental variable tests, alternative variable specifications, varying clustering levels for standard errors, and different data cleaning procedures. Mechanism analyses suggest that robots improve energy efficiency, lower overall energy consumption, and facilitate a transition from fossil fuels to cleaner electricity sources. Additionally, the emission-reducing effects are more substantial in regions characterized by stable electricity supply, higher degrees of digitalization, and in industries with greater technological intensity. Our findings respond to concerns that industrial robots may trigger a “Jevons Paradox”, emphasizing the potential of industrial automation to promote low-carbon manufacturing and offer policy-relevant insights for advancing sustainable industrial development

title: DLA-HybridNet: Governance-Aware Edge Segmentation via Hybrid Aggregation Distillation
authors: Soma Dasgupta, Swarnava Dey, Arijit Mukherjee, Arpan Pal
publish: Proceedings of the 13th ACM IKDD International Conference on Data Science

title: Brain Network Sparsification for Energy-Efficient Motor Learning: Insights From a Driving Simulator Study
authors: Jiajia Li, Xin Liu, Jian Song
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The human motor-learning system rapidly optimizes brain networks into energy-efficient patterns to enhance motor execution in novel environments—a capability pivotal for advancing robotic adaptability in human-machine interaction (HMI). However, the specific energy-efficient neural patterns underlying this optimization remain unelucidated. Inspired by the self-organization and energy efficiency of biological neural networks, this study investigates brain network optimization mechanisms in low-sample repetitive motor skill acquisition. A high visual-motor coordination task (urban driving simulator) revealed consistent cognitive performance improvements that peaked with repeated practice. Dynamic high-order brain connectivity analysis identified three critical findings with direct relevance to HMI: first, brain connectivity undergoes sparsification, characterized by a transition from bilateral to unilateral parietal activation that forms an energy-saving neural pattern for motor control; second, network modularity is enhanced, which correlates with stabilized motor execution accuracy; third, the visual network exhibits progressive reduction in reliance on real-time visual input, which demonstrates an energy-efficient motor learning leveraging prior visual memory and holds critical value for lowering “perceptual load” in robotic systems. These results provide empirical evidence for brain network simplification during repetitive motor practice, and offer actionable guidance for designing energy-efficient, adaptive neural networks in robots navigating unfamiliar environments, thereby advancing HMI performance and robotic adaptability.

title: Research on Multi-Source Track Association Algorithm for Maritime Targets Based on Semi-Supervised Representation Learning
authors: Sitang Ren, Chunfeng Hou
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Addressing the problems of high cost of acquiring multi-source track annotation data in maritime surveillance scenarios, reliance on large numbers of labeled samples and prior motion models in traditional track association methods, insufficient alignment accuracy of heterogeneous tracks, and difficulty in integrating fragmented tracks, this paper proposes a multi-source track association algorithm for maritime targets based on semi-supervised representation learning. The algorithm first constructs a contrastive learning framework integrating instance-level and temporal consistency constraints to achieve unsupervised discriminative representation of multi-source heterogeneous tracks. Then, a Support Vector Machine (SVM) classification layer is introduced, and a semi-supervised optimization objective combining contrastive loss and hinge loss is designed. This allows for end-to-end fine-tuning with only a small number of labeled samples, achieving target-level alignment of large-scale unlabeled tracks. Finally, secondary association and complete trajectory reconstruction of track segments with the same target are completed based on temporal semantic knowledge. Five-fold cross-validation results show that the proposed algorithm achieves an average track completion accuracy of 87.97% and an average position deviation of 9.26 km in scenarios with a track data missing rate exceeding $40 \%$, significantly outperforming traditional correlation algorithms. This effectively reduces the reliance on high-quality labeled data and provides reliable technical support for maritime situational awareness.

title: IG-StoryDeep: A Multimodal Collaborative Storytelling System Powered by DeepSeek for Enhancing Intergenerational Bonds in Chinese Colocated Care Facilities
authors: Lihong Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: China’s co-located kindergarten-nursing home facilities are expanding rapidly to tackle childcare shortages and elderly isolation. However, large gaps in language, cognitive, and expressive abilities between preschoolers (3–6 years) and seniors (65+, often with mild cognitive impairment) restrict meaningful interaction. This study presents IG-StoryDeep, a DeepSeek-V3-powered real-time multimodal system that co-creates stories and illustrations from children’s drawings and elders’ prompts. Key components include (1) a drawing encoder fine-tuned on >50,000 Chinese preschool sketches, (2) a dual-LoRA Intergenerational Consistency Module blending childlike imagination with elderly authenticity, and (3) DeepSeek-V3 paired with Stable Diffusion for narrative and visual generation. A 2024 field study with 126 child–elder dyads across ten facilities showed IG-StoryDeep significantly outperforming baselines in creativity, bonding (IOS scale), satisfaction (adapted SUS), coherence, and engagement (all p < .001, large effect sizes). This is the first validated Chinese-native multimodal LLM for intergenerational shared-care settings. The encoder and consistency module will be open-sourced.

title: Gap Probability of a Singularly Perturbed Gaussian Unitary Ensemble
authors: Jiwei Zhang, Shulin Lyu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: We study the Hankel determinant $D_{n}(a, t)$ generated by a singularly perturbed Gaussian weight with two jump discontinuities \begin{equation*}\mathrm{e}^{-x^{2}-\frac{t}{x^{2}}}\left(A+B \theta\left(x^{2}-a^{2}\right)\right), \quad x \in(-\infty,+\infty), t, a \geq 0,\end{equation*}where $\theta(\cdot)$ is the Heaviside step function, $A \geq 0$ and $A+B \geq 0$. Using the ladder operators for the associated monic orthogonal polynomials and three supplementary conditions, we express the recurrence coefficient in terms of the auxiliary variables which satisfy fourth-order difference equations. In addition, we consider the asymptotic behavior of $D_{n}(a, t)$ with $A=1, B=0$ under a double scaling where $n \rightarrow \infty, a \rightarrow 0^{+}, t \rightarrow 0^{+}$such that $S:=4 n a^{2}$ and $T:=(2 n+1+\alpha) t$ are fixed. Based on the results in prior literature, we deduce the asymptotic expansions for the scaled $D_{n}(a, t)$. The asymptotics of the recurrence coefficient, $D_{n}(a, t)$ with $A=1, B=0$ and its logarithmic derivative when $t \rightarrow \infty$ are also established.

title: Research on Transient Overload of Transmission Sections Under the N-1 Principle
authors: Yuanyuan Xu, Xiaoxiao Zhao, Teng Chi, Chengzhi Li, Haoran Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Combined with production practice, this paper first gives an overview of the theory of transient overload of transmission sections. Subsequently, it focuses on the transmission section overload accident caused by the phase-to-phase permanent fault (N-1) at Tower #21 of the 220kV Chongsan I Line. It elaborates in detail on the operation mode of the power grid (including the main wiring of Chongwen Substation and Sanqing Substation as well as the regional system) before the fault, sorts out the protection actions and circuit breaker tripping information of the substations on both sides when the fault occurred, and clarifies the core handling points such as adjusting power plant output, changing operation mode, and handling overload. The complete process of information collection, fault diagnosis, overload disposal, onsite verification, and isolation and maintenance of faulty equipment is presented step by step, providing practical reference for the disposal of similar N-1 line faults and section overloads in power grids.

title: Dominance Scoring Method Using Multi-layer Graph Convolutional Neural Network and Geometric Formation Features
authors: Shunsuke Takagi, Yohei Nakada
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: The collection and utilization of player and ball tracking data have advanced in various team sports, including soccer. Particularly, geometric formation analysis methods using players’ dominant areas and their adjacency relationships have received increasing attention. Additionally, the use of multi-layer neural networks for data analysis in team sports has grown rapidly. Against this backdrop, previous studies have proposed a new formation analysis method that combines a multi-layer graph convolutional neural network with geometric formation features extracted from tracking data. Its effectiveness has also been confirmed through its application to a classification problem of soccer shooting scenes. Building upon the previous studies, the present study proposes a dominance scoring method for soccer based on the formation analysis method. Specifically, by extending the shooting scene classification problem previously addressed into an ordinal classification problem, this method trains a multi-layer graph convolutional neural network to calculate dominance scores from input geometric formation features. Furthermore, the present study conducts an initial experiment using tracking data collected from actual professional soccer matches, and evaluates the effectiveness of the proposed method through rank correlation analysis of the experimental results.

title: Pattern Recognition of GIS Metallic Tip Defect by Fusing Time-Frequency Features from Phase-Resolved Photon Counting
authors: Shu Niu, Hao Liang, Zhuo Wang, Haohe Lu, Houming Shen, Xianhao Fan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Accurate identification of discharge states triggered by metallic tip defects in Gas insulated switchgear (GIS) is crucial for insulation assessment. To address the issues that traditional electrical signal detection methods are susceptible to interference and existing photon counting data analyses are limited in dimensionality, this paper proposes an unsupervised clustering identification method based on multi-dimensional feature fusion. Building upon phase-resolved photon counting (PRPC) data, this method systematically constructs fused features from three dimensions-statistical properties, phase distribution morphology, and frequency-domain characteristics-to build a highly discriminative feature space. Subsequently, the t-distributed stochastic neighbor embedding (t-SNE) algorithm is employed for visual dimensionality reduction, and K -means clustering is utilized to achieve automatic partitioning of discharge states. The results demonstrate that this method successfully and unsupervisedly divides the discharge process into three distinct stages, revealing the intrinsic patterns of discharge state evolution with increasing voltage. This study provides a novel datadriven approach, requiring no prior labels, for the intelligent diagnosis of GIS insulation conditions based on optical techniques.

title: Interoperability Challenges in Wearable Iot for Healthcare With Federated Learning: A Review
authors: Adil Akhmetov, Meraryslan Meraliyev, Mohammed Alaa Ala'anzy
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: The Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) has the potential to change the healthcare industry with the help of sensing technologies. However, the increasing number of heterogeneous devices creates critical data silos due to the use of proprietary data formats, such as Apple Health and Fitbit. These data formats are not compatible with standardized electronic health record (EHR) systems. Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources (FHIR) and Federated Learning (FL) have the potential to bridge the gap between the incompatibilities of data formats while maintaining the privacy of the patient. This systematic review has included the recent literature from the year 2020 to 2025. New findings on the integration of FL and FHIR have also been included in this systematic review. A novel FedFHIR architecture has been introduced with the help of on-device converters that standardize raw data before FL processing. This helps in overcoming the problems of interoperability, governance, and edge resource constraints. The regional issues faced by people living in rural and Central Asia have also been addressed in this systematic review. Semantic interoperability is a prerequisite for the scaling of privacy-preserving artificial intelligence (AI) in a distributed healthcare system.

title: Research on Unified Speech Generation Method Based on Prompt Learning
authors: Wenjia Jiang, Zhuang Bai
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: In recent years, Prompt Learning has demonstrated significant effectiveness in the field of Natural Language Processing (NLP). Its core idea is to guide pre-trained large models to complete specific downstream tasks by designing appropriate prompts. This paradigm is increasingly being adopted in the speech processing domain, particularly in speech generation tasks. This paper systematically explores the practical value and inherent challenges of applying Prompt Learning to speech generation tasks, emphasizing its crucial role in improving the quality of speech generation and enhancing task adaptability. Based on an analysis of the characteristics and requirements of speech generation tasks, we propose a Prompt-based speech generation framework that effectively integrates both textual and speech information, enabling a unified approach to multiple speech generation tasks. The results demonstrate that Prompt Learning methods offer significant advantages in tasks such as speech translation and speech restoration, improving not only the naturalness and accuracy of generated speech but also the system’s flexibility and controllability. Furthermore, this paper discusses the challenges currently faced by the technology and provides an outlook on future research directions, offering valuable theoretical insights for the further development of speech generation technology.

title: A Geometric Abstention Mechanism for Language Models
authors: C. M. Wyss
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Due to the nature of their architecture, Large Language Models (LLMs) typically produce responses even when no coherent answer exists. In this paper, we introduce a principled, geometric mechanism that enables LLMs to abstain in such circumstances. Our approach is grounded in the notion of a Continuous State Machine (CSM), which is a glass-box model of LLM inference dynamics. In particular, a CSM can be instantiated from an ontology so that the geometry of its latent semantic space reflects that ontology. This means, among other things, that input probes trace trajectories through semantic space until either converging to a concept attractor (in-domain) or remaining isolated in null space (out-of-domain), enabling the system to distinguish realizable from non-realizable input.

authors: Mohammed El Amine Sehaba, Xavier Marsault, Serge Miguet
