title: A Study on the Application of Artificial Intelligence in Financial Analysis
authors: Minglei Lv
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: In this paper, a Hybrid Dynamic Risk Engine (HDRE) framework integrating rule engine and machine learning is proposed to solve the problems that traditional financial Analysis methods can’t capture the dynamic fluctuation and nonlinear correlation of the market, and a single algorithm can’t meet the requirements of regulatory interpretability. This framework uses Bayesian networks as the logical skeleton, integrates LightGBM to process static features, and Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) to capture temporal dynamics. It achieves comprehensive evaluation of credit risk and market risk through probability inference, and enforces the use of SHAP tools to ensure model interpretability. The empirical part uses Lending Club loan data and Shanghai and Shenzhen 300 Index data. The comparative analysis shows that in the credit risk assessment, HDRE has an AUC of 0.828 and a KS (Kolmogorov-Smirnov statistic) value of 0.523, which is better than the single LightGBM model, and shows lower performance volatility in the economic downturn; In the market risk VaR prediction, the exception rate of HDRE (1.03%) is the closest to the theoretical value, and the tail risk description is more accurate. The research shows that the hybrid architecture can effectively balance the prediction accuracy and interpretability, enhance the robustness of the model to the change of data distribution, and provide an operational optimization path for financial institutions to build a compliant and dynamic risk assessment system.

title: A Comparative Evaluation of One-Stage and Two-Stage Deep Learning Models Using an Augmented Industrial Dataset
authors: Hossein Malekmohamadi, Xin Lu, Immanuel Ojji, Serhan Cosar
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence

title: Uncertainty Estimates for Support Vector Machines in the Transfer Learning Paradigm
authors: Tameem Adel
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence

title: Enhancing Stellar Distance Estimation with Uncertainty-Weighted Loss Functions in Deep Learning Models
authors: Nicholas Kania, Deborah Busonero, Chiara Contoli, Valerio Freschi, Emanuele Lattanzi
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence

title: Evaluation of Large Language Model’s Cross-Cultural Non-Verbal Behavior Understanding
authors: Zimeng Li, Yuntao Zou, Caichun Cen, Tong Xiao, Pingting Tang, Zihan Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: This study investigates limitations in the cross-cultural non-verbal behavior understanding of large language models (LLM) within embodied virtual agents on Metaverse social platforms. A dedicated high-conflict benchmark, A dedicated high-conflict benchmark, Cross-Cultural Non-Verbal Behavior mini (CC-NVB-mini), comprising 100 rigorously annotated samples, is introduced and made publicly available. Systematic evaluation is conducted on four contemporary models :Gemini 1.5 Flash, Grok-4, ChatGPT-4, DeepSeek-V3, under Zero-Shot, Few-Shot, and Cross-Cultural Prompting (CCP) conditions. In the Zero-Shot setting, the models yield an average score of 5.92, substantially below the human baseline of 7.85. Introduction of the parameter-free, example-free CCP strategy raises the average score to 8.22, exceeding the human reference by 0.37 points, with Grok-4 reaching 8.74. These results indicate that cross-cultural competence is present in latent form within current LLMs, and that performance bottlenecks arise primarily from activation rather than absence of relevant knowledge. The CCP approach offers a lightweight, real-time deployable mechanism for cultural-conflict mitigation in embodied avatars. The work aligns closely with the EI-OAHV 2026 focus on Embodied Intelligence by providing an activation-based engineering solution for cross-cultural non-verbal understanding, supporting safer and more inclusive human-embodied interaction in multicultural virtual-physical environments.

title: Conservative Risk-Sensitive Reinforcement Learning for Reliable Decision-Making under Uncertainty
authors: Yinghao Zhao, Yilin Li, Yingzi Wang, Yunfei Nie, Yixuan Lu, Nuo Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: This paper addresses complex decision-making scenarios characterized by high uncertainty and high-cost errors, researching a risk-sensitive decision-oriented reinforcement learning mining method. It focuses on resolving the reliability issues arising from tail instability in the reward distribution and out-of-distribution actions under offline data conditions. Methodologically, the decision-making process is modeled using a Markov framework, with the reward distribution as the learning object to retain value information under adverse conditions. Based on this, a conditional risk-value metric is introduced to explicitly characterize and suppress tail risk, ensuring that policy optimization no longer relies solely on expected returns. To mitigate estimation bias and over-extrapolation in offline learning, conservative constraints based on behavioral distribution are further incorporated. By limiting the deviation between the policy and the implicit behavioral distribution in the data, out-ofdistribution action expansion is suppressed, and the controllability of policy updates is improved. The overall framework unifies risk measurement and conservative learning into a single optimization form, forming a policy learning mechanism that balances returns and safety. Comparative experimental results show that this method exhibits superior overall performance in terms of average returns, tail reward robustness, and safety-related indicators, validating the effectiveness of the co-modeling of risk-sensitive objectives and conservative constraints, and providing an auditable and adjustable risk control approach for highly reliable intelligent decision-making systems.

title: Dynamic Regularized Stripe Pruning with Latent Structure Search
authors: Chenxi Yao, Zequan Wang, Man Yuan
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Stripe pruning has emerged as a promising structured pruning paradigm, offering a balance between fine-grained compression and hardware efficiency. However, existing methods suffer from uncontrollable sparsity levels, potential layer collapse, and inefficient initialization due to their reliance on static regularization and threshold truncation. To address these limitations, we propose DRSP-LSS, a novel framework for Dynamic Regularized Stripe Pruning with Latent Structure Search. First, we introduce an iterative SynFlow-based budgeting mechanism to rigorously determine layer-wise stripe quotas based on gradient flow conservation. Second, we propose Latent Structure Search (LSS), which identifies a high-quality initial sub-network topology using Straight-Through Estimators (STE) before weight training. Finally, we design a dynamic regularization strategy with periodic re-ranking, allowing the model to rectify pruning decisions during training. Experimental results on CIFAR and ImageNet demonstrate that DRSP-LSS achieves precise sparsity control and superior accuracy compared to existing methods, particularly under extreme sparsity.

title: NetBurst-X: A Burst-Informed Learning Framework for Detecting Microbursts and Data Exfiltration in NetFlow
authors: Mohammed Saad Javeed, Jannatul Maua, M. F. Mridha, Jie Huang, Jungpil Shin
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Real-time anomaly detection is a core requirement in high-speed networks, yet most intrusion detection systems struggle with short-lived microbursts and stealthy data exfiltration, especially when attackers hide within bursty traffic. This paper proposes NetBurst-X, a hybrid burst–flow learning framework that combines burst-aware traffic statistics with temporal deep learning for NetFlow-based security analytics. The framework introduces a Burst Severity Index (BSI) and derives ByteRate and PacketRate features inside dynamic sliding windows, which are then fused with gated recurrent unit (GRU) representations of NetFlow sequences. Experiments on the NF-UNSW-NB15-V2 and UNSW-NB15 datasets show that NetBurst-X outperforms Random Forest, SVM, XGBoost, BiLSTM, and CNN-GRU baselines in precision, recall, F1-score, ROC-AUC, and Matthews correlation coefficient for both microburst detection and data exfiltration classification. On a combined microburst–exfiltration traffic mix, NetBurst-X achieves an F1-score of 0.950, a ROC-AUC of 0.973, and an MCC of 0.938, with F1-scores of 0.963 for microburst detection and 0.923 for exfiltration alone. Streaming evaluations further indicate low inference latency (27.4 ms per batch) and high throughput (16 920 flows/s), demonstrating that the framework is suitable for deployment in practical high-speed network environments. These results highlight the benefit of combining burst-aware traffic indicators with temporal flow modeling to improve real-time intrusion detection.

title: Dual Flattening of Radial Force and Torque Waveforms in Switched Reluctance Motors
authors: Fares S. El-Faouri, Shou Qiu, Yifei Cai, Chenyang Lyu, Kyohei Kiyota, Wejdan Abu Elhaija, Akira Chiba
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Industry Applications
abstract: This article presents a novel current-waveform shaping method for simultaneously eliminating radial-force fluctuation and torque ripple in switched reluctance motors. Unlike existing approaches that address these two issues separately, the proposed method unifies both objectives. The proposed current profile not only smooths the torque waveform but also flattens the radial-force sum, leading to a significant reduction in vibration. The proposed method is compared with the conventional square-wave current and with strategies dedicated solely to either vibration reduction or torque ripple minimization. Experimental validation and finite-element analysis are conducted to assess performance in terms of current, torque, radial force, and stator vibration.

title: Research on E-commerce Recommendation Algorithm Based on DistilBERT and EfficientNet Multimodal Fusion Network
authors: Jia Li, Lirong Li, Bi Ye, Heung Kou
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: In the rapidly evolving digital economy, ecommerce platforms face the dual challenge of information overload and the cold-start problem for new products. Traditional recommendation systems, predominantly based on Collaborative Filtering (CF) and Matrix Factorization (MF), rely heavily on historical interaction data, rendering them ineffective for sparse datasets and emerging items that lack user feedback. Furthermore, these conventional methods often treat items as abstract identifiers, neglecting the rich semantic information embedded in multimodal content such as product descriptions and visual imagery. To address these limitations, this research proposes a novel, high-performance recommendation architecture: the Multimodal Fusion Network (MMFN). This framework synergistically integrates DistilBERT for lightweight, semantic-rich textual feature extraction and EfficientNet for high-fidelity visual representation learning. By employing a sophisticated gated co-attention mechanism, the MMFN dynamically aligns and fuses heterogeneous modalities to construct robust item embeddings. These content-based embeddings are subsequently integrated into a PinSAGE graph neural network, which leverages efficient random-walk sampling to capture high-order structural connectivity within massive user-item interaction graphs. The proposed system effectively bridges the semantic gap between visual and textual data while simultaneously mitigating the cold-start problem through inductive learning. Extensive experiments conducted on largescale e-commerce datasets demonstrate that the MMFN-based approach significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, including Neural Collaborative Filtering (NCF) and LightGCN, particularly in scenarios involving new item induction and sparse user behaviors. The framework not only enhances recommendation accuracy metrics such as NDCG and Recall but also optimizes computational efficiency, making it viable for realtime industrial deployment.

title: A Data Cleaning Method for Enhancing BeiDou Positioning Quality
authors: Mingxia Fan, Lijun Zhang, Li Zheng
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: To address issues such as multipath errors, missing values, and abnormal values in BeiDou Navigation Satellite System (BDS) data, this paper proposes a comprehensive data-cleaning framework.The framework sequentially addresses multipath errors, data gaps, and outliers through integrated mitigation, adaptive imputation, and intelligent detection modules.Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly improves data quality, enhances the precision of Precise Point Positioning (PPP) in the E, N, and U directions, and substantially reduces convergence time.

title: Industrial Internet Robot Collaboration System and Edge Computing Optimization
authors: Haopeng Zhao, Dajun Tao, Tian Qi, Jingyuan Xu, Zijie Zhou, Lipeng Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: In industrial-Internet environments, mobile robots must generate collision-free global routes under stochastic obstacle layouts and random perturbations in commanded linear and angular velocities. This paper models a differential-drive robot with nonholonomic constraints, then decomposes motion into obstacle avoidance, target turning, and target approaching behaviors to parameterize the control variables. Global path planning is formulated as a constrained optimization problem and converted into a weighted energy function that balances path length and collision penalties. A three-layer neural network represents the planning model, while simulated annealing searches for near-global minima and mitigates local traps. During execution, a fuzzy controller uses heading and lateral-offset errors to output wheel-speed differentials for rapid correction; edge-side computation is discussed to reduce robot-server traffic and latency. Matlab 2024 simulations report deviation within $\pm 5 \mathrm{~cm}$, convergence within 10 ms, and shorter paths than two baseline methods. The approach improves robustness of global navigation in practice.

title: AdaCMoE: An Adaptive Conditional MoE Fusion Framework for Multi-Channel Recall
authors: Xinhua Dong, Juan Mao, Zhigang Xu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: The matching phase in recommender systems aims to efficiently retrieve potential items from massive candidates. Multi-channel recall is widely adopted in industry, but adaptively fusing heterogeneous results remains challenging, as heuristic fusion lacks personalization, and mixture-of-experts models are rarely explored for this task. This paper proposes AdaCMoE, an adaptive multi-channel recall fusion framework. Its core is a conditional gated mixture-of-experts network that dynamically generates fusion weights using user features and real-time recall context. We further design an adaptive weighting mechanism that takes static weights as prior and performs constrained adjustments to balance personalization and stability. Experiments on three public datasets show that AdaCMoE outperforms all baselines on Recall@20 and NDCG@20. Ablation studies verify the effectiveness of each component. This work provides a reliable solution for building robust industrial recall systems.

title: A Study on the Construction of a Temporal Knowledge Graph Driven by Multi-Source Heterogeneous Data Fusion for Traditional Chinese Medicine Pharmaceutical Manufacturing
authors: Siqi Chou, Bo Zhu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: To address the challenges in traditional Chinese medicine (TCM) pharmaceutical manufacturing, including diverse data sources, strong structural heterogeneity, difficulty in unified representation of dynamic process knowledge, and limited capability for deviation traceability, this study proposes a temporal knowledge graph construction method driven by multisource heterogeneous data fusion. Based on a quadruple modeling framework ($s, r, o, t$) consisting of head entity, relation, tail entity, and timestamp, key elements throughout the production lifecycle of TCM granules-such as raw material batch information, process parameters, equipment status, and quality inspection indicators-are represented in a unified temporal structure to enable structured organization of process knowledge along the time dimension. A top-down ontology construction approach is adopted, integrating GMP regulations, industry standards, and expert knowledge to define conceptual schemas and semantic relationships. For heterogeneous data sources, including structured databases and semi-structured batch records (JSON), data preprocessing and semantic alignment strategies are designed to accomplish entity extraction, relation identification, time normalization, and knowledge fusion. The temporal knowledge graph is implemented and dynamically updated using the Neo4j graph database. A case study on an industrial-scale TCM granule production line demonstrates that the proposed method effectively integrates multi-source production data, captures dynamic process correlations, and supports deviation traceability and causal path analysis, thereby providing technical support for intelligent manufacturing and quality risk control in TCM pharmaceutical production.

title: Result on the Riemann Integral of Single-variable Discontinuous Functions
authors: Dongsheng Luo, Rui Zhang, Peiyong Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: In this paper, we study the Riemann integrability of univariate discontinuous functions, a topic with significant implications for applied disciplines including biology, physics, and medicine. We first establish necessary and sufficient conditions for the Riemann integrability of certain classes of discontinuous functions. Subsequently, we develop integration methods that extend beyond the classical Newton - Leibniz framework. Specifically, we derive explicit integral formulas for functions that, despite lacking continuity, exhibit particular structural regularities. Furthermore, we compute the Riemann integrals of several specific discontinuous functions that are not amenable to direct evaluation via the Newton - Leibniz theorem, thereby illustrating alternative analytical techniques.

title: Can Computers Understand Banter? Building and evaluating classifiers and Large Language Models on banter detection and generation
authors: Kinga Dobolyi, Sophia Tamburrino, Zoe Borden, Jess Faruque
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Although various types of humor have been previously studied in natural language processing, banter detection and generation have yet to be explored. In this work, we first examined whether large language models (LLMs)–including both BERT- and GPT-style models–can reliably detect banter found in dialogue pairs mined from fictional novels. This task involved a novel benchmark, with 4,475 hand-annotated samples of banter and non-banter used for model training and evaluation. Overall, GPT-style models were more successful than BERT-style models at detecting banter in dialogue. In our second task, we evaluated whether the best GPT-style models could generate banter, finding that these models were reliably able to generate banter $50-97 \%$ of the time, but rarely produced dialogue that humans found humorous.

title: Learning Complex Robotic Manipulation Tasks Using Neural Networks: A Dynamical Systems Approach
authors: Jian Zhang, Shaoliang Zhang, Chenguang Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Mastering complex manipulation tasks remains a major challenge in the field of robotics. Although significant progress has been made in learning long-term manipulation tasks for robots, most methods generally lack rigorous mathematical stability proofs, resulting in unguaranteed system stability. This article proposes a neural network-based method for learning complex robotic manipulation tasks from demonstrations. First, the demonstration data is discretized into multiple sub-demonstration trajectories, and sub-goal points are identified within each segment. Next, the demonstration trajectory is modeled as an autonomous dynamic system, and a neural network-based learning dynamic system algorithm is introduced, balancing fitting accuracy with global system stability. Then, incorporating an attention mechanism into the network enhances system stability while improving the accuracy of trajectory reproduction. Subsequently, a high-level controller is developed to execute the entire task sequence, ensuring the successful completion of tasks. Finally, the effectiveness and reliability of the proposed method are validated through simulations and real-robot experiments.

title: Modeling and Analysis of Two-Degree-of-Freedom Flux-Switching Permanent Magnet Machine Based on 3-D Field Modulation Method
authors: Peixin Wang, Jikai Si, Rui Nie, Shuai Xu, Yanqi Wei, Zhiheng Zhang, Wei Hua
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: To quickly calculate the performance of the two-degree-of-freedom flux-switching permanent magnet (2DoFFSPM) machine, a three-dimensional (3-D) field modulation method is proposed for the construction of feasible 3-D analytical models (AMs). First, the 3-D radial, tangential, and axial modulation functions of the stator and mover modulators are derived, respectively. Second, the 3-D radial, tangential, and axial permanent magnet (PM) magnetic fields of the machine are modeled based on 3-D initial PM magnetomotive force (MMF) and modulation functions. Then, the open-circuit performances of the 2DoFFSPM machine, including the cogging torque/force and back electromotive forces (EMF), are established. Furthermore, based on the AMs and 3-D finite element method (FEM), the PM magnetic fields, cogging torque/force, and back-EMFs at different motion forms are predicted and analyzed. Finally, a module and a prototype of the 2DoFFSPM machine are manufactured to test the rotary PM magnetic fields, cogging torque/force, and back-EMFs, verifying the results of the 3-D FEM and AMs.

title: Overflow-Driven Dynamic Precision Scaling Fixed-Point Multiply-Accumulator Unit
authors: Andrea Fasolino, Rosalba Liguori, Luigi Di Benedetto, Alfredo Rubino, Gian Domenico Licciardo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: A novel full-hardware multiply-accumulate (MAC) unit capable of dynamic precision scaling (DPS) and specifically designed for embedded applications is introduced. The MAC autonomously detects and mitigates on-chip overflow and over-representation and eliminates the need for any external software controller. A compact run-time monitoring unit (RMU), within each MAC, dynamically monitors the transitions of carry-out and sign bit, and adjusts the operand representation at the bit level so that any rounding error remains bounded by <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2^{-(N_{\text {in}}-1)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. Bit-sliced input partitioning enables run-time reconfigurability of operand width and accumulation depth without altering the logic topology. Prototyped on a Xilinx Artix-7 FPGA, the proposed unit achieves up to 14% lower dynamic power and 15% higher maximum clock frequency than a conventional fixed-width MAC with the same precision; in a Skywater CMOS 130 nm, it occupies <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3.9 \times 10^{3}~\mu \text {m}^{2}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, reaches a critical-path delay of 2.68 ns, and consumes <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$6.07~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>W/MHz.

title: VLAD-Grasp: Vision-Language Adaptive Depth Grasping for Part-Specific Manipulation_supp1-3686670.mp4
authors: Yanli Zou
publish: N/A
abstract: In household service robot grasping tasks, precisely identifying and grasping specific object parts are essential for ensuring safety and execution efficiency. Although existing Vision-Language Models (VLMs) support open-vocabulary understanding, they still struggle with fine-grained semantic instructions such as “grasp the handle of a cup” or “hold the blade when passing a knife.” They also often fail to identify the correct object when multiple identical items appear in the scene. Conventional 6-DoF grasping approaches also suffer from unstable grasp depth and high collision risk. To overcome these challenges, we construct a new vision-language dataset, OVPGrasping, tailored for fine-grained, task-oriented grasping in household scenarios. We further propose two key modules: a Bidirectional Cross-Modal Enhancement Module (BCEM) that enables mutual enhancement between image and text features, and a Gated Cross-Modal Fusion (GCMF) module that selectively reinforces decoder queries with object-level semantic embeddings. In addition, we design an adaptive 6-DoF depth-aware grasping strategy that improves grasp stability and reduces collision probability. On the OVPGrasping dataset, our method improves detection accuracy by 1.67% over the perception Baseline. In real-robot experiments, the single-object success rate increases from 70.83% to 78.33%, and the cluttered-scene success rate rises from 64% to 72%, validating the effectiveness of our fine-grained semantic understanding and adaptive grasping strategy.

title: SSCTAD: Semi-Supervised Contrastive Learning for Trajectory Anomaly Detection
authors: Jiaqi Zhang, Zipei Fan, Xuan Song, Ryosuke Shibasaki
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Trajectory anomaly detection is critical for transportation safety, solving traffic congestion, optimizing routes, and other smart city applications. Existing methods often misclassify reasonable but non-standard trajectories, such as route changes due to traffic congestion, as anomalies. Additionally, many methods require extensive labeled datasets for training, however, in real-world scenarios, trajectory anomaly labeled data are often scarce and costly to obtain. To address the challenges, we investigate a Semi-Supervised Contrastive learning approach for Trajectory Anomaly Detection, namely SSCTAD. Specifically, SSCTAD integrates a transformer based method to process trajectories and utilizes the in-out flow encoder to capture the spatial-temporal patterns from the trajectories, which can help the model capture the traffic conditions. Then, by leveraging a small subset of labeled data along with extensive unlabeled data, SSCTAD utilizes a semi-supervised contrastive learning component enables the model to better distinguish normal and anomalous trajectories by maximizing the similarity of latent representation from the same class and diversifying those from different classes. This method improves the performance of trajectory anomaly detection and effectively reduces the dependence of the model on the labeled dataset. Finally, experiments show that SSCTAD outperforms the other baseline models.

title: Guided Diffusion Cross-Domain Recommendation With Bilateral Alignment
authors: Wei Zhao, Jiazhe Zhang, Rui Tang, Xian Mo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Recommendation systems have been widely applied across various domains. However, they have consistently been hindered by the cold-start problem. One of the recent solutions involves extracting knowledge from other domains and applying it to the target domain’s recommendations. This is also one of the issues that cross-domain recommendation (CDR) aims to address. Mapping methods serve as a means to translate data across distinct domains, thereby boosting the performance of CDR. Meanwhile, diffusion probabilistic models can also be regarded as a superior data transformation model. They have achieved significant breakthroughs in image synthesis tasks, capable of recovering images from noise-added samples. To further improve the recommendation performance of CDR, we propose a novel CDR diffusion model [guided diffusion with bilateral alignment cross-domain recommendation (GDBACDR)]. First, a diffusion model (DM) is designed to guide the feature vectors of target domain users in the source and target domains. Simultaneously, a bilateral alignment module between the source and target domains is utilized to enhance the stability introduced by the DM, mitigate the negative impacts of randomness, and ensure the consistency of the reconstructed user embeddings with the target domain. The loss function of GDBACDR is formulated to achieve specific tasks. Finally, extensive experiments were conducted on existing datasets, and the results showed that the proposed method outperformed baseline models in every CDR task under the cold-start scenarios, demonstrating the effectiveness and adaptability of the proposed method.

title: GOSPA and T-GOSPA quasi-metrics for evaluation of multi-object tracking algorithms_supp1-3686336.pdf

publish: N/A

title: A Beam-Squint Suppression Method for an Electronically Beam-Scanning Leaky-Wave Antenna
authors: Yudong Zhang, Zheng Li, Meie Chen, Junhong Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper presents a beam-squint suppression method for an electronically beam-scanning leaky-wave antenna (LWA). The method utilizes a 66-element low-frequency slot array (LFSA) and a 72-element high-frequency slot array (HFSA). The two slot arrays simultaneously radiate to suppress the beam squint. A phase adjustment method is implemented by introducing two non-radiation sections and a radiation section, enabling the combination of the generated beams. A prototype is fabricated and measured, showing good agreement between the simulated and measured results. The proposed LWA achieves a beam-scanning range of 89° in the operating band from 14.8 GHz to 16.0 GHz (7.8% fractional bandwidth), while maintaining a relatively high gain (10.8dBi to 15.5dBi). Additionally, the minimal beam squint is only 2.4°, and the desired gains (gain at the desired angle) are above 10.1dBi. The fluctuation of the desired gain ranges from 1.4dB to 3.4dB, indicating stable and high desired gain performance. These results demonstrate the stable beam-scanning capabilities of the proposed LWA with suppressed beam squint.

title: Multistability of Self-Attention Dynamics in Transformers
authors: Claudio Altafini
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: In machine learning, a self-attention dynamics is a continuous-time multiagent-like model of the attention mechanisms of transformers. In this paper we show that such dynamics is related to a multiagent version of the Oja flow, a dynamical system that computes the principal eigenvector of a matrix corresponding for transformers to the value matrix. We classify the equilibria of the “single head” self-attention system into four classes: consensus, bipartite consensus, clustering and polygonal equilibria. Multiple asymptotically stable equilibria from the first three classes often coexist in the self-attention dynamics. Interestingly, equilibria from the first two classes are always aligned with the eigenvectors of the value matrix, often but not exclusively with the principal eigenvector

title: Miniaturized Pattern Reconfigurable SPP Antenna with Full Coverage in the Azimuth Plane
authors: Zhan Wang, Zetao Huang, Yuandan Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: A novel miniaturized pattern reconfigurable surface plasmon polariton (SPP) antenna with full 360° radiation coverage in the azimuth plane is proposed. The proposed design consists of an SPP-loaded patch and a multi-mode switchable feeding network. To reduce the sizes, the SPP media is introduced, and a small SPP-loaded patch is explored due to the slow-wave features. More importantly, a novel multi-mode reconfiguration method is developed for 360° beam-steering designs. Using different feeding probes enables the SPP-loaded patch to excite a pair of orthogonal modes, namely, a TM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">10</sub>/TM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">01</sub> mode with a broadside pattern (Odd mode) and an omnidirectional monopole-like mode (Even mode). The synthesis of the even mode and the odd mode (Aperture-shared even-odd mode system) allows for the control of the main-lobe directions and pattern shapes of the SPP-loaded patch. Furthermore, when the feeding path of the multi-mode network is switched, the even-odd modes in different directions are excited. Consequently, these switchable patterns can fully cover the 360° azimuth plane. To validate the presented concept, a pattern reconfigurable SPP antenna with a small size of 0.32 λ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</sub> × 0.32 λ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</sub> × 0.10 λ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</sub> is designed, fabricated, and tested. The fabricated antenna demonstrates a bandwidth of 21.8 % (covering the 2.40 GHz Wi-Fi band well), a nine-beam steering capability, and a peak gain of 6.5 dBi. With the merits of small sizes, simple design, low cost, broadband, and 360° beam-steering functions, the proposed novel SPP antenna is a good candidate for intelligent wireless applications.

title: Adaptive Path Planning for Autonomous Navigation of Reconfigurable Magnetic Microswarms
authors: Siyu Liu, Mingxue Cai, Chenyang Huang, Guoyao Ma, Tiantian Xu
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Microrobot swarms have attracted growing attention due to their ability to adapt to environments during navigation through reconfigurable distributions. However, current microrobot swarms lack the intelligent behaviors to adjust their distributions and motion in response to dynamic environments and target changes. This work proposes a novel adaptive nonlinear model predictive path planning method incorporating distribution adaptation to achieve real-time adjustment of swarm distributions and paths in dynamic environments. First, we develop a criterion to evaluate navigation performance in the presence of obstacles, termed avoidance efficiency. Second, we develop a dynamic distribution planning strategy that adapts distributions based on environmental conditions, leveraging the reconfigurability of the magnetic nanoparticle swarm. Subsequently, we propose an adaptive nonlinear model predictive control-based strategy (A-NMPC) for swarm path planning with real-time distribution adaptation. Finally, magnetic nanoparticle swarms exhibit superior navigation performance in the presence of both static and dynamic obstacles, as well as moving targets. Notably, real-world experiments demonstrate that the proposed A-NMPC method, incorporating distribution adaptation, achieves a 24% reduction in path length and improved adaptability in complex, dynamic environments compared to conventional methods.

title: Observation and Numerical Calculation of Induced Voltage caused by M component on 10 kV Overhead Distribution Line
authors: Li Cai, Changfeng Xu, Fang Li, Jinxin Cao, Jianguo Wang, Mi Zhou, Yadong Fan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Delivery
abstract: This article analyzes the lightning-induced voltage (LIV) waveforms generated by M-components recorded by rocket-triggered lightning experiments and performs numerical calculations to investigate the impact of variations in M-component process parameters on the LIV waveforms. The LIVs were measured on a vertically arranged, 1513-meter-long, 10 kV three-phase overhead distribution line. Based on the occurrence time of the M-component, the characteristics of the current waveform, and the polarity of the induced voltage, the M-components measured can be classified into three Types. In addition, this study employs the method of controlling variables and utilizes the hybrid MTL-PEEC (Multi-Conductor Transmission Line – Partial Element Equivalent Circuit) model to analyze the influence of M-component parameters on the induced voltage. The results indicate that an increase in the current rise time leads to a decrease in the absolute value of the negative peak of the induced voltage at the nearest pole, while the positive peak and polarity reversal time increase. An increase in the current half-width results in a longer polarity reversal time and extended decay time after the positive peak. A higher ground reflection coefficient causes increases in both the negative and positive peaks of the induced voltage. Furthermore, an increase in channel height leads to a greater absolute value of the negative peak, a decrease in the positive peak, and a delay in the time to reach peak voltage.

title: Dual-Population Cooperative Algorithm Integrating an LLM and an Evolutionary Algorithm for AUV Path Planning in Three-Dimensional Marine Environments_supp1-3686625.docx
authors: Zhiyong Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: Existing AUV path planning methods still face limitations in modeling real three-dimensional (3D) maritime search and rescue (SAR) environments, multi-objective evaluation and safety constraint expression, and maintaining convergence stability in complex search spaces. To address these issues, we propose a dual-population cooperative algorithm that integrates a large language model (LLM) with an evolutionary algorithm (EA) for marine SAR tasks, named LLM_EA. Firstly, we integrate real seabed topography and 3D ocean currents to construct a unified navigation environment. A multi-objective evaluation framework is further designed to balance energy consumption, safety risk, and attitude stability. During the evolutionary process, LLM_EA performs parallel co-evolution between an EA population for numerical search and an LLM population for semantic reconstruction. A feasibility assurance and cross-population information exchange mechanism is designed to improve feasibility and efficiency, and a topological diversity preservation mechanism is introduced to mitigate topological collapse and maintain structural diversity. Comparative experiments show that LLM_EA consistently generates feasible trajectories under diverse geographical environments and complex constraints while achieving better overall performance and more stable convergence than multiple representative algorithms. Ablation experiments further confirm the contributions of the topological diversity preservation mechanism and the LLM strategy. This framework offers a reusable solution for efficient and robust AUV path planning in real-world 3D maritime SAR scenarios.

title: Experimental Study on Insulation Discharge Induced by Metal Particles Based on AC-DC Alternating Withstand Voltage Simulation
authors: Yi Yang, YongQi Yao, SanPai Ye, ZhiJun Wang, Yu Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: UHV(Ultra-High Voltage) AC power transmission boasts significant advantages such as large transmission capacity, long distance, low loss, and small land occupation. However, the reliability of its key insulation components needs to be improved. Particularly in recent years, flashover or breakdown accidents of insulation parts have occurred frequently. Even though GIL(Gas-Insulated Transmission line)has passed on-site commissioning tests, flashover still occurs after commissioning. This indicates severe problems including deficiencies in on-site commissioning test methods and unclear flashover fault mechanisms. Taking three-post insulators as an example, this paper introduces the construction of an AC-DC alternating withstand voltage test platform, particle arrangement, and withstand voltage test procedures. Through charge tracing observation of insulation components, the surface charge distribution law of insulation parts is obtained. The research provides a withstand voltage screening reference for the product upgrading and fault reduction of UHV insulation components, as well as experimental methods and references for partial discharge detection of insulation parts.

title: Credit Card Fraud Detection Based on Complex Relationship Modeling and Analysis
authors: Jing Liu, Jia You, Xintian Zou
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Credit card fraud detection typically relies on tabular raw transaction records. Traditional machine learning models achieve respectable performance by leveraging these features, but they often treat transactions as independent instances, thereby overlooking potential dependencies between actions. This limits the models’ robustness against increasingly complex and collaborative fraud patterns. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a complex relationship modeling and analysis based detection framework that transforms raw transaction tables into transaction graphs. Specifically, it treats each transaction record as a node and constructs edges by measuring pairwise similarity across a subset of important features. These features are identified using Shapley-based attribution strategy, enabling edge construction to be guided by information-rich dimensions while reducing noise connections caused by irrelevant features. Furthermore, to address severe class imbalance, the graph SMOTE algorithm is integrated to synthesise minority class nodes. Then, we infer their corresponding edge relationships using the same similarity principle, ensuring topologically consistent oversampling. Extensive experiments on credit card fraud detection benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms robust baseline approaches across all standard evaluation metrics, including precision, recall, F1 score, accuracy, and AUC. The results indicate that explicitly modeling inter-transaction relationships provides an effective and practical solution for robust fraud detection.

title: Research on Spatiotemporal Prediction of Electric Vehicle Charging Load Based on Scene Adaptive Modeling and Combined Algorithm Under the Dual Carbon Goals
authors: Huanhuan Fang, Lu Zhang, Chengcheng Xu, Daifu Fan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the advancement of the “Dual Carbon” strategy, large-scale unordered charging of Electric Vehicles (EVs) poses severe challenges to the safe operation of distribution networks. Taking the jurisdiction of State Grid Shanghai Fengxian Power Supply Company as the research object, this study constructs a refined model for the spatiotemporal characteristics of charging load, integrating scene-adaptive probabilistic modeling and Monte Carlo simulation. This model systematically analyzes the temporal sequence and spatial distribution laws of charging load under different scenarios. Furthermore, a combined prediction model (GM(1,1)-Markov Chain (MC)-LSTM) is proposed to achieve long-term high-precision prediction of charging load. The results show that charging load exhibits significant scene heterogeneity: the residential scene presents a single nighttime peak, the commercial scene shows double peaks, and the public transport scene features concentrated nighttime charging. The Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of the combined prediction model is as low as 3.2%, outperforming single prediction models. This research provides a scientific basis for distribution network planning under high EV penetration, with important academic value and engineering significance.

title: Cross-lingual Emotion Recognition in Speech Based on Bidirectional LSTM
authors: Bo Zhu, Lu Cao, Zhanbo Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: This paper proposes the DSM-BiLSTM framework for cross-lingual speech emotion recognition through dynamic segment MFCC sampling and bidirectional LSTM. The approach extracts 180-dimensional dynamic MFCC features with their derivatives, applies three-layer BiLSTM after linear interpolation and normalization to capture temporal dependencies, and incorporates dynamic class weights to address data imbalance. Single-language UAR reaches 87.32% and 84.45%, while bilingual joint training achieves 79.92% across four emotion categories. The confusion matrix shows stable cross-lingual performance for neutral and sad, while angry-happy confusion reflects cultural differences. This lightweight method validates that dynamic sampling effectively preserves language-independent prosodic features.

title: The Versatility of Large Language Models in Traffic and Mobility Simulations
authors: Maren Schnieder, Gary B. Glesener
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: The transformative potential of large language models (LLMs) in urban mobility planning and traffic simulations is undeniable. This position paper seeks to cultivate curiosity in the application of LLMs by underscoring their versatility and potential to support various facets of traffic simulations. They enable natural-language-based interaction with software. LLMs have been harnessed to generate or refine simulation models, as well as to write the code or files necessary to create the simulations or models. They have proven effective in predicting travel choices and forecasting passenger demand for both typical and rare events. Furthermore, LLMs can enhance agent-based simulations by creating realistic daily activity patterns, enabling agents to reason, and allowing them to dynamically adapt to their environment, thus closely emulating human behavior. Notably, LLMs have successfully replicated human decision-making processes in areas such as mode choice experiments and economic decision-making. Given this breadth of proven utility—from intuitive user interfaces, model development and demand forecasting to the accurate replication of human decision-making in areas like agent-based modeling, mode choice and economics—this paper argues that adopting LLMs is critical to transform current mobility planning workflows.

title: Development of Multimodal Neural Network for Suicidal Ideation Detection Using Kazakh-Russian BERT and Attention Mechanism
authors: Ayazhan Ramazanova, Tamara Zhukabayeva, Bakhtiyor Meraliyev, Ualikhan Sadyk
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: The screening of suicidal ideation on social media is a serious element of the proactive intervention of a mental disorder, especially in linguistically heterogeneous areas like Kazakhstan. Conventional text-only models are usually incapable of dealing with the vagueness of colloquialism and the absence of behavioural context. In the current paper, there is a proposal of a multimodal neural network architecture that combines multilingual BERT-base-cased Kazakh and Russian text embeddings with auxiliary temporal-engagement and demographic streams of data. The framework applies Bi-directional Long ShortMemories (BiLSTM) network, which is improved with an attention mechanism focusing on prioritizing high-risk linguistic features and abnormal social behaviors. It was tested and trained in a curated dataset of 2,500 posts of 1,200 respondents in five major regions of Kazakhstan. The results of the experiment indicate that the multimodal approach performs much better than the unimodal baselines, with the accuracy of 0.95, F1-score of 0.94 and Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 0.97. These results indicate that incorporation of social engagement measures and time features offer crucial context to resolve linguistic ambiguities and is a very powerful tool to detect early cases of suicidal ideation in Central Asian social media scenarios.

title: Educational Data Mining for Predicting Student Retention and Performance: a Supervised Learning Approach
authors: Samuel-Soma M. Ajibade, Sarah Jane Colina, Ivy Batican, Lislee Valle, Edralin General, Joshlen Lim, Arnulf Jairo P. Velos, Hazna Quiñanola
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Student dropout and academic failure affect both academic performance and student futures, with them being major concerns in higher education. Predictive analytics has become a valuable tool for identifying at-risk students and supporting timely interventions with the increasing availability of educational data. Many educational institutions often encounter huge struggles in identifying which influential factors most accurately or precisely affect or predict dropout and academic failure outcomes despite having large amounts of student data. This research study addresses the need for data- driven techniques in being able to quickly identify at-risk students, to develop specific study interventions in support of those who are at risk of dropping out. The objective of this study is to support early intervention strategies in higher education by applying machine learning algorithms to analyze student data to predict dropout and academic success and identifying the most influential factors that affect student dropout or academic outcomes. A student dataset from the UCI Machine Learning Repository is studied by this analysis. Supervised machine learning algorithms such as Decision Tree, Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and XGBoost predict dropout and academic success and conduct feature selection, followed by model evaluation using performance metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. Students with a lower number of approved courses in the second semester are most likely to drop out according to the conduction of preliminary investigations. This study can help educational institutions identify at-risk students in time and implement targeted interventions to reduce dropout rates.

title: Research on Feature Extraction and Sales Forecasting Algorithm of Cross-category Sales Data
authors: Xiaowen Liu, Bo Zhu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: It is difficult for traditional sales forecasting models to capture cross-category dynamic correlation and multi-modal external factors. In this study, a cross-category sales forecasting framework is proposed, which integrates graph neural network (GNN) and triple attention mechanism. Firstly, this method constructs a dynamic correlation graph based on the analysis of sliding window shopping basket, and quantifies the time series evolution of complementarity and substitution among categories by using graph attention network (GAT). Then, a hybrid framework of time sequence attention, graphic attention and external factor attention is designed to realize the adaptive integration of heterogeneous information such as sales time sequence, category association, weather and promotion. In order to solve the cold start problem of new products, an adaptive weight distribution mechanism based on attribute similarity is developed, and accurate prediction without historical data is realized through knowledge transfer. In experiments conducted on a real-world dataset spanning 126 product categories from a retail enterprise, the proposed model achieved root mean square error (RMSE), mean absolute percentage error (MAPE), and weighted average percentage error (WAPE) metrics of $28.13,15.42 \%$, and $13.57 \%$, respectively. These results significantly outperformed baseline methods including ARIMA, LSTM, and MTGNN (Multivariate Time Series Graph Neural Network). Ablation experiments show that dynamic correlation diagram and external attention mechanism are the key to improve performance, and show stronger robustness and adaptability in promotion scenarios and cold start category prediction, which provides effective technical support for retail intelligent decision-making.

title: Multi-Scale Temporal Convolutional Network for the Long-Term Fault Early Warning of Gas Turbines
authors: Changjun Liu, Shuoyang Zhang, Tingting Wang, Xiaomo Jiang, Xiaofang Wang, Haitao Liu
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Fault early warning based on gas turbine sensor networks is critical for intelligent predictive maintenance and operational safety in modern power systems. While data-driven techniques are prevalent, balancing computational efficiency with long-term modeling of measurement transients remains challenging under complex temporal drifts. This paper proposes a novel Multi-Scale Temporal Convolutional Network (MS-TCN) specifically designed for the long-term time series forecasting (LTSF) task in industrial environments. The proposed architecture introduces a mathematical decomposition framework featuring: 1) an autocorrelation-guided detrending module to mitigate non-stationary distribution shifts by extracting inherent periodic trends directly from multi-sensor streams; and 2) a dual-pooling mechanism that decomposes measurement sequences for highly efficient cross-scale dependency learning. Additionally, a full-history convolution layer provides the architectural capacity to integrate broader historical context. Rigorous evaluations on Long-Term Time Series Forecasting (LTSF) benchmarks demonstrate that the MS-TCN architecture reduces estimation errors by up to 43.2% (with improvements ranging from 18.5% to 43.2% depending on the specific dataset and forecasting horizon) compared to state-of-the-art computational models. Furthermore, to translate these LTSF predictions into practical industrial applications, a adaptive residual-weighted multi-level warning framework is established using adaptive weights to quantify decaying prediction confidence. A robust four-level alert system based on <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3\sigma $ </tex-math></inline-formula> thresholds is introduced to explicitly account for sensing uncertainties and helps reduce false positives. Validation on real-world gas turbine operational data confirms that the proposed computing framework triggers warnings 4–6 hours earlier than conventional baselines, offering a practical and effective tool for industrial predictive maintenance.

title: Latency Minimization in Integrated Sensing, Communication, and Computation Networks
authors: Yingsheng Peng, Jinbei Zhang, Jingpu Duan, Weichao Li, Yong Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) is a key enabling technology of 6G networks, allowing networks to jointly perform data transmission and environmental sensing. However, ISAC devices often have limited computing resources, making it difficult to process complicated tasks and meet stringent low-latency requirements. To address this, this paper introduces a novel integrated sensing, communication, and computation (ISCC) network. We aim to minimize the maximum overall latency for communication and computation among the users under radar sensing performance constraints by jointly optimizing the transmit power of users, the allocation of computing resources at the edge server (ES), and the transmit and receive beamforming of the base station (BS). To solve this non-convex optimization problem, we design an overall iterative algorithm based on an alternating optimization (AO) framework. The algorithm decomposes the original problem into three non-convex subproblems, and solves them using the convex relaxation techniques based on the mean inequality, Taylor expansion, and semi-definite relaxation (SDR), respectively. The final solutions are obtained by alternatively optimizing the subproblems until convergence. Simulation results show that the proposed algorithm outperforms several baseline schemes in terms of latency reduction, user fairness and sensing quality. In addition, it reveals the impact of relevant parameters on system performance.

title: Research on Deep Learning-Based Multimodal Big Data Fusion Analysis Methods
authors: Yu Shi, Hongchang Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: To enhance the fusion analysis capability of multi-source heterogeneous data, this study proposes a multi-modal fusion recognition model based on deep neural structures. The model employs a joint feature extraction architecture combining image CNN, text Transformer, and audio Bi-GRU, incorporating cross-modal attention mechanisms and shared representation learning to achieve unified semantic space alignment. It integrates and optimizes ResNet50, BERT, and multi-scale convolutional networks. Experimental results demonstrate that the tri-modal fusion model achieves an accuracy of 88.5%, representing a maximum improvement of 12.9% over single-modal models. Its F1-score stability outperforms various control models, validating the effectiveness and generalization capability of its fusion strategy.

title: Radar Anti-Jamming Effectiveness Evaluation Fusing Sensitivity Analysis and KDE Hybrid Sampling
authors: Lei Bao, Lei Bao, Dongfang Li, Xianzhong Gao, Ting Wu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: To address challenges in high-dimensional parameter space exploration and computationally expensive physical simulations during radar anti-jamming performance evaluation, this paper proposes a sequential sampling method based on sensitivity analysis and critical region identification. First, a static BDNN serves as a surrogate model to replace timeconsuming simulations. Second, Sobol global sensitivity analysis identifies a set of critical influencing factors. Finally, a hybrid sampling strategy based on kernel density estimation (KDE) is proposed to sequentially sample the fuzzy performance boundary within the low-dimensional critical subspace. Experimental results demonstrate that this method successfully identifies the critical performance region of the radar, with the surrogate model achieving a prediction accuracy of $93.33 \%$. This approach significantly enhances the sampling efficiency and precision in delineating the performance boundary for radar anti-jamming capability evaluation.

title: Understanding Adversarial Vulnerabilities and Emergent Patterns in Multimodal RL
authors: Shayan Jalalipour, Danielle Justo, Banafsheh Rekabdar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Deep multi-modal models are becoming increasingly wide spread due to their ability to solve complex tasks with more detailed information and context across various modalities. Not limiting a model to a single modality allows nuanced information and can unify tasks that require multiple models. However we must also consider how the use of multi-modal models effects robustness against adversarial attacks. We extend open-source tools to contribute a test bed for creating datasets, evaluating attacks and defenses on a baseline multi-modal reinforcement learning (RL) model, and explore the effects of attacking and defending each of its modalities together and individually. We find emerging dynamics between modalities and different defense methods. We also show attack success rate on individual or combined modalities are attack, defense, and modality dependent.

title: Research on Intelligent Verification of Transformer Carbon Footprint Based on Multi-Agent Large Language Models
authors: Changtao Xie, Fengzhu Zheng, Lin Deng, Xiaoying Guo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the advancement of China’s “Dual Carbon” goals (Carbon Peak and Carbon Neutrality), carbon footprint management in the power industry has garnered significant attention. Transformers, as core assets of power grids, exhibit complex life-cycle carbon footprints where manual verification faces inherent limitations. As research on large language model (LLM) technology deepens, Multi-Agent Large Language Models (MLLMs) have demonstrated autonomous decisionmaking capabilities and can collaboratively accomplish complex tasks, offering a viable solution to address the intricacies of transformer carbon footprint verification in the power sector. This paper addresses the issues of low efficiency and insufficient accuracy associated with traditional manual verification. We propose an intelligent verification framework based on multi-agent large language models. Innovatively, we establish a three-tier multi-agent system comprising Multimodal Information Extraction, Intelligent Data Verification, and Automated Report Generation. This framework enables: Efficient Parsing of Multi-format Supporting Materials, Crossvalidation of Production Process Data, and Intelligent Generation of Standardized Reports.It provides the power equipment carbon management sector with a solution that integrates both standardization and intelligence.

title: Research on API Interface Calls of OpenAI Large Models Based on Python
authors: Yong Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: To address the difficulties users face with proxy configuration and high usage costs when accessing major overseas models, this study uses the relay service provided by the CloseAI proxy platform to overcome access restrictions and explores the implementation path of calling OpenAI large model APIs based on Python. This article covers three core aspects: preliminary preparation, basic API calls, and advanced LangChain calls, providing complete code implementation examples for environment setup, single conversations, character definition, and continuous conversations. The output effects of different parameter combinations were verified, clarifying the typical application scenarios of interface calls. The results indicate that the CloseAI platform can access multiple foreign large models stably without a proxy, and the pay-as-you-go model significantly reduces usage costs, while Python-based API integration enables workflow automation. This study provides a feasible solution for users to use foreign large models efficiently at low cost, and the related implementation methods are of reference value in the development of large model interface applications.

title: Empowering Non-destructive Testing Technology with Artificial Intelligence
authors: Shichao Lu, Hongwei Wang, Qi Guo, Haisheng He, Yi Su
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Because of aviation equipment’s particularity, the aviation equipment has high requirements for safety and reliability, so the application of non-destructive testing (NDT) technology is particularly critical. By monitoring the structure, performance and system status of aviation equipment, potential faults and damage can be detected and warned in time, which provides important decision support for equipment maintenance optimization and preventive maintenance. This paper aims to introduce the NDT technology enabled by artificial intelligence and discuss its future development trend.

title: Deep Reinforcement Adaptive Learning with LLM Enrichment for Cybersecurity Ontology Alignment
authors: Mehrnoosh Zaeifi, Faezeh Kalantari, Mehdi Zaeifi, Srividya Bansal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: This paper investigates the effectiveness of the Deep Reinforcement Adaptive Learning for Ontology Alignment (DRALOA) framework in aligning cybersecurity ontologies. Given the domain’s complex terminology and evolving structure, we evaluate whether DRAL-OA performs adequately and explore how its performance can be enhanced. We compare three alignment strategies: (1) a baseline using general-purpose BERT embeddings, (2) four cybersecurity-specific embeddings (CyBERT, CTI-BERT, CySecBERT, SecureBERT), and (3) LLMenriched pipelines that pair these embeddings with zero-shot large language models, including ChatGPT 4.1, ChatGPT 4o, their mini variants, and LLaMA models. Experiments on two well-established cybersecurity ontologies assess alignment performance using precision, recall, and F-measure at Top-1, Top-3, and Top-5 levels. Among embedding-only methods, CTI-BERT yields the highest performance, while only ChatGPT 4.1 and ChatGPT 4o consistently improve alignment across all thresholds. These high-capacity LLMs also close the gap between BERT and CTI-BERT, enabling general-purpose models to achieve domain-specific performance. Our findings reveal that DRALOA is effective in cybersecurity settings but can be substantially improved through LLM enrichment, making it a more adaptable and domain-agnostic solution for ontology alignment.

title: Using Machine Learning to Predict Arterial Hypertension From a Clinical Dataset
authors: Aisulu Igimbayeva, Adel Kunuspekova, Bakhtiyor Meraliyev
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Arterial hypertension (AH) is a major modifiable risk factor for cardiovascular disease and remains a significant public health challenge. Early identification of hypertension severity is essential for effective risk stratification, yet traditional approaches struggle to integrate heterogeneous clinical data effectively. This study presents an integrated framework combining large language model (LLM)-based feature extraction with machine learning for multi-stage hypertension prediction using real-world hospital data from Kazakhstan. We developed an automated pipeline that transforms unstructured cardiology consultation notes into structured datasets with 83 clinical features. Four supervised machine learning models (Logistic Regression, Random Forest, Support Vector Machine, and XGBoost) were evaluated for four-class hypertension stage classification on 553 patient records that were collected from three clinics in the Karaganda region. XGBoost achieved the best performance with 85.58 % accuracy and a macro-averaged F1-score of 0.8406, outperforming the logistic regression baseline. In addition, a recommendation generation module was implemented to produce structured outputs that support individualized blood pressure management. The proposed framework demonstrates the possibility of applying machine learning to real-world clinical data for stage-aware hypertension prediction and provides a foundation for scalable clinical decision support and risk assessment on population level.

title: A Blockchain-Integrated Confidence-Weighted Fusion Model for Diagnosing the Stages of Thyroid Cancer
authors: Nabanita Das, Ayan Das, Monu Bhagat, Mohammed Alaa Ala'anzy, Abhishek Bhattacharya, Soumi Dutta
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: For accurate detection of thyroid cancer stages, including I, II, III, IVA, and IVB, proper treatment planning is necessary in a modern, sustainable medical system. Nowadays, researchers suggest the use of machine learning models for detecting complex patterns from laboratory test data. Hence, this work presents a novel, secure, and reliable fusion model that efficiently predicts stages of thyroid cancer. To design a strong ensemble model, it integrates BERT and XGBoost, which exploits both structural and categorical data to identify patterns from clinical data. Along with this, to improve security and transparency, this model was then integrated with a Blockchain platform, where patient records were securely deployed in an immutable platform. Finally, Performance analysis shows that the proposed framework outperforms other competitive classifiers in detecting the stages accurately. Thus, this work demonstrates an effective and secure framework that has the power to identify deep contextual meaning for predicting thyroid cancer stages and utilizes blockchain technology for enhancing privacy and transparency for securing diagnostic information.

title: Fraud Risk Perception and Online Payment Choice Model on Online Marketplaces: Evidence from the Mekong Delta, Vietnam
authors: Duy Vu Le, Hong Minh Ngoc Tran
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: This study focuses on the fact that online marketplaces in the Mekong Delta, Vietnam, the consumers' perception of fraud risk influences online payment choice. Data were gathered by an online survey among a sample of 317 frequent shoppers at the marketplaces recruited from consumer communities on Facebook, and that was a convenience sampling process. Fraud risk perception was operationalized into five dimensions on the risk of payment credential theft, risk of account take over, fraud and non delivery risk for sellers, risk of checkout scam and phishing, and risk of refund and dispute failure. Scale reliability was demonstrated by the acceptable Cronbach's alpha values across all scales and results of exploratory factor analysis suggested the presence of a 5 factor clear structure with good enough sampling adequacy. Binary logistic regression was then estimated using online payment choice coded one if made online and zero if made by cash on delivery. All five dimensions of fraud risk had negative coefficients, meaning that lower perceived fraud risks decreased the odds to choose online payment. Checkout scam and phishing risk and risk of fraud/seller non delivery were the risk factors with the strongest negative relationship, with the risk of payment credential theft and the risk of refund and dispute failure also considered significant. These findings extend risk and trust perspectives in showing that differentiated fraud concerns translate into observable marketplace payment behavior. The study provides practical implications for platforms that include prioritization of anti phishing safeguards, stronger seller governance and clearer and faster refund and dispute processes that is central to remedy confidence.

title: Coordinated Optimization of Production Scheduling and Auxiliary Materials Storage Based on Time Series Prediction and Heuristic Search
authors: Xiaohui Jiang, Ke Xu, Wenyu Liao, Xin Yao, Yang Zhou, Shibo Ouyang, Xin Li, Jiamin Lei
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: The cigarette making and packing workshop exemplifies discrete manufacturing with multi-brand, multimachine, and multi-zone parallel operations, where production planning, auxiliary material supply, and warehouse scheduling are tightly integrated. Yet in many enterprises, production calendars still rely on manually maintained Excel spreadsheets and empirical judgments, while auxiliary material planning primarily uses coarse-grained estimates and safety redundancies. The lack of unified scheduling logic and feedback mechanisms between material requisition execution and on-site PLC systems makes it challenging to meet the demands for precision and intelligence in the context of rapidly changing product specifications and continuous improvement of production rhythm. This paper addresses the practical scenarios of a cigarette factory’s making and packing workshop and auxiliary material balance warehouse, proposing an integrated forecastingoptimization framework that spans the entire production chain from “production calendar - auxiliary material demand balance warehouse - on-site execution”. At the decisionmaking level, the automatic generation of production calendars is formalized as a discrete allocation model with multidimensional constraints. An algorithm combining rulebased heuristic and local search is designed to achieve approximate optimal scheduling within seconds. At the material level, auxiliary material requisition is modeled as an inventory control problem under uncertain demand conditions. A time series forecasting model and inventory optimization strategy are introduced to generate requisition plans, with reinforcement learning interfaces reserved to enhance adaptive strategy performance. At the execution level, edge gateways integrate with PLCs, sensors, and AGV/conveyor systems to achieve closed-loop control from decision-making to execution. The system was deployed in a making and packing workshop with approximately 50 machines, 30 major brands, 200 auxiliary materials, and 400 valid storage locations. Results demonstrated that the proposed framework could complete monthly production calendar recalculations and material ordering optimization cycles within seconds, enabling real-time inventory balance updates. It significantly reduced manual scheduling and material ordering workloads, minimized production downtime due to auxiliary material shortages, and improved storage utilization rates. The framework also shows potential for migration to other discrete manufacturing scenarios.

title: Research on the Influence of VFTO on Voltage Transformer in Electric Locomotives
authors: Xubin Li, Fuqiang Tian, Zeyu Ma, Xiaoming Li, Yike Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Delivery
abstract: Frequent insulation breakdown in voltage transformer (VT) is a critical issue that needs to be solved in the high-voltage electrical system of electric locomotives (HVES-EL). Existing research indicates that overvoltage is an important cause of insulation breakdown in VT. This paper firstly proposed an equivalent circuit model of the VT winding involving parasitic parameters. The parasitic parameters were determined by combining the finite element method and analytical calculations. Then we analyzed the switching overvoltage characteristics in the HVES-EL by establishing an equivalent simulation model. Finally, we studied the longitudinal voltage gradient distribution within the VT winding based on the equivalent circuit models of HVES-EL and VT. The results show that the switching overvoltage exhibits the characteristics of a high amplitude and steepness specific to very vast transient overvoltage (VFTO). The voltage distribution in the VT windings is extremely uneven under VFTO, the inter-layer voltage decays exponentially from the beginning to the end of the winding. The winding-to-ground voltage exhibits an oscillatory decay trend, with the maximum value occurring in the first layer. The results indicate that VFTO can easily lead to insulation breakdown between layers and turns near the VT's first layer of the winding, potentially inducing ferromagnetic resonance and causing thermal breakdown and damage to the VT. This study is of great significance for the insulation optimization design of the VT.

title: ADAPT: Advanced Differential and Agile Parallel Transmission Mechanism for High-Performance Humanoid Robots_supp1-3686707.mp4
authors: Baek-Kyu Cho
publish: N/A
abstract: Agile humanoid locomotion requires high joint torque and velocity, but conventional serial actuation, whether using high-reduction or Quasi-Direct Drive (QDD) actuators, faces limitations in size, weight, and efficiency. This paper proposes the Advanced Differential and Agile Parallel Transmission (ADAPT), a novel leg architecture that efficiently utilizes commercial actuators. Specifically, ADAPT integrates a Hip Pitch- Knee Differential Mechanism with a Knee-Ankle Parallel Transmission. This mechanism mechanically couples multiple actuators to generate output exceeding a single actuator's capability, while concentrating the leg's mass near the hip to minimize inertia. We validated the proposed design on a self-developed single-leg platform using a nonlinear kino-dynamic trajectory optimization framework. Experimental results from dynamic tasks including soccer kicking and vertical jumping demonstrate that the robot generates joint torques significantly exceeding the capability of a single actuator. This validates that the proposed architecture effectively overcomes the limitations of serial actuation, enabling high-performance dynamic behaviors efficiently.

title: Hetero-core fiber optic LSPR based glucose sensor with glucose oxidase immobilized on pH sensitive polymer multilayer
authors: Deepro Banerjee, Atsushi Seki, Kazuhiro Watanabe, Michiko Nishiyama
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: This paper explores a novel hetero-core fiber optic localized surface plasmon resonance based glucose sensor that has been coated with a polymer multilayer of poly-L-lysine and polyacrylic acid. The polymer multilayer has been functionalized with glucose oxidase, which reacts to free glucose and produces an acidic product, which causes a swelling-deswelling of the polymer multilayer thereby altering the transmission light characteristics. The paper attempts to characterize the sensor’s response in terms of intensity change at specific wavelengths of 540 nm, 650 nm, and 850 nm, rather than wavelength shift aiming at practical application simplicity. It was also observed that the sensor could differentiate glucose from sucrose and fructose even in lower concentration aqueous solutions.

title: Glutameter- A pre-diagnostic biosensor to detect L-glutamine in cancer_supp1-3686680.docx
authors: Swathi Sudhakar
publish: N/A
abstract: L-glutamine's role in cancer metabolism makes it a more versatile biomarker for cancer detection. A plasmonic fiberoptic absorption biosensor (P-FAB), an emerging technology that utilizes a compact U-shaped optical fiber with an enhanced evanescent field and plasmonic labels to give rise to an ultra-high analyte detection sensitivity, was employed to detect L-Glutamine (L-Gln) as a biomarker using a competitive immunoassay for early cancer detection.
