title: Supervised Cardinality Estimation for Similarity Queries on Knowledge Graphs
authors: Georgios Chatzigeorgakidis, Dimitrios Skoutas, Alkis Simitsis
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Over the past decades, query cardinality estimation on relational databases has been proven crucial, enabling the result size estimation of all sub-plans of each query. This allowed query optimizers to design efficient query plans, towards minimizing the query response time. Lately, knowledge graphs are being preferred over traditional databases in various applications, due to their ability to represent complex relationships and semantic meaning in a domain-specific context. In this work, we present a novel approach for supervised similarity-aware cardinality estimation on knowledge graphs that incorporates similarities into the model training process, thus treating them as first-class citizens. To this end, we present SCE-KG, a model that employs a novel encoding scheme for cardinality estimation, named $\mathbf{S G}_{\text {split. }}$. Our experimental evaluation reveals that our approach outperforms the state-of-the-art techniques in terms of prediction error, achieving 40 x and 37 x improvement for star and chain queries, respectively (95 th percentile), whereas its online overhead accounts for less than $0.05 \%$ of the average query execution time and less than $1 \%$ of the average query planning time, showing a sub-millisecond latency in all cases.

title: A MEMS-Free 4096-Pixel CMOS E-Nose Array With MOF-Based Molecular Selectivity, In-Pixel Thermal Regeneration, and a Compact Single-Coefficient Bandpass Sigma–Delta ADC
authors: Marco Saif, Adam Y. Wang, Shao-Wei Lo, Fuze Jiang, Zhikai Huang, Jiachen Wang, Hangxing Liu, Chih-Jen Shih, Thomas Burger, Hua Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Solid-State Circuits
abstract: This work presents a CMOS-only [micro-electromechanical systems (MEMS)-free] electronic nose (e-nose) for concurrent multi-gas-sensing applications. The proposed system integrates 4096 capacitance-to-digital converter (CDC) pixels, each implementing a compact bandpass sigma–delta (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\Sigma \Delta $</tex-math> </inline-formula>) ADC with a single feedback coefficient and no additional feedforward or feedback paths, achieving each pixel footprint of only <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$45\times 45~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m. The e-nose can achieve full baseline recovery and sensor reusability through an in-pixel heater to enable rapid and complete desorption of gas molecules from the sensing surface. The chip also includes 128 temperature-sensing pixels for on-chip thermal monitoring, utilizing a bandgap-based temperature sensor followed by a hybrid active–passive <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\Sigma \Delta $</tex-math> </inline-formula> ADC, maintaining the same compact pixel footprint as the CDC pixels. The system operates from a 1.1-V supply with a power consumption of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10.3~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>W per pixel at a 1-MHz sampling rate. To achieve high molecular selectivity, metal–organic framework (MOF) materials are utilized as the sensing interface. Two MOFs: CoNi–Pyz and HKUST-1 are employed for selective detection of ammonia (NH<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>) and isopropyl alcohol (IPA), respectively, on the same chip. For NH<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> sensing, the proposed e-nose achieves a limit of detection (LoD) of 5 ppm, a sensitivity of 0.012 (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\Delta $</tex-math> </inline-formula>C/C)/ppm, and a wide sensing range from 5 to 1000ppm. To the best of authors’ knowledge, this work achieves the highest reported density for a CMOS solid-state e-nose and represents the first demonstration of integrating both single and multiple MOF transducer materials directly on a CMOS chip. It also establishes a thermodynamically modulated sensing approach enabled by the in-pixel heater, ensuring full sensor reset and reusability.

title: RGB-D Perception-Enhanced 3D Gaussian Splatting SLAM: a Robust Framework for Mapping Underground Spaces_supp1-3686359.mp4
authors: Xiaohu Lin
publish: N/A
abstract: The efficient utilization of underground spaces is a crucial strategy for mitigating land scarcity and expanding habitable environments. 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has emerged as a key enabler for enhancing robotic perception and spatial digitalization in underground spaces due to its unique advantages. However, illumination variation, sensor noise, and geometric degradation of underground spaces significantly degrade the localization accuracy, which compromises the robustness of existing Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) systems. Therefore, we propose an RGB-D perception enhanced 3DGS SLAM method. First, a multi-dimensional data enhancement and correction pipeline is introduced, integrating Multi-Scale Retinex with Color Restoration (MSRCR), Side Window Filtering (SWF), and adaptive gamma correction in the Hue-Intensity-Saturation (HIS) color space to improve low-light visual fidelity, while a depth completion network is developed to perform hole-filling in depth data. Second, a keyframe selection method based on the hybrid metric is proposed, which incorporates consistency constraints and multi-view overlap analysis to balance computational efficiency and representational completeness. Finally, a dual-constraint Gaussian management strategy is introduced, integrating an opacity threshold and observation frequency to filter out invalid Gaussian ellipsoids. At the same time, loop closure detection ensures global trajectory consistency and mapping accuracy. To validate the proposed method, experiments were conducted in typical underground spaces, including coal mine tunnels and underground parking garages, using a custom-designed underground mobile robot platform. The results demonstrate that, compared to state-of-the art methods, the proposed method achieves a 13.8% improvement in Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) over the best benchmark, while also achieving competitive trajectory accuracy and computational efficiency. These findings provide strong support for the development of digital twin systems in underground spaces.

title: A Stochastic FCC-FDTD Method for Uncertainty Quantification of Electromagnetic Field in Biological Tissues
authors: Boyan Cui, Guangzhi Chen, Xinsong Wang, Hanhong Liu, Yaru Zhang, Shunchuan Yang, Qiang Ren
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: A stochastic face-centered cubic finite-difference time-domain (S-FCC-FDTD) method is proposed to calculate the mean and standard deviation of the electromagnetic fields in biological tissues, which takes into account the uncertainty and variability of the electrical properties of the media. To improve accuracy, the updating formulas for the mean and standard deviation fields are derived from the deterministic FCC-FDTD method by using the Delta method. Due to the frequencydependent properties of biological tissues, the dispersive S-FCC-FDTD method for the Debye model is also developed to solve problems involving dispersive media. To truncate the computational domain constructed by the S-FCC-FDTD method, the complex-frequency-shifted perfectly matched layer (CFS-PML) is designed for absorbing the standard deviation waves, which is derived from the existing CFS-PML for mean electromagnetic waves, and exhibits excellent absorption performance. Then, the stability of the S-FCC-FDTD method is analytically and numerically analyzed in detail. Three numerical examples, pulse propagation in a fat medium and a multilayer human tissue, specific absorption rate (SAR) of an extremely complex head model are carried out to validate its performance. The numerical results demonstrate that the S-FCC-FDTD method has good accuracy and stability in the analysis of statistical variation, with broad applications in bioelectromagnetic effects.

title: A Multi-Mode Hybrid Control Strategy with Seamless Transition for Constant-Current Charging in LCC Resonant Capacitor Charging Power Supply
authors: Gan Lijin
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Power Electronics
abstract: A multi-mode hybrid control strategy with seamless switching capability is proposed to address the issues of excessively wide operating frequency range and low light-load efficiency in traditional LCC resonant capacitor charging power supplies(CCPS) under constant-current charging control. The strategy achieves smooth transition of charging voltage by switching topologies in stages: half-bridge mode is adopted in the initial charging phase, PWM-PFM hybrid mode in the middle phase, and full-bridge mode in the later phase. A steady-state model of the LCC resonant converter under PWM-PFM hybrid control is established based on the fundamental harmonic approximation (FHA), and the DC voltage gain expression is derived. A seamless mode-switching mechanism is designed, effectively suppressing current surges during transitions. A 650 V/1 A constant-current charging experimental platform was built for validation. Experimental results show that, compared to conventional constant-current charging control, the proposed strategy reduces the converter's operating frequency range by approximately 56.8%, improves the overall average efficiency by 7.3%, achieves a maximum efficiency increase of 15.7%, and eliminates current surge during mode transitions.

title: Purity Tests for Euler Continued Fractions
authors: Nurdaulet Shynarbek, Alibek Orynbassar, Muhammad Ateeq Tahir
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: A family of alternating series \begin{equation*} S_{r}\left(u; v_{1}, \ldots, v_{r-1}\right)=\sum_{n \geq 1} \frac{(-1)^{n-1}}{(n+u)^{2} \prod_{j=1}^{r-1}\left(n+v_{j}\right)} \end{equation*} is studied, where <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$u, v_{j}$</tex> are nonnegative integers and the poles are distinct except for the double pole at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-u$</tex>. By a classical Euler transform, each series is converted into a polynomial continued fraction, so that every admissible parameter choice yields a structured continued fraction value. An exact rational triple (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha, c, \beta$</tex>) is computed symbolically by partial fraction decomposition, and the identity <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{r}=\alpha \pi^{2}+c \log 2+\beta$</tex> is obtained. An exact purity test is thereby obtained, because the presence of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\log 2$</tex> component is decided by the coefficient <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$c$</tex> before numerical computation is performed. For the two-parameter family <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{2}(u; v)$</tex>, a complete purity rule is proved, namely <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$c=0$</tex> if and only if <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$u$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$v$</tex> have the same parity. On the numerical side, continued-fraction convergents are evaluated and are accelerated by Aitken <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta^{2}$</tex> and Wynn's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon$</tex> algorithm under a deterministic selection rule. Truncation is controlled by the alternating-series remainder bound, and numerical stability is checked by guard precision and depth variation. Parameter maps and rankings on bounded grids show that, even in the pure region, large differences in practical extraction performance for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pi^{2}$</tex> are produced by different parameter choices.

title: Serverless Visual Voice Assistant Widget for Website Integration
authors: Radmir Gumerov, Ruslan Isaev
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: The increasing volume and diversity of web content pose significant challenges for efficient information retrieval. To address this, we propose a lightweight, browser-based visual voice assistant widget designed for seamless website integration. Operating exclusively on the client side within Chrome, our system leverages native speech recognition and natural language processing modules to facilitate real-time voice queries without compromising user privacy. Additionally, we develop a comprehensive testing framework for speech recognition accuracy and system robustness. Experimental results demonstrate that our assistant effectively improves search efficiency and user interaction while maintaining high privacy standards, making it a practical solution for enhancing human-computer interaction in web environments.

title: FPGA-Based Solid State Power Controller Architecture for Fault-Tolerant Aircraft Power Distribution
authors: Bilal Talha Tekin, Dogukan Pacal, Beril Bulak, Mustafa Ocal, Hidir Duzkaya
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: The More Electric Aircraft concept has increased the need for rapid fault detection, real time load management and reliable aircraft power distribution systems. This study proposes an aircraft electrical power distribution system based on Solid State Power Controllers(SSPC) and a Field Programmable Gate Array(FPGA) based central control unit. The FPGA manages rapid fault detection, bus monitoring and real-time load distribution. SSPCs integrate protection and switching functions in a single device to reduce wiring complexity and aircraft mass. According to previous studies SSPCs can respond in microsecond range whereas electromechanical circuit breakers typically operate in the millisecond range. The electrical power distribution system was developed and analyzed in MATLAB/Simulink for a 28 V DC aircraft electrical network in accordance with MIL-STD-704F requirements. The system consists of two power distribution units and each unit includes a main bus rated at 60 A together with a 40A emergency bus. This configuration allows the system to maintain power continuity during normal operation as well as in single-converter and dual-converter failure conditions. When such faults occur power can be transferred through the cross-feed connection while the backup battery continues supplying critical loads. Simulation results show the behavior of the proposed power distribution architecture under different operating conditions and single/dual converter failure scenarios. In these scenarios, it was observed that the system supplies all buses with the cross-feed mechanism, isolates the faulty buses and supplies critical loads using the batteries. Transitions between power sources, isolation of buses during fault conditions and an FPGA-based overcurrent protection mechanism were also observed. Overall, the results show that the proposed architecture can support reliable aircraft power distribution and ensure power continuity under different operational and fault scenarios.

title: Research on an Intelligent Inspection System for Railway Infrastructure Based on Deep Learning Neural Networks
authors: Xingguo Wei
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: In a railway infrastructure intelligent inspection system based on deep learning neural networks, the hardware utilizes RAID 6 technology for data storage, categorizing and storing different types of data. Video surveillance systems are divided into fixed and mobile configurations, and high-performance embedded processors are used for real-time image capture and transmission. Regarding software, a dynamic model of railway infrastructure is constructed to provide a basis for inspection route planning. Defect images undergo joint dynamic-image preprocessing to enhance features. Multimodal data fusion and dynamic weight allocation techniques are employed, along with elastic net regularization to improve model generalization. A multi-level confidence decision mechanism is used to trigger the inspection process, enabling real-time defect detection and data transmission, ensuring safe and stable railway operation. System application results demonstrate that the intelligent inspection system successfully identifies multiple types of anomalies and provides warnings of potential risk points, achieving 100% recognition accuracy. The system achieves a recall rate exceeding 87% for eight typical defect categories, with an F1 score above 86%. The model training loss converges rapidly and stabilizes at a low level, demonstrating the system’s comprehensive, efficient, and adaptable inspection performance.

title: Investigating National Security Risks in the Metaverse and Big Data Environments
authors: Jodhbir Singh, Saeed Ur Rehman, Shafiq Alam, Alireza Jolfaei
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Big Data (BigData)
abstract: The explosive development of the metaverse as a socio-technical system is a major challenge to national security and governance. The current paper analyses the existing academic and industry literature in order to determine the key risks and its implications. We find that the metaverse is bringing in novel vectors of misinformation and disinformation, radicalisation, financial crime, terrorism, and state-sponsored hybrid warfare, whereas among the most specific dangers are the recruitment of extremists in the virtual realm of immersion, laundering of illicit funds through decentralised economies, identity theft, and critical infrastructure exploitation. The majority of the existing mitigation measures suggested include cybersecurity tools, identity verification, content moderation, and regulatory measures but according to our analysis, coordinated governance, interdisciplinary research, and adaptable policy frameworks are required. The paper offers an in-depth insight into ways of reducing metaverse-associated threats to national security through the combination of technical, social, and policy approaches.

title: Cyclist Detection in Urban Traffic Using Fine-Tuned YOLO Models and Diverse Real-World Video Data
authors: Saba Izadkhah, Manikanta Kotthapalli, Banafsheh Rekabdar, Andrew Wagner, Sirisha Kothuri, Nathan McNeil
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Reliable detection of cyclist and person samples in urban traffic is critical for high-safety autonomous driving systems, traffic analytics, and intelligent transportation systems. These classes present complementary challenges, as pedestrians vary widely in pose and scale, while cyclists show high intra-class variation, frequent occlusions, and ambiguous context, similar to pedestrians walking bicycles. We built a cyclist detector for urban traffic by combining COCO, a cyclist-focused image set, and in-house driving videos. Because most public datasets lack an explicit cyclist label, we extract it from person and bicycle detections, merge them into a single cyclist box if their Intersection over Union (IoU) exceeds $\tau=0.3$, and then perform targeted manual corrections. Using these cyclist-enriched data, we fine-tune YOLOv8n, YOLOv11n, and Faster R-CNN under the same training and evaluation protocol to jointly detect person and cyclist. On a held-out test split, fine-tuning with enriched labels significantly improves cyclist detection over off-the-shelf models. YOLOv11n obtains mAP@ 0.5 $=0.818$, cyclist $\mathrm{AP} \text{@} 0.5: 0.95=0.47$, and 74 FPS, achieving the best accuracy-latency balance; YOLOv8n runs at 84 FPS, which is faster with a small drop in recall, while Faster R-CNN yields the highest recall but lower precision and much higher latency. We report detailed per-class precision-recall curves, mAP scores at multiple IoU thresholds, and discuss common failure modes such as occlusion, rider–bicycle disambiguation, and crowded scenes. The code and scripts required for data preparation, training, and evaluation, to ensure repeatability, are available on GitHub at https://github.com/manikantakotthapalli/Cyclist-detection-using-YOLO.

title: LLMs in Fake News Detection: A Comparative Study of Zero-Shot and Fine-Tuned Open-Source Models
authors: Imran Fayyaz, G. G. Md. Nawaz Ali, Samantha S. Khairunnesa
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: This paper presents a comparative evaluation of large language models (LLMs) for binary fake news classification under two experimental setups: zero-shot inference and parameter-efficient fine-tuning. In the zero-shot configuration, instruction-tuned models are evaluated without taskspecific updates to measure their inherent generalization ability. Fine-tuning introduces lightweight low-rank adapters, improving classification accuracy, precision, and recall while remaining feasible on modest hardware (single T4 GPU, 16 GB, 4-bit NF4 quantization). Four open-weight models are examined-DeepSeek-Coder-1.3B, Nous-Hermes-2-Mistral-7B-DPO, Meta-LLaMA-2-7B-Chat-HF, and Microsoft Phi-3-Mini-4K-Instructrepresenting diverse architectures and alignment strategies. Experiments use a balanced subset of the Kaggle Fake and True News corpus with combined Title+Text inputs to preserve full semantic context. Results demonstrate substantial accuracy gains across models after fine-tuning (ranging from $17.6 \%$ to $\mathbf{9 3 . 0 \%}$ relative improvement), with substantial improvements in precision and recall, particularly reducing false positives (fake-as-true misclassifications). The findings highlight that efficient fine-tuning substantially enhances factual reliability and decision consistency while maintaining low computational cost, offering a reproducible path toward scalable misinformation detection.

title: FedVar: A Variance-Aware Federated Method for Non-IID Data Distribution
authors: Jixiang Fang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Federated learning suffers from performance degradation under non-IID data due to inconsistent client updates. We propose FedVar, a variance-aware aggregation method that adaptively reweights client contributions according to their update deviation. By suppressing highly divergent updates, FedVar improves training stability and fairness. Experiments on Fashion-MNIST with Dirichlet-based heterogeneous partitioning show that FedVar achieves faster convergence, higher accuracy, and lower client performance variance compared to FedAvg, FedProx, and SCAFFOLD. The results demonstrate that variance-aware aggregation is an effective strategy for robust federated learning under data heterogeneity.

title: Event camera-based digital autoradiography for detection of alpha and beta particle emissions_supp1-3684302.docx
authors: K.L. Bertschinger
publish: N/A
abstract: Autoradiography is an established technique for imaging and quantifying ionizing radiation emission from samples, with applications that span medical imaging, nuclear forensics, and environmental monitoring. This study explores the potential of using an event camera to perform quantitative digital autoradiography of samples emitting alpha and beta particles. Event cameras mimic the human eye’s efficient architecture, detecting asynchronous changes in pixel intensity and providing capabilities such as low latency, high dynamic range, and sparse data representation. The event camera was swapped for a standard CMOS (complementary metal-oxide-semiconductor) camera and optically coupled to a scintillator screen and an image intensifier—key components of the ionizing quantum radiation detector (iQID) autoradiography system. Comparative measurements were conducted with the CMOS and event cameras in otherwise identical iQID configurations. Alpha and beta particles were imaged concurrently with the event camera, demonstrating a potential wider sensitivity and dynamic range than that of a CMOS camera. Furthermore, tests with high activity sources were performed to assess the count rate capability of event cameras. These advances highlight the potential of event-based imaging in radiation imaging and its broader implications for various critical fields, including scientific research, healthcare, and nuclear security.

title: Composite Learning Prescribed-Time Tracking Control of Robotic Manipulators With Guaranteed Performance and Safety
authors: Keli Pang, Changchun Hua, Yu Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Guaranteeing stability, control performance, and safety in the control of uncertain robotic manipulators is a challenging issue. This article develops a composite learning prescribed-time tracking control strategy for uncertain robotic manipulators, which has the following two appealing features: 1) both precise trajectory tracking and accurate estimation of uncertain model parameters can be achieved within the prescribed time under the interval excitation (IE) condition; and 2) the controller, benefiting from accurate parameter estimation, is still effective after the prescribed time, which means that the system can operate continuously with the tracking error stabilized at the origin. These two features endow the uncertain robotic manipulators with prescribed-time stability capability. In addition, considering the prescribed performance requirements and limited joint space, a new dual-constrained-dependent barrier function (DCDBF) is developed by integrating a tracking error-based constraining function and a joint position-based constraining function. The proposed DCDBF decouples the constraints on tracking error and joint position, allowing the robotic manipulators to dynamically adjust their motion with prescribed performance while maintaining their joint position within the user-specified time-varying safety range. Ultimately, the effectiveness of the proposed control strategy is validated through simulation and experiment.

title: Survey of Deep Learning and Physics-Based Approaches in Computational Wave Imaging
authors: Youzuo Lin, Shihang Feng, James Theiler, Yinpeng Chen, Umberto Villa, Jing Rao, John Greenhall, Cristian Pantea, Mark A. Anastasio, Brendt Wohlberg
publish: Proceedings of the IEEE
abstract: Computational wave imaging (CWI) extracts hidden structure and physical properties of a volume of material by analyzing wave signals that traverse that volume. Applications include seismic exploration of the Earth’s subsurface, acoustic imaging and nondestructive testing (NDT) in material science, and ultrasound computed tomography (USCT) in medicine. Current approaches for solving CWI problems can be divided into two categories: those rooted in traditional physics and those based on deep learning. Physics-based methods stand out for their ability to provide high-resolution and quantitatively accurate estimates of acoustic properties within the medium. However, they can be computationally intensive and are susceptible to ill-posedness and nonconvexity typical of CWI problems. Machine learning (ML)-based computational methods have recently emerged, offering a different perspective to address these challenges. Diverse scientific communities have independently pursued the integration of deep learning in CWI. This review discusses how contemporary scientific ML techniques, and deep neural networks in particular, have been developed to enhance and integrate with traditional physics-based methods for solving CWI problems. We present a structured framework that consolidates existing research spanning multiple domains, including computational imaging, wave physics, and data science. This study concludes with important lessons learned from existing ML-based methods and identifies technical hurdles and emerging trends through a systematic analysis of the extensive literature on this topic.

title:
A Magnetic Colorimetric Aptasensor Based on Fe
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>4</sub>
                    @CQDs Nanocomposites for Ultrasensitive Detection of Glypican-3
authors: Lingling Fan, Cunhong Cen, Yuru Wu, Xiaohua Deng, Yuxuan Shi, Yuxing Huang, Xiaohong Tan, Shidong Li, Guiyin Li
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) remains one of the most lethal malignancies worldwide, underscoring the urgent demand for early diagnostic strategies to improve patient survival rates. Glypican-3 (GPC3), a highly specific biomarker of HCC, has emerged as a promising target for early detection. Herein, we developed an innovative magnetic colorimetric aptasensor using ferrosoferric oxide@carbon quantum dots (Fe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub>@CQDs) nanocomposites. The Fe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub>@CQDs nanocomposites combine the magnetic properties of Fe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub> with the unique optical characteristics of CQDs, exhibiting intrinsic peroxidase‑like activity. The animo-modified GPC3 aptamer (GPC3<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Apt</sub>) was covalently conjugated onto the Fe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub>@CQDs nanocomposites surface via EDC/NHS chemistry to form the recognition probe (Fe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub>@CQDs-GPC3<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Apt</sub>). Upon specific binding between the aptamer and GPC3, the resulting complexes (Fe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub>@CQDs-GPC3<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Apt</sub>-GPC3) modulate the catalytic oxidation of o-phenylenediamine (OPD) by H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>, generating a yellow oxOPD product whose absorbance intensity correlated with GPC3 concentration. The proposed colorimetric aptasensor demonstrated a detection limit of 3.16 ng/mL and a linear range of 10–200 ng/mL, with high specificity against interfering proteins. It was successfully validated in human serum samples, yielding recoveries between 99.27% and 109.33%. Owing to its operational simplicity and cost‑effectiveness, this platform offers transformative potential for point-of-care HCC screening. Beyond advancing GPC3 detection, the work provides a flexible design paradigm that can be adapted for assays targeting other disease biomarkers.

title: An Approximate Point Algorithm for a Time-Varying Ill-Posed Quadratic Programming Problem
authors: Jingbo Feng, Hamid Arian, Cuicui Luo
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: This paper addresses the numerical instability in time-varying quadratic programming caused by ill-conditioned matrices, which often leads to error amplification and significant deviation from the true optimum. We propose a timevarying proximal point algorithm that reformulates the illposed problem into a sequence of well-conditioned subproblems, thereby improving the Hessian condition number while enforcing temporal consistency along the solution trajectory. In the static-parameter case, the method reduces to a proximal point algorithm with linear convergence, for which we establish a refined convergence rate. In the time-varying setting, we further prove convergence of the resulting solution sequence. Numerical experiments demonstrate that, relative to conventional approaches, the proposed framework significantly suppresses solution oscillations in high-condition-number regimes, yielding substantial improvements in numerical stability and decision smoothness.

title: Information Propagation in Signed Dynamic Hypernetworks Driven by Attribute Homophily and Structural Balance
authors: Bowen Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Individual-level homophily and network structure play critical roles in information propagation, yet existing models rarely integrate homophily with higher-order network structures. This study proposes a homophily-driven hypernetwork model in which higher-order structures emerge through clustering regulation of node attributes. Compared with pairwise networks, the hypernetwork exhibits faster spreading, broader propagation range, and stronger persistence, driven by enhanced transmission efficiency of higher-order hyperedges. Moreover, a threshold in homophilous clustering is identified, beyond which propagation effectiveness increases significantly.

title: Frequent Circular Spatial Pattern Searching
authors: Zehao Wang, Andi Ji
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Frequent Circular Spatial Pattern Searching (FCSP) is a fundamental task in unlocking valuable insights from massive geo-spatial datasets. However, existing approaches, particularly those employing fixed-step sliding windows for candidate generation, are plagued by two critical issues: prohibitive computational costs due to exhaustive search space enumeration, and inevitable precision loss (false negatives) caused by discrete sampling. In this paper, we propose FCSP+, a novel Rectangular Sketchbased framework that guarantees both mining completeness and high efficiency. FCSP+ abandons traditional blind traversal in favor of a Spatial Pruning mechanism based on a sweep-line algorithm. This approach precisely identifies valid continuous regions satisfying keyword constraints without discretization errors, effectively eliminating redundant candidates. Extensive experiments on real-world datasets demonstrate that FCSP+ achieves orders-of-magnitude speedup over baseline methods while maintaining $100 \%$ recall of frequent patterns.

title: A Multi-Strategy Enhanced Black-Winged Kite Algorithm for Wireless Sensor Network Coverage Optimization
authors: Diya Yang, Shengli Xie, Wei Liu, Chunlei Cai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: Addressing the issue of large coverage blind spots in wireless sensor networks caused by the random node deployment, this paper presents a Black-winged Kite Algorithm with Multi-strategy Enhancement (MBKA) based on multi-strategy fusion. Firstly, the Lévy flight strategy is incorporated into the attack phase of the original algorithm, utilizing its heavy-tailed distribution jumping characteristics to optimize the search step size, effectively balancing the algorithm’s global exploration capability in early iterations and its local exploitation capability in later stages. Meanwhile, an optimal boundary control strategy is introduced, which uses the global best solution to guide the position reset of out-of-bounds individuals, thus accelerating the convergence toward the optimal region. Lastly, an elite opposition-based learning strategy is adopted, constructing inverse solutions for elite solutions to further expand the search domain and enhance the algorithm’s capacity to break away from local optima during the middle and later iterations. Experimental findings demonstrate that the average coverage rate of the MBKA attains 91.1565%, outperforming the original algorithm and other swarm intelligence algorithms, validating its effectiveness in WSN coverage optimization.

title: Three-Dimensional Optimization of XR Application Reputation Based on K-means Clustering and Multidimensional Analysis
authors: Yue Wang, Xiandong Lv, Junjie Huang, Shanshan Jin
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: The rapid development of XR (Extended Reality) has made optimizing user experience and commercialization essential for successful technology deployment. This paper examines 505 valid XR applications, analyzing how content attributes and price types influence user reputation. A quantitative approach is applied, using K-means clustering, Welch’s t-test, Pearson correlation, and two-way ANOVA. Results show that historical content ranks highest in user reputation, with educational and social science content performing lower. Content attributes have a significant effect, while price types show a weak yet significant correlation with user reputation ($\mathrm{r}=0.133, \mathrm{p}=0.027$). No significant interaction effect between content and price was found ($p=0.455$). Based on these findings, a three-dimensional optimization framework for XR-focusing on experience, content, and price-is proposed, driving a closed-loop system for continuous improvement and monitoring. This research provides insights for enhancing XR applications’ user experience and system iteration in consumer-facing scenarios.

title: An Online/Offline Attribute-Based Rankable Keyword Search Scheme for Cloud-Edge Environments
authors: Pei Wei, Zifeng Xu, Shuyuan Li, Feng Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: This paper proposes OOABRKS, an online/offline attribute-based rankable keyword search scheme for cloud-edge environments. Traditional ABKS schemes suffer from high online computation costs that scale linearly with policy complexity, making them impractical for resource-constrained devices. OOABRKS introduces a dual-phase paradigm: computationally intensive operations are shifted to an offline pre-computation stage, while the online phase performs only lightweight matrix assembly with $O\left(d^{2}\right)$ complexity-decoupled from the number of attributes. By integrating TF-IDF with secure inner-product computation, the scheme enables direct relevance scoring on ciphertexts for accurate Top-k retrieval. Security analysis under the random oracle model proves its resilience against chosen-keyword attacks. Experiments demonstrate that online encryption and token generation require merely 1.65 ms and 1.82 ms respectively, outperforming existing schemes by an order of magnitude while maintaining high ranking accuracy (NDCG@10{>} 0.83). OOABRKS provides a practical path for deploying secure, efficient, and expressive search over encrypted cloud data.

title: Dynamic Credible ESG Valuation and Accounting Synchronization via Permissioned Blockchains and Oracles
authors: Jingyi Du
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: To address the key risk of “greenwashing” caused by information asymmetry in the capital market, this study aims to establish an anti-tampering asset pricing framework for enterprise environmental performance. The traditional auditing paradigm is unable to handle unstructured ESG data, which directly distorts the market risk premium. This paper proposes an integrated framework that combines large language models, licensed blockchains, and decentralized oracle networks. This method utilizes artificial intelligence for data dimensionality reduction and achieves Nash equilibrium price feed through cross-chain node interactions, achieving millisecond-level isomorphism mapping of business and financial ledgers. Simulation results show that this architecture reduces cross-chain retrieval computing resource consumption by 87.5%, block confirmation delay below 1.2 seconds. Moreover, the root mean square error during the shock period is reduced to 0.104. This research makes significant contributions to the decentralized finance and blockchain engineering fields by providing a powerful automated infrastructure for real-time asset pricing and risk management.

title: Uncertainty-Aware Fake News Detection: An Entropy-Driven Hybrid Framework
authors: Yilian Yu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Detecting fake news on social media is critical to safeguarding public information integrity. Unlike existing methods that isolate textual content and social propagation, an original entropy-driven hybrid framework is proposed that models misinformation as a dual-source uncertainty problem. Its core innovation is the unified probabilistic quantification of multidimensional uncertainties: linguistic semantic uncertainty via headline token Shannon entropy, and propagation dynamic uncertainty via entropy of reconstructed social engagement time-lines and URL traffic signals. Integrating these uncertainty signatures with fine-grained temporal descriptors, a lightweight, highly discriminative fused representation is constructed. Experiments on FakeNewsNet show the framework achieves high ROC-AUC and F1-score, outperforming traditional content-only baselines significantly. These findings confirm entropy-based metrics offer a robust, scalable, interpretable approach to identifying orchestrated misinformation in high-velocity social media environments.

title: Research on Diabetes Prediction Model Based on Digestive Factors Using MHA-CNN-BiLSTM and Interpretability Analysis
authors: Lei Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: In recent years, the rapid advancement of deep learning has revolutionized predictive healthcare. Diabetes, a growing global health crisis, necessitates early detection strategies, particularly through diet and digestion analysis-key modifiable factors closely linked to glucose metabolism. However, existing methods often overlook complex feature interactions in health data and lack interpretability, limiting clinical applicability. To address this, this study proposes a CNN-BiLSTM hybrid model enhanced with a Multi-Head Attention Mechanism to capture multidimensional semantic features, and employs LIME for explainability. Comparative experiments on the BRFSS2015 dataset (containing 70,692 balanced samples) validate the proposed framework’s effectiveness. The hybrid CNN-BiLSTM structure initially improved the F1-score by 0.28% over the standalone CNN, and subsequent integration of MHA further boosted performance across key metrics including accuracy and precision. LIME analysis clarifies the contribution of core digestive factors, aligning with medical insights and enhancing model trustworthiness.

title: Communication-Efficient Federated Learning via Frequency-Domain Quantization and Client-Side Network-Aware Scheme Selection
authors: Syeda Faiza Ahmed, Lafifa Jamal
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Federated learning (FL) enables collaborative model training without moving raw data, but it is limited by heavy and uneven network traffic, particularly when many edge devices share updates. In this work, we present two novel quantization schemes, Frequency Conditional Quantization (FCQ) and Linear-Frequency Projection (LFP), which are frequency-domain-based compression methods tailored for FL systems. At each training round, each client quantizes its model weights using the most suitable scheme according to its current bandwidth and traffic conditions. We validate the proposed adaptive compression framework through extensive experiments on FEMNIST and CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 image classification tasks. Across the methods, the proposed adaptive algorithm attains accuracy close to the uncompressed baseline (FEMNIST: 0.938 vs. 0.943; CIFAR-10: 0.811 vs. 0.814) while reducing latency relative to high-accuracy baselines (adaptive: 0.13 s vs. FP8: 0.35 s). A stress test under three bandwidth regimes (Balanced, Mostly Slow, Mostly Fast) indicates stable performance (94.43%, 92.54%, and 93.22% accuracy, respectively) with modest variation in total duration. The results support frequency-domain quantization combined with client-aware scheme selection as a practical approach to reduce communication and per-round time while maintaining model quality in bandwidth-constrained federated networks.

title: Topology-Cognitive Task Offloading and Resource Allocation: A GAT-Enhanced MADRL Approach
authors: Liangqi Yan, Jiale Zhang, Dongtao Cao, Zheyi Chen, Hongju Cheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: As a key computing paradigm for next-generation networks, multi-access edge computing effectively reduces the transmission delay and network load pressure by offloading real-time computing tasks to computing nodes and allocating resources across cloud, edge, and end levels. However, this cloud-edge-end collaborative architecture is characterized by time-varying dynamics and device heterogeneity, which bring some challenges to the system performance. On the one hand, in large-scale dynamic network environments, traditional multi-agent reinforcement learning approaches lack more comprehensive and effective coordination mechanisms. On the other hand, the complex topological structure of heterogeneous networks inherently introduces uncertainty into the processes of task offloading and resource allocation. To address these challenges, we propose a Graph Attention Multi-Agent (GATMA) reinforcement learning algorithm to explore the optimal task and resource scheduling strategy. Firstly, GATMA trains multiple agents effectively based on an improved Centralized Training and Distributed Execution (CTDE) framework that decouples learning objectives and learning ways. Secondly, we construct the graph data structure of network representation for the further process, and utilize the graph attention mechanism and local communication to empower agents with local topology awareness. Finally, we achieve optimal decisions for task offloading and resource scheduling. Experimental results show that compared to the latest baseline method, the proposed algorithm achieves 51.8% improvements in global rewards, and exhibits significant advantages in task latency, completion rate and generalization.

title: LLM-Driven Ontology Construction for Enterprise Knowledge Graphs
authors: Abdulsobur Oyewale, Tommaso Soru
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Enterprise Knowledge Graphs have become essential for unifying heterogeneous data and enforcing semantic governance. However, the construction of their underlying ontologies remains a resource-intensive, manual process that relies heavily on domain expertise. This paper introduces OntoEKG, a LLM-driven pipeline designed to accelerate the generation of domain-specific ontologies from unstructured enterprise data. Our approach decomposes the modelling task into two distinct phases: an extraction module that identifies core classes and properties, and an entailment module that logically structures these elements into a hierarchy before serialising them into standard RDF. Addressing the significant lack of comprehensive benchmarks for end-to-end ontology construction, we adopt a new evaluation dataset derived from documents across the Data, Finance, and Logistics sectors. Experimental results highlight both the potential and the challenges of this approach, achieving a fuzzy-match F1-score of 0.724 in the Data domain while revealing limitations in scope definition and hierarchical reasoning.

title: Design and Implementation of Enterprise Salary Management System Based on Web Technology
authors: ZhengWei Fang, ChengZhang Wang, XueJiao Ma, ZhenHua Guo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: To address the inefficiencies faced by enterprises in the digital transformation of payroll management, this study focuses on the design and implementation of a web-based enterprise payroll management system. The system hardware design utilizes servers with multi-core processors and hybrid storage architectures, configured with load balancers and firewalls to create a stable, efficient, and scalable hardware environment. Regarding software design, payroll plans are designed for different positions, information is collected from multiple data sources, pre- and post-tax wages and workload performance are calculated, attendance data is maintained, and absentee pay is calculated, completing the automatic calculation and payment of payroll. Batch editing services are provided, comprehensive records of payroll project operations are recorded, and new employee payroll information is supported. Payroll file management features import and export functions, enabling flexible and dynamic enterprise payroll configuration. A standardized login portal is implemented, user roles are determined by querying information tables, and a hierarchical authorization process is implemented, gradually allocating permissions from system administrators to department administrators to ensure payroll data security. Test results show that the research system effectively improves system throughput in multi-concurrency scenarios, highly guarantees salary calculation accuracy in multi-dimensional salary calculation scenarios, and rationally optimizes CPU resource utilization during high-concurrency testing, effectively meeting the needs of enterprise salary management.

title: A Performance Evaluation of Flask (WSGI) and Fastapi (ASGI) Under High-Concurrency Workloads
authors: Mohammed Alaa Ala'anzy, Zhandos Yeshpatov, Adil Akhmetov, Ramis Akhmedov, Omar Alnaseri
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Python web development is experiencing a paradigm shift from the traditional Synchronous Web Server Gateway Interface (WSGI) to the modern Asynchronous Server Gateway Interface (ASGI). While WSGI frameworks like Flask have existed for a long time as the industry standard for microservices, their blocking architecture inherently restricts performance under high-concurrency workloads. This research fills this specific gap completely by introducing a rigorous quantitative benchmark comparing Flask WSGI with FastAPI (ASGI). Through the construction of two functionally identical applications and rigorous controlled I/O-bound load testing using Apache JMeter, throughput, latency, and error rates are measured. Results show a stark contrast in stability; ASGI kept 100% reliability, WSGI failed by 76% under identical load. These findings have actionable implications for software developers that moving to asynchronous frameworks is not just an adaptation but a requirement in our current and high-throughput API environment.

title: From APIs to Semantic Agents: Evolving Enterprise Platforms through Knowledge-Driven AI
authors: Sureshkumar Karuppuchamy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Modern enterprise systems expose functionality through APIs, yet these interfaces remain static and unaware of business context. This paper introduces a knowledge-driven semantic agent architecture that transforms traditional APIs into intelligent, goal-oriented services by combining large language models (LLMs) with symbolic reasoning and enterprise knowledge. The framework defines a semantic contract for each API (linking all inputs and outputs to an ontology) and embeds a neuro-symbolic control loop that interprets user intent, applies domain knowledge and business rules, and dynamically plans actions. A prototype was developed in realistic e-commerce scenarios (free-form product listing and adaptive order handling) to evaluate feasibility. Methodologically, two agent-augmented API services were evaluated on 40 test cases (20 per scenario) against baseline implementations, measuring task success, policy compliance, and latency. The semantic agents consistently achieved higher success rates ($\sim 90 \%$ task completion) with no observed policy violations, while introducing only a modest latency overhead of a few hundred milliseconds-well within acceptable ranges for enterprise workflows. These results demonstrate that knowledge-driven agents can significantly improve automation, adaptability, and explainability in enterprise platforms. The paper details the system architecture, implementation, and quantitative evaluation, and discusses how semantic contracts and neuro-symbolic reasoning enable “APIs that think” in an AI-powered enterprise.

title: Emergency Power Dispatch Method for Grid-Connected Microgrids under Islanded Operation Mode
authors: Bing Yu, Xiaolei Ma, Ran Liu, Xiangtao Yu, Pengfei Duan, Yandong Yang, Hui Huang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: A crude dispatch method based on local optimization can only allocate and adjust power based on simple preset logic and local information, resulting in low dispatch efficiency. To address this issue, an emergency power dispatch method for grid-connected microgrids under islanded operation mode is proposed. Leveraging a multi-source data fusion recognition framework, a joint Kalman filter algorithm is used to correct and fuse historical microgrid operating data, extract state features, and then employ a naive Bayesian algorithm to identify emergency states in the grid-connected microgrid. By comprehensively considering the impact of emergency states on microgrid power supply reliability, equipment safety, and load satisfaction, a proposed priority determination formula, combined with the influence coefficients and weights of various factors, clearly defines emergency state priorities. A two-stage optimization strategy is proposed. In the emergency response phase, a function is constructed to minimize grid losses and actions are taken based on priority. In the task scheduling phase, multi-objective optimization is implemented to differentiate between different priority states, achieving emergency power dispatch. Experiments show that this method enables rapid convergence of node voltage adjustment consistency variables within a relatively small number of iterations, ensuring high coordination and flexible response among components under complex operating conditions, and maintaining a high overall system full-service rate. Compared to comparable methods, this method reduces the probability of system power outages by an average of approximately $\mathbf{5 0 \% - 7 0 \%}$ across all scenarios, achieving efficient and stable emergency power dispatch for grid-connected microgrids operating under islanded operation.

title: A Family of Single-Stage Isolated Bridgeless Cuk PFC Rectifiers with Reduced Current Stress
authors: Hui Ma, Meng Yue, Yutian Li, Chen Yang, Hengshan Xu, Yuehua Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Single-stage topologies are highly desirable in power factor correction (PFC) applications due to their compact structure and high efficiency. This paper proposes a family of single-stage isolated bridgeless Cuk PFC rectifiers with reduced current stress. Based on the totem-pole bridgeless structure and the Cuk circuit, four modified switching cells (MSCs) are introduced, and a corresponding current decoupling pulse distribution strategy is designed for the MSCs. During the energy storage stage of inductors, this strategy can alter the freewheeling path of the output inductor, and decouple the common current path between the input and output inductors, which exists in the conventional Cuk circuit, thereby significantly reducing the current stress and conduction losses of the semiconductors. In addition, the presented circuits feature a simple structure and require a simple control scheme. The operational principle, design and selection of key components, and the design of a dual closed-loop digital controller are presented under continuous conduction mode. Finally, simulations and two 250 W laboratory experimental prototypes are implemented to validate the feasibility and reduced current stress characteristics of the proposed rectifiers. Experimental results demonstrate that the prototype achieves a peak efficiency of 96.15%, a current THD of 3.35%, and a power factor of 0.997.

title: LoRa Communication over Time-Frequency Doubly Selective Channels
authors: Jiaojiao Liu, Peng Huang, Zheng Wang, Biyun Ma, Quansheng Guan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: In response to diverse applications of the Internet of Things (IoT), this paper analyzes the error mechanisms caused by carrier frequency offset (CFO) and multipath effect in LoRa symbol detection, and combines them to systematically discuss the LoRa performance over time-frequency doubly selective channels. From the perspective of energy leakage, the interference caused by CFO and multipath effects in demodulation is collectively referred to as combined interference (CI) and can be modeled as a Gaussian distribution. Then the study quantitatively analyzes the CI impact on symbol detection, derives an approximate Bit Error Rate (BER) formula, and verifies the theoretical model using Monte Carlo simulations. Moreover, by evaluating the resistance of various schemes against CFO and multipath effects, the phase rotation based CFO estimation and compensation method (PREC) and its advantages against other schemes are explored. Experiment results show that in unsynchronized scenarios, phase-rotation-induced energy convergence in PREC still yields accurate CFO estimation and good mobile robustness, particularly for a small preamble number.

title: A 28-nm System-in-One-Macro Computing-in-Memory Chip Utilizing Leakage-Eliminated 2T1C and Capacitor-Over-Logic 1T1C eDRAM
authors: Zhihang Qian, Zhaori Cong, Shengzhe Yan, Zeyu Guo, Zhuoyu Dai, Yifan He, Wenyu Sun, Chunmeng Dou, Feng Zhang, Di Geng, Jinshan Yue, Yongpan Liu, Qi Liu, Ming Liu
publish: IEEE Journal of Solid-State Circuits
abstract: Computing-in-memory (CIM) is a promising paradigm for energy- and area-efficient implementation of the heavy general matrix multiplication (GEMM) operations, especially in the evolving deep learning algorithms. Though existing CIM macros have demonstrated remarkable energy/area efficiency, the corresponding metrics of the system-level CIM chips degrade due to the peripheral components, including external SRAM and accumulation circuits. First, the separated design of CIM macros and peripheral components leads to suboptimal solutions, suffering high input/output SRAM access power, loose layout with long data paths, bulk clock trees, and so on. Second, the input/output SRAM takes a majority area proportion, which is inevitable in existing system-level CIM chips. Besides, continuous power/area optimization on the CIM macro itself also contributes to the system-level energy/area efficiency. Third, the accumulation module after the CIM macro leads to redundant power consumption due to the unnecessary high-bit-position computation. This work presents a system-in-one-macro CIM chip, integrating all system-level components (except the control logic) into one single macro, indicating a significant difference in the design flow with three detailed innovations: 1) to replace the power/area consuming external SRAMs, a high-density leakage-eliminated 2T1C embedded DRAM (eDRAM) is designed for input activation and output result storage; 2) a capacitor (cap)-over-logic one-transistor-one-capacitor (1T1C) eDRAM array with shared-preprocessing MUX-based CIM circuits is proposed to further improve the macro-level CIM energy/area efficiency and density; and 3) utilizing the low-activity property of the high-bit-position accumulation, a low-MAC-aware near-memory-computing (NMC) circuit is designed to reduce the redundant accumulation power. The 28-nm fabricated system-in-one-macro CIM chip demonstrates 51.6 TOPS/W energy efficiency, 1.53 TOPS/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> area efficiency, and 2.22-Mb/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> storage density.

title: RankFL: Robustness and Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning Scheme Against Poisoning Attacks_supp1-3672641.pdf
authors: Tom Luan
publish: N/A
abstract: Distinguishing between benign and poisoned gradients hidden behind cryptographic masks is a critical challenge in privacy-preserving federated learning (FL). Existing robust aggregation defenses suffer from two primary limitations: (1) susceptibility to manipulation, where adversaries induce deviations from standard protocols to bypass statistics-based defenses (e.g., mean or median), and (2) limited detection granularity, where the reliance on coarse statistics under encryption fails to identify subtle or coordinated poisoning behaviors. To address these issues, we propose RankFL, a poison-robust and privacy-preserving FL scheme that leverages order sorting over ciphertext gradients. RankFL utilizes an efficient Paillier-based two-party comparison protocol to construct a joint order tree, facilitating quartile-driven filtering of malicious updates without compromising individual gradient privacy. Furthermore, we introduce RankFL-Extend, which incorporates zero-knowledge proof-of-knowledge and bidirectional verification to secure the ranking process against active adversaries. We provide a rigorous theoretical analysis to establish the scheme's privacy, indistinguishability, and convergence guarantees. Extensive experiments across diverse datasets and attack scenarios demonstrate that the proposed scheme achieves a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> accuracy improvement over state-of-the-art defenses under poisoning attacks.

title: Filtering Markov Jump Systems with Partially Known Dynamics: A Model-Based Deep Learning Approach
authors: George Stamatelis, George C. Alexandropoulos
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal Processing
abstract: This paper presents the Markov Jump Filtering Network (MJFNet), a novel model-based deep learning framework for real-time state-state estimation in Markov jump systems with unknown noise statistics and mode transition dynamics. A hybrid architecture comprising two Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) is proposed: one for mode prediction and another for filtering that is based on a mode-augmented version of the recently presented KalmanNet architecture. The proposed RNNs are trained jointly using an alternating least squares strategy that enables mutual adaptation without supervision of the latent modes. Extensive numerical experiments on linear and nonlinear systems, including target tracking, pendulum angle tracking, Lorenz attractor dynamics, and a real-life dataset demonstrate that the proposed MJFNet framework outperforms classical model-based filters (e.g., interacting multiple models and particle filters) as well as model-free deep learning baselines, particularly in non-stationary and high-noise regimes. It is also showcased that MJFNet achieves a small yet meaningful improvement over the KalmanNet framework, which becomes much more pronounced in complicated systems or long trajectories. Finally, the method’s performance is empirically validated to be consistent and reliable, exhibiting low sensitivity to initial conditions, hyperparameter selection, as well as to incorrect model knowledge.
