title: Machine Learning Algorithm for Tourism Analytics Using Google Travel Review Data
authors: Mohammad Alsulami, Samuel-Soma M. Ajibade
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Social and web analytics have become increasingly important with the rapid growth of online review platforms, where large volumes of user-generated data provide valuable insights into customer preferences and behavior. In the tourism domain, effectively analyzing such high-dimensional review data remains challenging due to its complexity and lack of explicit labels. This study explores the use of unsupervised machine learning techniques to identify patterns in user satisfaction based on Google Travel review ratings across 24 tourism-related categories, including resorts, restaurants, parks, and cultural attractions. Four clustering algorithms KMeans, Agglomerative Clustering, DBSCAN, and Gaussian Mixture Models (GMM) are applied and comparatively evaluated. A real-world dataset obtained from the UCI Machine Learning Repository is used alongside a synthetically generated dataset to assess model robustness under controlled conditions. Data preprocessing and dimensionality reduction using Principal Component Analysis (PCA) are performed to support visualization and interpretation. Clustering performance is evaluated using Silhouette Scores and PCA-based visual analysis. The results indicate that centroid-based methods, particularly K-Means, produce more balanced and interpretable clusters on real-world data, while DBSCAN demonstrates superior performance on synthetic data with clear density structures and noise. These findings highlight the importance of aligning clustering techniques with data characteristics and contribute to the understanding of user segmentation in tourism analytics. The study provides insights that can support customer profiling, personalized recommendations, and data-driven decision-making in digital marketing and tourism management.

title: Comparative Analysis of Embedding Strategies for Turkish Sentiment Analysis
authors: Ayaulym Parmash, Zhibek Myrzaliyeva, Selcuk Cankurt
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Sentiment analysis for the Turkish language is particularly challenging due to the language's agglutinative nature, its complex morphology, and the relative paucity of high-quality labeled datasets compared to English. This paper presents a comprehensive comparative study of different feature representation methods and classification algorithms for Turkish sentiment analysis. We benchmark a traditional machine learning baseline-TF-IDF combined with Random Forest-against several Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) deep learning models. To address lexical sparsity, we examine four embedding strategies: randomly initialized embedding layers, custom Word2Vec embeddings trained on our corpus, and pre-trained Turkish FastText models in both trainable and non-trainable configurations. Our experiments, conducted on three publicly available datasets, demonstrate that LSTM models consistently outperform the statistical baseline, providing a significant accuracy boost over TF-IDF. Notably, the non-trainable pre-trained Turkish FastText embeddings achieved the best performance, reaching a peak accuracy of 95.33%. These results indicate that leveraging large-scale external resources is particularly effective for morphologically rich languages, compared to relying solely on task-specific data. Moreover, freezing the weights of a pre-trained model can help prevent overfitting, yielding more robust generalization. This study provides a clear roadmap for researchers and practitioners in selecting the most effective embedding models for Turkish natural language processing tasks.

title: Performance Analysis of Wireless Communication to Remote Client User of Battery Management System Using HM10 Module
authors: Monika Verma, Devender Kumar, Madhusudan Singh, Dhratika Singh, Mini Sreejeth
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: With the motivation of eliminating the need of wired interfaces, this paper presents the design and implementation of wireless communication in a battery management system (BMS) using a Bluetooth-enabled scheme (BES), called BES-BMS. An HM-10 Bluetooth module is integrated with an ST32 microcontroller, enabling the realtime data transmission to a consumer device. A Galaxy M30S (model: SM-M307F/DS) is used to run the Serial Bluetooth Terminal application, allowing for the display of essential battery parameters, including charging status, state of charge (SoC), and alert notifications. The experimental results confirm that the proposed system is reliable and accurately provides instantaneous visual information via on-board LED indicators. This work makes a significant contribution to making the BMS user-friendly and continuously accessible for the remote monitoring of Electric Vehicle (EV) batteries. It also bridges the communication gap in BES-BMS by enabling seamless and real-time interaction between the battery system and the user, as needed.

title: Predicting Solar Power Generation Using Machine Learning
authors: Diyor Khabibullayev, Khaled Mohamad
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: The growing integration of photovoltaic systems into modern power grids requires accurate solar power generation forecasting to support reliable supply-demand balance and efficient energy management. In recent years, the rapid expansion of machine learning applications in this field has led to substantial methodological diversity, which complicates direct comparison across studies. This paper presents a systematic review of peerreviewed research on machine-learning-based solar power forecasting. The analysis focuses on commonly used input features, modeling approaches, evaluation metrics, and reporting practices across different geographic regions. The review shows that deep learning methods, particularly long short-term memory (LSTM) networks, are increasingly adopted, while traditional approaches such as linear regression and random forest remain widely utilized. Meteorological variables - including temperature, solar irradiance, humidity, and wind speed - consistently form the core input features. However, considerable inconsistencies in reporting practices are observed. Geographic information is often insufficiently detailed, and standardized or normalized evaluation metrics that would enable cross-study comparison are rarely applied. Several important research gaps are identified, including limited model generalization across locations, insufficient incorporation of uncertainty quantification, and inadequate validation under real operational conditions. In addition, Central Asian countries, including Kazakhstan, are entirely absent from the reviewed literature despite their significant solar energy potential. Overall, the findings highlight the need for more standardized evaluation protocols, expanded geographic representation, and improved reporting transparency in future research.

title: Research on Torque Characteristics and Control of a Dual-Rotor Flux-Switching Permanent-Magnet Motor
authors: Chen Liu, Dewei Xu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Adjusting the geometric structure of a fluxswitching permanent-magnet motor can reduce torque ripple. This paper focuses on the dual-rotor flux-switching permanent-magnet motor (DR-FSPM), which consists of a stator and two rotors. The outer rotor delivers the primary torque output, while the inner rotor is used to modulate the torque. The torque and loss characteristics of the motor are investigated through parametric analysis. Torque ripple can be effectively affected by controlling the speed difference between the two rotors. These results provide a foundation for further research into the output characteristics of DR-FSPMs.

title: Truth in the Age of AI: Fine-Tuning LLMs for Reliable Fake News Detection
authors: Sajan Poudel, Nazmus Sadat, G. G. Md. Nawaz Ali
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: With the rapid rise of social media platforms, people increasingly rely on them not only for entertainment but also as a primary source of news. However, the authenticity of information shared on these platforms is of paramount importance, as misinformation can have catastrophic societal consequences. In this paper, we focus on detecting fake news with high confidence using fine-tuned large language models (LLMs). Leveraging an extensive open-source news dataset, we fine-tuned both open-source and proprietary LLMs to classify news articles as real or fake. Our experiments demonstrate that models such as RoBERTa (open-source) and GPT (proprietary) achieve significantly improved performance after fine-tuning compared to their original pre-trained versions. The evaluation, conducted using standard metrics including accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score, highlights the effectiveness of fine-tuning in enhancing model performance. The model training cost is also considered in the comparative performance analysis. The findings of this study provide a foundation for future research on LLM-based misinformation detection and offer valuable insights for advancing the development of reliable AI-driven solutions to combat fake news.

title: Zeppelin: Balancing Variable-length Workloads in Data Parallel Large Model Training
authors: Chang Chen, Tiancheng Chen, Jiangfei Duan, Qianchao Zhu, Zerui Wang, Qinghao Hu, Peng Sun, Xiuhong Li, Chao Yang, Torsten Hoefler
publish: Proceedings of the 21st European Conference on Computer Systems

title: Distributed Anomaly Detection and Protection Mechanism Integrating Blockchain and Federated Learning
authors: Ruilin Huang, Junyu Ren
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Distributed systems such as industrial IoT, cyber-physical production lines, and edge clouds increasingly suffer from data silos, privacy leakage, weak generalization to unseen anomalies, and model tampering during collaborative analytics. This paper proposes a blockchain-enhanced federated learning (BC-FL) mechanism for distributed anomaly detection and protection. Without sharing raw data, each node trains a local anomaly model and uploads encrypted and signed updates. Smart contracts enforce format validation, rate limiting, and provenance logging, while an auditable aggregation pipeline securely iterates the global model. To strengthen robustness against unknown anomalies and adversarial participants, we combine adaptive thresholding with robust aggregation and a lightweight reputation mechanism, and accelerate response through parallel edge execution. A prototype evaluation shows that the proposed design improves detection robustness while preserving data privacy and providing tamper-evident model lifecycle management, which is suitable for IoT and industrial interconnection scenarios and can be extended to smart city and medical IoT deployments.

title: Data-Driven Reconstruction of Latent User Preferences in Hybrid Ranking Systems: A Constraint-Satisfaction Monte Carlo Approach
authors: Chaoyi Guo, Zhikang Fan, Ruiqin Bai
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: In modern hybrid evaluation systems—ranging from competitive reality shows to crowdsourced content moderation—final rankings are often determined by aggregating expert scores with public popularity votes. However, a critical challenge in auditing the fairness and dynamics of such systems is data sparsity: typically, only the expert scores and the final elimination outcomes are observable, while the exact distribution of public votes remains a “black box.” This paper formulates the recovery of these hidden votes as an Inverse Ranking Problem (IRP) within an incomplete information system [5]. We propose a novel Constraint-Satisfaction Monte Carlo (CS-MC) framework to reconstruct the latent vote distributions. Our method integrates Sequential Bayesian Updating to model [2] the temporal momentum of user preferences and employs a Rejection Sampling mechanism to enforce strict historical elimination constraints. Experimental results on a real-world dataset (34 seasons of Dancing with the Stars) demonstrate that our model recovers latent voting patterns with 99.70% consistency regarding historical outcomes. Furthermore, we introduce an uncertainty quantification metric, revealing that eliminated candidates exhibit significantly lower entropy in their vote distributions compared to survivors. This study provides a generalizable computational framework for inferring latent variables in rank-based decision systems.

title: Mitigating Judge-Audience Scoring Discrepancies in DWTS: A Data-Driven Optimization Approach
authors: Yuhan Zhang, Jianmei Cao
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: The U.S. Dancing with the Stars (DWTS) has long been troubled by scoring controversies, as contestants with relatively weak technical performance often achieve high rankings due to the combination of professional judges’ scores and public fan voting. Based on the dataset across 34 seasons, this paper investigates four core research issues using data-driven modeling approaches: (1) This paper proposes an optimization model that combines constraint satisfaction, the maximum entropy principle, and cross-week smoothing to estimate fan voting proportions. By mapping the official rule of “combined score $=$ judge score + fan vote” into a set of inequality constraints, the model achieves a perfect elimination reproduction accuracy (Acc) of $\mathbf{1. 0}$ and a low constraint violation rate (Viol) of 0.09. (2) Ridge regression analysis demonstrates that professional dancers exert a 3.2-fold greater influence on judges’ scores than on fan votes. A dynamic weighting-threshold system is further constructed to reduce the advancement rate of controversial contestants-those with inferior technical performance but high fan votes from $28 \%$ to $9 \%$. (3) Dual parallel regression confirms a weak positive correlation between judges’ and audiences’ preferences (Spearman’s $\rho=0.22$, direction agreement rate $=0.55$), indicating that judges prioritize technical proficiency whereas audiences focus on contestant popularity. (4) Finally, a novel integrated scoring system incorporating standardized score normalization, dynamic weight aggregation and dual-threshold stratified intervention is developed. This system achieves an $88.3 \%$ elimination judgment success rate in the 264 -week historical validation, with fully defined boundary conditions and elimination logic. The proposed methodologies provide an optimized solution for DWTS’s scoring system design and are scalable to professional evaluation systems and public voting scenarios of other competitions.

title: A Data-Driven Statistical Framework for Scalable Dynamic Resource Rebalancing: Two-Layer Modeling and Experience-Guided Optimization
authors: He Pan
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Dynamic rebalancing in shared mobility systems requires decisions that adapt to time-varying demand, spatial imbalance, and evolving inventory states. Rolling-horizon optimization is widely used, yet station-level MILP formulations scale poorly in real time, and independently solved rolling windows lead to redundant computation and unstable decisions. This paper proposes a closed-loop rolling optimization framework that integrates structural dimensionality reduction with solver-level warm-start inheritance. A two-layer MILP architecture decomposes the problem into zone-level coordination and station-level feasibility restoration, significantly reducing model size while preserving operational fidelity. A demand-aware warm-start mechanism transfers validated solutions across rolling windows to enhance continuity without altering objectives or solver internals, and an adaptive zoning module dynamically updates spatial aggregation under non-stationary demand. Simulation results show that the two-layer formulation reduces lost demand from $\mathbf{7 . 4} \boldsymbol{\%}$ to $\mathbf{3 . 7} \boldsymbol{\%}$ compared with a station-level rolling MILP. With warm-start, lost demand further decreases to $2.1 \%$ while average solve time drops from 1.21 s to 1.11 s per window. Under irregular demand, adaptive zoning achieves up to $\mathbf{3 1} \boldsymbol{\%}$ additional reduction in lost demand and nearly halves computation time. These results demonstrate improved scalability and robustness while maintaining compatibility with standard optimization pipelines.

title: Multimodal Late Fusion for Obstructive Sleep Apnea Phenotyping and Risk Stratification
authors: Itunuoluwa Ayo-Durojaiye, Aijuan Dong, Sophia Isabel P. Ferrer, Silvia Crivelli, Daniel Coblentz, Cheng Qian
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Obstructive sleep apnea (OSA) is a sleep disorder associated with elevated cardiovascular and metabolic risks. This study presents a multimodal late-fusion framework to identify OSA patients most at risk using data from the MIMIC-IV database. Structured electronic health record data were encoded with an autoencoder trained through a joint reconstruction and classification loss, while unstructured discharge notes were represented by topic distributions extracted with BERTopic using BioClinicalBERT embeddings. The fused data were combined, reduced with UMAP, and clustered using HDBSCAN. Results show that multimodal fusion outperformed single-modality approaches, achieving a Silhouette Score of 0.8186 and an Adjusted Rand Index of 0.2802, compared with 0.8025/0.2797 for structured data and 0.4878 / -0.0012 for unstructured data. These findings highlight the synergistic value of multimodal data and its potential to advance precision medicine.

title: Improving Multi-turn Task Completion in Task-Oriented Dialog Systems via Prompt Chaining and Fine-Grained Feedback
authors: Moghis Fereidouni, Md Sajid Ahmed, Adib Mosharrof, A.B. Siddique
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Task-oriented dialog (TOD) systems facilitate users in accomplishing complex, multi-turn tasks through natural language. While instruction-tuned large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong performance on a range of single-turn NLP tasks, they often struggle with reliable multi-turn task completion in TOD settings, particularly when generating API calls required to interact with external systems. To address this, we introduce RealTOD, a novel framework that improves LLM-based TOD systems through (1) prompt chaining and (2) fine-grained feedback. Prompt chaining enables zero-shot generalization to new domains by automatically synthesizing a schema-aligned in-context example for the target task. Fine-grained feedback verifies each generated API call against the domain schema, identifies specific errors, and provides targeted correction prompts. To evaluate task completion reliability, we introduce full API Call Accuracy as a robust metric, along with detailed sub-metrics to capture common failure modes. We conduct extensive experiments on the SGD and BiTOD benchmarks using four LLMs. RealTOD improves Full API accuracy, surpassing state-of-the-art AutoTOD by 37.10% on SGD and supervised learning-based baseline SimpleTOD by $\mathbf{1 0. 3 2 \%}$ on BiTOD. Human evaluations further confirm that LLMs integrated with RealTOD achieve superior task completion, fluency, and informativeness compared to existing methods.

title: Can Large Language Models Rank Tabular Data Fairly?
authors: Oluseun Olulana, Kathleen Cachel, Fabricio Murai, Elke Rundensteiner
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: As large language models (LLMs) are increasingly applied to critical tasks, it is imperative to understand both the promise and the risks of LLM use cases that can directly impact humans, such as ranking and, in particular, fair re-ranking of applicants for jobs or college admissions. Similar to traditional ranking solutions, LLM outputs risk reflecting societal biases from the training data. In many social applications, the data to be ranked is tabular, yet such an input format is not optimized for LLM consumption (in contrast to textual documents). This raises the question: Can LLMs be designed to fairly re-rank tabular data while preserving utility? To address this open question, we design a new evaluation methodology, titled Fairer-TabLLM. FAIRER-TABLLM incorporates a simple but effective serialization strategy to transform tabular rankings into a structured input representation ready for LLM consumption. Our modular prompting strategy gives users the option to explicitly state a fairness objective, choosing between (1) exposure fairness and (2) representation fairness. Our results show that, under carefully specified prompts, LLMs can achieve fairness levels comparable to traditional algorithms (e.g., DetConstSort) while maintaining ranking utility. Additionally, incorporating shots to leverage in-context learning consistently improves fairness outcomes. Source code and experimental artifacts are available here: https://github.com/sewen007/FaiReRTabLLM

title: Machine Learning-Based Calculation and Analysis of LCA Transformer Carbon Footprint
authors: Fengzhu Zheng, Tian Gao, Lin Deng, Yuanyuan Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Against the backdrop of global energy transition and climate change mitigation, the power industry, being a major carbon-emitting sector, requires rigorous carbon footprint management for its critical equipment transformers. This study examines the carbon footprint of transformers using life cycle assessment combined with artificial intelligence and machine learning. It develops a model covering five lifecycle stages: raw material acquisition (including production and transportation), manufacturing, distribution, operation, and end-of-life recycling. The research identifies key emission sources, with raw material acquisition (mainly silicon steel and copper production) contributing the most emissions and operational emissions correlating strongly with transformer efficiency. Based on these insights, the study proposes decarbonization strategies, including green supply chain management, manufacturing optimization, low-carbon transportation, operational efficiency enhancement, and circular end-of-life solutions. The findings offer scientific support for the transformer industry’s lowcarbon transformation and practical guidance for corporate decarbonization, advancing sustainable energy development globally.

title: Approximate Inverse Model Explanations as a Semantic Evaluator for Large Language Models
authors: Takafumi Nakanishi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are crucial in knowledge-intensive applications; however, systematic and explainable methods for evaluating their outputs are limited. We propose an explainable evaluation framework that adapts Approximate Inverse Model Explanations (AIME) to LLMs. Using 600 QA-style prompts (200 HotpotQA-distractor, 200 NQ-Open, 200 WikiQA), our method estimates inverse sensitivities between prompt design factors and multiple quality axes, focusing on offline-computable axes (F_grounded, F_actual, length, cite_line, cite_url). The default proxy is BM25 + embeddings, with ablations including BM25-only and entailment-inspired proxies. The results show stable association patterns: prompts for WIKIQA-like and NQ-like domains are positively associated with factuality, whereas FEVER-like prompts are negatively associated, and temperature and top p exhibit non-significant effects (with the $\mathbf{9 5 \%}$ CI crossing zero). AIME yields four artifacts—global importance, local attribution, representative prototypes, and similarity plots—providing a unified semantic evaluation layer. The coefficients represent correlational associations and not causality. This framework enhances the transparency and reproducibility of semantic computing. Although the framework is generally applicable to diverse generative tasks, this study focuses on QA-style prompts and five offline-computable axes (F_grounded, F_actual, length, cite_line, cite_url) owing to API availability constraints. Therefore, the presented results should be interpreted as proof-of-concept for an explainable, multidimensional evaluation rather than as an exhaustive benchmark.

title: A Meta-Learning Prediction Framework for Collocation Acceptability Based on Fused L1 Transfer
authors: Jiyan Liu, Guanyu Jiang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Collocation acceptability prediction in second language learners is complicated by native language transfer effects, which conventional BERT fine-tuning often overlooks. Meta-L1 is introduced as a meta-learning framework that dynamically adapts BERT's parameters based on L1 typological features to predict collocation acceptability. The model employs an L1 feature encoder, a parameter generator, and bilevel optimization to produce L1-specific adjustments without per-learner retraining. Evaluations across eight L1 groups show that Meta-L1 outperforms static baselines, especially for underrepresented languages, while aligning with psycholinguistic patterns of cross-linguistic influence. The approach offers a scalable, personalized solution for L2 collocation analysis.

title: Unveiling Customer Technostress in Indonesian Mobile Banking: A Topic Modeling and Sentiment Analysis of User Reviews
authors: Goesniawan Xena Adikara, Muhardi Saputra, Riska Yanu Fa'rifah
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: This paper studies customer technostress by analyzing a total of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0, 0 0 0}$</tex> reviews on users of the application BCA Mobile-among the top applications for mobile banking in Indonesia-adopting a comprehensive Knowledge Discovery in Databases (KDD) analytic framework. Topic modeling with Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) and sentiments analyzed lexicon-based, nine latent topics were extracted that synthesized five key operational themes. The findings reveal a critical paradox in user experience: while the “User Satisfaction” aspect indicates high trust in the application's core financial utility, this positive reception is severely compromised by significant friction in “Login & Access Security.” Biometric authentication failures were identified as the primary driver of negative sentiment, confirming that strict security protocols unintentionally function as barriers to entry for legitimate users. The study concludes that to mitigate technostress, banking institutions must shift their strategic priority from feature expansion to stabilizing digital resilience, specifically by recalibrating authentication thresholds to balance security with usability.

title: Comparison Study: Glacier Calving Front Delineation in Synthetic Aperture Radar Images With Deep Learning_supp1-3685700.pdf
authors: Nora Gourmelon
publish: N/A
abstract: Continuous monitoring of glacier calving fronts is essential for sea level rise projections. This study benchmarks Deep Learning systems for front delineation in Synthetic Aperture Radar imagery. While Deep Learning systems exhibit errors up to 221 m, human annotators deviate by only 38 m, underscoring the need for further research.

title: Identification and Observation of Spatiotemporal Evolution Features of Coalfield Fire Areas Using Landsat Time Series Decomposition_supp1-3685950.docx
authors: Jinchang Deng
publish: N/A
abstract: Globally uncontrolled and persistent coal fires cause substantial resource depletion and long-term environmental and safety risks. However, their long-term detection is hindered by complex subsurface dynamics, combustion behaviour and insufficient data from traditional monitoring methods. This study developed an integrated framework for the dynamic characterisation of fire hazards. The approach combines time-series decomposition, abrupt change analysis, and robust regression, using Landsat-derived land surface temperature (LST) time-series data (2013-2024) for the Miquan coalfield, Xinjiang, China. The LST was first retrieved from Landsat thermal infrared imagery via the Radiative Transfer Equation model. A hybrid Seasonal-Trend decomposition using Loess (STL) and Breaks for Additive Season and Trend (BFAST) model then separated trend, seasonal, and residual components, enabling the detection and quantification of abrupt LST changes related to coal fire dynamics. Thermal trends and anomalies were further quantified using the Mann–Kendall test, Sen’s slope, and a novel Annual Anomaly Deviation (AAD) metric. Crucially, using RANSAC-based regression, persistent potential fire pixels were extracted and categorised into four frequency regimes, with high robustness to noise and data gaps. The resulting coal fire frequency maps achieved over 77% spatial accuracy relative to field records, demonstrating enhanced continuity and reliability in long-term coal fire monitoring. The framework provides a versatile, pixel-level, and analytical paradigm for understanding the spatio-temporal evolution of these challenging landscape phenomena.

title: PointShuffler: Accelerating Point Cloud Neural Networks on General-Purpose GPUs

authors: Xiaohu Chai, Keyang Hu, Jianfeng Tan, Tiwei Bie, Guotao Tan, Tianyu Zhou, Anqi Shen, Dawei Shen, Xinyao Yang, Xin Chen, Xu Wang, Feng Yu, Zhengyu He, Dong Du, Yubin Xia, Kang Chen, Yu Chen

title: Evaluation of Grid-based Uncertainty Propagation for Collaborative Self-Calibration in Indoor Positioning Systems
authors: Paul Schwarzbach, Andrea Jung
publish: IEEE Journal of Indoor and Seamless Positioning and Navigation
abstract: Radio-based localization systems conventionally require stationary reference points (e.g. anchors) with precisely surveyed positions, making deployment time-consuming and costly. This paper presents an empirical evaluation of collaborative self-calibration for Ultra Wideband (UWB) networks, extending a Bayesian approach based on grid-based uncertainty propagation. The enhanced algorithm reduces measurement availability requirements while maintaining positioning accuracy through probabilistic state estimation. We validate the approach using real-world data from controlled indoor experiments with 12 nodes in a static environment. Experimental evaluation yields 0.28 m mean ranging error under line-of sight conditions and 1.11 m overall ranging error across mixed propagation scenarios. Results confirm the algorithm's resilience to measurement noise and partial connectivity scenarios typical in industrial deployments. We evaluate algorithm robustness under NLOS-contaminated initialization, showing graceful accuracy degradation (median error 0.62–0.99 m) compared to closed-form methods that exhibit substantial performance collapse (median error up to 2.43 m). The findings contribute to automated UWB network initialization for indoor positioning applications, reducing infrastructure dependency compared to manual anchor calibration procedures.

title: Membership Deviation-Aware Attack Scheduling Mechanism and Its Secure Defense for Mining Truck Suspension Systems: Mode-Correlated Polynomial Framework and HIL Validation
authors: Jiayi Wang, Xiangpeng Xie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Fuzzy Systems
abstract: This paper proposes a unified attack-defense frame work to address security threats in the remote collaborative regulation of active mining truck suspension systems (AMTSSs) under cloud integration, while tackling challenges such as more cunning attack behaviors and inflexible defense responses under heavy loads. Firstly, a membership deviation-aware attack scheduling mechanism is formulated based on the target system's state perception from the attacker's perspective to overcome the limitation of existing attack models that often carry out indiscriminate attacks. By extracting system fluctuations from membership information, this strategy enables dynamic attack mode scheduling and thus boosts attack performance. Secondly, a polynomial-driven mode-correlated controller is correspondingly developed from the perspective of defenders, which integrates polynomial techniques and membership functions to enable attack mode determination and execution of differentiated defense strategies. Additionally, multiple offset correction tuning matrices (OCTMs) are established for the purpose of improving the flexibility of dynamic gain configuration, meeting the resilient control demands of AMTSSs under heavy-load attributes. Finally, the hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) experimental validation verifies the superiority of the integrated attack-defense scheme.

title: Quantum Communication Complexity of Regularized Linear Regression Protocols
authors: Sayaki Matsushita
publish: IEEE Transactions on Quantum Engineering
abstract: Linear regression is fundamental to statistical analysis and machine learning, but its application to large-scale datasets necessitates distributed computing. The problem also arises in quantum computing, where handling extensive data requires distributed approaches. This paper investigates distributed linear regression in the quantum coordinator model. Building upon the distributed quantum least squares protocol developed by Montanaro and Shao, I propose improved and extended quantum protocols for solving both ordinary (unregularized) and L2-regularized (Tikhonov) least squares problems. For ordinary least squares methods, my protocol reduces the quantum communication complexity compared to the previous protocol. In particular, this yields a quadratic improvement in the number of digits of precision required for the generated quantum states. This improvement is achieved by incorporating advanced techniques such as branch marking and branch-marked gapped phase estimation developed by Low and Su. Furthermore, I establish a setting for the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\ell _{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula>-regularized least squares problem specifically in the quantum coordinator model and derive its quantum communication complexity. I analyze the effect of regularization parameters on the quantum communication complexity.

title: Adviser-Actor-Critic: A Precision-oriented Reinforcement Learning Framework for Space Robotics Control
authors: Donghe Chen, Jiaxuan Yue, Yubin Peng, Lin Cheng, Shengping Gong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: High-precision control is critical for autonomous space robotics tasks such as space-pointing observation, debris removal, and on-orbit assembly, where even sub-millimeter or sub degree errors can jeopardize mission safety. Classical feedback controllers, such as PID, can effectively eliminate steady-state error but are for nonlinear multi-input multi-output systems, whereas deep reinforcement learning (DRL) offers strong real-time optimal decision-making and adaptability to complex dynamics but suffers from significant steady-state error due to neural approximation errors. This paper proposes an Adviser-Actor-Critic (AAC) framework that couples traditional control theory with RL through a dual-loop architecture featuring a lightweight PI-based adviser. During deployment, the adviser generates virtual goals that proactively compensate accumulated tracking errors, while a pre trained goal-conditioned actor handles complex dynamics without modification. A control-theoretic analysis shows that, under mild assumptions, AAC can eliminate steady-state error for a broad class of systems. Simulations across standard manipulation, dexterous hand benchmarks, and space-relevant docking scenarios demonstrate over 80% average steady-state error reduction across diverse RL backbones. Hardware experiments on a quadrotor platform validate AAC improves attitude regulation from 1° to 0.03° under realistic disturbances, highlighting its practicality for precision-critical space applications.

title: Application of End-to-End Speech Recognition and Scoring Algorithms in the Quantitative Analysis of Japanese Oral Fluency
authors: Yi Wei
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the increasing demand for Japanese learning, accurate assessment of oral Japanese fluency has become the key. Focusing on the quantification of spoken Japanese fluency, this article studies the end-to-end speech recognition and scoring algorithm. By collecting 5000 pieces of audio data covering different learners’ levels and various oral forms, and manually marking fluency scores by professional teachers, this article builds an experimental environment based on the mainstream deep learning framework. In this article, an end-to-end speech recognition model combining CNN (Convolutional Neural Network) and RNN (Recurrent Neural Network) is constructed, and a fluency scoring algorithm is designed by combining speech characteristics such as speech speed, pause and prosody, and optimization means such as data enhancement and attention mechanism are used. The results show that the accuracy of speech recognition is finally stable at about 92%, and the correlation coefficient between the fluency score of model prediction and manual labeling is 0.85. At different learners’ levels, the accuracy of fluency score for beginners is 80%, for intermediate students, 85%, and for advanced students, 88%, and it takes only 1.5 seconds to process 60 seconds of audio on average. The model is excellent in speech recognition accuracy, fluency score accuracy and operation efficiency, which provides a reliable technical means for oral Japanese teaching and evaluation.

title: Personalized timing optimization for non-invasive prenatal testing (NIPT) based on multi-model fusion and intelligent determination of chromosomal abnormalities in female fetuses
authors: Jiamin Zhuang, Huaying Sun, Weibin Zhang, Yankai Lin
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: The accuracy of non-invasive prenatal testing (NIPT) is influenced by the physiological characteristics of pregnant women, and the existing unified testing strategies have the problem of insufficient individual adaptability. This paper addresses the two core requirements of optimizing the testing time point and determining the chromosomal abnormalities of the female fetus, and constructs a multi-model fusion technology system: through Spearman correlation analysis and multiple linear regression, the key influencing factors of $\mathbf{Y}$ chromosome concentration (such as pregnant woman’s BMI - correlation coefficient - 0.1485, gestational age - correlation coefficient 0.0892) are identified (model $\mathbf{F}=12.65, \mathbf{p}\lt 0.001$); based on K-means clustering, pregnant women are divided into 3 groups (BMI intervals [20.7, 31.9), [31.9, 35.8), [35.8, 46.9]), combined with NSGA-II multiobjective optimization and enhanced random forest regression, the optimal testing time point for the low/middle BMI group is determined as 10.0 weeks, and for the high BMI group ($\geq 36$) as 12.0 weeks, with the compliance rate increasing from $24.1 \%-36.5 \%$ to $\mathbf{7 6. 4 \%}-\mathbf{9 5. 0 \%}$; for the problem of the lack of $\mathbf{Y}$ chromosome reference for the female fetus, an integrated model is constructed by combining XGBoost, SVM and MLP, through SMOTE oversampling and GC bias correction to handle sample imbalance and sequencing errors, the five-fold cross-validation shows that the model accuracy rate is $85.49 \%$, ROC-AUC is 0.6645, effectively improving the detection ability of chromosomal abnormalities such as T18, T21, etc. The personalized testing strategy and intelligent determination model proposed in this study maintain good robustness even with a $\pm 15 \%$ testing error, providing theoretical support and practical solutions for the precise application of clinical NIPT.

title: Privacy-Preserving Anomaly Detection in Cloud Services using Hierarchical Federated Learning with Differential Privacy
authors: Sijia Li, Bolin Chen, Yueting Li, Zhijun Wang, Yihan Xue, Chengda Xu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Identifying abnormal behaviors plays a vital role in ensuring cloud infrastructure remains secure and operationally stable. Conventional methods that aggregate data at a single location create substantial privacy concerns, especially within shared cloud platforms hosting multiple organizations with confidential operational information. This paper proposes HierFedDP, a hierarchical federated learning framework integrated with a two-stage differential privacy mechanism for privacy-preserving anomaly detection in cloud services. Our approach employs a three-tier architecture consisting of local clients, edge servers, and a central cloud server, where clients apply local differential privacy to their updates before transmission. We introduce an edge aggregation frequency parameter that enables edge servers to perform multiple local aggregation rounds before communicating with the central cloud. Experiments on the CICIDS2017 dataset demonstrate that HierFedDP achieves detection performance comparable to standard local differential privacy approaches while reducing wide-area network (WAN) communication overhead by $49 \%$. This significant communication reduction, achieved without sacrificing privacy guarantees or detection accuracy, makes HierFedDP particularly suitable for bandwidth-constrained, geo-distributed cloud deployments.

title: Semantics of Aviation Data
authors: Jon C. Haass, Joseph R. Barr
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Modern air traffic management increasingly relies on satellite-based navigation and surveillance, with GPSintegrated systems providing high-precision positioning data for aircraft separation, routing, and collision avoidance. However, the growing vulnerability of GPS to deliberate or unintentional denial-of-service (DoS) events poses significant operational risks, particularly in congested airspace where loss of positional integrity can rapidly escalate into unsafe proximity. This work investigates a synchronous broadcast-based collision-avoidance framework designed to maintain situational awareness and aircraft separation during partial or complete GPS outages. The ADS-B system enables aircraft to exchange state vectors-such as velocity, heading, and inertial-derived position-over a resilient, low-latency communication channel independent of satellite navigation infrastructure. By aligning transmissions through a synchronized time base and incorporating redundancy across multiple sensors, the architecture provides robust relative positioning even under degraded satellite navigation conditions. Air Traffic Control (ATC) systems tie into the data from radar, pilot to ground radio communications and the automated broadcast signals. [8].

title: Generating Hierarchical JSON Representations of Scientific Sentences Using LLMs
authors: Satya Sri Rajiteswari Nimmagadda, Ethan Young, Niladri Sengupta, Ananya Jana, Aniruddha Maiti
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: This paper investigates whether structured representations can preserve the meaning of scientific sentences. To test this, a lightweight LLM is fine-tuned using a novel structural loss function to generate hierarchical JSON structures from sentences collected from scientific articles. These JSONs are then used by a generative model to reconstruct the original text. Comparing the original and reconstructed sentences using semantic and lexical similarity we show that hierarchical formats are capable of retaining information of scientific texts effectively.

title: DRL-Adapt: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Adaptive Routing Convergence Optimization in Large-Scale Networks
authors: Boyuan Wang, Zimeng Wang, Wenyu Zhao, Fengyuan Zhang, Wenbin Shang
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Computer Society
abstract: Large-scale fully-meshed networks often experience prolonged routing convergence after failures because routing protocol parameters are statically configured and cannot adapt to changing network conditions. To address this limitation, this paper proposes DRL-Adapt, a deep reinforcement learning-based framework that dynamically tunes routing protocol parameters using network event-stream observations. The framework employs a dueling prioritized deep Q-network agent to jointly adjust Hello/Dead interval timers and routing weights according to real-time network states. We evaluate the proposed method using a high-fidelity simulator constructed from CAIDA topologies under diverse failure scenarios and network scales. Experimental results demonstrate that DRL-Adapt significantly accelerates recovery and reduces control overhead compared with conventional static configurations. On average, the proposed approach decreases fault convergence time by 32.7% and lowers control-plane overhead by 24.3%, while preserving routing stability. Moreover, the learned policy generalizes across networks ranging from 50 to 500 nodes and incurs minimal inference latency (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$< $</tex-math></inline-formula> 15 ms/decision), indicating practicality for practical deployment.

title: Automatic Similarity Feature Combination for Knowledge Graph Alignment via Adaptive-Designed Particle Swarm Optimization With Genetic Programming
authors: Xingsi Xue, Syed Muhammad Waqas, Wenxi Liu, Jerry Chun-Wei Lin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Knowledge Graphs (KGs) enable structured representation of domain-specific knowledge, but their integration is challenged by ontology heterogeneity due to varying terminologies and levels of granularity. KG alignment addresses this issue by identifying correspondences between semantically similar entities across diverse KGs, relying heavily on the effective combination of Similarity Features (SFs). However, manually determining the optimal combination of SFs is inefficient and often inaccurate, especially given the complex interactions among entities in different domains. To address this problem, this paper develops an Adaptive-Designed Particle Swarm Optimization (ADPSO) to automate SF combination for KG alignment. First, an adaptive framework is developed to dynamically adjust solution movement and evaluation rules, mitigating premature convergence. Second, a novel evaluation rule construction method is proposed, leveraging Genetic Programming (GP) to refine the fitness function, enhancing solution evaluation accuracy. Finally, a new velocity update mechanism is designed using a success update archive and GP, improving search efficiency. Experiments on diverse KG alignment tasks demonstrate that ADPSO significantly enhances matching accuracy and efficiency, achieving superior performance compared to state-of-the-art methods with an average f-measure of 0.90.

title: Clustering Based Neural Combinatorial Optimization for Scanning Path Planning of Heterogeneous Multi-Agent System
authors: Bocheng Zhao, Hang Shi, Jirou Feng, Bo Qi, Mingying Huo, Naiming Qi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: An effective solution method is proposed for the scanning path planning problem of heterogeneous multi-agent systems. Target mission is divided into multiple task clusters by density clustering to balance the task difficulty. Afterwards, the method innovatively first performs path planning for each task cluster, then systematically allocates tasks by utilizing mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) based on the planning outcomes and the diverse capabilities within the heterogeneous system, ensuring that agents possess unique adaptability to tasks that align with their specific capabilities. In the optimization solving phase, a neural combinatorial optimization (NCO) algorithm based on graph neural network (GNN) is proposed. Through supervised learning and cross-instance parallel training, the automation of heuristic design and the enhancement of heuristic rules are achieved, avoiding the inefficiency and suboptimality of expert design and manual parameter tuning. Simulation results show that the algorithm has better performance in terms of task completion time, execution time and deviation rate, showing its application potential in the rapid task planning problem of heterogeneous multi-agent systems.

title: Title Page iii
authors: N/A

title: Behavioral Conflict Modeling for AI-Enabled Intelligent Manufacturing: A Risk-Attitude Extension of Graph Model for Conflict Resolution
authors: Peide Liu, Qian Pan, Baoying Zhu, Peng Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management
abstract: Intelligent transformation in manufacturing enterprises (MEIT) aims to reduce costs, improve efficiency and quality, and enhance market competitiveness. However, conflicts arise among stakeholders in the artificial intelligence-enhanced MEIT (AI+MEIT) ecosystem because of divergent interests, unequal constraints, and heterogeneous responses to transformation-related risks and uncertainties. This study examines how heterogeneous risk attitudes can be incorporated into the Graph Model for Conflict Resolution (GMCR) for AI+MEIT conflict analysis. First, based on multi-criteria information, an aspiration-driven mechanism is developed to characterize stakeholders' perceived gains and losses across alternative states and to identify their risk attitudes from the decision environment. Second, risk-sensitive adjustment factors are introduced to capture differences in perceived gains and losses under heterogeneous risk attitudes. Third, a mixed-attitude equilibrium concept is proposed to support stability analysis under behavioral diversity. Finally, the improved GMCR framework is illustrated through an application example and sensitivity-based robustness analysis, showing how heterogeneous risk attitudes reshape conflict evolution paths and equilibrium patterns. The proposed framework extends GMCR in a risk sensitive direction and provides structured support for conflict analysis and decision-making in AI+MEIT.

title: Improved Wheel Synchronization in eCM-Based Electric Vehicles via Mode-Decoupled Steering Control Framework
authors: Insu Chung, Daesu Park, Minjun Kim, Kanghyun Nam
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: This article proposes a mode-decoupled control framework for steering position regulation in electronic corner modules based vehicles. In vehicles based on eCM, where each module integrates steering, braking, driving, and suspension, the absence of mechanical linkage can cause unequal steering responses between wheels and reduced driving stability. To address this issue, the proposed framework transforms the steering dynamics of individual wheels into two modal dynamics: a common steering motion and wheel synchronization. Based on this two-mode formulation, steering patterns are expressed as combinations of the two modal components. The experiments show improved steering angle reference tracking and reduced wheel synchronization error compared to conventional wheel-level control.

title: Radial Spectral Projection-Based Axial Localization Using an Encoded Pattern for High-Resolution Four-DOF Motion Measurement
authors: Jiyu Li, Shusheng Bi, Yuliang Wang
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: High-precision, multi degree-of-freedom (DOF) motion sensing across scales is critical for advanced mechatronic systems. Here, we propose a pseudoperiodic encoded pattern-based visual sensing method for four-DOF (X, Y, Z, and θZ) pose estimation. Lateral displacement and in-plane rotation are extracted via phase and spectral analysis, while axial localization is achieved by exploiting defocus-induced spectral variations combined with a template-matching strategy. Specifically, the two-dimensional (2D) frequency spectrum is converted into a 1D radial vector, which is then matched against a pre-calibrated, depth-dependent model to estimate the axial position. By applying principal component analysis, lateral-axial coupling is effectively suppressed. In addition, defocus-phase correction and a point spread function model associated with axial positions are incorporated for real-time image restoration, enabling robust lateral positioning under defocused imaging conditions. The proposed method supports decimeter-scale in-plane displacement measurement and in-plane rotation estimation, together with nanometer-level axial localization within a validated range of ±20 μm. Experimental results demonstrate root-mean-square resolutions of 0.57 nm, 0.61 nm, 1.18 nm, and 2.45 μrad along the X, Y, Z, and θZ axes, respectively. This approach provides an effective solution for ultrahigh-precision pose sensing and motion control across multiple scales.

title: Goal-Directed Multi-AP Coordination with Contextual Adaptation: A Hierarchical Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning Approach
authors: Wudan Han, Xianbin Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Multi-access point (AP) coordination has been recognized as a key enabling technique for next-generation densified Wi-Fi networks (IEEE 802.11bn) to achieve ultra-high reliability in dynamic environments. However, due to the fluctuating traffic patterns of heterogeneous service demands from coexisting stations (STAs), along with the ever-changing resource conditions and varying channel states, the coordination guided by conventional situation-agnostic optimization goals often leads to suboptimal performance or even counterproductive outcomes. Therefore, this paper proposes a novel goal-directed multi-AP multi-dimensional resource coordination scheme that explicitly incorporates a coordination goal adaptation process atop the distributed resource allocation mechanism. Specifically, to maximize the long-term multi-objective network performance, a temporally hierarchized two-level learning-empowered framework is developed. The coarse-timescale meta-level focuses on contextual aware coordination goal adaptation using the semi-Markov based proximal policy optimization (PPO) method, whereas the fine-timescale execution-level targets the collaborative resource allocation decisions for optimizing current goals, using the designed goal-conditioned multi-agent hybrid QMIX algorithm with parameterized actor networks. Furthermore, an alternate joint-training algorithm is designed to tackle the coupled non-stationarity and timescale disparity of the proposed two level solution. Simulations validate the operational effectiveness and situational responsiveness of the goal-directed coordination method under evolving network contexts and service demands in terms of both goal adaptation and goal realization.

title: Effective On-Hardware Fuzzing of Embedded Operating Systems
authors: Yuheng Shen, Jianzhong Liu, Qiming Guo, Yifei Chu, Qiang Zhang, Heyuan Shi, Yu Jiang
publish: Proceedings of the 21st European Conference on Computer Systems

title: Flexible Job Shop Scheduling via Multi-Attention Network Reinforcement Learning Considering Worker Skill Levels
authors: Dongdong Zhou, Xuesong Chen, Yang Xu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Aiming at the complex many-to-many optional relationships among operations, machines, and workers in the flexible job shop scheduling problem, as well as the shortcomings of existing scheduling methods in feature extraction and dynamic relationship modeling, We proposes a dual-resource flexible job shop scheduling optimization method that integrates multi-attention networks and deep reinforcement learning. The proposed framework fully integrates the strengths of self-attention models in feature extraction and the adaptive decision-making capabilities of reinforcement learning, thereby effectively capturing the multi-dimensional coupling relationships within the scheduling system. Experimental findings indicate that the proposed approach delivers superior performance in minimizing the total makespan, achieving higher solution accuracy and scheduling efficiency compared to conventional scheduling methodologies. It is suitable for complex dual-resource flexible job shop scheduling scenarios.

title: Uncertainty-Adaptive Exploration for Deep Q-Learning via Bootstrapped Ensembles with Schedule-Mixed ϵ-Greedy Policies
authors: Siwei Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Efficient exploration remains a central challenge for deep reinforcement learning, where a fixed ϵ-greedy schedule can be overly conservative early on or unnecessarily exploratory after the value function stabilizes. We propose Boot-Adaptive- ϵ, an uncertainty-adaptive exploration framework built on Bootstrapped DQNs with K bootstrap heads. At each step, we compute an epistemic uncertainty proxy from head disagreement, standardize it online via exponential moving statistics, and map it through a sigmoid to obtain a bounded exploration rate $\epsilon \in\left[\epsilon_{\text {min }}, \epsilon_{\text {max }}\right]$. To prevent late-stage over-exploration while preserving adaptivity, we further introduce a schedule-mixed controller that interpolates the uncertainty-driven ϵ with a conventional decay schedule, with optional smoothing to reduce exploration jitter. We implement a experimental pipeline with multi-seed sweeps, greedy evaluations, and unified reporting of time-to-threshold (TTT), evaluation AUC, and final performance. Experiments on classic-control benchmarks show that Boot-Adaptive- ϵ improves seed-wise reliability over a fixed-schedule bootstrapped ablation and provides competitive sample-efficiency under a fixed interaction budget.

title: Image Processing and Data-Driven Control for Tobacco Moisture at Moisture Regain Machine Outlet
authors: Xiaonan Huang, Minhui Luo, Feng Guo
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: To address the issues of low precision and poor stability in tobacco moisture control during low-temperature moisture regain, an intelligent control system centered on image processing and data analytics is proposed. The system integrates high-precision visual feature extraction, multi-source data fusion, and data-driven adaptive control, aiming to solve the technical bottlenecks of traditional moisture control methods such as lag, environmental interference, and disconnection between detection data and actual moisture state. A professional machine vision system is deployed to capture high-quality tobacco surface images at the inlet and outlet of the moisture regain machine. A series of image processing techniques-including Gaussian filtering for noise reduction, OTSU thresholding for background segmentation, and perspective correction for geometric normalization-are adopted to extract three types of moisture-related features: HSV color space parameters, gray level co-occurrence matrix (GLCM) texture parameters, and Sobel edge features. These visual features are then fused with traditional sensor data and environmental parameters to construct a comprehensive multi-dimensional dataset. A Random Forest prediction model, optimized via 5-fold cross-validation and grid search, is established to predict the outlet moisture content accurately. Furthermore, an area-integral adaptive control algorithm is designed to dynamically adjust the water addition coefficient based on the prediction results and feature importance analysis. Experimental results conducted in a real tobacco primary processing workshop show that the system achieves a feature extraction accuracy of 98.7%, with the prediction model exhibiting a root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.18 and a coefficient of determination ($R^{2}$) of 0.94. Compared with the traditional PID control system, the proposed system reduces the moisture standard deviation (SD) from 0.61 to 0.32, achieves a 100% excellence rate in SD ($\mathrm{SD}{\lt}0.5$), and increases the process capability index (CPK) by more than 0.80.

title: Computer-Aided Parallel Corpus Construction and Intelligent Retrieval System for English Translation
authors: Jia He
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: To address the problems of low alignment accuracy and poor retrieval efficiency in existing parallel corpora for English translation, as well as the poor adaptability of traditional algorithms to professional translation scenarios, this paper designs and implements a related computer-aided system and proposes a semantically enhanced bidirectional cross-lingual alignmentretrieval integrated algorithm (SE-BLAR). This algorithm breaks through the limitations of the traditional two-step approach, incorporating translation scenario features to construct a bidirectional matching model, achieving simultaneous optimization of alignment and retrieval. Experiments were conducted using three types of corpora (a test set of $\mathbf{2 6 0, 0 0 0}$ sentence pairs) for comparison with traditional algorithms. The results show that the algorithm achieves the highest alignment accuracy of 98.52%, a retrieval $\mathbf{P} \text{@} 10$ accuracy of 98.25%, and an average response time of 0.32 s, significantly outperforming the comparison algorithms. The constructed system is functionally complete and easy to use, providing accurate corpus support for English translation, improving translation efficiency and quality, and possessing high engineering application value and theoretical significance.

title: Research on Ship Hull Number Text Detection in Complex Inland Waterway Environments
authors: Yipeng Li, Hua Yang, Chengjie Zhu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: To address the challenges of small targets, tilted text, and strong background interference in ship hull number recognition under complex inland scenarios, this paper proposes an end-to-end detection and recognition framework that integrates an improved YOLOv11_CSG detector with PP-OCR. A representative ship hull number dataset is constructed via scenariodriven data augmentation to enhance data diversity and model generalization. The proposed YOLOv11_CSG incorporates SelfCalibrated Convolution (SCConv) into the C3k2 module and introduces weighted bidirectional feature pyramid fusion network (WBFPN) in the neck, thereby strengthening multi-scale feature fusion and improving the localization of small-scale hull numbers. PP-OCR is then applied to decode detected regions into character sequences, forming a practical “Detection + OCR” recognition method. Experimental results show that data augmentation improves $\mathrm{mAP} \text{@} 0.5$ by 5.4%, and the proposed detector achieves an $\mathrm{mAP} \text{@} 0.5$ of 0.935, demonstrating robust performance and practical applicability in complex inland environments.

title: Research on Optimal Design of Digital Intelligent Security System
authors: Yunhua Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: To address the bottlenecks of traditional security systems such as low integration, insufficient intelligence, and poor scalability, this study takes Dai Garden Hotel as the research object and proposes a “full-link digitization” system architecture. Through four-layer optimization of “high-definition acquisition, standardized transmission, dynamic storage, and integrated application”, the deep integration of security and fire protection systems is realized. The architecture adopts 1080P / 4K high-definition network cameras, intelligent Ethernet I/O modules, and a security-fire integration management platform, integrating 8 subsystems including video surveillance, fire alarm, and water pressure monitoring, with cross-system linkage via the Modbus-TCP protocol. Empirical results show that after transformation, the system’s image resolution is improved by 4 times, storage occupancy is reduced by 35%, fault response time is shortened by 50%, and expansion cost is decreased by 60%. This research provides a reusable technical paradigm for the digital transformation of security in complex scenarios such as hotels and tobacco industry, realizing cross-platform docking through protocol standardization and promoting the upgrade of security systems from passive monitoring to active defense.

title: Context Definition for Large Language Model Validation and Enhancement
authors: Brian Feng, David Alfred Ostrowski
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated substantial promise across the enterprise ranging from software development to task automation. Most notably among the challenges specifically in support of agentic-based implementations are management of their tendency to present fabricated information as factually accurate data. This necessitates the implementation of methodologies to support improvement in LLM accuracy followed by a means of selfcorrection via model enhancement. In support of model validation and mitigation of factually incorrect data, a framework is identified to enhance the performance of LLMs allowing for enhanced efficiency and credibility. This framework emphasizes a consensus-based means of model validation by comparing output from multiple competing models. In addition, the framework supports a means of enhancement via a feedback mechanism leveraging a retrieval augmented generation (RAG) methodology allowing for improved context in subsequent iterations. Preliminary results show promise with mitigation of hallucinations in the majority of test cases.

title: Study on Joint Denoising Algorithm Based on Improved Threshold Function
authors: Enxiu Shi, Wenjing Li, Weiming Yuan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: To address the issue of low localization accuracy caused by noisy pipeline leakage acoustic emission signals, this paper proposes a Joint Denoising Algorithm (JDA) based on Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD) and an Improved Threshold Function (ITF). Firstly, an ITF is designed to resolve problems such as discontinuity at the threshold and constant deviation. Secondly, the signal is decomposed into the Intrinsic Mode Functions (IMFs) via VMD. The IMFs low-correlation with the original signal are processed using the IWTF, then reconstructed with high-correlation IMFs to obtain a high-quality denoised signal. Simulation results demonstrate that, under various noise levels, JDA outperforms methods such as EMD and VMD in terms of Signal-to-Noise Ratio and Mean Square Error. Furthermore, experimental validation on a pipeline leakage test platform, combined with SCOT-weighted generalized cross-correlation for time delay estimation and localization, shows that JDA effectively suppresses noise and significantly improves localization accuracy (e.g., achieving an average error of 2.3 % – 6.0 % at a sensor spacing of 5 meters). This provides an effective preprocessing solution for precise pipeline leak localization using acoustic methods.

title: Gated Recurrent Unit Based Speech Emotion Recognition Using Korean Prosodic Features
authors: Lu Cao, Bo Zhu, Zhanbo Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: This study proposes a GRU based architecture for Korean speech emotion recognition that explicitly models temporal dynamics of prosodic features. Using the KEMDY-19 dataset with 12,457 audio samples, we extract seven prosodic parameters per accentual phrase including F0 statistics, vowel duration, speaking rate, and sentence final $F 0$ shifts. The five layer bidirectional GRU with hierarchical attention captures long range dependencies across phrase sequences, achieving 64.69% accuracy and 64.71% unweighted average recall. Results demonstrate linguistically motivated segmentation outperforms static feature approaches. Disgust achieves highest F1 at $\mathbf{7 2. 2 8 \%}$ while Happy performs worst at $\mathbf{4 9. 6 2 \%}$ due to class imbalance and cultural expression subtleties. This work establishes a foundation for language aware affective computing by prioritizing prosodic structure over language agnostic spectral classification, providing insights for languages with rich prosodic systems and culture specific emotion patterns.

title: Multiband RF-Based Non-Invasive Glucose Sensing Using a Fractal-Slotted Patch Antenna
authors: Zaid A. Abdul Hassain, Malik J. Farhan, T. A. Oleiwi, Raed Abd-Alhameed
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: A multiband microwave sensor integrated within a compact RF transceiver system has been developed and experimentally validated for non-invasive glucose monitoring. The proposed architecture employs a pair of identical fractal-slotted patch antennas operating across multiple resonant frequencies—2.78, 6, 6.8, 8.62, and 9.08 GHz—allowing the system to probe the dielectric response of biological tissue over a broad spectral range. Utilizing a realistic five-layer finger phantom (skin, fat, muscle, blood, and bone) and modeling blood permittivity based on the Cole–Cole equation, the sensor’s transmission response S21 was analyzed under varying glucose concentrations from 0 to 500 mg/dL. The system demonstrated measurable and repeatable shifts in S21 magnitude, phase, and resonance frequency across the operating bands. The key sensitivity outcomes include: A peak frequency-shift-based sensitivity of 0.561 MHz/(mg/dL) in the 9.08–9.28 GHz band, a maximum magnitude sensitivity of 0.041 dB/(mg/dL) at 9.08 GHz, and a notable maximum phase sensitivity of 0.176°/(mg/dL) at 8.62 GHz. These results emphasize the multiband nature of the sensor and its ability to leverage different RF features—magnitude, frequency shift, and phase—for robust and versatile non-invasive glucose detection. The integration of such RF-based multi-dimensional sensing into a single system provides a promising pathway toward compact, calibration-free, and wearable microwave biosensors. Moreover, experimental validation confirmed the agreement between simulated and measured responses, demonstrating the practical feasibility of the proposed sensor design. To further exploit the rich multiband and multi-parameter RF responses, a deep learning framework is employed to model the nonlinear relationship between glucose concentration and the extracted electromagnetic features, improving estimation robustness and noise resilience without explicit per-band calibration. Experimental validation demonstrates good agreement with full-wave simulations, confirming the practical feasibility of the proposed multi-dimensional RF sensing and learning-based glucose monitoring platform. All full-wave electromagnetic simulations were performed using CST Studio Suite with a frequency-domain finite element solver.

title: Learning and Adaptation via Contractive Vector Fields and Non-Euclidean Metrics
authors: Ali Tavasoli
publish: IEEE Control Systems Letters
abstract: This letter studies adaptive control and online learning for contractive dynamical systems. In the first part, we develop adaptive control laws based on contractive vector fields, highlighting the role of metric selection in shaping adaptation behavior. The proposed framework is sufficiently general to incorporate unconventional non-Euclidean metrics and vector-field-based adaptation laws that more faithfully capture the local differential geometry of the dynamics. In the second part, we formulate online learning with control as a hybrid continuous–discrete dynamical system, reflecting the sampled-data nature of learning algorithms coupled with continuously evolving plant dynamics. We establish sublinear control regret by leveraging tools from online convex optimization. We present different numerical examples to illustrate key features of the proposed approaches.
